  combo_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable marketing name of the combo meal as displayed on menu boards, POS screens, and digital ordering channels (e.g., \'Classic Value Meal\', \'Family Pack\', \'Kids Meal\'). Used for guest-facing display and reporting.']
  combo_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the combo meal offering. Controls whether the combo is available for sale across channels. \'test\' indicates a market-test combo not yet in full rollout.']
  combo_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the combo meal by bundle format. Drives menu engineering analysis, PMIX reporting, and promotional targeting. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: value_meal|family_pack|kids_meal|party_pack|snack_combo|premium_combo — promote to reference product if additional types are needed]']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code indicating the country where this combo meal is offered. Supports global menu management, regulatory compliance, and multi-currency pricing.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the combo meal record was first created in the data platform. Follows ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports audit trail and data lineage in the Silver layer.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code applicable to all monetary fields on this combo meal record (e.g., USD, CAD, GBP). Supports multi-currency operations across global franchise markets.']
  daypart varchar [note: 'Daypart(s) during which this combo meal is available for sale. Daypart segmentation is a core foodservice operational concept used in PMIX analysis, labor scheduling, and revenue management.']
  discontinue_date date [note: 'The date on which the combo meal was or is scheduled to be removed from the menu. Nullable for permanent menu fixtures. Used for menu lifecycle management and supply chain wind-down planning.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date on which the current version of the combo meal record ceases to be operationally effective. Nullable for open-ended records. Supports menu versioning and temporal pricing management.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date from which the current version of the combo meal record (including pricing, components, and availability) is operationally effective. Supports menu versioning and temporal pricing management.']
  food_cost_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Cost of Goods Sold as a percentage of the bundle price (item_cost / bundle_price * 100). Core menu engineering KPI used to classify combo meals and drive pricing decisions. Aligns with CoGS% jargon in foodservice P&L management.']
  image_url varchar [note: 'URL of the primary product image for the combo meal used on digital menu boards, Olo OLO platform, and 3PD channel listings. Must be a publicly accessible HTTPS URL.']
  individual_items_price_sum decimal(10,2) [note: 'The sum of the base prices of all individual component menu items if purchased separately. Used to calculate the bundle discount amount and communicate value to guests. Sourced from Oracle MICROS POS item pricing.']
  is_3pd_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this combo meal is available through Third-Party Delivery (3PD) partners (e.g., DoorDash, Uber Eats, Grubhub). Controls 3PD channel menu syndication via Olo integration.']
  is_customizable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether guests can substitute or modify component items within the combo meal (e.g., swap side item, upsize drink). Drives modifier group display logic in Oracle MICROS POS and Olo.']
  is_dine_in_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this combo meal is available for dine-in service. Relevant for casual and fine-dining formats where combo availability may differ by service mode.']
  is_dt_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this combo meal is available for sale through the Drive-Thru (DT) channel. Controls channel-specific menu display in Oracle MICROS POS and KDS routing.']
  is_national_launch boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this combo meal is part of a national rollout versus a regional or test-market offering. Drives marketing campaign scope, supply chain planning, and franchise communication.']
  is_olo_available boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this combo meal is available through the Olo Digital Ordering Platform (OLO) for web and mobile ordering. Controls digital menu availability.']
  is_taxable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the combo meal bundle price is subject to sales tax. Drives tax calculation logic in Oracle MICROS POS and SAP S/4HANA AR module. Tax applicability may vary by jurisdiction.']
  item_cost decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) for the combo meal, representing the combined food cost of all component items. Used to calculate COGS% and support P&L and menu engineering decisions. Sourced from MarketMan Inventory Management and SAP S/4HANA CO module.']
  launch_date date [note: 'The date on which the combo meal became or is scheduled to become available for sale. Used for menu lifecycle management, supply chain lead time planning, and marketing campaign alignment.']
  menu_engineering_class varchar [note: 'Menu engineering quadrant classification for the combo meal based on profitability and popularity: \'star\' (high profit, high popularity), \'plow_horse\' (low profit, high popularity), \'puzzle\' (high profit, low popularity), \'dog\' (low profit, low popularity). Core menu engineering decision-support field.']
  olo_item_code varchar [note: 'Olo Digital Ordering Platform external identifier for this combo meal. Used for menu synchronization between the internal menu master and the Olo platform for web, mobile, and 3PD channel ordering.']
  ownership_model varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this combo meal is available at franchise-operated locations, company-owned locations, or both. Supports franchise compliance tracking via FranConnect and royalty reporting.']
  pmix_target_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target Product Mix (PMIX) percentage representing the expected share of total transactions this combo meal should represent within its category. Used for menu performance benchmarking and sales forecasting.']
  pos_item_code varchar [note: 'Oracle MICROS POS-specific item code used to ring up the combo meal at the point of sale. This is the PLU/button code used by FOH (Front of House) staff and KDS (Kitchen Display System) routing.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Geographic region code where this combo meal is available (e.g., \'NORTHEAST\', \'WEST\', \'SOUTH\'). Supports regional menu variation, pricing strategy, and PMIX analysis across the restaurant network.']
  restaurant_format varchar [note: 'Restaurant format(s) for which this combo meal is applicable. QSR (Quick-Service Restaurant) combos differ structurally from casual or fine-dining bundles. Drives menu engineering and franchise compliance.']
  sort_order varchar [note: 'Display sequence order for the combo meal on menu boards, POS screens, and digital ordering interfaces. Lower values appear first. Controls guest-facing menu presentation.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifies the operational system of record from which this combo meal record originated (e.g., Oracle MICROS POS, Olo Digital Ordering Platform). Supports data lineage tracking in the Databricks Lakehouse Silver layer.']
  tax_category_code varchar [note: 'Tax category code assigned to the combo meal for sales tax calculation in Oracle MICROS POS and SAP S/4HANA. Determines applicable tax rates by jurisdiction and product type.']
  total_calories varchar [note: 'Total calorie count for the combo meal as configured with default component items. Required for FDA menu labeling compliance on menus with 20+ locations. Displayed on menu boards and digital ordering platforms.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the combo meal record was last modified in the data platform. Follows ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX). Supports change tracking, audit trail, and incremental data processing in the Silver layer.']

  Note: 'Master record for bundled combo meal offerings — pre-defined combinations of menu items sold together at a bundle price (e.g., Value Meal, Family Pack, Kids Meal). Captures combo name, component items, bundle price, individual item price sum, bundle discount amount, applicable channels, daypart availability, and active status. Distinct from LTOs in that combos are permanent or semi-permanent menu fixtures.'
}

Table menu.item_86_event {
  item_86_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each 86 event record — when a menu item is temporarily pulled from service at a restaurant. Primary key of this table.']
  equipment_asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific kitchen equipment unit that failed and caused the 86 event, when reason_code = \'equipment_failure\'. Supports Repairs and Maintenance (R&M) tracking and equipment reliability analysis.']
  equipment_equipment_asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific kitchen equipment unit that failed and caused the 86 event, when reason_code = \'equipment_failure\'. Supports Repairs and Maintenance (R&M) tracking and equipment reliability analysis.']
  ingredient_lot_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.ingredient_lot. Business justification: REQUIRED: Food‑safety incident reports must link an event to the specific ingredient lot used, enabling traceability and regulatory recall reporting.']
  primary_item_reported_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee (Back of House (BOH) or Front of House (FOH) staff member) who first reported or logged the 86 event. Links to workforce employee master.']
  primary_menu_item_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the menu item that was 86\'d (removed from service). Links to the menu.menu_item master record.']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the primary supplier of the ingredient or item involved in the 86 event, particularly relevant for out_of_stock and supplier_delay reason codes. Supports supply chain root cause analysis.']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the restaurant location where the 86 event occurred. Enables operational and geographic analysis of item unavailability.']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the restaurant location where the 86 event occurred. Enables operational and geographic analysis of item unavailability.']
  channel_affected varchar [note: 'The service channel(s) impacted by the 86 event. \'all\' = item unavailable across all channels; \'dine_in\' = in-restaurant only; \'drive_thru\' (DT) = drive-through only; \'olo\' = Online Ordering (OLO) platform; \'3pd\' = Third-Party Delivery (3PD) partners such as DoorDash or Uber Eats.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this 86 event record was first created in the data platform. Audit trail field for data lineage and Silver layer ingestion tracking.']
  daypart_affected varchar [note: 'The daypart during which the 86 event was active or first occurred. Enables PMIX (Product Mix) and daypart-level impact analysis. Aligns with menu.daypart definitions.']
  duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total elapsed time in minutes from start_timestamp to end_timestamp. Populated upon resolution. Used for Speed of Service (SOS) impact analysis and operational KPI reporting.']
  end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the menu item was restored to service and the 86 event was closed. Null if the event is still open. Used to calculate total downtime duration.']
  escalated_to_team varchar [note: 'The internal team to which the 86 event was escalated, if applicable. Supports accountability tracking and cross-functional response coordination.']
  escalation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the 86 event was escalated to operations management, supply chain, or franchise support teams. Null if the event was resolved without escalation. Supports SLA and response time analysis.']
  estimated_lost_covers varchar [note: 'Estimated number of guest covers (transactions) that could not be fulfilled due to the 86 event during the affected period. Derived operationally at the restaurant level for guest experience impact reporting. Not a calculated KPI — captured as a manager estimate at time of resolution.']
  event_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable, externally referenceable identifier for the 86 event (e.g., 86-2024-07-15-A3F9K2). Used in operational communications, Zenput audit tasks, and guest experience escalations.']
  event_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the 86 event. \'open\' = item currently unavailable; \'resolved\' = item restored to service; \'escalated\' = issue elevated to operations or supply chain team; \'cancelled\' = event logged in error.']
  guest_notification_sent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a guest-facing notification was sent (e.g., via OLO or 3PD platform) to inform customers that the item is temporarily unavailable. True = notification dispatched. Supports Customer Satisfaction (CSAT) and Net Promoter Score (NPS) impact tracking.']
  haccp_ccp_reference varchar [note: 'Reference code to the specific HACCP Critical Control Point (CCP) that was breached or triggered this 86 event, if applicable. Used for food safety audit trails and regulatory compliance reporting.']
  inventory_quantity_on_hand decimal(10,3) [note: 'The recorded inventory quantity on hand for the primary ingredient or item at the time the 86 event was triggered. Captured from MarketMan at event time. Supports PAR Level (Periodic Automatic Replenishment Level) analysis and stockout root cause investigation.']
  inventory_unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the inventory_quantity_on_hand value (e.g., each, oz, lb, kg, liter, gallon, portion). Ensures correct interpretation of the quantity field. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: each|oz|lb|kg|liter|gallon|portion — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  is_food_safety_related boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the 86 event was triggered by a food safety concern (e.g., HACCP Critical Control Point (CCP) failure, quality issue, or regulatory recall). True = food safety event requiring Zenput audit task and potential regulatory notification.']
  is_lto_item boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the 86\'d item is a Limited Time Offer (LTO). True = LTO item. 86 events on LTO items carry heightened business impact due to marketing commitments and guest expectations, and may require immediate escalation.']
  is_recall_related boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the 86 event was triggered by a formal supplier or regulatory product recall. True = item must not be served and recall protocol must be followed per FDA/USDA guidelines.']
  kds_alert_sent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Kitchen Display System (KDS) alert was sent to BOH staff notifying them of the 86 event. True = KDS alert dispatched. Supports BOH operational awareness and throughput management.']
  olo_item_code varchar [note: 'The item identifier in the Olo Digital Ordering Platform at the time of the 86 event. Used to confirm whether the item was also suppressed on the OLO channel during the event.']
  olo_suppressed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the item was suppressed on the Olo Digital Ordering Platform during the 86 event. True = item hidden from online ordering menu. Prevents guest orders for unavailable items on digital channels.']
  ownership_model varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the restaurant where the 86 event occurred is a franchise location or a company-owned location. Drives escalation routing and compliance accountability.']
  par_level_quantity decimal(10,3) [note: 'The Periodic Automatic Replenishment (PAR) Level quantity set for the item at this restaurant at the time of the 86 event. Compared against inventory_quantity_on_hand to assess whether the stockout was preventable.']
  pos_item_code varchar [note: 'The Point of Sale (POS) item code as configured in Oracle MICROS POS at the time of the 86 event. Captured at event time to preserve the POS state even if the menu item code changes later.']
  pos_suppressed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the item was actively suppressed (hidden/disabled) on the Oracle MICROS POS system during the 86 event. True = item removed from POS ordering screens. Ensures operational accuracy and prevents staff from ringing up unavailable items.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized reason code explaining why the menu item was removed from service. \'out_of_stock\' = inventory depleted; \'equipment_failure\' = kitchen equipment down; \'quality_issue\' = item failed quality/HACCP check; \'recall\' = supplier or regulatory recall; \'supplier_delay\' = ingredient not delivered; \'prep_time_exceeded\' = throughput constraint. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: out_of_stock|equipment_failure|quality_issue|recall|supplier_delay|prep_time_exceeded — promote to reference product]']
  reason_detail varchar [note: 'Free-text narrative providing additional context beyond the reason_code. For example, specifying which piece of equipment failed, which ingredient was out of stock, or the nature of the quality issue. Supports root cause analysis.']
  reported_by_role varchar [note: 'Job role of the employee who reported the 86 event (e.g., manager, Back of House (BOH) staff, Front of House (FOH) staff, shift lead, franchisee). Supports accountability and training gap analysis.']
  resolution_action varchar [note: 'The corrective action taken to resolve the 86 event and restore the item to service. \'restocked\' = inventory replenished; \'equipment_repaired\' = kitchen equipment fixed; \'item_substituted\' = alternative item offered; \'recalled_item_removed\' = item permanently pulled per recall; \'supplier_order_placed\' = emergency procurement initiated; \'no_action\' = event closed without resolution (e.g., end of daypart). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: restocked|equipment_repaired|item_substituted|recalled_item_removed|supplier_order_placed|no_action — promote to reference product]']
  restaurant_format varchar [note: 'The restaurant format (Quick-Service Restaurant (QSR), casual, or fine-dining) of the location where the 86 event occurred. Supports format-level operational benchmarking.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this 86 event was originated or captured. \'micros_pos\' = Oracle MICROS POS; \'zenput\' = Zenput operational compliance; \'marketman\' = MarketMan inventory alert; \'olo\' = Olo platform suppression event; \'manual\' = manually entered by operations staff.']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the menu item was first removed from service (86\'d). This is the principal real-world event timestamp. Stored in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this 86 event record was last modified in the data platform, such as when the event was resolved, escalated, or corrected. Supports data lineage and change tracking in the Silver layer.']
  zenput_task_code varchar [note: 'Reference to the Zenput food safety audit task or operational compliance task created in response to this 86 event. Enables traceability between the 86 event and the corrective action workflow in Zenput.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing an \'86\' event — when a menu item is temporarily removed from service at a specific restaurant due to stockout, quality issue, or equipment failure. Captures the item, restaurant, channel affected, start timestamp, end timestamp, reason code (out-of-stock/equipment/quality/recall), reported-by, and resolution action. Critical for operational compliance and guest experience tracking.'
}

Table menu.dietary_tag {
  dietary_tag_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the dietary_tag data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']
  parent_dietary_tag_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on dietary_tag (parent_dietary_tag_id)']
  category varchar [note: 'Broad classification of the tag indicating its origin: allergen‑derived, lifestyle, religious, health‑condition, or other.']
  certification_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the tag must be backed by an external certification (true) or is self‑declared (false).']
  certifying_body varchar [note: 'Name of the organization that provides certification for the tag, e.g., "USDA Organic", "Halal Certification Authority".']
  code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code used for system integration and display, e.g., "VG" for vegan.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the tag record was first created in the system.']
  display_order varchar [note: 'Integer determining the sequence in which tags appear in UI lists; lower numbers appear first.']
  effective_from timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the tag becomes valid for use in menu displays and filtering.']
  effective_until timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the tag expires or is no longer valid; null if indefinite.']
  guest_label varchar [note: 'Text label displayed to customers on menus and digital channels.']
  icon_url varchar [note: 'Web URL pointing to the icon image shown to guests for this tag.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the dietary tag, e.g., "Vegan", "Gluten‑Free".']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for internal comments, audit trails, or special handling instructions.']
  priority_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric weight used by recommendation engines to rank tags when multiple apply.']
  scope varchar [note: 'Geographic or market scope where the tag is applicable.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the tag: active (available for use), inactive (temporarily unavailable), or deprecated (to be retired).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the tag record.']

  Note: 'Master record for dietary and lifestyle classification tags applied to menu items — e.g., vegan, vegetarian, keto, halal, kosher, gluten-free, dairy-free, low-sodium, heart-healthy. Captures tag name, tag category (allergen-derived, lifestyle, religious, health-condition), certification requirement flag, certifying body reference, display icon, guest-facing label, and active status. Enables OLO/3PD menu filtering, personalized recommendations, and regulatory compliance for voluntary dietary claims. Distinct from allergen_declaration (which is mandatory FDA/FALCPA compliance) — dietary_tag covers voluntary marketing and guest-experience classifications.'
}

Table menu.combo_component {
  combo_component_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the combo_component association']
  combo_meal_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the combo_meal']
  menu_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the menu_item']
  is_featured boolean [note: 'Indicates if the item is highlighted in the combo presentation']
  item_price_override decimal [note: 'Adjusted price for the menu_item when sold as part of the combo']
  quantity varchar [note: 'Number of units of the menu_item included in the combo']
  sort_order varchar [note: 'Order in which the item appears within the combo listing']

  Note: 'Represents the association between a combo_meal and a menu_item. Each record captures the specific items that make up a combo, including quantity, any price override, display order, and whether the item is featured in the combo.. Existence Justification: A combo meal is a bundle that consists of multiple menu items, and a single menu item can appear in many different combo meals. The business actively manages the composition of each combo, tracking quantities, price overrides, display order, and whether the item is featured within the combo. This many‑to‑many relationship is a core operational entity used for menu engineering and pricing.'
}

Table menu.dietary_tag_assignment {
  dietary_tag_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the dietary_tag_assignment association']
  dietary_tag_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to dietary_tag']
  menu_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to menu_item']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the tag assignment expires or is removed']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the tag assignment becomes effective for the menu item']
  is_primary boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this tag is the primary dietary classification for the menu item']

  Note: 'Represents the assignment of dietary tags to menu items, capturing which tags apply to each item and the validity period of each assignment.. Existence Justification: Each menu item can be associated with multiple dietary tags (e.g., vegan, gluten‑free) and each tag can apply to many menu items. The business actively manages these assignments, tracking which tags are primary for an item and the effective start/end dates of each assignment.'
}

Table guest.profile {
  profile_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the guest profile record.']
  bank_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.bank_account. Business justification: Direct‑debit setup for recurring meal subscriptions links guest profile to finance bank_account for payment processing.']
  corporate_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to guest.corporate_account. Business justification: Corporate accounts are a B2B guest entity; linking profile to corporate_account enables reporting of corporate guest activity and eliminates the isolated corporate_account table.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Corporate guest expense allocation requires linking profile to finance cost_center for accurate cost accounting reports.']
  franchisee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to franchise.franchisee. Business justification: Franchisee‑level loyalty aggregation report needs each guest linked to the franchisee that owns their primary restaurant.']
  location_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the location the guest most frequently visits.']
  loyalty_program_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the loyalty program membership associated with the guest.']
  menu_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to menu.menu_item. Business justification: Required for personalized marketing: the last ordered menu item is used to drive targeted offers and recommendation engines, a standard practice in restaurant loyalty programs.']
  preferred_store_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific store the guest prefers.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Assigning guest to a profit_center enables revenue attribution in multi‑brand restaurant profit reporting.']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific store the guest prefers.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the guest\'s street address.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the guest\'s street address (apartment, suite, etc.).']
  average_check_value decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average monetary value per transaction for the guest.']
  birthday_day varchar [note: 'Day of month (1‑31) of the guest\'s birth date.']
  birthday_month varchar [note: 'Numeric month (1‑12) of the guest\'s birth date, used for birthday promotions.']
  city varchar [note: 'City component of the guest\'s mailing address.']
  consent_email boolean [note: 'Guest\'s consent to receive marketing emails.']
  consent_privacy boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the guest has consented to privacy policy and data processing.']
  consent_sms boolean [note: 'Guest\'s consent to receive marketing SMS messages.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the guest\'s residence. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USA|CAN|MEX|GBR|FRA|DEU|JPN|CHN|IND|BRA — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  data_source varchar [note: 'System of record that supplied the guest data.']
  data_source_code varchar [note: 'Original identifier of the guest in the source system.']
  date_of_birth date [note: 'Guest\'s birth date for age verification and personalization.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email used for electronic communication and marketing.']
  first_name varchar [note: 'Given name of the guest.']
  full_name varchar [note: 'Complete legal name of the guest as stored in the master record.']
  gender varchar [note: 'Self‑declared gender of the guest for demographic analysis.']
  guest_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the guest based on relationship to the business.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Family name of the guest.']
  last_visit_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent guest interaction.']
  loyalty_tier varchar [note: 'Current tier level of the guest within the loyalty program.']
  marketing_opt_in boolean [note: 'Overall opt‑in flag for marketing communications across all channels.']
  marketing_source varchar [note: 'Origin channel through which the guest was first acquired.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Unstructured notes entered by staff about the guest.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Main telephone number for SMS, voice contact, and verification.']
  picture_url varchar [note: 'Link to the guest\'s profile image stored in the digital asset system.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the guest\'s mailing address.']
  preferred_language varchar [note: 'Language the guest prefers for communications. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: en|es|fr|de|zh|ja|pt — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  primary_contact_method varchar [note: 'Channel the guest most frequently uses for contact.']
  profile_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the guest profile.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the profile record was first created.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent modification to the profile.']
  secondary_phone varchar [note: 'Optional additional telephone number for the guest.']
  state varchar [note: 'State or province of the guest\'s mailing address.']
  total_lifetime_visits varchar [note: 'Cumulative count of all visits (in‑store, drive‑thru, online) made by the guest.']
  total_spent decimal(12,2) [note: 'Aggregate monetary amount the guest has spent across all transactions.']

  Note: 'Master record for every guest identity across all service channels (dine-in, drive-thru, OLO, 3PD). Single source of truth for WHO the business serves — captures full identity and demographic attributes including name, contact details, date of birth, language preference, age band, gender identity, household income band, education level, employment status, geographic market classification, demographic data source (self-declared vs. third-party enrichment), enrichment provider and date. Also owns digital account attributes: username, account status (active/suspended/deactivated), registration date, registration channel, last login timestamp, device type, app version, two-factor authentication status, and account tier. Sourced primarily from Salesforce CRM, Olo Digital Ordering Platform, and brand mobile app. This is the anchor entity for the entire guest domain.'
}

Table guest.identity_resolution {
  identity_resolution_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for each identity resolution event.']
  golden_guest_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the canonical guest profile that results from the resolution process.']
  loyalty_member_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the guest within the restaurant loyalty program.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the canonical guest profile that results from the resolution process.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the guest.']
  city varchar [note: 'City component of the guest\'s address.']
  consent_status varchar [note: 'Guest\'s current consent state for marketing communications (consented, revoked, pending).']
  country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the guest\'s residence.']
  csat_score varchar [note: 'Latest CSAT rating provided by the guest (0‑10).']
  data_source_confidence_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Confidence score assigned to the source system\'s data quality for this record.']
  date_of_birth date [note: 'Guest\'s birth date, used for age‑based offers and compliance.']
  duplicate_flag boolean [note: 'True if the source record was identified as a duplicate of an existing profile.']
  full_name varchar [note: 'Legal full name of the guest as provided in the source system.']
  gender varchar [note: 'Self‑identified gender of the guest.']
  golden_record_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this record is the current authoritative (golden) guest profile.']
  guest_status varchar [note: 'Operational status of the guest record (e.g., active, inactive, blacklisted).']
  guest_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the party (e.g., regular guest, employee, vendor, franchisee, or anonymous visitor).']
  last_interaction_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent interaction with the guest across any channel.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the guest record within the identity resolution process.']
  loyalty_tier varchar [note: 'Current tier level of the guest in the loyalty program.']
  match_confidence_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric confidence (0‑100) that the source record matches the golden guest profile.']
  match_event_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why the match was performed (new record, update, or reconciliation).']
  match_method varchar [note: 'Algorithmic approach used for identity matching: deterministic (rule‑based) or probabilistic (statistical).']
  merge_event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the source record was merged into the golden profile.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for manual annotations about the identity resolution event.']
  nps_score varchar [note: 'Latest NPS rating given by the guest (0‑10).']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal/ZIP code of the guest\'s address.']
  preferred_communication_channel varchar [note: 'Channel the guest prefers for receiving messages.']
  preferred_language varchar [note: 'Guest\'s preferred language for communications.']
  primary_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address used for guest communication.']
  primary_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for contacting the guest.']
  privacy_opt_out boolean [note: 'True if the guest has opted out of data collection or profiling.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the identity resolution record was first created.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the identity resolution record.']
  segment varchar [note: 'Business‑defined segment to which the guest belongs for targeting purposes.']
  source_record_reference varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the guest record in the source system.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that supplied the source record (e.g., Oracle MICROS POS, Olo, Salesforce CRM).']
  source_system_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the source system recorded the original guest event.']
  state varchar [note: 'State or province component of the guest\'s address.']
  total_lifetime_spend decimal(12,2) [note: 'Cumulative monetary value of all purchases attributed to the guest.']

  Note: 'Tracks the matching, merging, and cross-system mapping of guest identity records across channels and systems (POS, OLO, loyalty, 3PD). Stores all channel-specific guest identifiers including source system (Oracle MICROS POS, Olo, Salesforce CRM, 3PD partner, loyalty app), external identifier, identifier type (email, phone, loyalty card number, device ID, 3PD customer ID), creation date, active status, match confidence scores, merge/split events, golden record designation, and resolution method (deterministic vs. probabilistic). Enables a unified guest view by linking fragmented identities into a single canonical profile. Supports cross-channel identity stitching, deduplication, and multi-system identifier management.'
}

Table guest.address {
  address_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the address record.']
  guest_profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the guest to whom this address belongs.']
  owner_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the owning entity (guest, restaurant, etc.).']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the guest to whom this address belongs.']
  address_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the address record.']
  address_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the address purpose.']
  building_name varchar [note: 'Name of the building, if applicable.']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality of the address.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code.']
  county varchar [note: 'County or equivalent administrative area.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'When the address record was first created in the system.']
  delivery_instructions varchar [note: 'Special instructions for delivering to this address (e.g., gate code, porch).']
  district varchar [note: 'Sub‑municipal district or neighborhood.']
  geocode_accuracy varchar [note: 'Quality level of the geocoding result.']
  is_primary boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this is the guest\'s primary address.']
  landmark varchar [note: 'Nearby landmark or point of reference to aid delivery.']
  last_verified date [note: 'Date of the most recent successful verification.']
  latitude double [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the address.']
  line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line (e.g., house number and street name).']
  line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information such as apartment, suite, or unit.']
  longitude double [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the address.']
  owner_type varchar [note: 'Entity type that owns or uses the address.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the address.']
  region varchar [note: 'Broad geographic region (e.g., Midwest, West Coast).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that supplied the address (e.g., OLO, POS, CRM).']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or region of the address.']
  suite_number varchar [note: 'Suite, unit, or floor number within a building.']
  time_zone varchar [note: 'IANA time zone identifier for the address location.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Most recent date and time the address record was modified.']
  validation_status varchar [note: 'Result of the most recent address validation attempt.']
  validation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the address was last validated.']
  validity_flag boolean [note: 'True if the address is currently considered valid for delivery.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to verify the address.']
  verification_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric confidence score (0‑100) from the verification service.']

  Note: 'Stores all physical and delivery addresses associated with a guest profile, including home address, saved delivery addresses, and billing addresses. Captures address type, street, city, state/province, postal code, country, geolocation coordinates, delivery instructions, and validation status. Supports OLO delivery fulfillment and personalized marketing by geography.'
}

Table guest.preference {
  preference_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for preference record. _canonical_skip_reason: Entity does not fit standard master or transaction roles; treated as OTHER.']
  favorite_menu_item_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the guest\'s most frequently ordered menu item.']
  guest_profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the guest to whom this preference belongs.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the guest to whom this preference belongs.']
  communication_channel_preference varchar [note: 'Guest\'s preferred channel for receiving communications.']
  consent_given boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the guest has given consent for storing this preference.']
  consent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when consent was recorded.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the preference record was first created.']
  data_source_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the source system recorded the preference.']
  device_preference varchar [note: 'Preferred device type for digital interactions.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the preference is considered effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the preference is no longer effective (null if open-ended).']
  favorite_cuisine varchar [note: 'Guest\'s preferred cuisine type (e.g., Italian, Mexican).']
  has_dairy_allergy boolean [note: 'Indicates if the guest has a dairy allergy.']
  has_gluten_allergy boolean [note: 'Indicates if the guest has a gluten allergy.']
  has_nut_allergy boolean [note: 'Indicates if the guest has a nut allergy.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the preference is currently active.']
  is_halal boolean [note: 'Indicates if the guest requires halal meals.']
  is_kosher boolean [note: 'Indicates if the guest requires kosher meals.']
  is_vegan boolean [note: 'Indicates if the guest prefers vegan meals.']
  is_vegetarian boolean [note: 'Indicates if the guest prefers vegetarian meals.']
  language_preference varchar [note: 'Guest\'s preferred language for communications.']
  loyalty_tier varchar [note: 'Current loyalty program tier of the guest.']
  marketing_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates if the guest has opted in to receive marketing communications.']
  marketing_opt_out_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided by the guest for opting out of marketing communications.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form notes or comments about the preference.']
  origin varchar [note: 'How the preference was captured.']
  preference_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the preference record.']
  preference_type varchar [note: 'Category of the preference indicating its domain. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: dietary|cuisine|menu_item|service_channel|daypart|communication|marketing|loyalty|other — promote to reference product]']
  preference_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'The actual value or description of the preference, such as "no peanuts" or "Italian".']
  preferred_daypart varchar [note: 'Time of day the guest most often dines.']
  preferred_payment_method varchar [note: 'Guest\'s favored payment method.']
  preferred_seating varchar [note: 'Guest\'s favored seating location within the restaurant.']
  preferred_service_channel varchar [note: 'Guest\'s preferred way to receive service.']
  privacy_consent_version varchar [note: 'Version of the privacy consent agreement under which the preference was collected.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System that supplied the preference data.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the preference record.']

  Note: 'Captures all guest-stated and inferred preferences, dietary restrictions, and food allergen declarations. Covers FDA major allergens (milk, eggs, fish, shellfish, tree nuts, peanuts, wheat, soybeans, sesame) with severity levels (intolerance vs. allergy), dietary restriction types (vegetarian, vegan, halal, kosher, gluten-free), declaration source (self-declared, healthcare provider), cuisine preferences, favorite menu items, preferred service channel (dine-in, DT, OLO), preferred daypart, communication channel preferences (email, SMS, push), and marketing opt-in/opt-out flags. Sourced from Salesforce CRM, Olo guest data, and guest self-declaration. Drives personalization, targeted marketing, and HACCP-aligned guest food safety compliance including FDA allergen labeling requirements.'
}

Table guest.consent_record {
  consent_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the consent record.']
  consent_policy_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the privacy policy document referenced by this consent.']
  guest_profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the guest to whom this consent applies.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the guest to whom this consent applies.']
  consent_expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the consent automatically expires, if applicable.']
  consent_language varchar [note: 'Two‑letter language code of the consent notice presented to the guest.']
  consent_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the guest expressed consent (opt‑in, opt‑out, implied).']
  consent_purpose varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of the business purpose for which consent was obtained.']
  consent_revoked_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text reason provided by the guest for withdrawing consent, if any.']
  consent_revoked_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the guest withdrew the consent.']
  consent_source_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the guest provided consent.']
  consent_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the consent.']
  consent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the consent was originally given.']
  consent_type varchar [note: 'Category of consent (e.g., marketing, SMS, email, data sharing, profiling).']
  consent_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the privacy policy or consent notice at the time of consent.']
  created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the consent record was first created in the system.']
  data_processing_scope varchar [note: 'Scope of data processing covered by the consent.']
  data_sharing_consent boolean [note: 'True if the guest consented to internal data sharing for analytics or personalization.']
  device_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the device used to capture consent (e.g., mobile device ID).']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the consent is considered active.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date until which the consent remains valid (null if open‑ended).']
  email_consent boolean [note: 'True if the guest consented to receive email communications.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address from which the consent was captured.']
  marketing_consent boolean [note: 'True if the guest consented to receive marketing communications.']
  privacy_notice_version varchar [note: 'Identifier of the privacy notice version referenced by this consent.']
  sms_consent boolean [note: 'True if the guest consented to receive SMS messages.']
  third_party_consent boolean [note: 'True if the guest consented to share data with approved third parties.']
  updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the consent record.']

  Note: 'Authoritative record of guest consent and privacy elections per regulatory requirement (GDPR, CCPA, CAN-SPAM). Tracks consent type (marketing email, SMS, data sharing, profiling), consent status (granted/withdrawn), consent timestamp, consent source channel, consent version/policy version, and expiry date. Mandatory for compliance with FDA labeling, FTC advertising regulations, and applicable data privacy laws. Immutable audit trail of all consent changes.'
}

Table guest.guest_segment {
  guest_segment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the guest_guest_segment data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']

  Note: 'Defines guest segmentation classifications used for targeted marketing, personalization, and operational planning. Captures segment name, segment type (behavioral, demographic, value-based, lifecycle stage), definition criteria, effective date range, segment owner (marketing vs. operations), and channel applicability. Examples include high-frequency QSR visitors, lapsed guests, LTO responders, and high-ACV guests. Managed in Salesforce CRM.'
}

Table guest.guest_segment_membership {
  guest_segment_membership_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the guest_segment_membership data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']
  guest_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to guest.guest_guest_segment. Business justification: guest_segment_membership must reference the specific segment definition within the guest domain.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to guest.profile. Business justification: guest_segment_membership needs to reference the guest profile to associate a segment membership with a specific guest.']

  Note: 'Association entity recording which guests belong to which segments at any point in time. Captures segment assignment date, expiry date, assignment method (rule-based, manual, ML-model), confidence score, and the source campaign or trigger that caused the assignment. Enables time-travel queries on segment composition and supports SSS (Same-Store Sales) cohort analysis and CSAT trending by segment.'
}

Table guest.household {
  household_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the household master record.']
  address_id varchar [note: 'FK to guest.address']
  primary_member_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary guest/member designated for the household.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary guest/member designated for the household.']
  address_verification_date date [note: 'Date when the household address was last verified.']
  address_verified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the household address has been validated.']
  average_check_value decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average monetary value of transactions per visit for the household.']
  average_transaction_count varchar [note: 'Average number of transactions per defined period for the household.']
  consent_privacy boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the household has given consent for data processing under privacy regulations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the household record was first created.']
  dissolution_date date [note: 'Date when the household was closed or merged; null if still active.']
  estimated_income_band varchar [note: 'Income bracket estimate for the household used for segmentation.']
  formation_date date [note: 'Date when the household was first created in the system.']
  household_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name for the household (e.g., Smith Family, Johnson Household).']
  household_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the household record.']
  household_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the household composition.']
  last_transaction_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent transaction recorded for the household.']
  last_update_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the household record was last modified.']
  loyalty_enrolled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the household participates in the loyalty program.']
  loyalty_tier varchar [note: 'Current loyalty tier assigned to the household.']
  marketing_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the household has opted in to receive marketing communications.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments about the household.']
  preferred_channel varchar [note: 'Channel most frequently used by the household for ordering.']
  primary_contact_method varchar [note: 'Preferred channel for primary household communications.']
  primary_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address used to contact the household.']
  primary_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for the household.']
  segment varchar [note: 'Analytical segment used for marketing and forecasting.']
  size varchar [note: 'Number of individuals associated with the household.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system that supplied the household record.']
  total_spend decimal(12,2) [note: 'Cumulative monetary spend of the household across all channels.']
  total_transactions varchar [note: 'Cumulative count of all transactions associated with the household.']

  Note: 'Groups individual guest profiles into household units for family-level analytics, shared loyalty benefits, and targeted household marketing. Captures household name, household size, estimated household income band, residential address, and household formation/dissolution dates. Includes member-level detail: member role (primary, secondary, dependent), relationship type, join/departure dates, and primary loyalty account holder designation. Supports cover count analysis, household-level ACV/ATC calculations, multi-member loyalty benefit sharing, and household spend aggregation.'
}

Table guest.household_member {
  household_member_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the household member record.']

  household_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the household unit to which the member belongs.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the guest (individual) linked to the household.']
  birthdate date [note: 'Member\'s date of birth.']
  consent_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member has opted in to marketing communications.']
  consent_opt_in_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the member provided consent for marketing communications.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the household member record was created in the system.']
  departure_date date [note: 'Date when the member left the household, if applicable.']
  gender varchar [note: 'Member\'s gender as reported.']
  is_primary_loyalty_holder boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member is the primary loyalty account holder for the household.']
  join_date date [note: 'Date when the member was added to the household.']
  loyalty_points_balance decimal(12,2) [note: 'Current balance of loyalty points for the member.']
  loyalty_tier varchar [note: 'Loyalty program tier assigned to the member within the household.']
  member_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the household member record.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about the household member.']
  relationship_type varchar [note: 'Type of relationship between the guest and the household (e.g., family, friend, employee).']
  role varchar [note: 'Role of the member within the household (e.g., primary, secondary, dependent).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the household member record.']

  Note: 'Association entity linking individual guest profiles to a household unit. Captures member role (primary, secondary, dependent), relationship type, join date, departure date, and whether the member is the primary loyalty account holder for the household. Enables household-level spend aggregation and multi-member loyalty benefit sharing.'
}

Table guest.lifetime_value {
  lifetime_value_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the lifetime value record.']
  guest_profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the guest to whom this lifetime value belongs.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the guest to whom this lifetime value belongs.']
  average_check_value decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average monetary value of a single transaction for the guest.']
  average_transactions_per_month decimal(5,2) [note: 'Mean count of transactions the guest completes each month.']
  consent_opt_in boolean [note: 'True if the guest has opted in to data collection and marketing communications.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the LTV record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter currency identifier for all monetary fields.']
  data_refresh_cycle varchar [note: 'Scheduled frequency at which the LTV metrics are recomputed.']
  days_since_last_visit varchar [note: 'Number of days between today and the most recent visit date.']
  first_visit_date date [note: 'Calendar date of the guest\'s inaugural purchase.']
  loyalty_member_flag boolean [note: 'True if the guest is enrolled in the restaurant\'s loyalty program.']
  ltv_calculation_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the LTV metrics were most recently refreshed.']
  ltv_last_updated timestamp [note: 'Exact moment the LTV record was last refreshed.']
  ltv_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the LTV record.']
  ltv_tier varchar [note: 'Business‑defined tier indicating the guest\'s overall value (e.g., platinum, gold).']
  most_recent_visit_date date [note: 'Calendar date of the guest\'s latest purchase.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional commentary or observations about the LTV record.']
  predicted_future_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Forecasted monetary contribution of the guest over the next 12 months.']
  segment varchar [note: 'Analytical segment assigned to the guest for targeting.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system for the transaction data used in LTV calculation.']
  total_historical_spend decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative monetary amount the guest has spent across all transactions to date.']
  total_visits varchar [note: 'Total number of distinct visits (in‑store, drive‑thru, or online) recorded for the guest.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the LTV record.']

  Note: 'Stores calculated and periodically refreshed guest lifetime value (LTV) metrics at the individual profile level. Captures total historical spend, visit frequency, average check value (ACV), average transaction count (ATC), predicted future value, LTV tier classification, first visit date, most recent visit date, and days since last visit. Refreshed on a scheduled cadence from order domain transactional data. Supports retention prioritization and loyalty tier management.'
}

Table guest.satisfaction_survey {
  satisfaction_survey_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each satisfaction survey instance.']
  franchisee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to franchise.franchisee. Business justification: Franchisee satisfaction KPI dashboard aggregates survey results per franchisee for performance monitoring.']
  guest_profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the guest who received the survey.']
  location_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific physical location (store, drive‑thru, etc.) of the visit.']
  primary_satisfaction_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant unit where the guest experience occurred.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the guest who received the survey.']
  server_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Needed for Service Quality Dashboard linking survey scores to the serving employee, supporting performance coaching and bonus calculations.']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant unit where the guest experience occurred.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑text comments entered by the guest.']
  completion_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the guest completed, partially completed, declined, or never received the survey.']
  consent_given boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the guest consented to be surveyed and to have their responses stored.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the survey record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  csat_score varchar [note: 'CSAT rating provided by the guest, typically on a 1‑5 scale.']
  daypart varchar [note: 'Business day segment during which the visit occurred.']
  delivery_channel varchar [note: 'Channel used to deliver the survey to the guest.']
  delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the survey was sent to the guest.']
  language varchar [note: 'ISO language code of the survey presented to the guest.']
  nps_score varchar [note: 'NPS rating provided by the guest on a scale of 0‑10.']
  response_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the guest submitted the survey response.']
  satisfaction_survey_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the survey record.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of record that originated the survey record.']
  survey_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the survey (Customer Satisfaction, Net Promoter Score, or post‑delivery feedback).']
  survey_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the survey questionnaire used.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the survey record.']
  visit_date date [note: 'Calendar date of the guest\'s visit, derived from visit_timestamp.']
  visit_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the guest\'s visit or order took place.']

  Note: 'Records guest satisfaction survey instances with full question-level response detail. Captures survey type (CSAT, NPS, post-delivery, post-visit), delivery channel (email, SMS, in-app, receipt QR), delivery timestamp, completion status, NPS score (0-10), CSAT score, restaurant unit, daypart, and respondent profile. Includes granular question-level data: question text, question type (rating, open-text, multiple-choice), response value, response timestamp, and sentiment classification for open-text responses. Sourced from Salesforce CRM and Olo guest feedback flows. Enables CSAT/NPS driver analysis at both survey and question level, supporting operational improvement across FOH and BOH.'
}

Table guest.survey_response {
  survey_response_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the survey response record.']
  guest_profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the guest who provided the response.']
  location_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant location where the survey was captured.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the guest who provided the response.']
  question_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the survey question being answered.']
  satisfaction_survey_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the survey to which this response belongs.']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant location where the survey was captured.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the response record was first created in the system.']
  device_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the device used to capture the response (e.g., tablet ID).']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address of the device/network used for online responses.']
  is_anonymous boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the response was submitted anonymously.']
  is_test_response boolean [note: 'Indicates if the response is a test record used for system validation.']
  open_text varchar [note: 'Free-form text provided by the guest for open-text questions.']
  rating_scale_max varchar [note: 'Maximum possible rating value for the rating scale.']
  rating_score varchar [note: 'Numeric rating score if response_type is rating or scale.']
  response_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the guest submitted the survey.']
  response_language varchar [note: 'ISO language code of the response text.']
  response_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequence number of the response within the survey for the guest.']
  response_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the response.']
  response_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the response was recorded.']
  response_type varchar [note: 'Type of the response format (e.g., rating, open text, multiple choice).']
  response_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Raw response value as captured (e.g., rating number, selected option, free text).']
  selected_option varchar [note: 'Chosen option text for multiple-choice responses.']
  sentiment_label varchar [note: 'Categorical sentiment label derived from open-text response.']
  sentiment_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Sentiment analysis score ranging from -1 (negative) to 1 (positive).']
  survey_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the survey template used.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the response record.']

  Note: 'Stores individual question-level responses within a guest satisfaction survey. Captures question text, question type (rating, open-text, multiple-choice), response value, response timestamp, and sentiment classification for open-text responses. Enables granular CSAT and NPS driver analysis at the question level, supporting operational improvement initiatives across FOH and BOH.'
}

Table guest.complaint {
  complaint_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the complaint record.']
  franchisee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to franchise.franchisee. Business justification: Franchisee complaint management system tracks complaints per franchisee to meet service quality standards.']
  guest_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the order associated with the complaint, if applicable.']
  guest_profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the guest who raised the complaint.']
  handling_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Required for Complaint Resolution Report that assigns a responsible employee to each complaint, enabling accountability and SLA tracking.']
  ingredient_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.ingredient. Business justification: Needed for food safety incident tracking: associating complaints with the specific ingredient allows root‑cause analysis, recall decisions, and FDA reporting.']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: Required for complaint resolution to identify the supplier of the product causing the complaint, enabling quality investigations and regulatory reporting.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the guest who raised the complaint.']
  restaurant_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant location where the complaint originated.']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant location where the complaint originated.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the complaint was received.']
  complaint_category varchar [note: 'Primary classification of the complaint reason.']
  complaint_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description provided by the guest detailing the issue.']
  complaint_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable business identifier assigned to the complaint (e.g., C‑20231234).']
  complaint_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the complaint.']
  complaint_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the complaint was initially recorded.']
  consent_given boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the guest consented to store and process their personal data for this complaint.']
  csat_score varchar [note: 'Post‑resolution CSAT rating provided by the guest (1‑10).']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the resolution amount.']
  escalated_to varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee or manager to whom the complaint was escalated.']
  escalation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the complaint was escalated to higher management.']
  feedback_comments varchar [note: 'Additional free‑text feedback from the guest regarding the complaint handling.']
  nps_score varchar [note: 'NPS rating captured after resolution (0‑10).']
  privacy_consent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the guest provided privacy consent.']
  record_created_at timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the complaint record was first persisted in the data lake.']
  record_updated_at timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the complaint record.']
  resolution_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary value associated with the resolution (e.g., refund amount).']
  resolution_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the complaint resolution process.']
  resolution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the complaint was resolved.']
  resolution_type varchar [note: 'Method used to resolve the complaint.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Business‑defined severity indicating impact on guest experience.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system (e.g., Salesforce Service Cloud).']

  Note: 'Operational record of a guest complaint or service recovery case raised through any channel (in-restaurant, phone, digital, social media). Captures complaint category (food quality, speed of service/SOS, order accuracy, cleanliness, staff behavior), severity level, channel of receipt, restaurant unit, associated order reference, resolution status, resolution type (refund, replacement, apology), resolution timestamp, and escalation flag. Managed in Salesforce CRM service cloud.'
}

Table guest.interaction {
  interaction_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the interaction event.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the marketing campaign associated with the interaction, if any.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: In‑Restaurant Interaction Log tracks the employee handling each guest interaction, required for training effectiveness and KPI reporting.']
  franchisee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to franchise.franchisee. Business justification: Marketing effectiveness analysis attributes guest interactions to the franchisee that ran the campaign.']
  guest_profile_id varchar [note: 'Surrogate identifier of the guest who generated the interaction.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Surrogate identifier of the guest who generated the interaction.']
  restaurant_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant unit where the interaction took place, if applicable.']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant unit where the interaction took place, if applicable.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the interaction was delivered.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the interaction record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  device_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the device or terminal that recorded the interaction (e.g., POS terminal, kiosk).']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the interaction occurred.']
  interaction_type varchar [note: 'Nature of the interaction event (e.g., email open, app click, order placement).']
  is_test boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the interaction is a test event (true) or a production event (false).']
  outcome varchar [note: 'Result of the interaction, indicating whether it succeeded or failed.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of record that generated the interaction event.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the interaction record.']

  Note: 'Unified event stream capturing every recorded touchpoint between the brand and a guest across all channels. Covers inbound interactions (app sessions, loyalty check-ins, drive-thru visits, dine-in visits, OLO sessions, 3PD orders) and outbound communications (marketing emails, SMS messages, push notifications, direct mail). Captures interaction type, direction (inbound/outbound), channel, timestamp, restaurant unit (if applicable), campaign or trigger reference, subject/content reference, delivery status, open status, click-through status, unsubscribe action, and interaction outcome. Sourced from Salesforce CRM marketing automation, Oracle MICROS POS, and Olo. Provides the raw engagement timeline for RFM modeling, communication frequency capping, suppression list management, and guest engagement scoring.'
}

Table guest.channel_identity {
  channel_identity_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each channel identity record.']
  guest_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the canonical guest profile that this channel identity belongs to.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the canonical guest profile that this channel identity belongs to.']
  channel_identity_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the channel identity record.']
  channel_name varchar [note: 'Logical name of the channel source (e.g., POS, Online Ordering, CRM, Third‑Party Delivery, Loyalty App).']
  channel_user_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the guest in the source channel.']
  channel_user_name varchar [note: 'Display name of the guest as known in the source channel.']
  channel_user_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the guest in the source channel.']
  consent_opt_in boolean [note: 'Guest\'s consent to receive marketing communications via this channel.']
  created_by_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that initially created the channel identity record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the channel identity record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the external identifier became valid for the guest.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the external identifier ceased to be valid (null if still active).']
  external_identifier varchar [note: 'Identifier assigned to the guest in the source system (e.g., email address, loyalty card number, device ID).']
  identifier_type varchar [note: 'Category describing the nature of the external identifier.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the channel identity is currently active for the guest.']
  is_primary boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this channel is the primary identity source for the guest.']
  is_test_account boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the record belongs to a test or sandbox account.']
  last_updated_by_system varchar [note: 'Name of the system that performed the most recent update.']
  last_verified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'When the external identifier was last verified as belonging to the guest.']
  loyalty_tier varchar [note: 'Guest\'s loyalty tier as recorded in the channel (if applicable).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Optional free‑text comments or remarks about the channel identity.']
  privacy_status varchar [note: 'Current privacy state for the channel identity (consented, revoked, unknown).']
  record_status varchar [note: 'Operational status of the record within the data lake (e.g., current, archived, deleted).']
  record_version varchar [note: 'Monotonically increasing version number for optimistic concurrency control.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Technical system that originated the external identifier.']
  source_system_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the source system first created the external identifier.']
  source_system_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent change to the external identifier in the source system.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the channel identity record.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to verify the external identifier.']

  Note: 'Stores channel-specific guest identifiers that map back to the canonical guest profile. Captures the source system (Oracle MICROS POS, Olo, Salesforce CRM, 3PD partner, loyalty app), the external identifier in that system, identifier type (email, phone, loyalty card number, device ID, 3PD customer ID), creation date, and active status. Enables cross-channel identity stitching and supports the identity resolution process.'
}

Table guest.communication {
  communication_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each outbound communication record.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the marketing campaign that generated this communication.']
  consent_consent_record_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the guest\'s consent record governing this communication.']
  consent_record_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the guest\'s consent record governing this communication.']
  content_template_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the content/template used to generate the message body.']
  guest_profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the guest who received the communication.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the guest who received the communication.']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to restaurant.unit. Business justification: Marketing communications are often store‑specific; linking to unit enables ROI measurement per restaurant and compliance with local regulations.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Medium used to deliver the communication (e.g., email, SMS, push notification).']
  click_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the recipient clicked any link in the communication.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this communication record was first created in the system.']
  delivery_status varchar [note: 'Final delivery outcome of the communication.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the communication was sent to the guest.']
  language_code varchar [note: 'ISO language code representing the language of the communication.']
  message_body_preview varchar [note: 'Truncated preview of the communication\'s body content.']
  open_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the recipient opened the communication.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority level assigned to the communication for processing.']
  recipient_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the guest who received the communication.']
  recipient_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the guest for SMS or voice communications.']
  scheduled_send_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned date and time for sending the communication, if scheduled.']
  send_attempt_count varchar [note: 'Number of attempts made to deliver the communication.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of record that originated the communication record.']
  subject varchar [note: 'Subject line or title of the outbound message.']
  suppression_flag boolean [note: 'True if the guest was on a suppression list at send time.']
  tracking_url varchar [note: 'URL used to track click‑throughs for the communication.']
  trigger_source varchar [note: 'Business event or rule that triggered the communication.']
  unsubscribe_flag boolean [note: 'True if the guest unsubscribed as a result of this communication.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to this communication record.']

  Note: 'Records every outbound communication sent to a guest including marketing emails, SMS messages, push notifications, and direct mail. Captures communication type, channel, subject/content reference, send timestamp, delivery status, open status, click-through status, unsubscribe action, and the campaign or trigger that initiated the communication. Sourced from Salesforce CRM marketing automation. Supports suppression list management and communication frequency capping.'
}

Table guest.demographic {
  demographic_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the demographic record.']
  guest_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the guest to which this demographic profile belongs.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the guest to which this demographic profile belongs.']
  age_band varchar [note: 'Age range classification for the guest.']
  consent_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the guest has opted in to marketing communications.']
  consent_opt_out boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the guest has opted out of marketing communications.']
  data_source varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the demographic data was self‑declared by the guest or supplied by a third‑party enrichment provider.']
  demographic_type varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the guest for analytics (e.g., resident, visitor).']
  education_level varchar [note: 'Highest education attained by the guest. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: no_formal|high_school|some_college|bachelor|master|doctorate|other — promote to reference product]']
  email_address varchar [note: 'Primary email address for guest communication.']
  employment_status varchar [note: 'Current employment situation of the guest.']
  enrichment_date date [note: 'Date on which the demographic enrichment was applied.']
  enrichment_provider varchar [note: 'Name of the third‑party vendor that supplied the enriched demographic data.']
