  channel_type varchar [note: 'Ownership classification of the channel: owned (company-operated channels like POS, DT, proprietary OLO), third_party (external platforms like 3PD aggregators), hybrid (co-branded or white-label solutions).']
  commission_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Commission or transaction fee percentage charged by the channel provider (applicable to 3PD channels). Null for owned channels. Used for COGS and P&L analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this order channel record was first created in the system.']
  deactivation_date date [note: 'Date when this order channel was deactivated or retired. Null if the channel is still active.']
  default_daypart varchar [note: 'Default daypart (e.g., breakfast, lunch, dinner, late_night) typically associated with this channel. Null if the channel operates across all dayparts equally.']
  display_order varchar [note: 'Numeric sort order for displaying this channel in reporting dashboards and user interfaces. Lower numbers appear first.']
  fulfillment_mode varchar [note: 'Primary method by which orders placed through this channel are fulfilled: dine_in (guest consumes on-premise), takeout (guest picks up), delivery (delivered to guest location), curbside (pickup at designated area), catering (scheduled bulk delivery).']
  integration_platform varchar [note: 'Name of the technology platform or system that powers this channel (e.g., Oracle MICROS, Olo, DoorDash, Uber Eats, Grubhub, proprietary mobile app). Null for native POS channels.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this channel is currently active and available for order placement. False indicates the channel has been retired or temporarily disabled.']
  is_digital boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this is a digital channel (true for OLO, mobile app, kiosk, 3PD; false for traditional POS, DT counter service).']
  kds_routing_enabled boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether orders from this channel are routed to the Kitchen Display System (KDS) for BOH preparation tracking. True for most channels; may be false for catering orders prepared off-site.']
  requires_restaurant_assignment boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether orders from this channel must be assigned to a specific restaurant location at order time (true for delivery, catering; false for walk-in POS).']
  supports_guest_data_capture boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this channel captures guest identification and contact information (true for OLO, mobile, 3PD; false for anonymous POS transactions).']
  supports_loyalty_integration boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this channel supports integration with the customer loyalty program for points accrual and redemption.']
  supports_payment_at_order boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether payment is collected at the time of order placement (true for OLO, 3PD, mobile; false for traditional counter service where payment occurs at pickup).']
  supports_scheduled_orders boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this channel allows customers to schedule orders for future fulfillment (true for catering, some OLO platforms; false for immediate-service channels like DT).']
  target_sos_seconds varchar [note: 'Target Speed of Service (SOS) threshold in seconds for this channel, representing the operational goal for order fulfillment time. Used for performance benchmarking and SOP compliance.']
  third_party_provider varchar [note: 'Name of the third-party delivery service provider if this channel is a 3PD channel (e.g., DoorDash, Uber Eats, Grubhub, Postmates). Null for non-3PD channels.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this order channel record was last modified.']

  Note: 'Reference master defining all service channels through which guest orders are placed: POS (dine-in, counter), drive-thru (DT), online ordering (OLO), third-party delivery (3PD — DoorDash, Uber Eats, Grubhub), catering, kiosk, and mobile app. Captures channel code, channel category, integration platform, fulfillment mode (dine-in, takeout, delivery, curbside), and active status. Serves as the FK target for guest_order channel attribution, channel-mix reporting, and ACV benchmarking.'
}

Table order.daypart {
  daypart_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the order_daypart data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']
  guest_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to order.guest_order. Business justification: order_daypart represents the assignment of a daypart to an order; the child table should hold the FK to the parent order.']

  Note: 'Reference entity defining the standard trading periods used to segment restaurant operations and sales: breakfast, morning snack, lunch, afternoon, dinner, late night. Captures daypart name, start time, end time, daypart category, and whether it is a primary or secondary daypart. Foundational dimension for PMIX analysis, labor scheduling, and ATC/ACV benchmarking by time-of-day.'
}

Table order.kds_ticket {
  kds_ticket_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the KDS ticket. Primary key for the kds_ticket product.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the BOH employee who was assigned to or completed this ticket. Used for individual performance tracking and labor productivity analysis.']
  guest_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent guest order that generated this KDS ticket. Links the ticket to the originating transaction captured at the POS.']
  shift_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the workforce shift during which this ticket was processed. Enables shift-level throughput and labor efficiency analysis.']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the restaurant location where this KDS ticket was generated. Enables location-level throughput and SOS analysis.']
  station_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific BOH kitchen station (grill, fryer, assembly, expo, beverage) to which this ticket was routed. Critical for station-level performance tracking.']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the restaurant location where this KDS ticket was generated. Enables location-level throughput and SOS analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp indicating when this record was first inserted into the data platform. Used for data lineage and ETL tracking.']
  daypart varchar [note: 'Time-of-day segment when the ticket was created. Used for daypart-specific throughput analysis and labor planning. Common values: breakfast, lunch, dinner, late_night, snack.']
  item_count varchar [note: 'Total number of menu items included on this KDS ticket. Used to analyze ticket complexity and its impact on preparation time.']
  modifier_count varchar [note: 'Total number of modifiers (customizations, substitutions, add-ons) applied to items on this ticket. Higher modifier counts typically increase ticket time.']
  order_channel varchar [note: 'Service channel through which the originating order was placed. Values include dine_in (FOH table service), drive_thru (DT), online (OLO direct), third_party_delivery (3PD platforms), catering, kiosk.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Priority classification assigned to the ticket. Rush and VIP orders receive expedited handling. Catering orders may have extended preparation windows.']
  re_fire_count varchar [note: 'Number of times this ticket was re-fired. Multiple re-fires indicate recurring quality or accuracy issues. Zero indicates no remakes were required.']
  re_fire_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this ticket was re-fired (sent back to the kitchen for remake) due to quality issues, order errors, or customer complaints. True indicates a remake event occurred.']
  re_fire_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of why the ticket was re-fired. Common reasons include incorrect preparation, quality defect, missing items, customer request, order entry error.']
  sos_met_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether the ticket was completed within the SOS target time. True if ticket_time_seconds <= sos_target_seconds, False otherwise. Critical for operational compliance reporting.']
  sos_target_seconds varchar [note: 'Target ticket time in seconds defined by restaurant operations standards for this station and order channel. Used to determine SOS compliance.']
  special_instructions varchar [note: 'Free-text field containing customer special requests, preparation notes, or allergy warnings that BOH staff must follow when preparing this ticket.']
  ticket_bumped_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the ticket was bumped (cleared) from the KDS screen by BOH staff, indicating the order was sent to the customer or next station.']
  ticket_completed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when all items on the ticket were finished and ready for service or pickup. Used to calculate total ticket time.']
  ticket_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the KDS ticket was generated and routed to the station. Start point for ticket time calculation and SOS measurement.']
  ticket_number varchar [note: 'Human-readable ticket number displayed on the KDS screen and printed on kitchen receipts. Used by BOH staff to identify and track production items.']
  ticket_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential order number of this ticket within the parent order. Multi-station orders generate multiple tickets; this field tracks the sequence.']
  ticket_started_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when BOH staff began actively preparing the items on this ticket. Marks transition from new to in_progress status.']
  ticket_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the KDS ticket. Tracks progression from new (just routed) through in_progress (being prepared), completed (finished), bumped (cleared from screen), voided (cancelled), or re_fired (sent back for remake).']
  ticket_time_seconds varchar [note: 'Total elapsed time in seconds from ticket creation to ticket completion. Core KPI for kitchen throughput and speed of service (SOS) performance measurement.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Audit timestamp indicating when this record was last modified in the data platform. Used for change tracking and data freshness monitoring.']
  void_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator of whether this ticket was voided (cancelled) before completion. True indicates the ticket was cancelled and items were not prepared.']
  void_reason varchar [note: 'Free-text description of why the ticket was voided. Common reasons include customer cancellation, order entry error, system error, duplicate ticket.']

  Note: 'Represents a kitchen display system (KDS) production ticket generated from a guest order, routed to a specific BOH station (grill, fryer, assembly, expo). Captures ticket creation time, station assignment, bump time, re-fire events, ticket time (seconds), SOS target compliance flag, and fulfillment handoff timestamp. Core operational record for kitchen throughput, ticket time compliance, BOH performance management, and FOH handoff coordination.'
}

Table order.drive_thru_event {
  drive_thru_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each drive-thru event record. Primary key for the drive_thru_event product.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who served the customer at the time of the event (e.g., order taker, cashier, or expediter).']
  guest_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the order associated with this drive-thru event, linking to the order captured in Oracle MICROS POS.']
  pos_terminal_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Oracle MICROS POS terminal that processed the order associated with this drive-thru event.']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant location where the drive-thru event occurred.']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant location where the drive-thru event occurred.']
  business_date date [note: 'The operational business date for the drive-thru event, which may differ from the calendar date for late-night operations (e.g., 2 AM transaction may belong to the previous business day).']
  cumulative_time_seconds decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total time in seconds from the vehicles initial menu board arrival to the current event. Represents the running total ticket time for the vehicle.']
  daypart varchar [note: 'Time-of-day segment when the drive-thru event occurred: breakfast, lunch, afternoon, dinner, or late_night. Used for daypart-specific Speed of Service (SOS) benchmarking and throughput analysis.']
  elapsed_time_seconds decimal(10,2) [note: 'Time elapsed in seconds from the previous event in the drive-thru sequence for this vehicle. Used to calculate stage-specific Speed of Service (SOS) metrics such as menu board time, window time, and total ticket time.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise date and time when the drive-thru event occurred, captured by drive-thru timer sensors integrated with Oracle MICROS POS. Used for Speed of Service (SOS) calculation.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Type of drive-thru event being recorded: menu_board_arrival (vehicle arrives at menu board), order_placed (order confirmed), pull_forward (vehicle directed to wait), window_arrival (vehicle reaches service window), payment_complete (payment transaction finalized), food_handoff (order delivered to customer), departure (vehicle exits drive-thru lane). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: menu_board_arrival|order_placed|pull_forward|window_arrival|payment_complete|food_handoff|departure — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  exception_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this drive-thru event represents an exception or anomaly (e.g., sensor malfunction, manual override, system error). True if exception occurred, False for normal operation.']
  exception_reason varchar [note: 'Description of the reason for the exception if exception_flag is True (e.g., sensor malfunction, customer service recovery, order modification, system timeout).']
  franchise_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the restaurant location is franchise-owned (True) or company-owned (False). Used for comparative Speed of Service (SOS) analysis between franchise and company-owned locations.']
  kds_ticket_number varchar [note: 'Ticket number assigned by the Kitchen Display System (KDS) for the order associated with this drive-thru event, used to correlate drive-thru timing with kitchen preparation timing.']
  lane_number varchar [note: 'Identifier for the specific drive-thru lane where the event occurred (e.g., 1 for single-lane, 1 or 2 for dual-lane configurations).']
  order_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the order was originally placed: drive_thru (ordered at menu board), mobile_order_ahead (pre-ordered via mobile app for drive-thru pickup), or third_party_delivery (3PD order picked up via drive-thru).']
  order_item_count varchar [note: 'Number of individual items in the order associated with this drive-thru event. Used to analyze the relationship between order complexity and Speed of Service (SOS).']
  order_total_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the order associated with this drive-thru event, used for Average Check Value (ACV) calculation and revenue analysis by Speed of Service (SOS) performance.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Method of payment used for the order associated with this drive-thru event: cash, credit_card, debit_card, mobile_payment, or gift_card. Used to analyze the impact of payment method on Speed of Service (SOS).']
  pull_forward_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vehicle was asked to pull forward to a waiting area during this drive-thru sequence. True if pull-forward occurred, False otherwise. Pull-forwards impact Speed of Service (SOS) metrics and throughput.']
  queue_length varchar [note: 'Number of vehicles in the drive-thru lane at the time of this event, used to analyze the relationship between queue depth and Speed of Service (SOS) performance.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this drive-thru event record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage and audit trail purposes.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this drive-thru event record was last updated in the system. Used for data lineage and audit trail purposes.']
  sensor_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the physical sensor or timer device that captured this event (e.g., menu board sensor, window sensor, exit sensor).']
  sos_target_seconds decimal(10,2) [note: 'Brand-defined target time in seconds for this specific drive-thru event type or stage (e.g., 25 seconds for menu board to order placed, 180 seconds for total ticket time). Used for performance benchmarking.']
  sos_variance_seconds decimal(10,2) [note: 'Difference in seconds between the actual elapsed time and the SOS target for this event. Positive values indicate slower-than-target service; negative values indicate faster-than-target service.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that generated this drive-thru event record (e.g., Oracle MICROS POS, drive-thru timer system vendor name).']
  vehicle_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number assigned to each vehicle entering the drive-thru lane during the business day, used for tracking vehicle progression and throughput analysis.']
  weather_condition varchar [note: 'Weather condition at the time of the drive-thru event, captured to analyze the impact of weather on Speed of Service (SOS) and throughput. Values include clear, rain, snow, fog, wind, extreme_heat, extreme_cold. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: clear|rain|snow|fog|wind|extreme_heat|extreme_cold — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Captures timestamped sensor and timer events for each vehicle progressing through the drive-thru (DT) lane: arrival at menu board, order placed, pull-forward, window arrival, payment complete, food handoff, departure. Enables Speed of Service (SOS) measurement at each DT stage, throughput analysis, and benchmarking against brand SOS targets. Sourced from DT timer systems integrated with Oracle MICROS POS.'
}

Table order.delivery_order {
  delivery_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the delivery order record. Primary key.']
  delivery_platform_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the third-party delivery (3PD) platform or first-party delivery service provider (DoorDash, Uber Eats, Grubhub, proprietary fleet).']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the courier or driver assigned to deliver the order. May be null if not yet assigned.']
  guest_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent order being fulfilled via delivery. Links to the core order transaction.']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the restaurant location fulfilling the delivery order.']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the restaurant location fulfilling the delivery order.']
  actual_delivery_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Actual time in minutes taken by the courier to deliver the order from pickup to guest, calculated from picked up timestamp to actual delivery timestamp.']
  actual_delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Actual date and time when the order was delivered to the guest, as confirmed by the courier or delivery platform.']
  actual_prep_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Actual time in minutes taken by the restaurant to prepare the order, calculated from order confirmation to ready for pickup.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this delivery order record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for all monetary amounts in this delivery order (e.g., USD, CAD, MXN).']
  customer_feedback varchar [note: 'Free-text feedback provided by the guest about the delivery experience. Used for quality improvement and service recovery.']
  customer_rating varchar [note: 'Guest rating for the delivery experience on a scale of 1 to 5. Used for Net Promoter Score (NPS) and Customer Satisfaction (CSAT) tracking.']
  delivery_address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line for the delivery destination. Contains personally identifiable information (PII).']
  delivery_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for apartment, suite, or unit number. Contains personally identifiable information (PII).']
  delivery_city varchar [note: 'City name for the delivery destination. Contains personally identifiable information (PII).']
  delivery_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the delivery destination (e.g., USA, CAN, MEX).']
  delivery_distance_km decimal(8,2) [note: 'Calculated distance in kilometers from the restaurant to the delivery destination. Used for delivery fee calculation and logistics analysis.']
  delivery_exception_type varchar [note: 'Type of exception or issue encountered during delivery fulfillment. Used for quality tracking and Customer Satisfaction (CSAT) analysis. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: none|late_delivery|missing_items|wrong_address|customer_unavailable|order_damaged|cancelled_by_customer|cancelled_by_restaurant — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  delivery_fee_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Delivery fee charged to the guest for the delivery service. Excludes taxes and tips.']
  delivery_instructions varchar [note: 'Special instructions provided by the guest for delivery (e.g., gate code, building entrance, leave at door). May contain personally identifiable information (PII).']
  delivery_latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the delivery destination. Used for routing optimization and distance calculation. Contains location PII.']
  delivery_longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the delivery destination. Used for routing optimization and distance calculation. Contains location PII.']
  delivery_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the delivery destination. Contains personally identifiable information (PII).']
  delivery_state_province varchar [note: 'State or province code for the delivery destination. Contains personally identifiable information (PII).']
  delivery_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the delivery order. Tracks progression from order placement through final delivery or cancellation. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: pending|confirmed|preparing|ready_for_pickup|picked_up|in_transit|delivered|cancelled|failed — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  estimated_delivery_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Estimated time in minutes for the courier to deliver the order from pickup to guest, as provided by the delivery platform routing algorithm.']
  estimated_delivery_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Estimated date and time for delivery to the guest, as communicated by the delivery platform or internal routing system.']
  estimated_prep_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Estimated time in minutes for the restaurant to prepare the order, as communicated to the delivery platform.']
  exception_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes describing the delivery exception or issue. Captured by courier, restaurant staff, or customer service.']
  is_contactless_delivery boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the delivery was requested as contactless (leave at door). True if contactless, False otherwise.']
  order_confirmed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the restaurant confirmed acceptance of the delivery order.']
  order_placed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the delivery order was placed by the guest via the online ordering (OLO) or third-party delivery (3PD) platform.']
  picked_up_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the courier picked up the order from the restaurant location.']
  platform_commission_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total commission amount paid to the third-party delivery platform for this order. Business-confidential financial information.']
  platform_commission_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Commission rate (as a decimal) charged by the third-party delivery platform on the order subtotal. Business-confidential pricing information.']
  platform_order_reference varchar [note: 'External order identifier provided by the third-party delivery platform. Used for reconciliation and support inquiries with 3PD providers.']
  proof_of_delivery_url varchar [note: 'URL to the proof of delivery image or document (e.g., photo of order at doorstep). Used for dispute resolution.']
  ready_for_pickup_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the order was marked as ready for courier pickup in the Kitchen Display System (KDS) or Point of Sale (POS).']
  tip_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Tip amount provided by the guest to the courier. May be pre-tip or post-delivery adjustment.']
  total_ticket_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Total elapsed time in minutes from order placement to actual delivery. Key Speed of Service (SOS) metric for delivery orders.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this delivery order record was last modified.']

  Note: 'Manages the delivery fulfillment record for orders dispatched via third-party delivery (3PD) platforms (DoorDash, Uber Eats, Grubhub) or first-party delivery. Captures 3PD platform reference ID, courier assignment, estimated and actual delivery times, delivery distance, delivery fee, platform commission rate, delivery status, and any delivery exception (late, missing item, cancelled). Bridges the Olo digital ordering platform data with internal order records.'
}

Table order.catering_order {
  catering_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the catering order. Primary key.']
  catering_package_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the pre-defined catering package selected by the guest, if applicable.']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.contract. Business justification: Catering contracts define pricing and terms; linking catering orders to contracts enables contract compliance, revenue recognition, and audit reporting.']
  corporate_account_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the corporate billing account if the catering order is billed to a corporate customer.']
  guest_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the guest or corporate account placing the catering order.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the guest or corporate account placing the catering order.']
  sales_rep_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sales representative or catering coordinator who managed this order.']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant location fulfilling this catering order.']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant location fulfilling this catering order.']
  balance_due decimal(15,2) [note: 'Remaining balance due after deposit, to be paid before or at event delivery.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided for catering order cancellation, if applicable.']
  cancelled_at timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the catering order was cancelled, if applicable.']
  catering_order_number varchar [note: 'Externally-visible unique business identifier for the catering order, used for guest communication and tracking.']
  confirmed_at timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the catering order was confirmed by restaurant staff or system.']
  contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary contact person for order confirmation and updates.']
  contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary contact person for the catering event.']
  contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the primary contact person for day-of-event coordination.']
  created_at timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this catering order record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for all monetary amounts in this catering order.']
  delivery_address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line for catering delivery location.']
  delivery_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line (suite, floor, building) for catering delivery location.']
  delivery_city varchar [note: 'City for catering delivery location.']
  delivery_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for catering delivery location.']
  delivery_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for catering delivery location.']
  delivery_state_province varchar [note: 'State or province code for catering delivery location.']
  deposit_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Deposit amount paid at time of order confirmation to secure the catering reservation.']
  dietary_accommodations varchar [note: 'Special dietary requirements or accommodations requested for the catering event (e.g., vegetarian, gluten-free, allergen restrictions).']
  event_date date [note: 'Date on which the catering event is scheduled to occur.']
  event_start_time timestamp [note: 'Scheduled start time for the catering event, used for delivery or pickup timing.']
  fulfilled_at timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the catering order was delivered or picked up and marked as fulfilled.']
  fulfillment_mode varchar [note: 'Method by which the catering order will be fulfilled: delivery to event location, guest pickup, or on-site service with staff.']
  gratuity_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Gratuity or service charge amount included in the catering order total.']
  headcount varchar [note: 'Expected number of guests to be served at the catering event.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of days in advance the catering order was placed before the event date.']
  order_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the catering order was placed.']
  order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the catering order in the fulfillment workflow. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: draft|confirmed|in_preparation|ready|delivered|completed|cancelled — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Payment instrument used for the catering order transaction.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current payment status of the catering order.']
  placed_at timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the catering order was initially placed by the guest.']
  quoted_price decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total price quoted to the guest for the catering order, including all items and services.']
  setup_instructions varchar [note: 'Detailed instructions for on-site setup, including table arrangement, buffet layout, and service requirements.']
  setup_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether on-site setup service is required for the catering order.']
  special_instructions varchar [note: 'Additional guest instructions or notes for order preparation and fulfillment.']
  tax_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total tax amount applied to the catering order based on delivery location and applicable tax rates.']
  total_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Final total amount for the catering order including all items, services, tax, and gratuity.']
  updated_at timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this catering order record was last modified.']

  Note: 'Manages large-format catering orders placed by corporate, event, or group guests. Captures catering event date, headcount, delivery or pickup mode, setup requirements, special dietary accommodations, catering package selected, quoted price, deposit amount, balance due, and fulfillment status. Distinct from standard guest orders due to advance booking lead time, custom BOM requirements, and dedicated fulfillment workflow.'
}

Table order.discount {
  discount_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the order discount record. Primary key.']
  authorization_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the employee who authorized or applied the discount, particularly for manager comps and exception-based discounts.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Tracks discount expense against a designated GL account, supporting discount expense reporting.']
  guest_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent guest order to which this discount was applied.']
  menu_item_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific menu item to which this discount was applied. Null if discount applies to entire order.']
  pos_terminal_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the POS terminal or workstation where the discount was applied.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to guest.profile. Business justification: Needed for discount usage analytics per guest, supporting marketing ROI analysis and compliance with promotional eligibility rules.']
  promotion_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the marketing promotion or Limited Time Offer (LTO) campaign that generated this discount.']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the restaurant location where this discount was applied.']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the restaurant location where this discount was applied.']
  amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Absolute monetary value of the discount applied, in the transaction currency. Used for Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) impact analysis.']
  applied_at timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the discount was applied to the order or item at the Point of Sale (POS).']
  authorization_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether manager or supervisor authorization was required to apply this discount.']
  channel_restriction varchar [note: 'Specifies the order channel(s) for which this discount is valid (e.g., dine_in, drive_thru, online_ordering, third_party_delivery). Null if no restriction.']
  cogs_impact_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'The calculated impact of this discount on Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) for financial and profitability analysis.']
  created_at timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this discount record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the discount amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  daypart_restriction varchar [note: 'Specifies the daypart(s) during which this discount is valid (e.g., breakfast, lunch, dinner, late_night). Null if no restriction.']
  discount_code varchar [note: 'The coupon code, promo code, or loyalty redemption code entered or scanned at the Point of Sale (POS).']
  discount_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or description of the discount as displayed on the receipt and Kitchen Display System (KDS).']
  discount_scope varchar [note: 'Defines the scope at which the discount is applied: entire order, individual item, menu category, or combo meal.']
  discount_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the discount mechanism applied to the order or item.']
  final_price decimal(10,2) [note: 'The final price after the discount was applied to the item or order.']
  is_pre_approved boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the discount was pre-approved through a promotional campaign (true) or applied as an exception at the Point of Sale (false).']
  is_stackable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this discount can be combined with other discounts on the same order or item.']
  is_voided boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this discount was voided or removed from the order after initial application.']
  loyalty_points_redeemed varchar [note: 'Number of loyalty program points redeemed to obtain this discount. Null if discount is not loyalty-based.']
  max_discount_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'The maximum allowable discount amount for this promotion or discount type, as configured in the promotion rules.']
  min_purchase_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'The minimum order subtotal required to qualify for this discount.']
  original_price decimal(10,2) [note: 'The original price of the item or order subtotal before the discount was applied.']
  percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount applied to the item or order subtotal. Null if discount is a fixed amount.']
  reason varchar [note: 'Free-text explanation or reason code for the discount, particularly for manager comps and exception-based adjustments.']
  revenue_impact_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'The calculated impact of this discount on gross revenue for Profit and Loss (P&L) reporting and Same-Store Sales (SSS) analysis.']
  tax_treatment varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the discount is applied before tax calculation, after tax calculation, or results in a tax-exempt transaction.']
  updated_at timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this discount record was last modified in the system.']
  valid_from_date date [note: 'The start date from which this discount or promotion is valid and can be applied.']
  valid_to_date date [note: 'The end date after which this discount or promotion is no longer valid.']
  void_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation or reason code for why the discount was voided. Null if discount was not voided.']
  voided_at timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the discount was voided or removed from the order. Null if discount was not voided.']

  Note: 'Records all discounts, promotions, coupons, and comp adjustments applied to a guest order or individual order items. Captures discount type (coupon, loyalty redemption, employee meal, manager comp, promotional LTO, bundle/combo deal), discount amount or percentage, promotion reference, authorization employee, and pre-approved vs. exception-based flag. Supports COGS% impact analysis, promotional ROI tracking, and combo savings attribution.'
}

Table order.refund {
  refund_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the refund transaction. Primary key for the order_refund product.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manager or supervisor who approved and authorized the refund transaction.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Posts refund amounts to a specific GL account to reflect refund expense in financials.']
  guest_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original guest order against which this refund was issued. Links to the parent order transaction.']
  guest_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the guest who received the refund, if known and captured in the Customer Relationship Management (CRM) system.']
  pos_terminal_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Point of Sale (POS) terminal or workstation where the refund was processed.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the guest who received the refund, if known and captured in the Customer Relationship Management (CRM) system.']
  refund_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manager or supervisor who approved and authorized the refund transaction.']
  refund_processing_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Front of House (FOH) employee who processed the refund transaction at the Point of Sale (POS).']
  refund_voided_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who voided or reversed the refund transaction.']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant location where the refund was processed.']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant location where the refund was processed.']
  amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total monetary value refunded to the guest, including tax adjustments.']
  approved_at timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the refund was approved by the authorizing manager.']
  batch_code varchar [note: 'Identifier for the financial batch in which this refund was grouped for accounting reconciliation and settlement.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the refund was initiated and processed, indicating the customer touchpoint.']
  created_at timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the refund record was first created in the system.']
  csat_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this refund is associated with a Customer Satisfaction (CSAT) incident requiring follow-up or root-cause analysis.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code indicating the currency in which the refund was issued.']
  daypart varchar [note: 'Time-of-day segment during which the original order and refund occurred, used for operational and sales analysis.']
  fraud_review_notes varchar [note: 'Internal notes from fraud review or investigation related to this refund transaction.']
  gl_posting_date date [note: 'Date on which the refund transaction was posted to the General Ledger (GL) for financial reporting.']
  guest_contact_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the guest communicated the refund request or complaint to the restaurant.']
  is_fraudulent boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this refund was flagged as potentially fraudulent during review or audit.']
  is_voided boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this refund transaction was subsequently voided or reversed.']
  loyalty_points_refunded varchar [note: 'Number of loyalty program points returned to the guest as part of the refund, if applicable.']
  nps_survey_sent boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether a Net Promoter Score (NPS) survey was sent to the guest following the refund.']
  order_channel varchar [note: 'Service channel through which the original order was placed, retained for refund analysis by channel.']
  original_payment_method varchar [note: 'Payment method used in the original order transaction, retained for reconciliation and audit purposes.']
  payment_processor_ref varchar [note: 'External reference number from the payment processor for the refund transaction, used for reconciliation with payment gateway.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code categorizing the primary reason for issuing the refund. Used for root-cause analysis and Customer Satisfaction (CSAT) improvement initiatives. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: wrong_item|quality_complaint|missing_item|guest_dissatisfaction|order_error|temperature_issue|allergy_concern|long_wait_time|incorrect_price|duplicate_charge|other — 11 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Free-text detailed explanation of the refund reason provided by the manager or customer service representative.']
  refund_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the refund value was returned to the guest, indicating the tender type used for reimbursement.']
  refund_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique refund transaction number displayed on receipts and used for customer service tracking.']
  refund_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the refund transaction indicating processing state.']
  refund_type varchar [note: 'Classification indicating whether the refund covers the entire order or only specific items.']
  refunded_at timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the refund transaction was completed and funds were returned to the guest.']
  requested_at timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the refund was initially requested by the guest or identified by staff.']
  subtotal decimal(15,2) [note: 'Pre-tax amount of the refund, representing the base value of items or services refunded.']
  tax_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Tax portion of the refund amount, calculated based on applicable sales tax rates.']
  third_party_delivery_provider varchar [note: 'Name of the third-party delivery service provider if the original order was fulfilled through Third-Party Delivery (3PD).']
  updated_at timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the refund record was last modified in the system.']
  void_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the refund transaction was voided or reversed.']
  voided_at timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the refund was voided or reversed, if applicable.']

  Note: 'Captures refund and void transactions issued against a completed or in-progress guest order. Records refund reason (wrong item, quality complaint, missing item, guest dissatisfaction), refund amount, refund method (original tender, gift card, loyalty points), authorizing manager, refund timestamp, and whether the refund was full or partial. Supports CSAT root-cause analysis and exception management.'
}

Table order.sos_target {
  sos_target_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the Speed of Service target record.']
  brand_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the brand (QSR, casual, fine-dining) for which this SOS target is defined.']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the restaurant location to which this SOS target applies. May be null if the target is a brand-wide standard.']
  superseded_by_target_sos_target_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the SOS target that supersedes this target. Null if this is the current active target.']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the restaurant location to which this SOS target applies. May be null if the target is a brand-wide standard.']
  applies_to_order_type varchar [note: 'The order type to which this SOS target applies: dine_in, takeout, delivery, or all order types.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'The username or employee identifier of the person who approved this SOS target for operational use.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this SOS target was approved for operational use.']
  compliance_threshold_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'The minimum percentage of orders that must meet this SOS target for the restaurant to be considered compliant (e.g., 85.00 means 85% of orders must meet the target).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this SOS target record was first created in the system.']
  daypart varchar [note: 'The daypart (time segment of the day) to which this SOS target applies: breakfast, lunch, dinner, late_night, or all_day.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'The date on which this SOS target expires or is superseded by a new target. Null indicates an open-ended target.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'The date from which this SOS target becomes effective and should be used for performance evaluation.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this SOS target is currently active and should be used for performance measurement and KDS compliance evaluation.']
  measurement_unit varchar [note: 'The unit of measure for SOS target times: seconds or minutes. Typically seconds for QSR and fast-casual, minutes for fine-dining.']
  modified_by varchar [note: 'The username or employee identifier of the person who last modified this SOS target record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this SOS target record was last modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes providing additional context, rationale, or special instructions for this SOS target.']
  peak_hours_only boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this SOS target applies only during peak hours (true) or throughout the entire daypart (false).']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'The priority level of this SOS target for operational compliance and performance evaluation: critical, high, medium, or low.']
  restaurant_format varchar [note: 'The restaurant format type to which this SOS target applies: QSR (Quick-Service Restaurant), casual, fine_dining, fast_casual, or food_court.']
  service_channel varchar [note: 'The service channel to which this SOS target applies: DT (Drive-Thru), counter (Front of House counter service), OLO (Online Ordering), 3PD (Third-Party Delivery), catering, or kiosk.']
  target_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the SOS target, used for external reference and reporting (e.g., DT_LUNCH_QSR, COUNTER_DINNER_CASUAL).']
  target_handoff_time_seconds varchar [note: 'The target time in seconds from order ready (on KDS) to order handoff to guest or delivery driver.']
  target_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name for the SOS target (e.g., Drive-Thru Lunch Target, Counter Service Dinner Target).']
  target_queue_time_seconds varchar [note: 'The target time in seconds a guest should wait in queue before placing an order, applicable to counter and drive-thru channels.']
  target_ticket_time_seconds varchar [note: 'The target time in seconds from order placement to order ready on the Kitchen Display System (KDS), representing the kitchen preparation speed target.']
  target_total_order_time_seconds varchar [note: 'The target total time in seconds from order placement to order fulfillment (handed to guest or delivered), representing the complete Speed of Service (SOS) target.']
  target_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the SOS target: brand_standard (corporate-mandated), local_goal (restaurant-specific operational goal), franchise_goal (franchisee-defined target), or regulatory (mandated by health or safety regulations).']
  version_number varchar [note: 'The version number of this SOS target, incremented each time the target is revised or updated.']
  warning_threshold_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage threshold below which a warning alert is triggered for operational management (e.g., 80.00 triggers a warning if compliance falls below 80%).']
  weather_adjusted boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this SOS target should be adjusted based on adverse weather conditions that impact throughput.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'The username or employee identifier of the person who created this SOS target record.']

  Note: 'Reference entity defining brand-mandated Speed of Service (SOS) targets segmented by service channel, daypart, and restaurant format (QSR, casual, fine-dining). Captures daypart definitions (name, start time, end time, category — breakfast, lunch, dinner, late night), target ticket time in seconds, target total order time, channel applicability, effective date range, and whether the target is a brand standard or local operational goal. Serves as the authoritative daypart and SOS benchmark reference for KDS ticket compliance evaluation, drive-thru SOS benchmarking, PMIX time-of-day analysis, ATC/ACV benchmarking, and labor scheduling alignment.'
}

Table order.tax {
  tax_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each tax line record applied to a guest order. Primary key for the order_tax data product in the Databricks Silver Layer.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Ensures tax liabilities from each order are posted to the correct tax GL account for compliance and reporting.']
  guest_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent guest order to which this tax line belongs. Establishes the header-to-line relationship between guest_order and order_tax.']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant location where the order was placed. Used to resolve the applicable tax jurisdiction and for location-level financial reconciliation.']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant location where the order was placed. Used to resolve the applicable tax jurisdiction and for location-level financial reconciliation.']
  adjusted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX) when a post-transaction tax adjustment was applied to this line. Populated only when tax_status = \'adjusted\'. Supports audit trail for tax corrections.']
  adjustment_reason varchar [note: 'The reason code for a post-transaction tax adjustment when tax_status = \'adjusted\' (e.g., rate_correction, jurisdiction_change, exemption_applied, refund, audit_correction, other). Required for audit trail and regulatory compliance.']
  amount decimal(12,4) [note: 'The computed tax amount for this tax line in the transaction currency (taxable_amount × tax_rate, or as overridden by the tax engine). Core field for financial reconciliation, P&L reporting, and tax remittance.']
  applied_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX) when this tax line was computed and applied to the order by the tax engine or POS system. Represents the principal business event time for this tax line record.']
  authority_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code identifying the tax authority or jurisdiction as defined by the tax engine or ERP (SAP S/4HANA Tax Condition). Used for automated tax remittance and reconciliation.']
  authority_level varchar [note: 'The governmental jurisdiction level that imposes this tax line. Supports multi-jurisdiction tax compliance across all restaurant locations. Values: federal, state, county, city, district, special.']
  authority_name varchar [note: 'The full name of the tax authority or jurisdiction imposing this tax (e.g., \'California State Board of Equalization\', \'Cook County Revenue Department\', \'City of Chicago\'). Used for regulatory reporting and remittance.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'The SAP S/4HANA cost center code associated with the restaurant location for this tax line. Used for internal cost allocation, P&L reporting by location, and management accounting.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the tax jurisdiction (e.g., \'USA\', \'CAN\', \'GBR\'). Required for international operations, VAT compliance, and cross-border tax reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX) when this tax line record was first created in the data platform. Audit trail field for data lineage and Silver Layer ingestion tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts on this tax line (e.g., \'USD\', \'CAD\', \'GBP\'). Required for multi-currency operations and financial reporting under GAAP/IFRS.']
  daypart varchar [note: 'The meal period or daypart during which the order was placed (breakfast, lunch, dinner, late_night, snack). Used for daypart-level tax and revenue analysis in foodservice operations.']
  engine_source varchar [note: 'Identifies the system or engine that calculated and applied this tax line (e.g., pos = Oracle MICROS POS built-in tax, vertex = Vertex O Series, avalara = Avalara AvaTax, sap_tax = SAP S/4HANA tax engine, manual = manually entered). Used for reconciliation and audit.']
  exemption_certificate_ref varchar [note: 'Reference number or identifier of the tax exemption certificate presented by the guest or organization. Required for audit documentation when is_exempt is True. Null when no exemption applies.']
  exemption_reason varchar [note: 'The reason code for a tax exemption when is_exempt is True (e.g., government, non_profit, resale, diplomatic, employee_meal, other). Required for audit trail and regulatory compliance. Null when is_exempt is False.']
  is_exempt boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this tax line was flagged as exempt (True) due to a guest exemption certificate, government order, or qualifying non-profit status. When True, tax_amount should be zero and exemption_reason should be populated.']
  is_refunded boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the tax amount on this line has been refunded to the guest (True) as part of an order refund or cancellation. Used for refund reconciliation and tax reversal tracking.']
  is_tax_inclusive boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this tax was already included in the menu price (True) or added at checkout on top of the menu price (False). Critical for VAT/GST jurisdictions and for accurate revenue recognition and tax remittance calculations.']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Sequential number ordering multiple tax lines within the same order (e.g., 1 = state sales tax, 2 = county tax, 3 = city beverage tax). Ensures deterministic ordering for receipt display and financial reporting.']
  order_channel varchar [note: 'The service channel through which the order was placed (pos = in-store Point of Sale, drive_thru = Drive-Thru (DT), olo = Online Ordering (OLO), third_party_delivery = Third-Party Delivery (3PD), kiosk = self-service kiosk, catering). Used for channel-level tax analysis and compliance.']
  original_tax_amount decimal(12,4) [note: 'The tax amount as originally computed at the time of order placement, before any post-transaction adjustments. Populated when tax_status = \'adjusted\' to preserve the audit trail of the original calculation.']
  period_date date [note: 'The business date (yyyy-MM-dd) to which this tax line is attributed for tax remittance and financial period reporting. May differ from the transaction timestamp for end-of-day batch processing or period adjustments.']
  pos_tax_line_ref varchar [note: 'The native tax line identifier or sequence number assigned by Oracle MICROS POS for this tax entry. Used for cross-system reconciliation between the POS transaction log and the lakehouse Silver Layer.']
  rate decimal(8,6) [note: 'The applicable tax rate expressed as a decimal fraction (e.g., 0.0875 for 8.75%). Sourced from the tax engine or POS tax configuration at the time of the transaction. Used for tax amount validation and audit.']
  refund_amount decimal(12,4) [note: 'The tax amount refunded to the guest when is_refunded is True. May be a partial refund (less than tax_amount) for partial order refunds. Used for financial reconciliation and tax reversal reporting.']
  remittance_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period (e.g., \'2024-Q1\', \'2024-03\') in which this tax amount is scheduled for remittance to the tax authority. Used for tax filing calendar management and period-end close.']
  remittance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this tax amount has been remitted to the applicable tax authority (pending = not yet remitted, remitted = successfully paid to authority, not_applicable = exempt or zero-rate, failed = remittance attempt failed). Supports tax compliance tracking.']
  sap_tax_document_ref varchar [note: 'The SAP S/4HANA FI-TX tax document number associated with this tax line for GL posting and tax remittance. Enables end-to-end traceability from POS transaction to ERP financial posting.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system of record from which this tax line record originated (micros_pos = Oracle MICROS POS, sap_s4hana = SAP S/4HANA FI-TX, olo = Olo Digital Ordering Platform, manual = manually entered). Used for data lineage and reconciliation.']
  state_code varchar [note: 'Two-letter US state code (or equivalent provincial code) of the tax jurisdiction where the restaurant is located (e.g., \'IL\', \'CA\', \'TX\'). Used for state-level tax rate lookup, remittance routing, and regulatory reporting.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'The internal or POS-assigned tax code identifying the specific tax rule applied (e.g., \'TX_CA_SALES\', \'TX_CHI_BEV\'). Maps to the tax condition record in SAP S/4HANA and Oracle MICROS POS tax configuration.']
  tax_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the tax as displayed on the guest receipt or financial report (e.g., \'Illinois Sales Tax\', \'Chicago Beverage Tax\', \'CA Bag Fee\'). Required for receipt compliance and guest transparency.']
  tax_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this tax line record. \'applied\' = tax successfully computed and applied; \'voided\' = tax line cancelled due to order void; \'adjusted\' = tax amount corrected post-transaction; \'pending\' = awaiting tax engine resolution; \'reversed\' = tax reversed for refund.']
  tax_type varchar [note: 'The category of tax applied to this line (e.g., sales_tax, vat, beverage_tax, bag_fee, excise_tax, service_tax). Supports differentiated tax reporting and compliance across jurisdictions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: sales_tax|vat|beverage_tax|bag_fee|excise_tax|service_tax|surcharge|environmental_fee — promote to reference product if additional types are needed]']
  taxable_amount decimal(12,4) [note: 'The portion of the order subtotal that is subject to this specific tax line, expressed in the transaction currency. Excludes non-taxable items, exempt amounts, and tax-inclusive components. Used for tax calculation validation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX) when this tax line record was last modified in the data platform. Used for change data capture (CDC), incremental loads, and audit trail.']
  voided_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time (yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX) when this tax line was voided, if applicable. Populated only when tax_status = \'voided\'. Used for void audit trail and financial reversal processing.']

  Note: 'Records tax line details applied to a guest order, supporting multi-jurisdiction tax compliance. Captures tax authority (federal, state, county, city), tax type (sales tax, VAT, beverage tax, bag fee), tax rate, taxable amount, tax amount, and whether the tax was included in the menu price or added at checkout. Supports financial reconciliation and regulatory tax reporting across all restaurant locations.'
}

Table order.order_ingredient_usage {
  order_ingredient_usage_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the ingredient_usage association']
  order_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the order line item']
  stock_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the inventory stock item']
  quantity_used decimal [note: 'Amount of the stock item consumed by the order line']
  unit_cost decimal [note: 'Cost per unit of the stock item at the time of consumption']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the quantity used (e.g., grams, ounces)']
  waste_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the consumed quantity was recorded as waste']
  waste_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for waste (e.g., spoilage, over‑prep)']

  Note: 'Association between order_item and stock_item that records the amount of each inventory item consumed by a specific line item, including cost and waste information. Each record links one order_item to one stock_item with attributes that exist only in the context of this consumption.. Existence Justification: Each order line (order_item) consumes one or more inventory stock items (ingredients) and each stock item can be consumed by many order lines. The restaurant tracks the quantity used, unit cost, waste flag and waste reason for every such consumption to support costing, waste analysis and traceability. This relationship is actively created and maintained by the kitchen and back‑office systems.'
}

Table order.catering_package {
  catering_package_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for catering_package']
  parent_catering_package_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on catering_package (parent_catering_package_id)']
  availability_end_date date [note: 'Date when the catering package is no longer available for ordering.']
  availability_start_date date [note: 'Date when the catering package becomes available for ordering.']
  calories varchar [note: 'Total calories per serving of the catering package.']
  category varchar [note: 'Category classification of the catering package.']
  code varchar [note: 'External code used to reference the catering package in POS and ordering systems.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the catering package record was created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the price.']
  delivery_zone_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of delivery zone codes where the package is offered.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the items and services included in the package.']
  dietary_restriction varchar [note: 'Dietary restriction tags applicable to the catering package.']
  image_url varchar [note: 'Link to the image representing the catering package.']
  is_customizable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the catering package can be customized by the customer.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of days required to prepare the catering package before delivery.']
  max_custom_items varchar [note: 'Maximum number of additional items that can be added to the package when customized.']
  min_order_quantity varchar [note: 'Minimum number of packages required per order.']
  name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the catering package offered to customers.']
  portion_size varchar [note: 'Standard portion size classification for the catering package.']
  price decimal(10,2) [note: 'Base price of the catering package before taxes and discounts.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the catering package.']
  surcharge_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Additional surcharge applied to the package for special handling.']
  tax_exempt boolean [note: 'Indicates if the catering package is exempt from sales tax.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the catering package record.']
  weight_kg decimal(6,2) [note: 'Total weight of the catering package in kilograms.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for catering_package. Referenced by catering_package_id.'
}

Table order.delivery_platform {
  delivery_platform_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for delivery_platform']
  parent_delivery_platform_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on delivery_platform (parent_delivery_platform_id)']
  average_delivery_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Typical time in minutes from order acceptance to delivery completion for this platform.']
  code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code used to reference the platform in internal systems.']
  contract_end_date date [note: 'Date when the contractual agreement with the platform expires or is terminated (nullable for open‑ended contracts).']
  contract_start_date date [note: 'Date when the contractual agreement with the platform became effective.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the delivery platform record was first created in the system.']
  fee_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Standard commission fee charged by the platform as a percentage of order value.']
  integration_method varchar [note: 'Technical method used to exchange data with the platform.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the delivery platform record.']
  name varchar [note: 'Official name of the delivery platform (e.g., DoorDash, UberEats).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information or remarks about the platform.']
  onboarding_date date [note: 'Date the platform was first integrated and made available for order routing.']
  platform_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the platform based on ownership and integration model.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the delivery platform.']
  support_contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for technical or operational support contacts at the platform.']
  support_phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary phone number for technical or operational support contacts at the platform.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for delivery_platform. Referenced by delivery_platform_id.'
}

Table supply.supply_supplier {
  supply_supplier_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the supply_supplier data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']

  Note: 'Master record for every supplier and vendor in the foodservice supply chain, including food and non-food suppliers, distributors, co-manufacturers, and their key contacts. Captures supplier identity, classification (broadline, specialty, local), approval status, diversity certification, payment terms, lead times, regulatory compliance status (FDA, USDA, HACCP), and primary/secondary contact information. SSOT for supplier identity and contact details across supply chain operations. Sourced from Coupa Procurement supplier master and SAP S/4HANA vendor master (MM).'
}

Table supply.ingredient {
  ingredient_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated surrogate key uniquely identifying each ingredient record.']
  haccp_plan_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to foodsafety.haccp_plan. Business justification: Required for HACCP compliance: each ingredient must be linked to its HACCP plan for safety documentation and audit reporting.']
  item_specification_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.item_specification. Business justification: Ensures Ingredient Quality Assurance by tying each ingredient record to its approved item specification, used in purchasing approval and regulatory compliance checks.']
  allergen_flags varchar [note: 'Pipe‑separated list of allergens present in the ingredient.']
  carbohydrate_content_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of carbohydrates by weight in the ingredient.']
  cost_per_unit decimal(12,4) [note: 'Standard purchase cost for one unit of the ingredient in the specified currency.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'Three‑letter country code indicating where the ingredient was produced or sourced.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the ingredient record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for cost_per_unit (e.g., USD, EUR).']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the ingredient becomes available for ordering.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the ingredient is no longer available (null if indefinite).']
  fat_content_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of total fat by weight in the ingredient.']
  haccp_classification varchar [note: 'Risk level of the ingredient according to HACCP guidelines.']
  halal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the ingredient meets halal certification requirements.']
  ingredient_category varchar [note: 'Broad classification of the ingredient for sourcing, menu engineering, and cost analysis.']
  ingredient_code varchar [note: 'Business code or SKU assigned to the ingredient for ordering and inventory tracking.']
  ingredient_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the ingredient as used in menus, procurement, and reporting.']
  ingredient_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the ingredient in the catalog.']
  inspection_status varchar [note: 'Result of the most recent inspection.']
  kosher_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the ingredient meets kosher certification requirements.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent food safety inspection for this ingredient.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Typical number of days from order placement to receipt of the ingredient.']
  non_gmo_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the ingredient is verified as non‑genetically modified.']
  nutritional_calories_per_unit decimal(8,2) [note: 'Energy content per unit of the ingredient, expressed in kilocalories.']
  organic_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the ingredient is certified organic.']
  packaging_type varchar [note: 'Standard packaging format for the ingredient.']
  par_level varchar [note: 'Periodic Automatic Replenishment Level – minimum inventory quantity to trigger re‑order.']
  protein_content_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of protein by weight in the ingredient.']
  shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'Maximum number of days the ingredient can be stored before it is considered expired.']
  sodium_mg_per_unit decimal(8,2) [note: 'Sodium content per unit of the ingredient, expressed in milligrams.']
  standard_weight_per_unit decimal(12,4) [note: 'Typical weight of one unit of the ingredient in the specified unit_of_measure.']
  storage_temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Recommended storage temperature range for the ingredient, expressed in degrees Celsius.']
  sub_category varchar [note: 'More granular classification within the main category (e.g., "leafy_green", "citrus_fruit").']
  traceability_batch_number varchar [note: 'Batch or lot number used for ingredient traceability throughout the supply chain.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Default unit used to quantify the ingredient in purchasing and inventory (e.g., kilograms, liters).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to the ingredient record.']
  usda_grade varchar [note: 'USDA quality grade assigned to the ingredient.']
  vitamin_c_mg_per_unit decimal(8,2) [note: 'Vitamin C content per unit of the ingredient, expressed in milligrams.']
  waste_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Typical percentage of the ingredient that is wasted during preparation or storage.']

  Note: 'Master catalog of all food ingredients, raw materials, beverages, and packaging SKUs procured across the foodservice supply chain. Captures SKU code, ingredient name, commodity category, unit of measure, allergen flags (Big 9), USDA grade, country of origin, shelf life days, storage temperature requirements (ambient/refrigerated/frozen), and HACCP critical control classification. Serves as the supply-side item master linking to menu domain BOM for recipe costing. SSOT for ingredient identity across supply, inventory, and menu domains. Sourced from SAP MM material master and MarketMan Inventory Management.'
}

Table supply.supply_purchase_order {
  supply_purchase_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the supply_purchase_order data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']
  franchisee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to franchise.franchisee. Business justification: Purchase orders are issued by individual franchisees; linking enables franchise‑level cost allocation and procurement performance reports.']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to realestate.site. Business justification: Internal purchase‑order entity must be associated with the restaurant site it serves for accurate inventory and financial reconciliation.']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to restaurant.unit. Business justification: PURCHASE_ORDER creation is done per restaurant location; linking PO to unit enables inventory budgeting and location‑specific spend reporting.']

  Note: 'Core transactional record for every purchase order issued to suppliers for food ingredients, beverages, packaging, and non-food supplies. Captures PO number, supplier reference, order date, requested delivery date, ship-to distribution center or restaurant, total PO value, currency, payment terms, approval status, and sourcing event linkage. Represents the contractual commitment to buy. Sourced from Coupa Procurement PO module and SAP S/4HANA MM purchasing.'
}

Table supply.purchase_order_line {
  purchase_order_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for purchase_order_line']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to restaurant.unit. Business justification: Each PO line item is fulfilled for a specific restaurant; the FK supports line‑level receipt, cost allocation, and audit trails.']

  Note: 'Line-item detail for each purchase order, capturing individual SKU/ingredient ordered, quantity ordered, unit of measure, agreed unit price, extended line value, COGS allocation, requested delivery date per line, and line status (open, partially received, closed, cancelled). Enables PMIX-level COGS tracking and ingredient-level spend analytics. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA MM (EKPO) and Coupa PO line items.'
}

Table supply.goods_receipt {
  goods_receipt_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for each goods receipt transaction.']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.contract. Business justification: Required for Contract Compliance Report that matches each goods receipt to the governing supplier contract, ensuring receipt quantities and terms align with contract obligations.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Links receipt of goods to cost center to allocate receiving costs and inventory valuation to the responsible cost center.']
  distribution_center_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the distribution center or restaurant where goods were received.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who performed the receipt inspection.']
  inspector_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who performed the receipt inspection.']
  procurement_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'System identifier of the purchase order linked to this receipt.']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the supplier who shipped the goods.']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the distribution center or restaurant where goods were received.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Internal batch identifier assigned during receiving for inventory control.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Optional textual remarks entered by the inspector during receipt.']
  condition varchar [note: 'Overall condition of the received shipment (accepted, rejected, or partially accepted).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the goods receipt record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the total_cost field.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the received goods should not be used or sold.']
  goods_receipt_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the receipt (e.g., open, partial, closed, cancelled).']
  is_cold_chain_compliant boolean [note: 'True if temperature remained within required range during transport and receipt.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Supplier‑provided lot or batch number for traceability.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'External purchase order number referenced by the receipt.']
  receipt_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable receipt number assigned by the receiving system (e.g., MIGO document number).']
  receipt_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the goods were recorded as received.']
  receiving_method varchar [note: 'How the goods arrived at the receiving location (e.g., dock, delivery, third‑party carrier, internal transfer).']
  temperature_celsius decimal(6,2) [note: 'Measured temperature (in Celsius) of the goods at the moment of receipt.']
  temperature_deviation_flag boolean [note: 'True if measured temperature fell outside the acceptable range.']
  total_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the goods received, before any discounts or taxes.']
  total_quantity decimal(12,3) [note: 'Sum of all item quantities received in this receipt (units may be pieces, kg, liters, etc.).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the goods receipt record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of inbound goods received at a distribution center or restaurant, including both header-level receipt information and line-level detail per SKU. Header captures receipt date/time, receiving location, PO reference, receiving condition, and inspector ID. Lines capture specific ingredient/SKU received, quantity accepted/rejected, lot number, expiration date, temperature at receipt (cold chain compliance), storage location assigned, and variance from PO quantity. Critical for three-way match (PO-receipt-invoice), HACCP traceability, and ingredient-level lot tracking. Sourced from SAP S/4HANA MM (MIGO) and MarketMan receiving module.'
}

Table supply.goods_receipt_line {
  goods_receipt_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the goods receipt line record.']
  contract_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.contract_line. Business justification: Needed for Three‑Way Match process linking receipt line to specific contract line for price verification and audit of contracted terms per SKU.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who recorded the receipt.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Associates each receipt line with a GL account for detailed expense posting and audit trail.']
  goods_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent goods receipt transaction.']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supplier providing the goods.']
  purchase_order_line_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.purchase_order_line. Business justification: Goods receipt lines must reference the originating purchase order line; the existing column name does not match the target PK, so a correctly named FK is added and the old column removed.']
