  reservation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this table turn was for a reserved party (True) or walk-in (False). Used to analyze reservation vs walk-in turn time differences.']
  revenue_per_cover decimal(12,2) [note: 'Average revenue per guest cover for this table turn. Calculated as check_total_amount divided by cover_count. Key metric for per-guest revenue analysis.']
  seating_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when guests were seated at the table. Marks the start of the table turn cycle and is used to calculate total turn time.']
  seating_to_order_minutes decimal(8,2) [note: 'Time in minutes from guest seating to order placement. Measures FOH greeting and order-taking efficiency.']
  server_station varchar [note: 'The FOH station or section assignment for the server handling this table. Used for workload balancing and station performance analysis.']
  sos_target_minutes decimal(8,2) [note: 'Target table turn time in minutes for this daypart and restaurant format. Used to measure actual performance against SOS benchmarks.']
  sos_variance_minutes decimal(8,2) [note: 'Variance between actual total turn time and SOS target in minutes. Positive values indicate slower than target; negative values indicate faster than target.']
  special_occasion_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this table turn was for a special occasion (birthday, anniversary, celebration). Special occasions may have longer turn times due to enhanced service.']
  table_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum seating capacity of the table. Used to calculate table utilization percentage (cover_count / table_capacity).']
  table_cleared_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the table was cleared and reset for the next party. Marks the end of the table turn cycle and availability for next seating.']
  table_number varchar [note: 'The unique identifier or number assigned to the table within the restaurant. Used for FOH (Front of House) table management and server station assignment.']
  table_section varchar [note: 'The physical section or zone of the restaurant where the table is located (e.g., main dining, patio, bar area). Used for section-level throughput analysis.']
  total_turn_time_minutes decimal(8,2) [note: 'Total elapsed time in minutes from seating to table cleared. Primary metric for table turn rate analysis and throughput optimization. Calculated as the difference between table_cleared_timestamp and seating_timestamp.']
  turn_date date [note: 'Business date on which the table turn occurred. Used for daily table turn rate reporting and trend analysis. May differ from seating_timestamp date for late-night service.']
  turn_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number indicating which turn this represents for the table during the service period (1st turn, 2nd turn, etc.). Used to track table utilization throughout the day.']
  turn_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the table turn event. Completed indicates normal turn cycle; incomplete indicates missing timestamps; voided indicates cancelled service; abandoned indicates guests left before service completion.']
  wait_time_minutes decimal(8,2) [note: 'Time in minutes that the party waited before being seated. Used to correlate wait times with table turn efficiency and guest satisfaction.']

  Note: 'Transactional log of table turn events at each restaurant unit capturing table identifier, cover count seated, seating time, order-placed time, food-delivered time, check-presented time, table-cleared time, total turn time (minutes), daypart, server station, and party size. Enables table turn rate analysis, cover count optimization, and FOH throughput benchmarking for casual and fine-dining formats.'
}

Table restaurant.capacity_config {
  capacity_config_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the capacity configuration record. Primary key.']
  restaurant_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant unit to which this capacity configuration applies.']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant unit to which this capacity configuration applies.']
  ada_compliant_seating_count varchar [note: 'Number of seating positions that meet ADA accessibility requirements for guests with disabilities.']
  avg_party_size decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average number of guests per dining party used for capacity planning and labor forecasting.']
  bar_seating_count varchar [note: 'Number of seats available at the bar or counter service area.']
  config_effective_date date [note: 'The date from which this capacity configuration becomes effective for the restaurant unit.']
  config_end_date date [note: 'The date on which this capacity configuration ceases to be effective. Null indicates the configuration is currently active.']
  config_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes documenting special considerations, constraints, or context for this capacity configuration.']
  config_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the capacity configuration record.']
  counter_service_positions varchar [note: 'Number of staffed counter service positions available for order taking and guest interaction.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this capacity configuration record was first created in the system.']
  curbside_pickup_spaces varchar [note: 'Number of designated parking spaces available for curbside pickup and online ordering (OLO) fulfillment.']
  delivery_staging_capacity varchar [note: 'Number of completed delivery orders that can be staged simultaneously in the designated pickup area for third-party delivery (3PD) drivers.']
  drive_thru_sos_target_seconds varchar [note: 'Target time in seconds for drive-thru order fulfillment from order point to pickup window.']
  drive_thru_stacking_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum number of vehicles that can queue in the drive-thru lane before reaching the order point.']
  fire_code_occupancy_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum number of occupants (guests and staff) permitted in the facility as determined by local fire safety regulations.']
  health_permit_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum seating capacity approved by the local health department on the restaurant operating permit.']
  indoor_seating_capacity varchar [note: 'Total number of indoor seats available for guest dining in the Front of House (FOH) area.']
  kiosk_count varchar [note: 'Number of self-service ordering kiosks available in the Front of House (FOH) for guest order placement.']
  kitchen_production_capacity_orders_per_hour varchar [note: 'Maximum number of orders the Back of House (BOH) kitchen can produce per hour at full operational capacity.']
  labor_staffing_ratio decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target ratio of Full-Time Equivalent (FTE) staff to seating capacity or covers, used for workforce scheduling and labor cost planning.']
  max_cover_count_breakfast varchar [note: 'Maximum number of guest covers (individual diners) that can be served during the breakfast daypart based on seating and throughput capacity.']
  max_cover_count_dinner varchar [note: 'Maximum number of guest covers that can be served during the dinner daypart based on seating and throughput capacity.']
  max_cover_count_late_night varchar [note: 'Maximum number of guest covers that can be served during the late night daypart based on seating and throughput capacity.']
  max_cover_count_lunch varchar [note: 'Maximum number of guest covers that can be served during the lunch daypart based on seating and throughput capacity.']
  outdoor_seating_capacity varchar [note: 'Total number of outdoor or patio seats available for guest dining.']
  peak_hour_throughput_capacity varchar [note: 'Maximum number of guest transactions or covers that can be processed during the busiest hour of operation.']
  private_dining_capacity varchar [note: 'Total seating capacity in private dining rooms or event spaces within the restaurant.']
  sos_target_seconds varchar [note: 'Target time in seconds from order placement to order delivery, used as a key operational performance metric.']
  table_turn_target_minutes varchar [note: 'Target time in minutes for a complete table turn cycle from guest seating to table reset for the next guest.']
  total_seating_capacity varchar [note: 'Aggregate seating capacity across all dining areas including indoor, outdoor, bar, and private dining.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this capacity configuration record was last modified.']

  Note: 'Defines the seating and service capacity configuration for each restaurant unit including total indoor seating capacity, outdoor/patio seating capacity, bar seating count, private dining room capacity, drive-thru stacking capacity (number of vehicles), kiosk count, counter service positions, maximum cover count per daypart, and ADA-compliant seating count. Used for throughput planning, labor staffing ratios, and health department permit compliance.'
}

Table restaurant.unit_status_history {
  unit_status_history_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each unit status history record. Primary key for the unit status history tracking entity.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'System user identifier of the individual who recorded or approved the status change. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  initiated_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'System user identifier of the individual who recorded or approved the status change. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  restaurant_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant unit to which this status history record applies. Links to the master restaurant entity.']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant unit to which this status history record applies. Links to the master restaurant entity.']
  actual_reopen_date date [note: 'The actual date when the restaurant unit returned to active status. Used to measure variance against expected reopen date and assess project execution.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the status change was formally approved by the appropriate authority. Null if no approval was required or approval is still pending.']
  approval_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the status change required formal approval from corporate leadership, franchise partner, or regulatory authority before implementation.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the individual or role that approved the status change. Used for governance and audit trail purposes.']
  closure_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the closure event distinguishing between planned maintenance closures, emergency shutdowns, regulatory actions, and other closure scenarios. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: planned|unplanned|emergency|seasonal|regulatory|voluntary|involuntary — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing compliance-related information associated with the status change, including regulatory requirements, corrective actions, or legal considerations.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date on which this status became effective for the restaurant unit. Used to determine SSS eligibility windows and operational reporting periods.']
  end_date date [note: 'The date on which this status ceased to be effective, marking the transition to the next status. Null for the current active status record.']
  estimated_revenue_impact decimal(15,2) [note: 'Projected financial impact on unit revenue during the status period, expressed in local currency. Used for financial planning and P&L forecasting.']
  expected_reopen_date date [note: 'Anticipated date when the restaurant unit is expected to return to active status following a temporary closure or renovation. Null if not applicable or unknown.']
  grand_opening_date date [note: 'Official grand opening date for the restaurant unit. Marks the beginning of full operational status and SSS eligibility countdown.']
  health_department_case_number varchar [note: 'Official case or incident number assigned by the local health department if the status change was triggered by a health inspection failure or food safety violation.']
  impact_on_franchise_agreement varchar [note: 'Description of how this status change affects the franchise agreement terms, royalty obligations, or compliance requirements. Relevant for franchise partner relations and legal review.']
  initiated_by varchar [note: 'The party or department that initiated the status change. Identifies accountability and decision authority for lifecycle transitions. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: corporate_operations|franchise_partner|health_department|real_estate|finance|legal|system — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  is_sss_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the restaurant unit is eligible for inclusion in Same-Store Sales (SSS) calculations during this status period. Typically requires 12+ months of continuous active operation.']
  notification_sent_date date [note: 'Date when formal notification of the status change was communicated to relevant stakeholders including franchise partners, corporate operations, and regulatory bodies.']
  nro_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this status record is associated with a New Restaurant Opening (NRO) event. Used to track grand opening milestones and ramp-up performance.']
  previous_status varchar [note: 'The status of the restaurant unit immediately prior to this status change. Enables transition analysis and lifecycle pattern identification.']
  reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating the reason for the status change. Examples include health_violation, lease_expiration, remodel, nro_launch, seasonal_closure, natural_disaster, underperformance, franchise_termination.']
  reason_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative explanation of the reason for the status change. Provides context beyond the standardized reason code for audit and operational review.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp indicating when this status history record was first created in the data platform. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'System timestamp indicating when this status history record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  renovation_scope varchar [note: 'Classification of the renovation or remodel scope when the status change is related to facility improvements. Impacts expected duration and capital expenditure (CapEx) tracking.']
  restaurant_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the restaurant unit. Human-readable store number used in operational systems and reporting.']
  soft_open_start_date date [note: 'Date when the restaurant unit began soft-open operations prior to the official grand opening. Used to track pre-launch performance and operational readiness.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Identifier of the operational system that originated this status change record. Examples include FranConnect, SAP S/4HANA, or manual entry systems.']
  source_system_record_code varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of this status history record in the source operational system. Enables traceability and reconciliation with upstream systems.']
  unit_status_history_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the restaurant unit at this point in the lifecycle. Determines eligibility for Same-Store Sales (SSS) calculations and operational reporting.']

  Note: 'Tracks the full lifecycle status history of each restaurant unit including status transitions (active, temporarily closed, permanently closed, under renovation, pre-opening, soft-open, NRO new restaurant opening), effective date of each status change, reason code, change initiated by (franchise partner, corporate operations, health department), and expected reopen date where applicable. Provides audit trail for unit lifecycle management and SSS eligibility determination.'
}

Table restaurant.area_management {
  area_management_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the area management record. Primary key.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the area manager or district manager responsible for this area. Links to workforce management system.']
  parent_area_area_management_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent area in the management hierarchy. Null for top-level areas.']
  area_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the area or district. Used in operational reporting and area manager assignment.']
  area_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the area or district (e.g., \'Northeast Metro\', \'Southern California Region\').']
  area_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the area management record.']
  area_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the area management level within the organizational hierarchy.']
  auv_target decimal(15,2) [note: 'Target Average Unit Volume (AUV) for restaurants in this area, representing expected annual sales per unit.']
  brand varchar [note: 'Restaurant brand or concept associated with this area (e.g., \'Quick-Service Brand A\', \'Casual Dining Brand B\').']
  cogs_percent_target decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) as a percentage of sales for restaurants in this area.']
  company_owned_unit_count varchar [note: 'Number of company-owned restaurant units within this area.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the primary country in which this area operates.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this area management record was first created in the system.']
  csat_target_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target Customer Satisfaction (CSAT) score for this area, typically measured on a scale of 0-100.']
  division varchar [note: 'Corporate division or business unit to which this area is assigned.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when this area management configuration became effective.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when this area management configuration expires or is superseded. Null for current configurations.']
  franchise_agreement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this area is governed by a franchise area development agreement.']
  franchise_unit_count varchar [note: 'Number of franchised restaurant units within this area.']
  geographic_region varchar [note: 'Broader geographic region to which this area belongs (e.g., \'West Coast\', \'Midwest\', \'Southeast\').']
  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Numeric level in the area management hierarchy, with 1 being the top level (e.g., national) and higher numbers representing more granular levels (e.g., region, district).']
  labor_percent_target decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target labor cost as a percentage of sales for restaurants in this area.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this area management record was last updated.']
  marketing_fund_contribution_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of sales contributed to the marketing fund by units in this area.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes or comments regarding this area management record.']
  nps_target_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target Net Promoter Score (NPS) for this area, measuring customer loyalty and likelihood to recommend.']
  nro_target_count varchar [note: 'Target number of New Restaurant Openings (NRO) planned for this area within the current fiscal year.']
  royalty_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Standard royalty rate percentage applied to franchise units in this area.']
  sos_target_seconds varchar [note: 'Target Speed of Service (SOS) in seconds for restaurants in this area, measuring average transaction time.']
  sss_target_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target Same-Store Sales (SSS) growth percentage for this area, used to measure year-over-year comparable store performance.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'Primary state or province for this area. May be null for multi-state areas.']
  territory_boundary_description varchar [note: 'Textual description of the geographic or operational boundaries defining this area (e.g., \'All units within 50 miles of downtown Chicago\').']
  unit_count varchar [note: 'Total number of restaurant units (company-owned and franchised) within this area.']

  Note: 'Defines the operational management hierarchy above the restaurant unit level. Captures area/district/region boundaries, area manager assignment (name, employee ID, assignment effective dates), number of units in area, geographic region, division, brand, and area performance targets (AUV target, SSS target, CSAT target, Labor% target). Supports multi-unit management accountability, area manager performance reviews, regional performance rollups, and franchise territory alignment. One row per area/district assignment period.'
}

Table restaurant.renovation_project {
  renovation_project_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the renovation project. Primary key.']
  approved_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who approved the renovation project for execution.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who approved the renovation project for execution.']
  franchisee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to franchise.franchisee. Business justification: Renovation projects are initiated, approved, and funded by the franchisee; linking enables franchise‑level renovation cost reporting and compliance tracking.']
  restaurant_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant unit undergoing renovation. Links to the restaurant location being renovated.']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant unit undergoing renovation. Links to the restaurant location being renovated.']
  actual_auv_lift_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Actual measured percentage increase in Average Unit Volume (AUV) achieved post-renovation, typically measured 6-12 months after completion.']
  actual_capex_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Actual total capital expenditure incurred for the renovation project in US dollars.']
  actual_completion_date date [note: 'Actual date when the renovation project was completed and the restaurant was ready for operations.']
  actual_start_date date [note: 'Actual date when the renovation project work commenced.']
  ada_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the renovation project includes ADA compliance upgrades to meet accessibility requirements.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the renovation project was officially approved for execution.']
  architect_firm_name varchar [note: 'Name of the architectural firm that designed the renovation plans.']
  brand_standard_version varchar [note: 'Version of the brand standards and design guidelines being implemented in this renovation project.']
  budget_variance_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Difference between actual and estimated CapEx (actual minus estimated) in US dollars, indicating over or under budget performance.']
  building_permit_number varchar [note: 'Official building permit number issued by local authorities for the renovation work.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for why the renovation project was cancelled, if applicable.']
  closure_duration_days varchar [note: 'Number of days the restaurant was closed to customers during the renovation project.']
  contractor_license_number varchar [note: 'State or local license number of the contractor performing the renovation work.']
  contractor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary general contractor or construction firm responsible for executing the renovation project.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the renovation project record was first created in the system.']
  energy_efficiency_upgrade_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the renovation includes energy efficiency improvements such as LED lighting, HVAC upgrades, or insulation enhancements.']
  estimated_capex_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated total capital expenditure budget for the renovation project in US dollars.']
  expected_auv_lift_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Expected percentage increase in Average Unit Volume (AUV) following completion of the renovation project.']
  final_inspection_date date [note: 'Date when the final building inspection was completed and the renovation was approved for occupancy.']
  financing_method varchar [note: 'Method used to finance the renovation project (corporate funds, franchise funded, loan, lease, mixed).']
  inspection_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the final building inspection (pending, passed, failed, conditional approval).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the renovation project record was last updated in the system.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-form notes and comments about the renovation project for internal reference.']
  partial_closure_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the restaurant remained partially operational during renovation (True) or was fully closed (False).']
  permit_issue_date date [note: 'Date when the building permit was issued by local authorities.']
  planned_completion_date date [note: 'Originally scheduled date for the renovation project to be completed.']
  planned_start_date date [note: 'Originally scheduled date for the renovation project to begin.']
  project_manager_name varchar [note: 'Name of the internal project manager overseeing the renovation project.']
  project_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the renovation project for easy identification and reporting.']
  project_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the renovation project, used for tracking and communication with contractors and stakeholders.']
  project_priority varchar [note: 'Business priority level assigned to the renovation project for resource allocation and scheduling decisions.']
  project_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the renovation project (planned, approved, in-progress, completed, cancelled, on-hold).']
  project_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the renovation project type indicating the scope and nature of work (full remodel, refresh, equipment upgrade, ADA retrofit, brand reimaging, expansion).']
  roi_payback_period_months varchar [note: 'Estimated number of months required to recover the renovation investment through increased revenue and operational savings.']
  scope_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the renovation scope including specific areas, systems, and improvements included in the project.']
  warranty_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the contractor warranty for the renovation work expires.']
  warranty_period_months varchar [note: 'Duration in months of the contractor warranty covering workmanship and materials for the renovation.']

  Note: 'Tracks restaurant unit renovation, remodel, and refresh projects including project type (full remodel, refresh, equipment upgrade, ADA retrofit, brand reimaging), project status (planned, approved, in-progress, completed, cancelled), planned start date, actual start date, planned completion date, actual completion date, estimated CapEx, actual CapEx, contractor name, closure duration (days), and expected AUV lift post-renovation. Coordinates with realestate domain for facility management and finance domain for CapEx tracking.'
}

Table restaurant.ops_visit {
  ops_visit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the operational field visit record. Primary key.']
  checklist_template_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the standardized checklist or audit template used during the operational visit to ensure consistent evaluation criteria.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier of the manager or supervisor who reviewed and approved the operational visit report and findings.']
  primary_ops_visitor_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier of the individual who conducted the operational visit, linking to the workforce management system.']
  restaurant_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant unit where the operational visit was conducted.']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant unit where the operational visit was conducted.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the operational visit report was officially approved by the reviewing manager or supervisor.']
  brand_standard_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Overall assessment of the restaurant units compliance with brand standards and Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) based on the visit findings.']
  checklist_completion_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of checklist items completed during the operational visit, indicating thoroughness of the inspection.']
  cleanliness_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Sub-score assessing the cleanliness and sanitation of Front of House (FOH) and Back of House (BOH) areas, including dining areas, restrooms, and kitchen.']
  corrective_action_due_date date [note: 'Target date by which all corrective actions identified during the visit must be completed and verified.']
  corrective_action_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) specifying whether corrective actions are required as a result of findings from the operational visit.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the operational visit record was first created in the source system.']
  critical_findings_count varchar [note: 'Number of critical violations or findings identified during the operational visit that require immediate corrective action.']
  customer_count_observed varchar [note: 'Approximate number of customers served or present during the operational visit, providing context for throughput and service observations.']
  daypart_observed varchar [note: 'The daypart (e.g., breakfast, lunch, dinner, late night) during which the operational visit was conducted, relevant for assessing performance during specific service periods.']
  follow_up_visit_required_flag boolean [note: 'Boolean indicator (True/False) specifying whether a follow-up visit is required to verify corrective action completion.']
  follow_up_visit_scheduled_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for a follow-up operational visit to verify that corrective actions have been implemented and findings have been resolved.']
  food_quality_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Sub-score evaluating the quality, freshness, preparation, and presentation of food items during the visit.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the operational visit record was last updated or modified in the source system.']
  non_critical_findings_count varchar [note: 'Number of non-critical observations or minor violations identified during the visit that should be addressed but do not pose immediate risk.']
  overall_visit_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Composite score representing the overall performance of the restaurant unit during the operational visit, typically on a 0-100 scale.']
  safety_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Sub-score assessing food safety compliance, Hazard Analysis Critical Control Points (HACCP) adherence, and workplace safety standards during the visit.']
  service_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Sub-score evaluating the quality of customer service, staff friendliness, order accuracy, and guest interaction during the visit.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system from which the visit record was sourced, typically Zenput operational compliance platform.']
  speed_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Sub-score measuring the Speed of Service (SOS), ticket time, and throughput efficiency during the visit, benchmarked against brand standards.']
  staff_count_observed varchar [note: 'Number of staff members (Full-Time Equivalent or FTE) observed working during the visit, relevant for labor efficiency and service quality assessment.']
  visit_category varchar [note: 'Broad category of the operational visit indicating whether it is routine, targeted at specific issues, driven by customer complaints, regulatory-mandated, or pre-opening inspection.']
  visit_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the operational field visit was conducted at the restaurant unit.']
  visit_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total duration of the operational visit in minutes, calculated from start to end timestamp.']
  visit_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the operational visit concluded, marking the completion of the on-site inspection or audit.']
  visit_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field capturing detailed observations, comments, recommendations, and contextual information from the operational visit.']
  visit_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier for the operational visit, used for tracking and reference in operational compliance systems.']
  visit_priority_level varchar [note: 'Priority classification of the operational visit based on the severity of findings, risk level, or strategic importance of the restaurant unit.']
  visit_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The precise date and time when the operational visit began, capturing the start of the on-site inspection or audit.']
  visit_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the operational visit indicating whether it is scheduled, in progress, completed, cancelled, or rescheduled.']
  visit_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the operational visit indicating whether it was scheduled, unannounced, a follow-up to previous findings, New Restaurant Opening (NRO) support, compliance audit, or quality inspection.']
  visitor_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the individual who conducted the operational visit, typically an area manager, brand consultant, or corporate operations team member.']
  visitor_role varchar [note: 'Job title or role of the individual who conducted the visit, such as Area Manager, Brand Consultant, Operations Director, or Quality Assurance Specialist.']
  weather_conditions varchar [note: 'Description of weather conditions during the visit, which may impact customer traffic, throughput, and operational performance observations.']

  Note: 'Records operational field visits conducted by area managers, brand consultants, or corporate operations teams at each restaurant unit. Captures visit date, visit type (scheduled, unannounced, follow-up, NRO opening support), visitor name and role, overall visit score, sub-scores by category (food quality, cleanliness, service, speed, safety), critical findings count, corrective actions required flag, and follow-up visit scheduled date. Sourced from Zenput operational compliance platform.'
}

Table restaurant.ops_visit_finding {
  ops_visit_finding_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the operational visit finding record. Primary key.']
  auditor_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier of the auditor who recorded this finding. Links to workforce management system for auditor credentials and certification tracking.']
  ops_visit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent operational field visit during which this finding was recorded. Links to the ops_visit product.']
  previous_finding_ops_visit_finding_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the ops_visit_finding_id of the prior occurrence if this is a repeat finding. Enables tracking of finding recurrence patterns and corrective action effectiveness.']
  primary_ops_employee_id varchar [note: 'Name of the manager or executive who approved the waiver. Populated only when status is waived. Used for accountability and audit trail.']
  restaurant_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the restaurant unit where this finding was observed. Links to the restaurant product.']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the restaurant unit where this finding was observed. Links to the restaurant product.']
  auditor_name varchar [note: 'Name of the field auditor or inspector who recorded this finding during the operational visit. Used for auditor performance tracking and finding validation.']
  brand_standard_code varchar [note: 'Reference code for the specific brand standard or SOP (Standard Operating Procedure) that was violated or not met. Links to the brand standards repository for detailed requirement documentation.']
  brand_standard_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the brand standard or SOP that was violated. Examples: Hand Washing Protocol, Temperature Log Maintenance, Uniform and Appearance Standard, Drive-Thru Speed of Service.']
  corrective_action_completed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the required corrective action has been completed and verified. True when action is complete and verified; False when pending or in progress.']
  corrective_action_completion_date date [note: 'Actual date when the corrective action was completed and verified. Populated when corrective_action_completed_flag is set to True. Used to calculate resolution time and track overdue items.']
  corrective_action_due_date date [note: 'Target date by which the corrective action must be completed. Set based on severity level: critical findings typically require same-day or next-day resolution, major findings within 7 days, minor findings within 30 days.']
  corrective_action_required varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the corrective action that must be taken to resolve the finding. Specifies what needs to be done, by whom, and any specific requirements or standards that must be met.']
  corrective_action_verification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to verify that the corrective action was completed satisfactorily. Options include on-site inspection by field manager, photographic evidence submitted by unit, document review, manager attestation, or scheduled follow-up visit.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this finding record was first created in the system. System-generated audit field for data lineage and compliance tracking.']
  escalation_level varchar [note: 'Organizational level to which this finding has been escalated. Unit: handled at restaurant level. Area: escalated to area manager. Region: escalated to regional director. Corporate: escalated to corporate compliance or executive leadership.']
  escalation_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this finding requires escalation beyond the restaurant unit level to area management, regional leadership, or corporate compliance. True for critical findings, repeat major findings, or regulatory violations requiring senior leadership notification.']
  financial_impact_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated financial impact of the finding in US dollars. May include cost of waste, potential fines, equipment repair costs, or revenue impact from service disruption. Used for prioritization and ROI analysis of corrective actions.']
  finding_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the finding type. Categories include food safety (HACCP violations, temperature control), cleanliness (sanitation, hygiene), equipment (maintenance, functionality), service (guest experience, speed of service), brand standard (SOP compliance, visual standards), labor (staffing, scheduling, training), and inventory (stock levels, waste management). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: food_safety|cleanliness|equipment|service|brand_standard|labor|inventory — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  finding_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the observed finding. Captures what was observed, where it was observed, and the specific condition or behavior that triggered the finding. Free-text field for auditor notes.']
  finding_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential ordering of findings within the parent visit. Used to maintain the order in which findings were recorded during the field visit.']
  finding_subcategory varchar [note: 'Detailed sub-classification within the finding category. Examples: temperature_control, hand_washing, equipment_calibration, uniform_compliance, waste_tracking. Provides granular segmentation for trend analysis.']
  finding_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the finding was observed and recorded during the field visit. Captures the precise moment of observation for time-sensitive findings such as temperature violations or service delays.']
  guest_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this finding has direct impact on guest experience, satisfaction, or safety. True if the finding affects FOH (Front of House) operations, service quality, food safety, or guest-facing brand standards; False if it is purely BOH (Back of House) or administrative.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this finding record was last updated. System-generated audit field for change tracking and data quality monitoring.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes, context, or observations related to the finding. May include mitigating circumstances, root cause analysis, or special instructions for corrective action.']
  ops_visit_finding_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the finding. Open: newly recorded, awaiting action. In Progress: corrective action underway. Resolved: action completed, awaiting verification. Closed: verified and complete. Waived: finding acknowledged but action waived with justification.']
  photo_evidence_url varchar [note: 'URL or file path to photographic evidence documenting the finding. Used for visual verification, training purposes, and dispute resolution. Links to document management system or cloud storage.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Citation of the specific regulatory code, section, or requirement that was violated. Examples: FDA Food Code 3-501.16, OSHA 1910.22, Local Health Code Section 12.4. Populated only when regulatory_violation_flag is True.']
  regulatory_violation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this finding constitutes a violation of regulatory requirements (FDA, OSHA, local health department). True if the finding violates a legal or regulatory standard; False if it is an internal brand standard deviation only.']
  repeat_finding_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this finding is a repeat of a previously identified issue at this restaurant unit. True if the same or similar finding was recorded in a prior visit; False if this is the first occurrence. Used to identify systemic compliance gaps.']
  repeat_occurrence_count varchar [note: 'Number of times this specific finding has been recorded at this restaurant unit. Incremented each time the same finding is observed in subsequent visits. Used for escalation and intervention triggers.']
  responsible_party_name varchar [note: 'Name of the individual assigned responsibility for completing the corrective action. Typically the restaurant general manager, area manager, or specific department lead depending on the finding category.']
  responsible_party_role varchar [note: 'Job title or role of the individual responsible for the corrective action. Examples: General Manager, Kitchen Manager, Area Manager, Facilities Manager, Training Coordinator.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Classification of the finding\'s impact and urgency. Critical: immediate health/safety risk or major brand violation requiring immediate action. Major: significant compliance gap with potential regulatory or brand impact. Minor: deviation from standard with limited impact. Observation: opportunity for improvement without compliance violation.']
  waiver_approval_date date [note: 'Date when the waiver was approved. Populated only when status is waived.']
  waiver_reason varchar [note: 'Justification for waiving the corrective action requirement. Populated only when status is waived. Requires approval from authorized manager and documentation of business rationale.']

  Note: 'Line-item findings recorded during an operational field visit at a restaurant unit. Each record captures the parent visit reference, finding category (food safety, cleanliness, equipment, service, brand standard, labor), finding description, severity level (critical, major, minor, observation), brand standard violated, corrective action required, corrective action due date, corrective action completed flag, and completion date. Enables granular compliance gap tracking and repeat-finding trend analysis.'
}

Table restaurant.unit_ownership {
  unit_ownership_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each unit ownership record. Primary key for the unit ownership history table.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'The identifier of the user who created this ownership record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  franchise_partner_franchisee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the franchise partner master record when ownership_type is franchised, licensed, joint_venture, master_franchise, or area_development. Null for company-owned units.']
  franchisee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the franchise partner master record when ownership_type is franchised, licensed, joint_venture, master_franchise, or area_development. Null for company-owned units.']
  last_modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'The identifier of the user who last modified this ownership record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  primary_unit_employee_id varchar [note: 'The identifier of the user who created this ownership record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  restaurant_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the restaurant unit for which this ownership record applies. Links to the restaurant master record.']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the restaurant unit for which this ownership record applies. Links to the restaurant master record.']
  area_development_agreement_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this ownership is part of a multi-unit area development agreement. Used for franchise development tracking and compliance reporting.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of this ownership arrangement with franchise agreement terms and brand standards. Used for franchise compliance reporting and risk management.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this ownership record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  financial_reporting_entity_code varchar [note: 'The code identifying the financial reporting entity for P&L consolidation. Maps to the ERP system company code or business unit for financial reporting.']
  franchise_agreement_expiration_date date [note: 'The expiration date of the franchise agreement. Used for renewal planning and compliance tracking.']
  franchise_agreement_number varchar [note: 'The legal agreement number governing this franchise ownership arrangement. Null for company-owned units. Used for compliance tracking and legal reference.']
  franchise_agreement_start_date date [note: 'The start date of the franchise agreement governing this ownership. May differ from ownership_effective_start_date if ownership transferred mid-agreement.']
  initial_franchise_fee_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'The one-time initial franchise fee paid at the start of this ownership arrangement. Null for company-owned units. Used for revenue recognition and franchise economics analysis.']
  joint_venture_partner_name varchar [note: 'The name of the joint venture partner if ownership_type is joint_venture. Used for partnership management and financial reporting.']
  last_compliance_audit_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent compliance audit for this ownership arrangement. Used for audit scheduling and compliance tracking.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this ownership record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  marketing_fee_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of gross sales contributed to the marketing fund under this ownership arrangement. Used for marketing fund accounting and campaign budget allocation.']
  master_franchise_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this ownership operates under a master franchise arrangement with sub-franchising rights. Used for franchise hierarchy management and royalty flow tracking.']
  next_compliance_audit_due_date date [note: 'The scheduled date for the next compliance audit. Used for audit planning and compliance management.']
  operating_entity_legal_name varchar [note: 'The full legal name of the entity that owns and operates this restaurant unit. Used for legal compliance, tax reporting, and franchise agreement documentation.']
  operating_license_expiration_date date [note: 'The expiration date of the operating license. Used for renewal tracking and compliance management.']
  operating_license_number varchar [note: 'The business operating license number issued by local authorities for this ownership entity. Used for regulatory compliance and legal documentation.']
  ownership_effective_end_date date [note: 'The date on which this ownership arrangement ended or will end. Null for current active ownership. Used to close out royalty periods and finalize P&L attribution.']
  ownership_effective_start_date date [note: 'The date on which this ownership arrangement became effective. Used to determine royalty calculation periods and P&L attribution windows.']
  ownership_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing additional context about this ownership arrangement, including special terms, conditions, or historical information.']
  ownership_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of ownership stake held by this entity in joint venture arrangements. 100% for sole ownership. Used for P&L attribution and profit distribution.']
  ownership_record_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique identifier for this ownership record, used in franchise agreements and legal documentation.']
  ownership_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of this ownership record. Active indicates current operational ownership; pending indicates ownership transfer in progress; transferred indicates completed ownership change; terminated indicates closure or reversion; suspended indicates temporary operational hold.']
  ownership_transfer_date date [note: 'The date on which ownership was transferred from a previous owner to the current owner. Used for historical tracking and transfer analysis.']
  ownership_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the ownership model for this restaurant unit. Determines royalty calculation, P&L attribution, and compliance reporting requirements.']
  previous_owner_name varchar [note: 'The name of the previous owner if this ownership record represents a transfer or resale. Used for historical tracking and franchise relationship management.']
  royalty_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'The percentage of gross sales paid as royalty fees under this ownership arrangement. Null for company-owned units. Used for royalty calculation and financial forecasting.']
  tax_identification_number varchar [note: 'The tax identification number (EIN, VAT, or equivalent) of the operating entity. Used for tax reporting and regulatory compliance.']
  territory_code varchar [note: 'The geographic territory code assigned to this franchise ownership. Used for territory management, market planning, and franchise compliance.']
  transfer_approval_date date [note: 'The date on which the franchisor approved the ownership transfer. Used for compliance tracking and transfer timeline analysis.']
  transfer_approved_by varchar [note: 'The name or identifier of the franchisor representative who approved the ownership transfer. Used for audit trail and compliance documentation.']
  transfer_reason varchar [note: 'The business reason for this ownership record being created or terminated. Used for franchise compliance reporting, trend analysis, and strategic planning. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: new_franchise_award|resale|corporate_acquisition|refranchising|closure|default|voluntary_termination|expiration — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']

  Note: 'Tracks the ownership and operational control history of each restaurant unit including ownership type (company-owned, franchised, licensed, joint-venture), franchise partner reference, ownership effective start date, ownership effective end date, transfer reason (new franchise award, resale, corporate acquisition, closure), and operating entity legal name. Provides the authoritative ownership timeline for royalty calculation, P&L attribution, and franchise compliance reporting. Complements the franchise domain without duplicating franchise partner master data.'
}

Table restaurant.performance_period {
  performance_period_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the performance period record. Primary key for the performance period entity.']
  prior_performance_period_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on performance_period (prior_performance_period_id)']
  prior_period_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the immediately preceding performance period of the same type. Used for sequential period-over-period trending and variance analysis.']
  prior_year_period_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the corresponding performance period from the prior fiscal year. Used for year-over-year variance analysis and SSS calculations.']
  auv_calculation_window_months varchar [note: 'The number of trailing months used to calculate Average Unit Volume (AUV) for this period. Typically 12 months for annual AUV or 3 months for quarterly AUV. Defines the rolling window for revenue averaging.']
  business_day_count varchar [note: 'The number of operating days in this performance period, excluding holidays and closures. Used for calculating business-day-adjusted metrics.']
  calendar_month varchar [note: 'The calendar month number (1-12) in which the period starts. Used for month-over-month trending and seasonality analysis.']
  calendar_quarter varchar [note: 'The calendar quarter number (1-4) in which the period starts. Used for calendar-based quarterly comparisons.']
  calendar_year varchar [note: 'The calendar year in which the period starts. Used for calendar-based reporting and alignment with external benchmarks.']
  comp_sales_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this period qualifies for comparable sales analysis. Synonymous with SSS eligibility; requires 12+ months of continuous operation for year-over-year performance comparison.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this performance period record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  day_count varchar [note: 'The total number of calendar days in this performance period. Used for normalizing metrics and calculating daily averages.']
  fiscal_month varchar [note: 'Month number (1‑12) of the fiscal year for the period.']
  fiscal_quarter varchar [note: 'The fiscal quarter number (1-4) within the fiscal year. Used for quarterly performance aggregation and reporting.']
  fiscal_week varchar [note: 'Week number (1‑53) of the fiscal year for the period.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year to which this performance period belongs. Represents the 12-month accounting period used for financial reporting and performance measurement.']
  holiday_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this period contains one or more major holidays that significantly impact restaurant traffic and sales patterns. Used for seasonality adjustments and forecasting.']
  holiday_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of major holidays occurring within this performance period (e.g., \'Thanksgiving, Black Friday\'). Used for detailed seasonality analysis and promotional planning.']
  is_closed boolean [note: 'True when the period has been finalized and no further data edits are allowed.']
  is_current boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this period is the active reporting window.']
  is_leap_year boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the fiscal year for this period is a leap year (366 days). Used for accurate day-count adjustments in financial calculations.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this performance period record was most recently updated. Used for change tracking and audit compliance.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing special circumstances, adjustments, or contextual information about this performance period (e.g., \'System outage on 3/15\', \'Major weather event impacted sales\').']
  performance_period_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the period record.']
  period_category varchar [note: 'High‑level business domain the period is primarily used for.']
  period_close_date date [note: 'The date on which the performance period was officially closed for reporting. Represents when preliminary financial and operational results were finalized.']
  period_code varchar [note: 'Business-friendly code used to reference the period in reports and systems.']
  period_duration_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days covered by the period (derived from start and end dates).']
  period_end_date date [note: 'The last calendar date included in this performance period. Defines the ending boundary for all transactions and metrics captured within this period.']
  period_lock_date date [note: 'The date on which the performance period was locked, preventing any further transactional or adjustment entries. Ensures data integrity for audited financial reporting.']
  period_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or label for the performance period (e.g., \'FY2024 Period 3\', \'Q1 2024\', \'Week 12\'). Used for reporting displays and user interfaces.']
  period_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the period within the fiscal year (e.g., Period 1, Period 2). Used for ordering and identification of periods within a fiscal year.']
  period_start_date date [note: 'The first calendar date included in this performance period. Defines the beginning boundary for all transactions and metrics captured within this period.']
  period_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the performance period. Future indicates the period has not yet started; open indicates the period is in progress and transactions are being recorded; closed indicates the period has ended and preliminary results are available; locked indicates the period is finalized and no further changes are allowed; restated indicates the period has been reopened for corrections or adjustments.']
  period_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the reporting period granularity. Daily for day-level tracking, weekly for 7-day cycles, period for 4-week accounting periods, quarter for 13-week fiscal quarters, and annual for full fiscal year.']
  reporting_currency_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code used for financial reporting in this period (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). All monetary metrics for this period are expressed in this currency.']
  seasonality_index decimal(5,2) [note: 'Statistical index representing the expected seasonal variation for this period relative to the annual average (1.00 = average, >1.00 = above average, <1.00 = below average). Used for demand forecasting and labor planning.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system of record that supplied the period data (e.g., SAP S/4HANA, Oracle MICROS).']
  source_system_record_code varchar [note: 'Native identifier of the period record in the source system.']
  sss_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this period is eligible for Same-Store Sales (SSS) / Comparable Store Sales (Comp Sales) calculations. True if the period is at least 12 months after the restaurant opening date, enabling year-over-year comparisons.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the period record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the period definition for change‑tracking.']
  week_number varchar [note: 'The ISO week number (1-53) within the calendar year. Used for weekly performance tracking and labor scheduling alignment.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for performance_period. '
}

Table restaurant.pos_terminal {
  pos_terminal_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the POS terminal. Primary key.']
  replaced_pos_terminal_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on pos_terminal (replaced_pos_terminal_id)']
  replacement_terminal_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the POS terminal that replaced this unit, if applicable.']
  restaurant_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant unit to which the terminal is assigned.']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant unit where this POS terminal is deployed.']
  assigned_station varchar [note: 'Physical station or service point where the terminal is deployed (e.g., FOH counter, DT window 1, DT window 2, bar, patio, expo).']
  cash_drawer_attached boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a cash drawer is attached to this POS terminal.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this POS terminal record was first created in the system.']
  decommission_date date [note: 'Date when the POS terminal was decommissioned and removed from active service.']
  encryption_key_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the encryption key used to secure transaction data on the terminal.']
  firmware_version varchar [note: 'Version of the firmware currently running on the terminal.']
  floor_number varchar [note: 'Numeric floor level of the terminal location, useful for multi‑story venues.']
  hardware_model varchar [note: 'Manufacturer model number or designation of the physical POS terminal hardware.']
  installation_date date [note: 'Date when the POS terminal was first installed and commissioned at the restaurant location.']
  ip_address varchar [note: 'Network IP address assigned to the POS terminal for connectivity and remote management.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the POS terminal is currently active and operational.']
  last_audit_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent compliance or security audit performed on the terminal.']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent maintenance or service performed on the terminal.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this POS terminal record was last updated.']
  last_pci_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent PCI DSS compliance audit or assessment for this terminal.']
  last_software_update_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent software update or patch applied to the terminal.']
  location_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of where the terminal is situated within the restaurant (e.g., "Front Counter – Station 3").']
  mac_address varchar [note: 'Hardware MAC address of the POS terminal network interface.']
  maintenance_frequency_days varchar [note: 'Scheduled interval in days between routine maintenance activities.']
  manufacturer varchar [note: 'Company that produced the POS terminal hardware.']
  model_number varchar [note: 'Model identifier assigned by the manufacturer.']
  network_type varchar [note: 'Physical or logical network connection type used by the terminal.']
  next_maintenance_due_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next routine maintenance.']
  next_pci_audit_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next PCI DSS compliance audit or assessment.']
  next_scheduled_maintenance_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next preventive maintenance or service check.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes for additional context, special configurations, or operational remarks about the terminal.']
  operational_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the POS terminal in the restaurant operations.']
  payment_methods_supported varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of payment method codes the terminal can accept (e.g., "credit,debit,cash,applepay").']
  payment_processing_capability varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of payment methods supported by this terminal (e.g., credit, debit, NFC, gift_card, mobile_wallet).']
  payment_processing_vendor varchar [note: 'Name of the third‑party processor handling card authorizations for this terminal.']
  pci_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current PCI DSS compliance status of the terminal for secure payment processing.']
  pos_terminal_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the terminal.']
  printer_attached boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a receipt printer is attached to this POS terminal.']
  security_compliance varchar [note: 'Compliance certification applicable to the terminal\'s payment processing.']
  serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer-assigned serial number for the POS terminal hardware unit.']
  service_channel varchar [note: 'Primary service channel that this terminal supports for order capture and fulfillment.']
  software_version varchar [note: 'Current version of the Oracle MICROS POS software installed on the terminal.']
  station_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the station area: Front of House (FOH), Back of House (BOH), Drive-Thru (DT), kiosk, or mobile.']
  supports_chip boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the terminal can read EMV chip cards.']
  supports_contactless boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the terminal can process contactless (NFC) payments.']
  supports_credit_card boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the terminal can process credit card payments.']
  supports_debit_card boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the terminal can process debit card payments.']
  supports_gift_card boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the terminal can process gift card payments.']
  supports_magstripe boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the terminal can read magnetic stripe cards.']
  supports_mobile_wallet boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the terminal supports mobile wallet payments.']
  supports_nfc boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the terminal supports contactless NFC payments (e.g., Apple Pay, Google Pay).']
  supports_olo boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the terminal can process online orders placed through digital channels.']
  supports_qr boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the terminal can scan QR codes for payment or ordering.']
  supports_third_party_delivery boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the terminal can process orders from third-party delivery platforms.']
  terminal_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or asset tag used in inventory and reporting.']
  terminal_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code used for transactions processed by this terminal.']
  terminal_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name assigned to the terminal for operational identification.']
  terminal_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing terminal number or code used for operational identification and reporting.']
  terminal_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the POS terminal by its operational role and form factor.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the terminal record.']
  warranty_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the manufacturer warranty for the POS terminal hardware expires.']
  zone varchar [note: 'Area of the restaurant where the terminal operates.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for pos_terminal. Referenced by: loyalty.offer_redemption.pos_terminal_id, loyalty.payment_method_link.pos_terminal_id, loyalty.redemption.pos_terminal_id, loyalty.visit.pos_terminal_id, order.drive_thru_event.pos_terminal_id'
}

Table restaurant.store_campaign_assignment {
  store_campaign_assignment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the store_campaign_assignment association']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the marketing campaign']
  sponsor_restaurant_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sponsoring restaurant (if applicable)']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the restaurant unit']
  actual_adt_lift_percent decimal [note: 'Measured lift in average daily transactions']
  actual_comp_sales_lift_percent decimal [note: 'Measured lift in comparable store sales']
  actual_spend decimal [note: 'Actual spend recorded for the unit on this campaign']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the assignment was approved']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval status of the unit\'s participation']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name of approver for the unit\'s campaign assignment']
  channel varchar [note: 'Marketing channel(s) used for the unit']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the unit complies with campaign guidelines']
  end_date date [note: 'Planned end date of the campaign for the unit']
  execution_end_date date [note: 'Actual execution end date at the unit']
  execution_start_date date [note: 'Actual execution start date at the unit']
  expected_adt_lift_percent decimal [note: 'Planned lift in average daily transactions']
  expected_comp_sales_lift_percent decimal [note: 'Planned lift in comparable store sales']
  initiative_code varchar [note: 'Business code for the initiative']
  initiative_name varchar [note: 'Name of the initiative linking unit and campaign']
  lmf_fund_amount decimal [note: 'Local marketing fund amount allocated']
  lmf_fund_used decimal [note: 'Local marketing fund amount used']
  lmf_remaining_amount decimal [note: 'Remaining local marketing fund amount']
  market_dma varchar [note: 'Designated market DMA for the unit in this campaign']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes about the unit\'s campaign execution']
  planned_spend decimal [note: 'Budgeted spend for the unit on this campaign']
  start_date date [note: 'Planned start date of the campaign for the unit']
  store_campaign_assignment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the unit\'s participation in the campaign']
  target_audience varchar [note: 'Target guest segment for the unit']

  Note: 'Represents the assignment of a marketing campaign to a specific restaurant unit. Each record captures spend, performance, compliance, and other campaign‑specific attributes that exist only in the context of that unit‑campaign pairing.. Existence Justification: A restaurant unit can participate in multiple marketing campaigns, and each campaign is executed across many units. The business actively tracks per‑unit spend, performance metrics, compliance flags, and other campaign‑specific details, making the relationship a managed entity rather than a simple reference.'
}

Table restaurant.kitchen_station {
  kitchen_station_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for kitchen_station']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee responsible for the station.']
  location_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the restaurant location where the station resides.']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to restaurant.unit. Business justification: Kitchen stations belong to a specific restaurant unit; adding unit_id creates the necessary parent link and eliminates isolation.']
  upstream_kitchen_station_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on kitchen_station (upstream_kitchen_station_id)']
  area_sqft decimal(8,2) [note: 'Physical floor area occupied by the station.']
  average_ticket_time_seconds varchar [note: 'Average time a ticket spends at this station.']
  capacity_dishes varchar [note: 'Maximum number of dishes the station can handle concurrently.']
  daypart_schedule varchar [note: 'Meal periods during which the station is active.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the station became operational.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the station is scheduled to be retired or decommissioned (null if open‑ended).']
  equipment_list varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of major equipment assigned to the station.']
  health_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent health inspection.']
  health_inspection_status varchar [note: 'Result of the most recent health inspection for the station.']
  is_automated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the station includes automated equipment.']
  last_maintenance_date date [note: 'Date when the station last underwent preventive maintenance.']
  maintenance_interval_days varchar [note: 'Scheduled number of days between routine maintenance events.']
  operational_hours varchar [note: 'Standard daily operating window (e.g., "08:00-22:00").']
  power_rating_kw decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum electrical power consumption of the station.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the kitchen station record was first created.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the kitchen station record.']
  speed_of_service_sos_seconds varchar [note: 'Target time in seconds to complete a dish at this station.']
  station_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code used in operational systems and SOPs.']
  station_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the kitchen station (e.g., "Grill Station 1").']
  station_type varchar [note: 'Category of the station based on function (e.g., prep, cook, plating).']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the station.']
  temperature_control boolean [note: 'Indicates if the station has temperature‑control capabilities.']
  temperature_range_c varchar [note: 'Allowed temperature range for the station (e.g., "0-5").']
  throughput_per_hour varchar [note: 'Average number of dishes processed by the station each hour.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for kitchen_station. Referenced by station_id.'
}

Table restaurant.brand {
  brand_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for brand']
  parent_brand_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent brand in a brand hierarchy, if applicable.']
  average_annual_sales_usd decimal(15,2) [note: 'Typical yearly sales revenue per brand unit, expressed in US dollars.']
  average_check_amount_usd decimal(10,2) [note: 'Typical spend per guest (average ticket) in US dollars.']
  average_daily_customers varchar [note: 'Mean number of guests served per day across brand locations.']
  average_employee_count varchar [note: 'Typical number of staff employed per restaurant for the brand.']
  average_store_size_sqft decimal(10,2) [note: 'Mean square‑footage of restaurants operating under the brand.']
  brand_category varchar [note: 'High‑level category describing the brand\'s product focus.']
  brand_code varchar [note: 'External alphanumeric code used to reference the brand in corporate systems.']
  brand_color_hex varchar [note: 'Hexadecimal representation of the brand\'s primary color.']
  brand_segment varchar [note: 'Target consumer segment for the brand.']
  brand_sos_target_seconds varchar [note: 'Targeted service time from order to delivery for the brand.']
  brand_tagline varchar [note: 'Short marketing slogan associated with the brand.']
  brand_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the brand by service format.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the brand record was first created in the system.']
  description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the brand\'s positioning and concept.']
  established_date date [note: 'Date the brand was first launched.']
  franchise_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the brand is offered as a franchise model.']
  franchise_fee_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Initial franchise fee expressed as a percent of initial investment.']
  headquarters_city varchar [note: 'City where the brand\'s corporate headquarters is located.']
  headquarters_country_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the brand\'s headquarters.']
  logo_url varchar [note: 'Link to the brand\'s primary logo image.']
  market_share_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated share of the market segment held by the brand.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the brand.']
  primary_market_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region where the brand has its strongest presence.']
  royalty_fee_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Ongoing royalty fee charged to franchisees, expressed as a percent of sales.']
  social_media_handle varchar [note: 'Primary social media identifier (e.g., @brand) used for marketing.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the brand.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the brand record.']
  website_url varchar [note: 'Public website address for the brand.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for brand. Referenced by brand_id.'
}

Table restaurant.department {
  department_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for department']
  franchisee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the franchise to which the department belongs (null for company‑owned).']
  parent_department_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the immediate parent department in the organizational hierarchy.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the department.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information (suite, floor, etc.).']
  area_sqft decimal(10,2) [note: 'Square‑footage occupied by the department.']
  average_service_time_minutes varchar [note: 'Average time in minutes to complete a core service task for the department.']
  budget_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Annual budget allocated to the department (in USD).']
  city varchar [note: 'City in which the department is situated.']
  classification varchar [note: 'Higher‑level classification used for reporting and cost allocation.']
  closing_date date [note: 'Date the department was closed (null if still operating).']
  code varchar [note: 'Business‑assigned short code that uniquely identifies the department within the enterprise.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Internal cost‑center identifier used for financial reporting.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 country code where the department is located.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the department record was first created in the system.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing the department\'s purpose and responsibilities.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the department became operational.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the department ceased operation (null if still active).']
  email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for department communications.']
  floor_number varchar [note: 'Physical floor on which the department is located.']
  headcount varchar [note: 'Number of full‑time equivalent staff assigned to the department.']
  is_franchise boolean [note: 'True if the department belongs to a franchised unit.']
  is_primary boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this department is the primary unit for its location.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the department.']
  opening_date date [note: 'Date the department first opened for business.']
  operational_hours varchar [note: 'Standard daily operating hours expressed as HH:MM‑HH:MM.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact telephone number for the department.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal code for the department\'s address.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter code representing the geographic region.']
  service_level varchar [note: 'Level of service the department is expected to deliver.']
  shift_schedule varchar [note: 'Primary shift schedule pattern used by the department.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the department.']
  turnover_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Annual percentage of staff turnover in the department.']
  type varchar [note: 'Categorizes the department by its functional area.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the department record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for department. Referenced by department_id.'
}

Table restaurant.checklist_template {
  checklist_template_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for checklist_template']
  parent_checklist_template_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on checklist_template (parent_checklist_template_id)']
  applicable_restaurant_type varchar [note: 'Restaurant format(s) for which the checklist is intended.']
