  approval_required boolean [note: 'True if the checklist must be approved before use.']
  code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or code used to reference the template in operational systems.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the template record was first created.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed free‑text description of the template purpose and scope.']
  duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Typical time required to complete the checklist, in minutes.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the checklist template becomes effective for use.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the checklist template expires or is superseded (null if open‑ended).']
  frequency varchar [note: 'How often the checklist should be executed.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether completion of the checklist is mandatory.']
  language varchar [note: 'ISO 639‑1 language code of the template content.']
  last_reviewed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the template was last reviewed.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the checklist template.']
  owner_role varchar [note: 'Organizational role responsible for maintaining the template (e.g., Operations Manager).']
  region varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country/region code where the template is applicable.']
  review_cycle varchar [note: 'Scheduled interval for formal review of the template.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the template.']
  template_file_path varchar [note: 'File system or storage path where the template document resides.']
  template_format varchar [note: 'File format of the stored template.']
  type varchar [note: 'Category that defines the purpose of the checklist (e.g., safety, training).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent update to the template record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for change control (e.g., v1.2).']

  Note: 'Master reference table for checklist_template. Referenced by checklist_template_id.'
}

Table workforce.employee {
  employee_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each employee.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Specific work site (e.g., kitchen, dining area) within the restaurant.']
  manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee\'s direct manager.']
  restaurant_unit_id varchar [note: 'Primary restaurant location to which the employee is assigned.']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Primary restaurant location to which the employee is assigned.']
  work_location_unit_id varchar [note: 'Specific work site (e.g., kitchen, dining area) within the restaurant.']
  address_line1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address of the employee\'s residence.']
  address_line2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address information (apartment, suite, etc.).']
  bank_account_number varchar [note: 'Bank account number used for direct deposit of payroll.']
  city varchar [note: 'City of the employee\'s residence.']
  country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code of the employee\'s residence. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USA|CAN|MEX|GBR|FRA|DEU|JPN|CHN — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  date_of_birth date [note: 'Birth date of the employee, used for age‑based compliance and benefits.']
  department varchar [note: 'Organizational department or functional group.']
  email varchar [note: 'Primary email address used for employee communications.']
  emergency_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the person to contact in case of emergency.']
  emergency_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the emergency contact.']
  employment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the employee within the organization.']
  employment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of employment relationship (Full‑Time, Part‑Time, Contractor).']
  first_name varchar [note: 'Legal first name of the employee.']
  full_name varchar [note: 'Concatenated first and last name for display purposes.']
  hire_date date [note: 'Date the employee started employment with the company.']
  job_title varchar [note: 'Official title or position held by the employee.']
  labor_percentage_target decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target labor cost as a percentage of sales for the employee\'s role.']
  last_name varchar [note: 'Legal last name of the employee.']
  overtime_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates if the employee is eligible for overtime pay.']
  pay_grade varchar [note: 'Internal pay grade code used for compensation planning.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary contact phone number for the employee.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the employee\'s residence.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the employee record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the employee record.']
  role_classification varchar [note: 'Primary functional area where the employee works: Back‑of‑House, Front‑of‑House, Administrative, Support.']
  salary_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Annual base salary amount before bonuses or overtime.']
  salary_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the salary amount.']
  servsafe_certified boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employee holds a valid ServSafe food safety certification.']
  servsafe_expiration_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the employee\'s ServSafe certification.']
  shift_pattern varchar [note: 'Standard shift pattern assigned to the employee.']
  state varchar [note: 'State or province of the employee\'s residence.']
  tax_identifier varchar [note: 'Government‑issued tax identification number (e.g., SSN, SIN).']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date the employee\'s employment ended, if applicable.']
  union_member boolean [note: 'True if the employee is a member of a labor union.']
  work_schedule_type varchar [note: 'Scheduling model applied to the employee.']

  Note: 'Master record for every restaurant employee across company-owned and franchised locations. Captures full employee lifecycle data including personal details, employment type (FTE/PTE), BOH/FOH role classification, hire date, termination date, employment status, pay grade, home restaurant assignment, Workday HCM employee ID, declared availability windows (preferred dayparts, max weekly hours, blackout dates, cross-location availability), and current benefit enrollment summary. Single source of truth for workforce identity and worker profile across the enterprise.'
}

Table workforce.position {
  position_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the job position.']
  reports_to_position_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supervisory position to which this role reports.']
  classification varchar [note: 'Primary classification indicating whether the role is Back‑of‑House (BOH), Front‑of‑House (FOH), or Support.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the position record was first created.']
  department varchar [note: 'Organizational department to which the position belongs (e.g., Kitchen, Service, Maintenance).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the position is retired or no longer available (nullable).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the position becomes active for hiring.']
  flsa_exempt boolean [note: 'True if the position is exempt from Fair Labor Standards Act overtime rules.']
  fte_equivalency decimal(5,2) [note: 'Full‑time equivalent factor used for labor forecasting (e.g., 1.0 = full‑time, 0.5 = half‑time).']
  hourly_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'Base hourly compensation for hourly‑paid positions.']
  is_manager boolean [note: 'True if the position includes managerial responsibilities.']
  labor_percentage_target decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target labor cost as a percentage of sales for this position.']
  minimum_age varchar [note: 'Minimum legal age required to be eligible for the position.']
  overtime_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the position is eligible for overtime pay.']
  pay_band varchar [note: 'Compensation band used for salary or hourly rate determination.']
  position_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code used to reference the position in HR and scheduling systems.']
  position_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of duties, responsibilities, and scope of the position.']
  position_level varchar [note: 'Career level of the position within the organization hierarchy.']
  position_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the position.']
  required_certifications varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of mandatory certifications (e.g., ServSafe, Food Handler).']
  required_experience_years varchar [note: 'Minimum years of relevant experience required for the position.']
  salary_max decimal(10,2) [note: 'Upper bound of the annual salary range for salaried positions.']
  salary_min decimal(10,2) [note: 'Lower bound of the annual salary range for salaried positions.']
  shift_type varchar [note: 'Typical shift pattern associated with the position.']
  title varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the job position (e.g., Crew Member, Shift Lead).']
  union_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the position is eligible for union representation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the position record.']

  Note: 'Defines authorized job positions within the restaurant organization, including role title (e.g., Crew Member, Shift Lead, Kitchen Manager, FOH Supervisor), BOH/FOH classification, pay band, FLSA exemption status, required certifications (e.g., ServSafe), FTE equivalency factor, and whether the position is eligible for overtime. Serves as the job catalog for workforce planning and scheduling.'
}

Table workforce.shift {
  shift_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the shift record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who scheduled the shift.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Restaurant location where the shift occurs.']
  shift_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee assigned to the shift.']
  shift_scheduled_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the user who scheduled the shift.']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Restaurant location where the shift occurs.']
  actual_end timestamp [note: 'Actual clock‑out timestamp recorded for the shift.']
  actual_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Actual worked hours for the shift (excluding breaks).']
  actual_start timestamp [note: 'Actual clock‑in timestamp recorded for the shift.']
  break_duration_minutes varchar [note: 'Total break time in minutes allocated for the shift.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the shift record was created.']
  daypart varchar [note: 'Part of the day the shift is scheduled for.']
  is_deleted boolean [note: 'Flag indicating soft deletion of the shift record.']
  labor_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total labor cost for the shift (rate multiplied by actual hours).']
  labor_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Labor cost expressed as a percentage of projected sales for the shift.']
  labor_rate_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the labor rate.']
  labor_rate_per_hour decimal(8,2) [note: 'Pay rate per hour for the employee on this shift.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments about the shift.']
  on_call_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the shift is an on‑call assignment.']
  overtime_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the shift includes overtime hours.']
  scheduled_end timestamp [note: 'Planned end timestamp of the shift.']
  scheduled_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Planned shift duration in hours (excluding breaks).']
  scheduled_start timestamp [note: 'Planned start timestamp of the shift.']
  shift_code varchar [note: 'Human‑readable code for the shift (e.g., SHIFT20230915-001).']
  shift_date date [note: 'Calendar date of the shift (derived from scheduled_start).']
  shift_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the shift.']
  shift_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the shift (e.g., regular, overtime, on‑call, training).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System that originated the shift schedule.']
  station varchar [note: 'Work station or area assigned to the employee (Back‑of‑House, Front‑of‑House, etc.).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the shift record.']

  Note: 'Represents a scheduled work shift for an employee at a specific restaurant location, daypart (breakfast, lunch, dinner, late-night), and station assignment (grill, fry, drive-thru, expo, host, bar, dish). Captures planned start/end times, actual clock-in/clock-out times, assigned BOH/FOH station, shift type (regular, overtime, on-call, training), break duration, scheduling source (Planday), and swap/coverage details (original assignee, covering employee, swap request reason, swap approval status, approval timestamp) when shift reassignment occurs. Core operational record for labor deployment, station coverage planning, Speed of Service (SOS) staffing optimization, and Planday shift coverage workflows.'
}

Table workforce.schedule {
  schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each labor schedule.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the manager or supervisor who approved the schedule.']
  manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the manager or supervisor who approved the schedule.']
  restaurant_unit_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the restaurant unit for which the schedule is created.']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the restaurant unit for which the schedule is created.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the schedule was formally approved by the manager.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Display name of the manager who approved the schedule.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the schedule record was first created in the system.']
  daypart_schedule_notes varchar [note: 'Optional comments or special instructions for each daypart (e.g., event staffing, overtime alerts).']
  fte_evening decimal(5,2) [note: 'Planned full‑time equivalent staff for the evening shift (e.g., 16:00‑22:00).']
  fte_midday decimal(5,2) [note: 'Planned full‑time equivalent staff for the midday shift (e.g., 12:00‑16:00).']
  fte_morning decimal(5,2) [note: 'Planned full‑time equivalent staff for the morning shift (e.g., 06:00‑12:00).']
  fte_night decimal(5,2) [note: 'Planned full‑time equivalent staff for the night shift (e.g., 22:00‑02:00).']
  fte_total decimal(5,2) [note: 'Sum of planned FTEs across all dayparts for the schedule period.']
  labor_pct_evening decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target labor cost as a percentage of sales for the evening shift.']
  labor_pct_midday decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target labor cost as a percentage of sales for the midday shift.']
  labor_pct_morning decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target labor cost as a percentage of sales for the morning shift.']
  labor_pct_night decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target labor cost as a percentage of sales for the night shift.']
  labor_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target labor cost expressed as a percentage of projected sales for the period.']
  period_end_date date [note: 'Last calendar date covered by the schedule.']
  period_start_date date [note: 'First calendar date covered by the schedule.']
  schedule_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable schedule code assigned by the scheduling system (e.g., SCHED‑2024‑W15).']
  schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the schedule: draft (editable), published (visible to staff), or locked (finalized).']
  total_scheduled_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Aggregate number of labor hours planned across all dayparts and positions for the schedule period.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the schedule.']
  version varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the schedule used for audit and rollback purposes.']

  Note: 'Weekly or period-level labor schedule published for a restaurant location, representing the planned staffing plan across all dayparts. Captures schedule period (start/end dates), restaurant unit, total scheduled hours, scheduled Labor%, FTE count by daypart, publication status (draft/published/locked), and the manager who approved the schedule. Links to individual shifts for granular staffing detail.'
}

Table workforce.time_entry {
  time_entry_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the time entry record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manager who performed the approval.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant location where the work was performed.']
  primary_time_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who performed the time entry.']
  shift_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the scheduled shift associated with this time entry.']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant location where the work was performed.']
  approved_by_manager boolean [note: 'True when a manager has approved the time entry.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the entry was approved by a manager.']
  break_flag boolean [note: 'True if a break was taken during the shift.']
  break_minutes varchar [note: 'Total minutes taken for breaks during the shift.']
  clock_in_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the employee clocked in.']
  clock_out_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the employee clocked out.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the time entry record was first created.']
  entry_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable unique code for the time entry.']
  job_role varchar [note: 'Functional role of the employee during the shift.']
  labor_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary cost of the labor for this entry (total_hours * labor_rate).']
  labor_rate decimal(8,4) [note: 'Hourly wage rate applicable to the employee for this entry (in local currency).']
  missed_punch_flag boolean [note: 'True if the employee missed a clock‑in or clock‑out punch.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Optional free‑form comments or remarks about the time entry.']
  overtime_flag boolean [note: 'True if any overtime hours were recorded.']
  overtime_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of overtime hours worked.']
  pay_code varchar [note: 'Payroll code indicating the type of pay (regular, overtime, sick leave, etc.).']
  regular_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of non‑overtime hours worked.']
  scheduled_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned end date‑time of the shift according to the schedule.']
  scheduled_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Planned start date‑time of the shift according to the schedule.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System that originated the time entry record.']
  time_entry_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the time entry.']
  time_entry_type varchar [note: 'Method by which the time entry was recorded.']
  total_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total number of hours (including overtime and breaks) recorded for the entry.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the time entry.']
  work_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the shift occurred.']

  Note: 'Captures actual clock-in and clock-out events for each employee per shift, sourced from Workday HCM time tracking or POS-integrated time clocks. Records regular hours, overtime hours, break time, missed punch flags, and manager approval status. Foundation for payroll processing, Labor% calculation, and compliance with FLSA/OSHA labor regulations.'
}

Table workforce.labor_forecast {
  labor_forecast_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the labor forecast record. _canonical_skip_reason: Entity does not fit standard role categories; treated as OTHER.']
  restaurant_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant location for which the labor forecast is generated.']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant location for which the labor forecast is generated.']
  confidence_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Statistical confidence level (0‑100) of the forecast output.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary values.']
  daypart varchar [note: 'Time segment of the day for which labor is forecasted.']
  expected_adt varchar [note: 'Projected average daily transaction count for the forecast period.']
  expected_atc varchar [note: 'Projected average number of items per transaction for the forecast period.']
  forecast_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the forecast record was initially created.']
  forecast_date date [note: 'The calendar date for which the labor forecast applies.']
  forecast_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the forecast record.']
  labor_cost_estimate decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated total labor cost (in currency) for the forecast period.']
  labor_forecast_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the forecast record.']
  lto_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a Limited Time Offer (LTO) is scheduled for the forecast period.']
  model_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the forecasting model used to generate the forecast.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations about the forecast.']
  projected_cover_count varchar [note: 'Estimated number of guests (covers) expected for the forecast period.']
  projected_fte_boh decimal(10,2) [note: 'Forecasted full‑time equivalent staff needed for back‑of‑house operations.']
  projected_fte_foh decimal(10,2) [note: 'Forecasted full‑time equivalent staff needed for front‑of‑house operations.']
  projected_labor_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Projected labor cost as a percentage of total sales for the forecast period.']
  projected_throughput varchar [note: 'Estimated number of transactions/orders expected during the forecast period.']
  promotion_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a planned promotion or marketing campaign is active during the forecast period.']
  scenario varchar [note: 'Scenario used for the forecast (e.g., baseline, optimistic, pessimistic).']
  week_number varchar [note: 'ISO week number of the forecast date.']
  year varchar [note: 'Four‑digit year of the forecast date.']

  Note: 'Projected labor demand by restaurant, daypart, and week based on historical transaction volume (ADT/ATC), seasonal patterns, and planned promotions or LTOs. Captures forecasted cover count, expected throughput, recommended FTE count by BOH/FOH, projected Labor%, and the forecasting model version used. Drives Planday schedule generation and labor budget alignment.'
}

Table workforce.payroll_record {
  payroll_record_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the payroll record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Financial cost‑center to which payroll costs are charged.']
  department_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal department (e.g., FOH, BOH) for cost allocation.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the employee to whom the payroll pertains.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant/location where the employee works.']
  payroll_run_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the payroll run batch that generated this record.']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant/location where the employee works.']
  benefit_deduction decimal(12,2) [note: 'Sum of employee benefit contributions deducted.']
  bonus_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary value of any bonus paid.']
  commission_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary value of commission earned.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payroll record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for monetary amounts.']
  employee_address varchar [note: 'Mailing address of the employee.']
  employee_type varchar [note: 'Classification of employment relationship.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Period number within the fiscal year (e.g., Q1, Q2).']
  gross_pay decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total earnings before any deductions.']
  is_bonus boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the payroll includes a bonus component.']
  job_title varchar [note: 'Official job title or position held by the employee.']
  labor_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Labor cost as a percentage of sales for the period.']
  net_pay decimal(12,2) [note: 'Take‑home earnings after all deductions.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or remarks about the payroll record.']
  other_deductions decimal(12,2) [note: 'Miscellaneous deductions such as garnishments.']
  overtime_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Total overtime hours worked.']
  overtime_rate decimal(8,2) [note: 'Hourly rate applied to overtime hours.']
  pay_date date [note: 'Date on which the net amount is disbursed to the employee.']
  pay_group varchar [note: 'Grouping used for payroll processing (e.g., weekly, bi‑weekly).']
  pay_period_end date [note: 'Last calendar day of the payroll period.']
  pay_period_start date [note: 'First calendar day of the payroll period.']
  pay_rate decimal(8,2) [note: 'Standard hourly or salary rate for the employee.']
  payroll_date timestamp [note: 'Exact time the payroll was run in the source system.']
  payroll_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable payroll reference number assigned by the payroll system.']
  payroll_record_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the payroll record.']
  payroll_type varchar [note: 'Nature of compensation for the period.']
  regular_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Total non‑overtime hours worked.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system for the payroll data (e.g., Workday HCM).']
  tax_withheld decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total tax amount deducted from gross pay.']
  tax_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year applicable for tax reporting.']
  tip_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Tips declared by the employee for the period.']
  union_member_flag boolean [note: 'True if the employee is a union member.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the payroll record.']

  Note: 'Period-level payroll summary for each employee, capturing gross pay, net pay, regular hours paid, overtime hours paid, tips declared, deductions (benefits, taxes, garnishments), pay period dates, and payroll run status. Sourced from Workday HCM payroll module. Serves as the authoritative payroll transaction record for finance integration and Labor% reporting.'
}

Table workforce.recruitment {
  recruitment_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the recruitment record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manager responsible for the hire.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant or site where the role is located.']
  recruitment_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manager responsible for the hire.']
  recruitment_hiring_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'FK to workforce.employee']
  recruitment_location_unit_id varchar [note: 'FK to restaurant.unit']
  recruitment_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant or site where the role is located.']
  applicant_count varchar [note: 'Total number of applicants received.']
  budget_usd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Allocated budget for recruitment advertising and agency fees.']
  closing_date date [note: 'Date the requisition was closed to new applications.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the recruitment process meets internal and regulatory compliance (e.g., equal‑opportunity reporting).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the recruitment record was first created.']
  department varchar [note: 'Business department or functional area the position belongs to.']
  employment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of employment relationship for the role.']
  interview_stage_count varchar [note: 'Number of interview stages defined for the requisition.']
  job_level varchar [note: 'Organizational level of the position.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the recruitment record.']
  location_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the restaurant or site.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes captured by recruiters or hiring managers.']
  offers_accepted varchar [note: 'Count of offers that were accepted by candidates.']
  offers_extended varchar [note: 'Count of job offers that have been extended.']
  position_title varchar [note: 'Official title of the position being recruited for.']
  posting_channel varchar [note: 'Channel used to publish the job opening.']
  posting_date date [note: 'Date the job was first posted.']
  recruitment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the recruitment process.']
  required_certifications varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of certifications (e.g., ServSafe) required for the role.']
  required_education_level varchar [note: 'Minimum education level required for the position.']
  required_experience_years varchar [note: 'Minimum years of relevant experience required.']
  requisition_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the job requisition.']
  salary_range_max decimal(10,2) [note: 'Upper bound of the advertised salary range (USD).']
  salary_range_min decimal(10,2) [note: 'Lower bound of the advertised salary range (USD).']
  source_of_candidate varchar [note: 'Origin of the candidate pool for this requisition.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Source system that supplied the recruitment data (e.g., Workday HCM).']
  target_headcount varchar [note: 'Number of hires planned for this requisition.']
  time_to_fill_days varchar [note: 'Number of calendar days from posting to hire acceptance.']

  Note: 'Tracks the full recruiting lifecycle for open positions across restaurant locations, including job requisition details, posting channels, candidate information (demographics, source, experience, availability), interview stages, offer status, and time-to-fill metrics. Captures both the requisition workflow and candidate pipeline in a single record per applicant-requisition combination. Sourced from Workday HCM Recruiting module. Supports NRO staffing ramp-up planning, turnover backfill management, diversity hiring tracking, and talent pipeline analytics.'
}

Table workforce.onboarding {
  onboarding_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the onboarding process instance.']
  department_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the department (e.g., FOH, BOH, HR) the employee belongs to.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier of the colleague assigned to mentor the new hire.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant location where the employee will work.']
  manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manager who signs off on onboarding completion.']
  onboarding_assigned_buddy_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee identifier of the colleague assigned to mentor the new hire.']
  onboarding_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the newly hired employee undergoing onboarding.']
  onboarding_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manager who signs off on onboarding completion.']
  position_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific job role (e.g., Shift Lead, Server, Cook).']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant location where the employee will work.']
  completion_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated percentage of completed onboarding tasks (0.00 – 100.00).']
  end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the onboarding process was completed or terminated.']
  food_handler_card_date timestamp [note: 'Date and time the food handler card was received.']
  food_handler_card_submitted boolean [note: 'True when the employee has submitted a valid food handler certification.']
  i9_completed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employee\'s I-9 employment eligibility form has been completed.']
  i9_completion_date timestamp [note: 'Date and time when I-9 verification was finalized.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional comments or observations related to the onboarding process.']
  onboarding_method varchar [note: 'Mode used to deliver onboarding activities.']
  onboarding_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the onboarding instance.']
  onboarding_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the onboarding scenario.']
  pos_access_provision_date timestamp [note: 'Date and time POS access was granted.']
  pos_access_provisioned boolean [note: 'True when the employee has been granted access to the POS system.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the onboarding record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the onboarding record.']
  servsafe_completion_date timestamp [note: 'Date and time the ServSafe training was completed.']
  servsafe_enrolled boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employee has enrolled in ServSafe food safety training.']
  start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the onboarding process officially began.']
  training_completed boolean [note: 'True when all required onboarding training modules have been completed.']
  training_completion_date timestamp [note: 'Date and time the final onboarding training was completed.']
  uniform_issuance_date timestamp [note: 'Date and time the uniform was issued to the employee.']
  uniform_issued boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employee has been provided with required uniform.']

  Note: 'Tracks the onboarding process for newly hired employees, including completion status of required tasks (I-9 verification, food handler card submission, uniform issuance, POS system access provisioning, ServSafe enrollment), onboarding start/end dates, assigned onboarding buddy, and manager sign-off. Ensures compliance with FDA food safety and OSHA workplace safety requirements at point of hire.'
}

Table workforce.certification {
  certification_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the certification record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who holds this certification.']
  certification_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or code assigned to the certification by the issuing body.']
  certification_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the certification (e.g., ServSafe Food Handler).']
  certification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the certification.']
  certification_type varchar [note: 'Category of certification required for foodservice operations.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Regulatory compliance assessment of the certification.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the certification record was first created in the system.']
  document_url varchar [note: 'Link to the scanned certification document stored in the document repository.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date the certification expires and must be renewed.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates if the certification is required for the employee\'s role.']
  issue_date date [note: 'Date the certification was originally issued.']
  issuing_body varchar [note: 'Organization that issued the certification.']
  last_verified_date date [note: 'Date when the certification was last validated by internal audit.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes regarding the certification (e.g., special conditions, comments).']
  related_role varchar [note: 'Operational role(s) for which the certification is applicable.']
  renewal_notice_date date [note: 'Date a renewal reminder is sent to the employee.']
  renewal_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the certification must be renewed before expiration.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the certification record.']

  Note: 'Single source of truth for all employee learning credentials, training completions, and regulatory certifications required for foodservice operations. Covers external certifications (ServSafe Food Handler, ServSafe Manager, allergen awareness, HACCP, alcohol service permits, OSHA safety) and internal training completions (new hire orientation, food safety modules, POS/KDS operation, BOH/FOH SOPs, LTO product training, management development). Captures credential type, issuing body (NRA ServSafe, local health department, internal L&D), delivery method (in-person, e-learning, OJT), issue/completion date, expiration date, assessment score, trainer/facilitator, and compliance status. Critical for food safety regulatory compliance, health department inspections, scheduling eligibility validation, and employee development tracking.'
}

Table workforce.performance_review {
  performance_review_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the performance review record.']

  reviewer_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manager or peer who performed the review.']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant location where the employee works.']
  accuracy_score decimal(3,2) [note: 'Score reflecting the employee\'s order accuracy.']
  acknowledgment_status boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employee has acknowledged the review.']
  actual_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total hours actually worked by the employee during the review period.']
  attendance_score decimal(3,2) [note: 'Score reflecting attendance and punctuality.']
  certification_status varchar [note: 'Current status of any required certifications (e.g., ServSafe).']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑form text comments from the reviewer.']
  competency_score_total decimal(5,2) [note: 'Sum of individual competency scores.']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Data classification of the review record.']
  corrective_action_flag boolean [note: 'True if corrective action is required based on the review.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the review record was first created in the system.']
  department varchar [note: 'Organizational department of the employee.']
  development_goals varchar [note: 'Narrative of agreed‑upon development objectives.']
  food_safety_score decimal(3,2) [note: 'Score reflecting adherence to food safety standards.']
  guest_service_score decimal(3,2) [note: 'Score reflecting the employee\'s guest service quality.']
  labor_percentage_actual decimal(5,2) [note: 'Actual labor cost as a percentage of sales for the review period.']
  labor_percentage_target decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target labor cost as a percentage of sales for the review period.']
  next_review_due_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next performance review.']
  overall_rating decimal(3,2) [note: 'Aggregated rating summarizing the employee\'s performance.']
  overtime_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of overtime hours logged in the review period.']
  performance_review_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the review.']
  promotion_recommendation boolean [note: 'True if the reviewer recommends promotion.']
  rating_scale varchar [note: 'Scale used for the overall and competency scores.']
  review_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the review was conducted or recorded.']
  review_document_url varchar [note: 'Link to the stored review document or PDF.']
  review_method varchar [note: 'How the review was conducted.']
  review_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable business identifier for the review, e.g., PR‑2023‑0001.']
  review_period_end date [note: 'Last day of the performance period covered by the review.']
  review_period_start date [note: 'First day of the performance period covered by the review.']
  review_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the review based on its trigger or schedule.']
  scheduled_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total hours scheduled for the employee during the review period.']
  shift_daypart varchar [note: 'Typical daypart of the employee\'s shift.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of record that originated the review data.']
  speed_score decimal(3,2) [note: 'Score reflecting the employee\'s speed of service.']
  training_completed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether required training was completed.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the review record.']

  Note: 'Captures all formal employee performance and conduct events including scheduled evaluations (90-day, annual), corrective actions (verbal warnings, written warnings, PIPs, suspensions), and promotion assessments. Records event type, overall rating, competency scores (speed, accuracy, guest service, food safety adherence), infraction details where applicable, reviewer identity, review period, development goals, progressive discipline step, and acknowledgment status. Sourced from Workday HCM Performance module. Serves as the single record of all formal employee assessments for both development and legal compliance.'
}

Table workforce.leave_request {
  leave_request_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the leave request record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manager who reviewed and approved/denied the request (source: Workday HCM).']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant/location where the employee is scheduled, used for shift‑coverage planning.']
  primary_leave_employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the employee submitting the leave request (source: Workday HCM).']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant/location where the employee is scheduled, used for shift‑coverage planning.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the leave request was approved or denied.']
  attachment_present boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the employee attached supporting documentation (e.g., medical certificate).']
  backfill_assigned_flag boolean [note: 'True when a replacement shift has been scheduled to cover the employee\'s absence.']
  coverage_needed_flag boolean [note: 'True if the requested leave creates a staffing gap that requires backfill.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the leave request record was first created in the system.']
  end_date date [note: 'Last calendar date of the requested leave period.']
  is_paid_leave boolean [note: 'True if the leave type is paid and will be reflected in payroll; false for unpaid leave.']
  leave_balance_after decimal(5,2) [note: 'Employee\'s accrued leave balance after the approved leave is deducted.']
  leave_balance_before decimal(5,2) [note: 'Employee\'s accrued leave balance before this request is applied.']
  leave_days_approved decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of leave days actually approved for the employee.']
  leave_days_requested decimal(5,2) [note: 'Total number of leave days (including fractions) the employee is asking for.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for the employee or manager to provide context or special instructions.']
  payroll_impact_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the approved leave should affect the employee\'s payroll calculations.']
  request_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable reference number assigned to the leave request for tracking and communication.']
  request_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the leave request.']
  request_submitted_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the employee submitted the leave request.']
  request_type varchar [note: 'Category of leave being requested, such as vacation, sick, FMLA, personal, or unpaid.']
  return_to_work_date date [note: 'Date the employee is scheduled to resume duties after the leave period.']
  start_date date [note: 'First calendar date of the requested leave period.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the leave request record.']

  Note: 'Tracks employee requests for time off including vacation, sick leave, FMLA, personal days, and unpaid leave. Captures request type, requested dates, approved dates, leave balance consumed, approval status, approving manager, and return-to-work date. Integrates with Planday scheduling to flag coverage gaps and trigger backfill shift assignments.'
}

Table workforce.labor_violation {
  labor_violation_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for each labor violation record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the employee involved in the violation.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific site (e.g., store, kitchen) within the restaurant.']
  restaurant_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant location where the violation occurred.']
  shift_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the work shift during which the violation occurred.']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant location where the violation occurred.']
  actual_break_time timestamp [note: 'The break time actually taken by the employee.']
  compliance_reported boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the violation has been reported to the required compliance authority.']
  corrective_action_due_date date [note: 'Target date by which the corrective action must be completed.']
  corrective_action_taken varchar [note: 'Description of the remediation steps performed to address the violation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the violation record was first created in the system.']
  daypart varchar [note: 'Part of the day (e.g., breakfast, lunch) when the violation happened.']
  detection_method varchar [note: 'Whether the violation was captured automatically or through manual review.']
  detection_source varchar [note: 'System or process that first identified the violation.']
  fine_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Fine levied by a regulatory body for the violation.']
  labor_violation_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text narrative describing the circumstances of the violation.']
  labor_violation_status varchar [note: 'Lifecycle status of the violation record.']
  overtime_hours decimal(5,2) [note: 'Number of overtime hours recorded beyond allowed limits.']
  penalty_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary penalty assessed for the violation, if any.']
  regulatory_body varchar [note: 'Government agency or authority to which the violation must be reported.']
  reported_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the person or system that logged the violation.']
  resolved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the violation was formally closed or resolved.']
  scheduled_break_time timestamp [note: 'The break time that was scheduled for the employee.']
  severity varchar [note: 'Severity rating assigned to the violation based on impact and risk.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that recorded the violation (e.g., Workday, Planday, Zenput).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the violation record.']
  violation_code varchar [note: 'Code that maps the violation to a regulatory or internal taxonomy.']
  violation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the labor violation was detected or occurred.']
  violation_type varchar [note: 'Categorized type of the violation (e.g., missed break, overtime, under‑age work).']

  Note: 'Records instances of labor compliance violations detected during operations, including missed meal/rest breaks, overtime threshold breaches, minor labor law violations (under-18 hour restrictions), predictive scheduling violations (where applicable), tip credit violations (failure to meet minimum wage with tips, improper tip pool inclusions), scheduling conflicts with certified food handler requirements, and OSHA-reportable workplace incidents. Captures violation type, severity, regulatory framework violated (FLSA, OSHA, state labor code), restaurant location, employee affected, detection source (automated time system, manager report, employee complaint), remediation deadline, and corrective action taken. Supports OSHA 300 log maintenance, DOL Wage & Hour Division audit responses, and state labor board compliance reporting.'
}

Table workforce.labor_budget {
  labor_budget_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each labor budget record.']
  restaurant_unit_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the restaurant location to which the labor budget applies.']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the restaurant location to which the labor budget applies.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the budget was approved by finance.']
  budget_category varchar [note: 'Classification of the budget purpose (e.g., operational labor, training, benefits).']
  budget_code varchar [note: 'External business code used to reference the budget in financial systems.']
  budget_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name for the labor budget (e.g., "2024 Q1 Labor Budget").']
  budget_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the budget figures (historical data, forecasting model, or manager input).']
  budget_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the budget record.']
  budget_type varchar [note: 'Granularity of the budget (annual, quarterly, monthly, weekly, or daypart specific).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the budget record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for monetary amounts.']
  daypart varchar [note: 'Specific daypart the budget applies to; use "all_day" for full‑day budgets.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Period within the fiscal year (e.g., "Q1", "M03").']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Four‑digit calendar year for which the budget is defined.']
  fte_budget_boh decimal(6,2) [note: 'Planned BOH full‑time equivalent headcount.']
  fte_budget_foh decimal(6,2) [note: 'Planned FOH full‑time equivalent headcount.']
  fte_budget_total decimal(6,2) [note: 'Planned full‑time equivalent headcount for the period (combined BOH and FOH).']
  hours_budget_boh decimal(8,2) [note: 'Planned BOH labor hours.']
  hours_budget_foh decimal(8,2) [note: 'Planned FOH labor hours.']
  hours_budget_total decimal(8,2) [note: 'Planned total labor hours for the period.']
  labor_cost_estimate decimal(12,2) [note: 'Projected labor cost derived from forecasting models, may differ from the budgeted amount.']
  labor_cost_estimate_boh decimal(12,2) [note: 'Projected BOH labor spend derived from forecasting.']
  labor_cost_estimate_foh decimal(12,2) [note: 'Projected FOH labor spend derived from forecasting.']
  labor_dollar_budget decimal(12,2) [note: 'Planned total labor spend in local currency for the period.']
  labor_percent_target decimal(5,2) [note: 'Planned labor cost as a percentage of total sales for the period.']
  labor_percent_target_boh decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target labor cost percentage for BOH operations.']
  labor_percent_target_foh decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target labor cost percentage for FOH operations.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or rationale for the budget.']
  period_end_date date [note: 'Last calendar date of the budgeting period.']
  period_start_date date [note: 'First calendar date of the budgeting period.']
  scenario varchar [note: 'Forecasting scenario used to generate the budget.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the budget record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Version number of the budget record for change tracking.']

  Note: 'Period-level labor budget targets by restaurant location, capturing budgeted Labor%, budgeted total labor dollars, budgeted FTE count by BOH/FOH, budgeted hours by daypart, budgeted SPLH (Sales Per Labor Hour), and the fiscal period. Serves as the financial target against which actual labor spend (from payroll_record and time_entry) and forecasted demand (from labor_forecast) are measured. Supports P&L management, AUV optimization, and manager accountability for labor cost control.'
}

Table workforce.tip_compliance {
  tip_compliance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the tip_compliance data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']
  corrected_tip_compliance_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on tip_compliance (corrected_tip_compliance_id)']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Tip compliance records are specific to an employee; adding employee_id FK enables joining to employee for employee details and eliminates any redundant employee attributes.']

  Note: 'Tracks tip pooling arrangements, tip credit elections, tip sharing ratios, and employee-level tip declarations required for DOL FLSA §3(m) compliance. Captures tip pool participant roster, contribution percentages, distribution method (hours-based, points-based), tip credit amount claimed per pay period, employee tip declaration forms, and audit trail for tip pool changes. Supports compliance with federal and state tip credit regulations, tip pooling legality verification (front-of-house vs back-of-house eligibility per 2021 DOL final rule), and provides evidence for DOL Wage & Hour Division audits.'
}

Table workforce.scheduling_template {
  scheduling_template_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for scheduling_template']
  base_scheduling_template_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on scheduling_template (base_scheduling_template_id)']
  daypart varchar [note: 'Primary daypart(s) the template applies to.']
  default_shift_length_minutes varchar [note: 'Standard length of a shift defined by the template, expressed in minutes.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed free‑text description of the scheduling template\'s purpose and usage.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the template becomes effective for scheduling.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the template expires or is superseded (null if open‑ended).']
  fte_target decimal(5,2) [note: 'Planned number of full‑time equivalents required when using this template.']
  is_default_template boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this template is the default choice for new locations or schedules.']
  labor_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target labor percentage of total operating hours for the template.']
  location_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic or organizational scope where the template can be applied.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional remarks or operational notes related to the template.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the scheduling template record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the scheduling template record.']
  role_applicability varchar [note: 'Workforce role(s) the template is intended for (Front‑of‑House, Back‑of‑House, or both).']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the template.']
  template_code varchar [note: 'Business code used to reference the scheduling template across systems.']
  template_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the scheduling template.']
  template_type varchar [note: 'Category of the template indicating its purpose or usage scenario.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version of the template to support change management and audit.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for scheduling_template. Referenced by labor_scheduling_template_id.'
}

  department_name varchar [note: 'Human readable name of the department.']
  department_type varchar [note: 'Category of department indicating its functional area (e.g., Front of House, Back of House).']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the department\'s purpose and responsibilities.']

  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the department ceased operation (null if still active).']

  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the department.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the department record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for department. Referenced by department_id.'
}

Table workforce.payroll_run {

  unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant location for which the payroll run was processed.']

  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Business cost center code linked to the payroll run.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for payroll amounts.']
  deductions_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total amount of taxes, benefits, and other deductions for the payroll run.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal period (e.g., Q1, Q2) for the payroll run.']
  gross_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total gross payroll amount before any deductions.']
  is_finalized boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the payroll run has been finalized and posted to the payroll ledger.']
  net_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Net payroll amount after deductions.']
  pay_cycle varchar [note: 'Frequency at which employees are paid for this run.']
  pay_period_end_date date [note: 'Last calendar date of the pay period covered by the payroll run.']
  pay_period_start_date date [note: 'First calendar date of the pay period covered by the payroll run.']
  payroll_run_description varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or description about the payroll run.']
  payroll_type varchar [note: 'Category of payroll processing for the run.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payroll run record was first created in the data lake.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the payroll run record.']
  run_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned by the payroll system.']
  run_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the payroll run was executed.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Source system that generated the payroll run data.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the payroll run.']
  tax_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal tax year applicable to the payroll run.']
  total_employee_count varchar [note: 'Number of employees included in this payroll run.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for payroll_run. Referenced by payroll_run_id.'
}
