  Note: 'Individual violation cited during an official health inspection, including violation code, description, severity classification (critical, non-critical), regulatory citation (FDA Food Code section, local ordinance), corrective action required, compliance deadline, and re-inspection outcome. Supports regulatory compliance tracking and trend analysis.'
}

Table foodsafety.foodsafety_corrective_action {
  foodsafety_corrective_action_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the corrective action record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Financial tracking of corrective action spend: each action is budgeted to a cost center for expense control.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manager accountable for executing the corrective action.']
  primary_foodsafety_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manager accountable for executing the corrective action.']
  related_audit_food_safety_audit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the food safety audit that generated the finding.']
  related_food_safety_audit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the food safety audit that generated the finding.']
  related_health_inspection_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the health inspection that uncovered the violation.']
  related_inspection_health_inspection_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the health inspection that uncovered the violation.']
  verified_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the individual who verified the corrective action.']
  action_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or code assigned to the corrective action for tracking and reporting.']
  action_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary cost incurred to implement the corrective action.']
  action_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the action as corrective, preventive, or both.']
  actual_completion_date date [note: 'Date when the corrective action was actually completed.']
  attachment_url varchar [note: 'Link to supporting documents or images attached to the corrective action.']
  ccp_deviation boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the corrective action is related to a Critical Control Point deviation.']
  closure_status varchar [note: 'Final status indicating whether the corrective action has been closed, verified, or rejected.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the corrective action record was first captured in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the action cost.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the corrective action became effective.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the corrective action was created or initiated.']
  foodsafety_corrective_action_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the corrective or preventive action to be taken.']
  foodsafety_corrective_action_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the corrective action.']
  is_effective boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the corrective action has been determined effective after verification.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information or comments.']
  party_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary party (e.g., restaurant unit or franchise) responsible for the corrective action.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Priority assigned to the corrective action for scheduling and resource allocation.']
  root_cause varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the underlying cause that triggered the corrective action.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Severity rating of the issue that prompted the corrective action.']
  target_completion_date date [note: 'Planned date by which the corrective action should be completed.']
  temperature_exceedance boolean [note: 'True if the action addresses a temperature limit breach.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the corrective action record.']
  verification_date date [note: 'Date on which the verification of the corrective action was performed.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'Method used to verify that the corrective action was effective.']

  Note: 'Tracks corrective and preventive actions (CAPA) initiated in response to food safety audit findings, health inspection violations, CCP deviations, temperature exceedances, pest control findings, or allergen incidents. Captures root cause analysis method (5-Why, fishbone), corrective action description, responsible manager, target and actual completion dates, verification method, effectiveness check outcome, and closure status. Serves as the central CAPA registry managed via Zenput task management, supporting FDA Food Code and ISO 22000 corrective action requirements.'
}

Table foodsafety.temperature_log {
  temperature_log_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each temperature reading record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who manually entered the reading, if applicable.']
  equipment_asset_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the equipment or sensor that generated the temperature reading.']
  equipment_equipment_asset_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the equipment or sensor that generated the temperature reading.']
  recorded_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who manually entered the reading, if applicable.']
  shift_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the workforce shift during which the reading was taken.']
  stock_location_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant location or specific area (e.g., kitchen, dock) where the reading occurred.']
  audit_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier linking the reading to a specific food safety audit or inspection.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Optional batch identifier grouping a set of readings for a specific audit period.']
  calibration_date date [note: 'Date when the sensor was last calibrated.']
  calibration_due_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next sensor calibration.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Result of compliance check for this reading against HACCP requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the temperature log record was initially created in the system.']
  critical_limit_high decimal(6,2) [note: 'Upper temperature threshold defined by HACCP for the monitoring point.']
  critical_limit_low decimal(6,2) [note: 'Lower temperature threshold defined by HACCP for the monitoring point.']
  data_quality_flag varchar [note: 'Indicator of the data quality assessment for the reading.']
  deviation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the temperature reading falls outside the defined critical limits.']
  maintenance_due_date date [note: 'Planned date for required maintenance activities.']
  maintenance_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the equipment requires maintenance based on the reading or schedule.']
  monitoring_method varchar [note: 'Method used to capture the temperature (manual probe or automated sensor).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑text field for additional observations or comments about the reading.']
  reading_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the temperature measurement was taken at the monitoring point.']
  reading_type varchar [note: 'Category of the monitoring point where the temperature was recorded.']
  sensor_serial_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturer serial number of the temperature sensor device.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of origin for the temperature data.']
  temperature_log_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the temperature log record.']
  temperature_trend varchar [note: 'Observed trend of temperature change relative to previous readings.']
  temperature_value decimal(6,2) [note: 'Measured temperature value captured by the sensor or manual probe.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the temperature reading (Fahrenheit or Celsius).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the temperature log record.']

  Note: 'Time-series log of temperature readings captured at critical monitoring points (walk-in coolers, freezers, hot-holding units, cooking equipment, receiving docks), including equipment ID, reading timestamp, measured temperature, unit of measure (°F/°C), critical limit thresholds, deviation flag, and monitoring method (manual probe, automated sensor). Core HACCP monitoring record per Principle 4.'
}

Table foodsafety.sanitation_schedule {
  sanitation_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the sanitation schedule.']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: Sanitation schedule defines cleaning chemicals; linking to the chemical supplier enables compliance tracking, cost allocation, and audit of purchased sanitation supplies required by health regulations']
  responsible_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Required: Sanitation schedule assigns a specific employee; FK provides traceability for health inspections and internal audits.']
  stock_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_location. Business justification: Sanitation schedules are defined per storage area; the FK associates each schedule with its location.']
  allergen_control_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the task includes allergen control measures.']
  area varchar [note: 'Physical area or zone where the task is performed (e.g., Front‑of‑House, Back‑of‑House).']
  audit_required_flag boolean [note: 'Whether a post‑task audit is required.']
  chemical_concentration varchar [note: 'Required concentration of the chemical (e.g., "200 ppm").']
  chemical_name varchar [note: 'Name of the chemical or sanitizer used.']
  cleaning_method varchar [note: 'Method used to perform the cleaning (e.g., manual, automated, steam).']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of the task against food‑safety regulations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the schedule record was created.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the schedule becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the schedule expires or is superseded (nullable).']
  equipment varchar [note: 'Equipment, fixture, or asset the task applies to.']
  frequency varchar [note: 'Number of times the task occurs within the defined frequency unit.']
  frequency_unit varchar [note: 'Time unit for the task frequency.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates if the task is mandatory for compliance.']
  last_performed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent execution of the task.']
  next_due_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Scheduled timestamp for the next required execution of the task.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information or comments.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Priority assigned to the task for scheduling and resource allocation.']
  responsible_role varchar [note: 'Job role responsible for executing the task (e.g., Shift Lead, Kitchen Manager).']
  sanitation_schedule_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the schedule purpose and scope.']
  sanitation_schedule_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the schedule.']
  schedule_code varchar [note: 'Business code used to reference the schedule.']
  schedule_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the schedule.']
  sop_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier of the Standard Operating Procedure that governs the task.']
  task_name varchar [note: 'Name of the specific cleaning or sanitizing task.']
  temperature_requirement_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Required temperature condition for the cleaning task, expressed in Celsius.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the schedule record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version number of the schedule for change management.']
  waste_disposal_method varchar [note: 'Method used to dispose of waste generated by the task (e.g., biohazard, recyclable).']

  Note: 'Master sanitation schedule (MSS) with execution log for each restaurant unit, including schedule template (task name, target area: FOH/BOH zone/equipment, frequency: hourly/daily/weekly, chemical/sanitizer, concentration requirements, responsible role, SOP reference) and task execution records (completion timestamp, employee, actual concentration measured, pass/fail, deviation notes). Managed via Zenput task management. Serves as both the authoritative sanitation template and the compliance evidence of task completion.'
}

Table foodsafety.sanitation_task_log {
  sanitation_task_log_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each sanitation task log entry.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the employee who executed the sanitation task.']
  equipment_asset_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the equipment item that was sanitized.']
  equipment_equipment_asset_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the equipment item that was sanitized.']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: Each sanitation task uses chemicals purchased from a vendor; the supplier FK allows traceability of chemical usage per task for regulatory reporting and supplier performance monitoring.']
  restaurant_unit_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the restaurant unit where the sanitation activity took place.']
  shift_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the employee shift associated with the task.']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to realestate.site. Business justification: Sanitation tasks are performed at a specific site; linking enables site‑level sanitation compliance reporting and audit.']
  stock_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_location. Business justification: Sanitation tasks are scheduled per storage area; the FK ties task logs to the exact location for compliance tracking.']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the restaurant unit where the sanitation activity took place.']
  audit_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time the sanitation task log entry was first inserted into the data lake.']
  audit_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the latest modification to the sanitation task log entry.']
  chemical_concentration decimal(10,4) [note: 'Numeric value of the chemical concentration measured during the task.']
  chemical_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the chemical or sanitizer applied during the task.']
  completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time the sanitation task was marked as completed.']
  compliance_regulation varchar [note: 'Regulation or standard that the sanitation task satisfies.']
  concentration_unit varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for the chemical concentration (e.g., parts per million).']
  corrective_action varchar [note: 'Description of any corrective measures applied after a failed sanitation check.']
  deviation_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of any deviations, issues, or observations noted during the task.']
  humidity_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Relative humidity percentage measured in the task area at the time of execution.']
  is_critical boolean [note: 'True if the sanitation task is deemed critical for food safety compliance.']
  location_area varchar [note: 'Specific area (e.g., Front‑of‑House, Back‑of‑House) where sanitation was executed.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Any supplemental information not captured in other fields.']
  pass_fail_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the sanitation task met compliance criteria.']
  photo_url varchar [note: 'Web address of a photo taken as evidence of task completion.']
  scheduled_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time the sanitation task was scheduled to occur.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of record that created the sanitation task log.']
  task_duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Total time taken to complete the sanitation task, measured in seconds.']
  task_status varchar [note: 'Operational status of the sanitation task record.']
  task_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time the sanitation task was completed.']
  task_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the sanitation task (e.g., surface cleaning, equipment sanitization).']
  temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Temperature in degrees Celsius measured in the task area at the time of execution.']
  verification_method varchar [note: 'Technique employed to confirm task compliance.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of each completed or missed sanitation task execution at a restaurant unit, capturing task completion timestamp, employee who performed the task, actual chemical concentration measured, pass/fail status, and any deviation notes. Linked to the master sanitation schedule and managed via Zenput task management.'
}

Table foodsafety.foodsafety_allergen_profile {
  foodsafety_allergen_profile_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the allergen profile record.']
  allergen_version varchar [note: 'Incremental version number for change tracking.']
  celery varchar [note: 'Indicates if celery is absent, present, or may‑contain in the item.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the allergen profile record was first created.']
  cross_contact_risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk rating for cross‑contact of allergens during preparation.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the allergen profile is considered valid.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date after which the allergen profile is no longer valid (nullable for open‑ended).']
  egg varchar [note: 'Indicates if egg is absent, present, or may‑contain in the item.']
  fish varchar [note: 'Indicates if fish is absent, present, or may‑contain in the item.']
  foodsafety_allergen_profile_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the allergen profile.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date the allergen profile was last reviewed for accuracy.']
  lupin varchar [note: 'Indicates if lupin is absent, present, or may‑contain in the item.']
  management_controls varchar [note: 'Description of controls (e.g., segregation, cleaning procedures) used to mitigate allergen cross‑contact.']
  milk varchar [note: 'Indicates if milk is absent, present, or may‑contain in the item.']
  mollusk varchar [note: 'Indicates if mollusk is absent, present, or may‑contain in the item.']
  mustard varchar [note: 'Indicates if mustard is absent, present, or may‑contain in the item.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations about the allergen profile.']
  peanut varchar [note: 'Indicates if peanuts are absent, present, or may‑contain in the item.']
  profile_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier used to reference the profile in external systems.']
  profile_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name for the allergen profile, typically the menu item or ingredient name.']
  profile_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the profile applies to a menu item or a raw ingredient.']
  review_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the most recent review cycle.']
  review_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the last review was completed.']
  reviewed_by varchar [note: 'Name of the employee or auditor who performed the last review.']
  sesame varchar [note: 'Indicates if sesame is absent, present, or may‑contain in the item.']
  shellfish varchar [note: 'Indicates if shellfish is absent, present, or may‑contain in the item.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of record that supplied the allergen information.']
  soy varchar [note: 'Indicates if soy is absent, present, or may‑contain in the item.']
  sulphites varchar [note: 'Indicates if sulphites are absent, present, or may‑contain in the item.']
  tree_nut varchar [note: 'Indicates if tree nuts are absent, present, or may‑contain in the item.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the record.']
  wheat varchar [note: 'Indicates if wheat is absent, present, or may‑contain in the item.']

  Note: 'Master allergen declaration record for each menu item or ingredient, specifying the presence, may-contain, or absence of each of the 14 major allergens (FDA Big 9 + EU 14), cross-contact risk level, allergen management controls in place, and last review date. Supports FDA FSMA labeling compliance and guest allergen inquiry management.'
}

Table foodsafety.allergen_incident {
  allergen_incident_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the allergen incident record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the staff member who responded to or recorded the incident.']
  guest_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the POS order associated with the incident, if applicable.']
  guest_profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the guest who reported the allergen reaction.']
  ingredient_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.ingredient. Business justification: Needed for root‑cause analysis: trace allergen incidents to the specific ingredient causing the reaction, supporting recall and corrective action reports.']
  lot_tracking_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.lot_tracking. Business justification: When an allergen incident involves a specific lot, linking to lot tracking provides precise traceability for recalls.']
  member_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.member. Business justification: Allergen incident handling awards loyalty points to members who report incidents; direct member link is required for points accrual and incident reporting.']
  menu_item_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the menu item implicated in the incident.']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: Allergen incident response must identify the supplier of the allergen‑containing ingredient to issue recalls and enforce supplier corrective actions per FDA allergen regulations.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the guest who reported the allergen reaction.']
  restaurant_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant location where the incident occurred.']
  shift_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee shift during which the incident happened.']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to realestate.site. Business justification: Allergen incidents must be traced to the exact site for corrective action, liability reporting, and regulatory notification.']
  stock_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_item. Business justification: Allergen incidents are traced to the offending ingredient; the FK connects the incident to the stock item for root‑cause analysis.']
  temperature_log_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the temperature monitoring record associated with the incident, if relevant.']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant location where the incident occurred.']
  allergen_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code for the allergen (e.g., ISO 22000 allergen code).']
  allergen_incident_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the incident.']
  allergen_name varchar [note: 'Name of the allergen involved in the incident (e.g., peanuts, shellfish).']
  complaint_description varchar [note: 'Narrative provided by the guest describing the allergic reaction and circumstances.']
  compliance_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the incident complies with internal SOPs and external regulations.']
  corrective_action varchar [note: 'Planned or executed corrective measures to prevent recurrence.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the incident record was first created in the system.']
  fda_medwatch_filed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the incident was reported to the FDA MedWatch system.']
  guest_contact_info varchar [note: 'Phone number or email address used to reach the guest.']
  guest_contact_method varchar [note: 'Preferred method used to contact the guest for follow‑up.']
  immediate_action_taken varchar [note: 'Actions performed at the time of the incident (e.g., administered epinephrine, called emergency services).']
  incident_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the incident type.']
  incident_location varchar [note: 'Physical location within the restaurant where the incident occurred (e.g., kitchen, dining area).']
  incident_notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes captured by staff during investigation.']
  incident_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing identifier assigned to the incident for tracking and reporting.']
  incident_resolution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the incident was formally closed or resolved.']
  incident_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the allergen incident was observed or reported.']
  investigation_complete boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the root‑cause investigation has been completed.']
  investigation_complete_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the investigation was marked complete.']
  is_repeat_incident boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a similar allergen incident has been recorded previously at the same location.']
  notification_date date [note: 'Date on which the regulatory notification was submitted.']
  regulatory_notification_status varchar [note: 'Status of required notifications to regulatory bodies (e.g., FDA MedWatch).']
  reported_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the staff member who logged the incident.']
  root_cause varchar [note: 'Identified underlying cause of the allergen exposure (e.g., cross‑contamination, mislabeling).']
  severity_score varchar [note: 'Numeric representation of incident severity (e.g., 1‑5 scale) for analytics.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the incident record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a reported allergen-related incident at a restaurant unit, including incident date, guest complaint details, allergen involved, menu item implicated, severity (mild reaction, anaphylaxis, hospitalization), immediate response actions taken, root cause determination, and regulatory notification status (FDA MedWatch if applicable).'
}

Table foodsafety.sop_document {
  sop_document_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each SOP document record.']
  applicable_restaurant_format varchar [note: 'Restaurant format(s) to which the SOP applies (e.g., Quick-Service Restaurant, casual, fine-dining).']
  approval_authority varchar [note: 'Name or role of the individual/committee that approved the SOP.']
  attached_files_count varchar [note: 'Number of supplemental files (e.g., checklists, diagrams) linked to the SOP.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Result of the latest compliance audit against this SOP.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the SOP record was first created in the system.']
  distribution_scope varchar [note: 'Scope of SOP distribution: global, regional, or specific to a site.']
  document_code varchar [note: 'External business code or number used to reference the SOP document in franchise and compliance systems.']
  document_url varchar [note: 'Link to the stored SOP file in the document repository.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the SOP becomes operationally binding.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the SOP is no longer valid; null if indefinite.']
  file_type varchar [note: 'File format of the SOP document.']
  is_mandatory boolean [note: 'Indicates whether compliance with the SOP is mandatory (true) or advisory (false).']
  language varchar [note: 'Language in which the SOP is authored.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent formal review of the SOP.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next scheduled review.']
  owner_name varchar [note: 'Name of the person or role responsible for maintaining the SOP.']
  regulatory_basis varchar [note: 'Regulatory framework or standard that mandates the SOP (e.g., FDA FSMA, OSHA safety).']
  revision_history varchar [note: 'Chronological log of changes made to the SOP, stored as free‑text.']
  sop_category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the SOP content area.']
  sop_document_description varchar [note: 'Full textual description of the SOP purpose, scope, and key steps.']
  sop_document_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the SOP.']
  title varchar [note: 'Descriptive title of the SOP, e.g., "Handwashing Procedure".']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the SOP record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Incremental version of the SOP; higher numbers indicate newer revisions.']

  Note: 'Master record for each Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) governing food safety and operational compliance, including SOP title, version number, effective date, expiration date, applicable restaurant formats (QSR/casual/fine-dining), regulatory basis (FDA, USDA, OSHA), approval authority, and distribution scope. Managed and distributed via Zenput.'
}

Table foodsafety.food_safety_certification {
  food_safety_certification_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for each food safety certification record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who holds the certification.']
  food_safety_audit_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the certification record in the source system.']
  attached_document_path varchar [note: 'Location of the digital copy of the certification document in the document repository.']
  audit_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system of record (e.g., Zenput) that originated the certification data.']
  certification_category varchar [note: 'High‑level grouping used for analytics and compliance reporting.']
  certification_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the certification (e.g., "ServSafe Manager").']
  certification_number varchar [note: 'Unique number or code provided by the issuing organization.']
  certification_type varchar [note: 'Category of the certification according to industry standards.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True when the certification satisfies all applicable FDA, USDA, and ISO 22000 requirements.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the certification is no longer valid.']
  expiration_notice_date date [note: 'Date on which the expiration reminder was sent.']
  expiration_notice_sent boolean [note: 'True when a reminder about upcoming expiration has been communicated to the employee.']
  food_safety_certification_status varchar [note: 'Operational status indicating if the certification is currently valid, pending renewal, revoked, etc.']
  issue_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the certification became effective.']
  issuing_body varchar [note: 'Name of the authority that granted the certification.']
  last_renewal_date date [note: 'Date when the certification was last renewed, if applicable.']
  next_renewal_due date [note: 'Projected date by which the certification must be renewed to remain active.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional remarks, auditor comments, or special conditions related to the certification.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the certification record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the certification record.']
  renewal_required boolean [note: 'True if the certification must be renewed prior to its expiration date.']
  revocation_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation why a certification was revoked or invalidated.']
  validity_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between issue_date and expiration_date.']

  Note: 'Unified food safety workforce compliance record covering both training events and resulting certifications held by restaurant employees. Training attributes: program name, type (onboarding/annual refresher/corrective retraining/allergen awareness), delivery method (in-person/e-learning/Zenput), completion date, assessment score, pass/fail, trainer identity. Certification attributes: type (ServSafe Manager/Food Handler/HACCP/allergen awareness), issuing body (NRA ServSafe, ANSI-accredited provider), certification number, issue date, expiration date, validity status. Supports regulatory staffing requirements, retraining triggers, and compliance dashboards.'
}

Table foodsafety.illness_report {
  illness_report_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the illness report record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who reported or is associated with the illness.']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: Illness investigations must trace the suspected ingredient back to its supplier to issue recalls and corrective actions, supporting FDA/health‑department reporting.']
  restaurant_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant location where the incident occurred.']
  shift_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the work shift during which the employee fell ill.']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant location where the incident occurred.']
  action_plan varchar [note: 'Planned actions to prevent recurrence.']
  action_plan_completed_date date [note: 'Date when the action plan was completed.']
  action_plan_due_date date [note: 'Target date for completing the action plan.']
  compliance_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific regulatory requirement or guidance applicable to the report.']
  corrective_action_taken varchar [note: 'Description of any corrective action performed in response to the incident.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the report record was first created in the system.']
  exclusion_decision boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the employee was excluded from work due to the illness.']
  exclusion_start_date date [note: 'Date when the employee\'s work exclusion began.']
  health_department_notification_date date [note: 'Date when the health department was notified about the incident.']
  health_department_notified boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the local health department was notified.']
  illness_report_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the illness report.']
  investigation_end_date date [note: 'Date when the health investigation concluded.']
  investigation_start_date date [note: 'Date when the health investigation began.']
  investigation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the health investigation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes captured by investigators or managers.']
  onset_date date [note: 'Date when the employee first experienced symptoms.']
  report_method varchar [note: 'Method by which the illness was reported.']
  report_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the illness report.']
  report_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the illness was reported.']
  return_to_work_date date [note: 'Date when the employee was cleared to return to work.']
  root_cause varchar [note: 'Identified root cause of the illness, if determined.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Categorical severity level derived from the severity score.']
  severity_score varchar [note: 'Numeric score representing the severity of the reported illness.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of record that originated the illness report (e.g., Zenput, HR portal).']
  suspected_food_item varchar [note: 'Food item or ingredient suspected to be the source of the illness.']
  suspected_pathogen varchar [note: 'Pathogen suspected to have caused the illness, if known.']
  symptoms varchar [note: 'Symptoms reported by the employee, captured for health analysis.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the report record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of a reported employee foodborne illness or suspected foodborne illness event at a restaurant unit, capturing report date, employee role, symptoms reported, onset date, suspected pathogen or food item, exclusion-from-work decision, return-to-work clearance date, and health department notification status. Supports FDA Food Code employee health policy compliance.'
}

Table foodsafety.food_recall {
  food_recall_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for each recall event.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Recall expense allocation: recall handling costs are charged to a cost center for financial impact analysis.']
  lot_tracking_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.lot_tracking. Business justification: Recalls target specific lots; the FK allows rapid identification of affected inventory records.']
  primary_food_procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the supplier in the enterprise master data.']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the manufacturer in the enterprise master data.']
  affected_units varchar [note: 'Total number of units (e.g., packages) identified as potentially unsafe.']
  corrective_action varchar [note: 'Planned or executed actions to mitigate the hazard (e.g., product pull, consumer notification).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the recall record was first created in the data lake.']
  disposal_method varchar [note: 'Method used to handle recalled product units.']
  distribution_region varchar [note: 'Geographic region(s) where the affected product was distributed (e.g., Midwest, Northeast).']
  hazard_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the health hazard or safety issue prompting the recall.']
  is_voluntary boolean [note: 'True if the recall was initiated voluntarily by the company.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturing lot or batch number associated with the recalled items.']
  manufacturer_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the manufacturer that produced the recalled product.']
  notification_date date [note: 'Date the regulatory agency was formally notified of the recall.']
  product_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the food product or ingredient subject to the recall.']
  public_communication_date date [note: 'Date the recall information was released to the public.']
  recall_class varchar [note: 'FDA classification indicating severity: Class I (most serious), Class II, or Class III.']
  recall_closure_reason varchar [note: 'Reason why the recall was closed or terminated.']
  recall_effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the recall became effective for the public and supply chain.']
  recall_end_date date [note: 'Date when the recall was closed or deemed completed; null if still open.']
  recall_initiation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date‑time when the recall was officially initiated.']
  recall_number varchar [note: 'Publicly assigned recall number used by regulatory agencies to track the event.']
  recall_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic extent of the recall.']
  recall_source_system varchar [note: 'System where the recall record originated.']
  recall_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the recall event.']
  recall_type varchar [note: 'Primary cause category for the recall such as contamination, mislabeling, or allergen issue.']
  regulatory_agency varchar [note: 'Government body overseeing the recall.']
  regulatory_reference_number varchar [note: 'Official reference number assigned by the regulatory agency.']
  root_cause varchar [note: 'Underlying cause identified for the recall (e.g., supplier contamination).']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Categorical severity derived from the severity score.']
  severity_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score (0‑100) representing the health risk severity.']
  sku varchar [note: 'Internal SKU or catalogue code identifying the recalled product.']
  supplier_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the supplier providing the recalled product.']
  units_disposed varchar [note: 'Number of recalled units that were destroyed or otherwise disposed of.']
  units_recalled varchar [note: 'Number of units that were actually recalled from the supply chain.']
  units_returned varchar [note: 'Number of recalled units that were returned to the supplier or manufacturer.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the recall record.']

  Note: 'Header-and-line record managing food product recall or withdrawal events, including recall header (FDA classification: Class I/II/III, affected SKU/ingredient, supplier, lot numbers, initiation date, scope, disposition instructions, regulatory reference) and unit-level response lines (unit ID, quantity on hand, disposition action taken: returned/destroyed/quarantined, completion date, responsible manager, verification status). Ensures end-to-end recall traceability from corporate notification through individual unit compliance.'
}

Table foodsafety.recall_unit_response {
  recall_unit_response_id varchar [pk, note: 'Surrogate primary key for the recall unit response record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Unit‑level recall response costs are tracked against a cost center for accurate cost accounting.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manager responsible for the recall response.']
  food_recall_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the food safety recall event that triggered this response.']
  lot_tracking_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.lot_tracking. Business justification: Unit response records which lot(s) were affected; linking to lot tracking supports detailed recall execution.']
  manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the manager responsible for the recall response.']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to realestate.site. Business justification: Recall responses are managed per restaurant location; site_id ties each response to the physical property for traceability.']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant location/unit responding to the recall.']
  affected_quantity decimal(12,2) [note: 'Quantity of the product on hand that is subject to the recall.']
  batch_number varchar [note: 'Manufacturing batch number associated with the recalled product.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Overall compliance outcome for the recall response.']
  corrective_action_taken varchar [note: 'Description of any corrective actions performed to address the recall.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'When the recall response record was first created.']
  disposition_action varchar [note: 'Action taken with the recalled product.']
  disposition_date date [note: 'Date the disposition action was completed.']
  event_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the recall event as it applies to this unit.']
  evidence_documentation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether supporting documentation (photos, logs) was attached.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the affected product batch, if applicable.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot identifier for the affected product batch.']
  manager_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the responsible manager.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes captured by staff during the recall response.']
  product_sku varchar [note: 'Stock Keeping Unit of the affected product.']
  recall_description varchar [note: 'Brief description of the recall issue and affected product.']
  recall_initiated_by varchar [note: 'Entity that initiated the recall (e.g., supplier, regulator, internal QA).']
  recall_severity varchar [note: 'Severity level assigned to the recall (high, medium, low).']
  recall_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the recall notice (e.g., FDA, USDA, Supplier, Internal).']
  recall_unit_response_status varchar [note: 'Overall lifecycle status of the recall response.']
  regulatory_compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True if the response meets all applicable regulatory requirements.']
  response_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable identifier for the recall response transaction.']
  temperature_log_reference varchar [note: 'Reference ID to temperature monitoring logs related to the recalled product.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the affected quantity.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'When the recall response record was last modified.']
  verification_status varchar [note: 'Current verification state of the recall response.']
  verification_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the response was verified.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing each restaurant unit\'s response to a food recall event, including unit ID, recall ID, quantity of affected product on hand, disposition action taken (returned to supplier, destroyed on-site, quarantined), completion date, responsible manager, and verification status. Ensures traceability and regulatory compliance at the unit level.'
}

Table foodsafety.pest_control_log {
  pest_control_log_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each pest control service record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Pest control service costs are allocated to the responsible cost center for budgeting and reporting.']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the external pest control vendor or contractor.']
  restaurant_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant location where the service was performed.']
  service_provider_procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the external pest control vendor or contractor.']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to realestate.site. Business justification: Pest‑control services are scheduled per site; linking logs to site_id enables site‑level pest‑control compliance monitoring.']
  stock_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_location. Business justification: Pest control services target specific storage locations; linking records pest findings and treatments per location.']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant location where the service was performed.']
  allergen_control_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether allergen control procedures were verified during the service.']
  audit_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the pest control log entry was initially created in the system.']
  audit_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the pest control log entry.']
  chemicals_used varchar [note: 'Names or codes of chemical agents applied during treatment.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the service met all regulatory and internal compliance requirements.']
  corrective_actions varchar [note: 'Specific actions taken to remediate identified pest issues.']
  findings_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative of observations made by the technician.']
  next_service_date date [note: 'Planned date for the subsequent pest control visit.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any extra comments or observations.']
  pests_identified varchar [note: 'List or description of pest species observed during the visit.']
  recommendations varchar [note: 'Suggested follow‑up measures or preventive steps.']
  record_status varchar [note: 'Overall lifecycle status of the log record.']
  regulatory_reference varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific regulation or standard (e.g., FDA FSMA, local health code) applicable to the service.']
  service_order_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the pest control service request.']
  service_provider_name varchar [note: 'Name of the pest control vendor or contractor.']
  service_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the service record.']
  service_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the pest control service was executed.']
  service_type varchar [note: 'Category of pest control activity performed.']
  severity_level varchar [note: 'Qualitative assessment of pest infestation severity.']
  severity_score varchar [note: 'Numeric score (1‑5) representing infestation severity.']
  temperature_log_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier linking to related temperature monitoring records, if applicable.']
  treatment_method varchar [note: 'Technique used to address the pest issue (e.g., baiting, spraying, trapping).']

  Note: 'Transactional record of pest control service visits and findings at a restaurant unit, including service date, pest control provider, service type (routine inspection, treatment, exclusion), pests identified, treatment method and chemicals used, findings severity, corrective recommendations, and next scheduled service date. Supports GMP and local health department compliance.'
}

Table foodsafety.foodsafety_supplier_certification {
  foodsafety_supplier_certification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for supplier_certification']
  supply_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supply_supplier. Business justification: Tracks each supplier\'s food safety certifications, required for supplier qualification and audit readiness.']

  Note: 'Master record of food safety certifications and compliance documents held by food and beverage suppliers, including certification type (SQF, BRC, FSSC 22000, GlobalG.A.P., USDA Organic), issuing body, scope of certification, issue date, expiration date, and current approval status. Supports approved supplier program management and FSMA Preventive Controls compliance.'
}

Table foodsafety.receiving_inspection {
  receiving_inspection_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for the receiving inspection record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Receiving inspection labor/material costs are allocated to a cost center for expense reporting.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who performed the inspection.']
  foodsafety_corrective_action_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the corrective action record linked to this inspection.']
  inspector_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who performed the inspection.']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supplier delivering the product.']
  receiving_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.receiving_order. Business justification: Required for the Receiving Inspection process to tie each inspection to its corresponding receiving order for compliance verification.']
  audit_reference varchar [note: 'Identifier linking this inspection to a broader food safety audit.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Overall compliance determination for the receiving inspection.']
  corrective_action_required boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether a corrective action is needed due to inspection failure.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the product must not be used.']
  inspection_number varchar [note: 'Unique external identifier for the receiving inspection.']
  inspection_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the inspection was performed.']
  inspector_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the inspector.']
  lot_number varchar [note: 'Lot or batch number assigned by the supplier.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑text observations recorded by the inspector.']
  product_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the product received.']
  product_sku varchar [note: 'Stock Keeping Unit of the product received.']
  quantity_received decimal(12,2) [note: 'Amount of product received, expressed in the unit of measure.']
  receiving_date date [note: 'Date the delivery was received at the restaurant dock.']
  receiving_inspection_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the inspection.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the inspection record was first captured in the system.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the inspection record.']
  rejection_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided for rejecting the delivery, if applicable.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of record where the inspection data originated.']
  supplier_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the supplying vendor.']
  temperature_celsius decimal(5,2) [note: 'Temperature reading at the time of receiving, in degrees Celsius.']
  temperature_fahrenheit decimal(5,2) [note: 'Temperature reading at the time of receiving, in degrees Fahrenheit.']
  temperature_pass_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the temperature reading met the acceptable range.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit for the quantity received.']
  visual_quality_pass boolean [note: 'Result of the visual quality check (pass/fail).']

  Note: 'Transactional record of food and supply receiving inspections conducted at a restaurant unit\'s back-of-house (BOH) dock, capturing delivery date, supplier, product/SKU received, lot number, quantity, temperature at receiving, visual quality check result (pass/fail), rejection reason if applicable, and inspector identity. Supports HACCP receiving CCP monitoring and FSMA traceability requirements.'
}

Table foodsafety.food_safety_training {
  food_safety_training_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for each food safety training record.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Training program costs are budgeted to the cost center responsible for food safety compliance.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the trainer or platform that delivered the training.']
  primary_food_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who completed the training.']
  restaurant_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant location where the employee works.']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant location where the employee works.']
  assessment_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score achieved on the training assessment (0‑100).']
  completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the employee completed the training.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Overall compliance outcome of the training record.']
  delivery_method varchar [note: 'Method used to deliver the training.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date by which the employee must retake the training to stay compliant.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations about the training session.']
  pass_fail_status varchar [note: 'Result of the assessment indicating whether the employee passed.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the training record was first created in the data lake.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the training record.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that captured the training record (e.g., Zenput, Workday).']
  training_program_name varchar [note: 'Name of the training program (e.g., ServSafe Certified Food Handler).']
  training_session_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable identifier or code assigned to the training session (e.g., FS‑2023‑001).']
  training_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the training session.']
  training_type varchar [note: 'Category of the training session.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of food safety training sessions completed by restaurant employees, including training program name, training type (onboarding, annual refresher, corrective retraining, allergen awareness), delivery method (in-person, e-learning, Zenput), completion date, assessment score, pass/fail status, and trainer or platform identity. Supports ServSafe and regulatory training compliance.'
}

Table foodsafety.environmental_monitoring {
  environmental_monitoring_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the environmental_monitoring data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']
  resampled_environmental_monitoring_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on environmental_monitoring (resampled_environmental_monitoring_id)']
  stock_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_location. Business justification: Environmental monitoring (e.g., humidity) is performed at storage locations; linking ties readings to the location.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of environmental monitoring samples collected at restaurant units, including sample type (swab, sponge, air plate), target organism (Listeria monocytogenes, Salmonella spp., E. coli, total plate count), sampling location (food contact surface, non-food contact surface, drain, floor), sample date, collected by, laboratory results, pass/fail against established limits, corrective action triggered flag, and zone classification (Zone 1-4). Supports pathogen environmental monitoring programs (EMPs) required by FSMA Preventive Controls and large QSR chain food safety standards.'
}

Table loyalty.member {
  member_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the loyalty program member. Primary key for the member entity.']
  account_manager_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Needed for assigning a dedicated employee to manage high‑value members, enabling personalized service and performance reporting.']
  acquisition_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Acquisition Campaign Attribution report requires linking each new member to the marketing campaign that drove the sign‑up, enabling ROI calculation of acquisition spend.']
  franchisee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to franchise.franchisee. Business justification: Supports franchisee‑level loyalty analytics by linking each member to the owning franchisee, used in franchise performance dashboards.']
  guest_profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the guest domain master record. Links loyalty membership to the guest profile system of record.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Member activity must be consolidated under the owning legal entity (franchise) for statutory financial statements.']
  member_preferred_location_unit_id varchar [note: 'Restaurant location that the member visits most frequently or has designated as their preferred location. Used for personalized offers and location-based campaigns.']
  member_unit_id varchar [note: 'Restaurant location where the member enrolled, if enrollment occurred at a physical location. Null for digital enrollments.']
  preferred_location_unit_id varchar [note: 'Restaurant location that the member visits most frequently or has designated as their preferred location. Used for personalized offers and location-based campaigns.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the guest domain master record. Links loyalty membership to the guest profile system of record.']
  referred_by_member_id varchar [note: 'Member ID of the existing member who referred this member to the loyalty program. Null if member enrolled without a referral.']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to realestate.site. Business justification: Needed for site‑specific loyalty marketing and reporting; members are assigned a primary restaurant site.']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Restaurant location where the member enrolled, if enrollment occurred at a physical location. Null for digital enrollments.']
  account_closure_date date [note: 'Date when the loyalty membership was closed or terminated. Null for active memberships. Used for retention analysis and right-to-be-forgotten compliance.']
  account_closure_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason for account closure. Examples: member request, fraud, inactivity, duplicate account, policy violation, data privacy request.']
  account_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the loyalty member record was first created in the system. Audit field for data lineage and compliance.']
  account_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the loyalty member record was last modified. Audit field for data lineage and change tracking.']
  badges_earned varchar [note: 'Total number of achievement badges earned by the member through gamification activities, challenges, and milestones.']
  birthday_month varchar [note: 'Month of the member\'s birthday (1-12). Stored without year or day for privacy. Used for birthday reward campaigns and personalized offers.']
  current_points_balance varchar [note: 'Current available points balance that the member can redeem. Calculated as lifetime earned minus lifetime redeemed minus expired points.']
  current_tier varchar [note: 'Current membership tier level in the loyalty program hierarchy. Determines benefits, rewards multipliers, and exclusive offers available to the member.']
  data_privacy_consent_date date [note: 'Date when the member provided explicit consent for data collection, processing, and storage under applicable privacy regulations.']
  direct_mail_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member has consented to receive physical direct mail offers and communications. True if opted in, False if opted out.']
  email_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member has consented to receive promotional and transactional emails. True if opted in, False if opted out.']
  enrollment_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the guest enrolled in the loyalty program. OLO (Online Ordering), POS (Point of Sale), mobile app, website, in-restaurant kiosk, or call center.']
  enrollment_date date [note: 'Date when the guest enrolled in the loyalty program. Marks the start of the member lifecycle.']
  gamification_level varchar [note: 'Current level in the loyalty program gamification system. Members progress through levels by completing challenges, earning badges, and accumulating points.']
  last_activity_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent loyalty program activity by the member, including points earned, points redeemed, or profile updates. Used for churn prediction and reactivation campaigns.']
  last_transaction_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent purchase transaction where the member earned or redeemed points. Subset of last_activity_date focused on revenue-generating activity.']
  lifetime_points_earned varchar [note: 'Total cumulative loyalty points earned by the member since enrollment. Never decreases; used for tier qualification and lifetime value analysis.']
  lifetime_points_redeemed varchar [note: 'Total cumulative loyalty points redeemed by the member for rewards, discounts, or offers since enrollment.']
  membership_number varchar [note: 'Externally-facing unique membership identifier displayed on loyalty cards, mobile app, and customer communications. Human-readable alphanumeric code.']
  next_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the next batch of points will expire. Null if no points are scheduled to expire.']
  nps_score varchar [note: 'Most recent Net Promoter Score provided by the member, ranging from 0 (detractor) to 10 (promoter). Measures member satisfaction and likelihood to recommend.']
  nps_survey_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent NPS survey was completed by the member.']
  points_expiring_soon varchar [note: 'Number of points scheduled to expire within the next 30 days. Used for proactive member engagement and retention campaigns.']
  preferred_language varchar [note: 'Member\'s preferred language for communications and app interface. Three-letter ISO 639-2 language code.']
  program_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the loyalty membership. Active: member in good standing. Suspended: temporarily blocked due to policy violation. Churned: member opted out or account closed. Inactive: no activity for extended period. Pending: enrollment initiated but not completed.']
  push_notification_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member has consented to receive push notifications through the mobile app. True if opted in, False if opted out.']
  referral_code varchar [note: 'Unique referral code assigned to this member for member-get-member campaigns. When shared and used by new enrollees, both parties receive bonus points.']
  sms_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member has consented to receive promotional and transactional SMS text messages. True if opted in, False if opted out.']
  status_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason or explanation for the current program status. Examples: fraud detection, member request, inactivity, policy violation, system migration.']
  terms_accepted_date date [note: 'Date when the member accepted the loyalty program terms and conditions. Required for legal compliance and dispute resolution.']
  terms_version varchar [note: 'Version number of the loyalty program terms and conditions that the member accepted. Format: v1.0, v2.1, etc.']
  third_party_sharing_opt_in boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member has consented to sharing their data with third-party partners for marketing purposes. True if opted in, False if opted out.']
  tier_effective_date date [note: 'Date when the member achieved or was assigned their current tier level. Used for tier anniversary calculations and renewal cycles.']
  tier_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the current tier status expires and member will be re-evaluated for tier qualification. Null for lifetime tiers.']
  total_visits varchar [note: 'Total number of restaurant visits or orders placed by the member since enrollment. Includes dine-in, drive-thru, OLO, and 3PD orders where loyalty was applied.']

  Note: 'Master record for every enrolled loyalty program member. Captures member identity linkage (guest_id FK to guest domain), enrollment channel (OLO, POS, in-app, kiosk, staff-assisted), enrollment date, current tier assignment, lifetime points earned/redeemed, opt-in preferences, program assignment, auto-accrue payment linkage flag, and program status (active, suspended, churned). This is the SSOT for loyalty membership identity — distinct from the guest profile master in the guest domain.'
}

Table loyalty.tier {
  tier_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the loyalty membership tier. Primary key.']
  annual_fee_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Annual membership fee charged to maintain this tier status, if applicable. Null or zero indicates no fee. Used for premium tier monetization.']
  benefits_summary varchar [note: 'Comma-separated or structured text summary of key benefits and perks associated with this tier (e.g., free delivery, birthday rewards, exclusive offers, priority support). Used for member communications and benefit entitlement logic.']
  birthday_reward_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether members in this tier receive a special birthday reward (e.g., free item, bonus points, discount). True if eligible, false otherwise.']
  color_code varchar [note: 'Hexadecimal color code used for tier branding in digital channels (e.g., #CD7F32 for bronze, #C0C0C0 for silver, #FFD700 for gold). Used in mobile app, website, and marketing materials.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this tier record was first created in the loyalty system. Used for audit trail and data lineage.']
  downgrade_threshold decimal(12,2) [note: 'Minimum threshold value required to maintain this tier status during re-evaluation period. Falling below this value triggers downgrade to lower tier. Null indicates no downgrade policy.']
  early_access_lto boolean [note: 'Indicates whether members in this tier receive early access to new Limited Time Offers (LTO) before general availability. True if eligible, false otherwise.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when this tier configuration was retired or replaced. Null indicates the tier is currently active. Used for tier lifecycle management and historical analysis.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when this tier configuration became active and available for member assignment. Used for tier lifecycle management and historical analysis.']
  exclusive_offers_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether members in this tier receive access to exclusive promotional offers and Limited Time Offers (LTO) not available to lower tiers. True if eligible, false otherwise.']
  free_delivery_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether members in this tier are eligible for free delivery on Online Ordering (OLO) and Third-Party Delivery (3PD) orders. True if eligible, false otherwise.']
  grace_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after tier expiration during which members retain tier benefits before downgrade. Used to provide buffer period for re-qualification.']
  icon_url varchar [note: 'URL path to the tier badge or icon image asset used in member portal, mobile app, and digital communications. Supports tier visual identity and gamification.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this tier record was last updated. Used for audit trail, change tracking, and data synchronization.']
  launch_date date [note: 'Date when this tier was officially launched to the market and communicated to guests. May differ from effective_start_date for soft launches or pilot programs.']
  max_redemption_discount_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum percentage discount that can be applied when redeeming points for rewards in this tier (e.g., 20.00 for 20% max discount). Used to control reward economics and prevent abuse.']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who last modified this tier configuration. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  points_multiplier decimal(5,2) [note: 'Multiplier applied to base points accrual for members in this tier (e.g., 1.0 for base, 1.5 for 50% bonus, 2.0 for double points). Used to calculate accelerated points earning.']
  priority_support_eligible boolean [note: 'Indicates whether members in this tier receive priority customer support (e.g., dedicated phone line, faster response times). True if eligible, false otherwise.']
  qualification_metric varchar [note: 'The metric used to determine tier qualification (points accrued, visit count, total spend amount, or transaction count). Defines how qualification_threshold is interpreted.']
  qualification_period_days varchar [note: 'Number of days over which the qualification threshold must be met (e.g., 365 for annual qualification, 90 for quarterly). Null indicates lifetime accumulation.']
  qualification_threshold decimal(12,2) [note: 'Numeric threshold value required to qualify for this tier (e.g., 1000 points, 25 visits, $500 spend). Interpretation depends on qualification_metric.']
  referral_bonus_points varchar [note: 'Bonus points awarded to members in this tier for each successful referral of a new loyalty program member. Used to incentivize member acquisition through word-of-mouth.']
  rollover_points_allowed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether unused points can roll over to the next qualification period for members in this tier. True if rollover is allowed, false if points expire at period end.']
  sort_order varchar [note: 'Integer defining the hierarchical display order of tiers (e.g., 1 for Bronze, 2 for Silver, 3 for Gold, 4 for Platinum). Lower values represent entry-level tiers; higher values represent premium tiers.']
  target_member_segment varchar [note: 'Description of the target guest segment this tier is designed to attract and retain (e.g., frequent visitors, high spenders, digital-first guests, family diners). Used for marketing strategy and program design.']
  tier_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the tier (e.g., BRONZE, SILVER, GOLD, PLATINUM). Used as business identifier in operational systems and reporting.']
  tier_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the tier, including value proposition, benefits summary, and marketing messaging. Used in loyalty program communications and member portal.']
  tier_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable display name of the loyalty tier (e.g., Bronze Member, Silver Member, Gold Member, Platinum Elite). Used in guest-facing communications and marketing materials.']
  tier_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the tier. Active tiers are available for member assignment; inactive tiers are temporarily disabled; retired tiers are no longer offered but may have legacy members; pending tiers are configured but not yet launched.']
  upgrade_notification boolean [note: 'Indicates whether members should receive automated notification when they qualify for upgrade to this tier. True if notification is enabled, false otherwise.']
  validity_days varchar [note: 'Number of days a member retains this tier status after qualification before re-evaluation (e.g., 365 for annual renewal). Null indicates permanent tier assignment.']
  welcome_bonus_points varchar [note: 'One-time bonus points awarded to a member upon first achieving this tier. Used to incentivize tier progression and enhance member engagement.']

  Note: 'Reference catalog of all loyalty membership tiers (e.g., Bronze, Silver, Gold, Platinum) defined in the Restaurants loyalty program. Stores tier name, tier code, qualification threshold (points or visit count), tier benefits summary, tier multiplier for points accrual, tier validity period, and sort order. Drives tier assignment logic and benefit entitlement across the program.'
}

Table loyalty.tier_history {
  tier_history_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each tier change event in the loyalty program. Primary key for the tier history transactional audit log.']
  guest_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific order, purchase, or points transaction that triggered this tier change event. Null for manual adjustments or scheduled reviews.']
  loyalty_member_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the loyalty program member who experienced this tier change event.']
  promotional_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the marketing campaign that granted this promotional tier upgrade. Null for non-promotional tier changes.']
  restaurant_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the restaurant location where the triggering transaction occurred. Null for tier changes not associated with a specific location (e.g., scheduled reviews, manual adjustments).']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the restaurant location where the triggering transaction occurred. Null for tier changes not associated with a specific location (e.g., scheduled reviews, manual adjustments).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this tier history record was first created in the data platform. Used for data lineage and audit tracking.']
  effective_date date [note: 'The date when the new tier status became active and the member began receiving benefits associated with the new tier level.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'The date when this tier status is scheduled to expire, requiring re-qualification or resulting in tier review. Null indicates indefinite or lifetime tier status.']
  is_manual_override boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this tier change was manually processed by an administrator or customer service representative rather than automatically triggered by system rules. True for manual adjustments, false for automated changes.']
  is_promotional_tier boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this tier change was the result of a promotional campaign or marketing offer rather than organic qualification. True for promotional upgrades, false for earned tier changes.']
  last_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this tier history record was most recently modified in the data platform. Used for change tracking and data quality monitoring.']
  new_tier_benefits_activated_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether benefits associated with the new tier were immediately activated and available for member use. Used for benefit provisioning tracking.']
  new_tier_code varchar [note: 'The loyalty tier code the member transitioned to as a result of this change event (e.g., BRONZE, SILVER, GOLD, PLATINUM).']
  notification_channel varchar [note: 'The communication channel used to notify the member of this tier change: email, SMS, push notification, in-app message, physical mail, or none if no notification was sent.']
  notification_sent_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a tier change notification was successfully sent to the member via email, mobile app push, or other communication channel. Used for member engagement tracking.']
  notification_sent_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the tier change notification was sent to the member. Used for communication audit and member experience analytics.']
  override_authorized_by varchar [note: 'The name or employee identifier of the administrator who authorized a manual tier override. Required for audit compliance when is_manual_override is true.']
  override_justification varchar [note: 'Business justification and approval notes for manual tier overrides, required for regulatory audit trails and program fairness documentation.']
  previous_tier_benefits_revoked_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether benefits associated with the previous tier were immediately revoked upon tier change. Used for benefit lifecycle management and member experience tracking.']
  previous_tier_code varchar [note: 'The loyalty tier code the member held immediately before this change event (e.g., BRONZE, SILVER, GOLD, PLATINUM).']
  processing_channel varchar [note: 'The channel through which this tier change was processed: POS (Point of Sale), OLO (Online Ordering), mobile app, web portal, call center, or admin portal. Used for channel analytics.']
  qualification_period_end_date date [note: 'The ending date of the measurement period used to evaluate tier qualification criteria.']
  qualification_period_start_date date [note: 'The beginning date of the measurement period used to evaluate tier qualification criteria (e.g., rolling 12 months, calendar year).']
  qualifying_points_balance varchar [note: 'The total loyalty points balance at the time of tier change that contributed to qualification or demotion decision.']
  qualifying_spend_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The cumulative spend amount that qualified the member for this tier change, measured in the program\'s base currency. Used for tier progression analytics.']
  qualifying_visit_count varchar [note: 'The number of restaurant visits or transactions during the qualification period that contributed to this tier change.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'The operational system that originated this tier change event (e.g., Olo Digital Ordering Platform, Oracle MICROS POS, Salesforce CRM). Used for data lineage and system integration tracking.']
  source_system_record_code varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of this tier change record in the source operational system, used for data reconciliation and troubleshooting.']
  tier_change_notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes or comments about this tier change event, used for customer service context and audit documentation.']
  tier_change_number varchar [note: 'Business-readable identifier for this tier change event, used for customer service reference and audit tracking.']
