  primary_enrollment_member_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the loyalty program member who enrolled. Links to the member master record.']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the loyalty program the member enrolled in. Supports multi-program environments.']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the restaurant unit where the enrollment occurred. Null for digital-only enrollments without a specific unit association.']
  welcome_offer_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the specific welcome offer or promotional reward issued at enrollment. Null if no offer was issued.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this enrollment event record was first created in the data platform. System audit field for data lineage.']
  email_opt_in_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member opted in to receive email communications at enrollment. Required for CAN-SPAM compliance.']
  enrollment_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the enrollment occurred. OLO (Online Ordering) for web/app orders, POS (Point of Sale) for in-restaurant, mobile_app for native app, kiosk for self-service terminals, staff_assisted for employee-facilitated, web for website.']
  enrollment_country_code varchar [note: 'Country where the enrollment occurred. ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 three-letter country codes. Used for geographic segmentation and compliance. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USA|CAN|MEX|GBR|FRA|DEU|AUS|JPN|CHN|BRA — 10 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  enrollment_device_type varchar [note: 'Type of device used to complete the enrollment. Supports channel and user experience analysis.']
  enrollment_geolocation varchar [note: 'Geographic coordinates (latitude, longitude) of the enrollment location if captured. Format: latitude,longitude. Used for location-based analytics.']
  enrollment_ip_address varchar [note: 'IP address from which the enrollment was initiated. Used for fraud detection and geographic analysis. May be considered PII in some jurisdictions.']
  enrollment_language varchar [note: 'Language preference selected by the member at enrollment. ISO 639-2 three-letter language codes. Used for personalized communications. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: eng|spa|fra|deu|ita|por|zho|jpn|kor — 9 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  enrollment_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments about the enrollment event. Used for special circumstances, exceptions, or customer service annotations.']
  enrollment_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing unique enrollment transaction number. Format: ENR followed by 10 digits. Used for customer service and audit tracking.']
  enrollment_source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that captured the enrollment event. Examples: Olo, Oracle MICROS, Salesforce CRM, Mobile App. Used for data lineage and troubleshooting.']
  enrollment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the enrollment event. Completed indicates successful enrollment; pending indicates awaiting verification; failed indicates enrollment did not complete; cancelled indicates member-initiated cancellation; reversed indicates system or compliance reversal.']
  enrollment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the enrollment event occurred in the source system. Represents the real-world business event time, distinct from record audit timestamps.']
  enrollment_type varchar [note: 'Type of enrollment action. New indicates first-time enrollment; re_enrollment indicates returning member; transfer indicates program migration; upgrade indicates tier promotion enrollment.']
  fraud_check_status varchar [note: 'Status of fraud detection checks performed at enrollment. Passed indicates clean enrollment; failed indicates suspected fraud; pending indicates checks in progress; not_required indicates no checks were performed.']
  fraud_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric fraud risk score assigned to the enrollment event by fraud detection systems. Range typically 0-100, with higher scores indicating higher risk. Null if fraud checking was not performed.']
  initial_points_awarded varchar [note: 'Number of loyalty points awarded to the member at enrollment as a sign-up bonus. Zero if no points were awarded.']
  marketing_opt_in_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member opted in to receive marketing communications at enrollment. True if opted in; False if declined.']
  push_notification_opt_in_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member opted in to receive mobile app push notifications at enrollment.']
  referral_code varchar [note: 'Specific referral or campaign code used during enrollment. Enables attribution tracking for member-get-member programs and promotional campaigns.']
  referral_source varchar [note: 'Source that drove the enrollment. Organic for unprompted; referral_code for member referral; campaign for marketing campaign; social_media for social channels; email for email marketing; in_store for restaurant promotion; partner for third-party partner. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: organic|referral_code|campaign|social_media|email|in_store|partner — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  sms_opt_in_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member opted in to receive SMS/text message communications at enrollment. Required for TCPA compliance.']
  terms_accepted_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the member accepted the loyalty program terms and conditions at enrollment. Required for compliance and legal purposes.']
  terms_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier of the terms and conditions accepted by the member at enrollment. Enables tracking of which terms version the member agreed to.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this enrollment event record was last modified in the data platform. System audit field for change tracking.']
  verification_completed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether required verification has been completed. True if completed; False if pending or not completed.']
  verification_completed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when enrollment verification was completed. Null if verification is not required or not yet completed.']
  verification_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the enrollment requires additional verification (e.g., email verification, identity verification). True if verification is required; False if not required.']
  welcome_offer_issued_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a welcome offer or sign-up bonus was issued to the member upon enrollment. True if issued; False if not issued.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of every loyalty program enrollment action — new enrollments, re-enrollments, and program transfers. Captures member reference, program reference, enrollment channel (OLO, POS, in-app, kiosk, staff-assisted), enrollment restaurant unit, enrollment timestamp, referral source (organic, referral_code, campaign), welcome offer issued flag, and enrollment status. Feeds acquisition funnel analysis and channel attribution for the loyalty program.'
}

Table loyalty.referral {
  referral_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the referral event. Primary key.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the marketing campaign or promotional period under which this referral was created. Supports campaign-level ROI analysis.']
  enrollment_location_unit_id varchar [note: 'Restaurant location where the referred guest completed enrollment. Null for online enrollments. Supports location-level referral performance tracking.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Guest who received the referral invitation. May be pre-enrollment or post-enrollment guest identifier.']
  referral_referred_member_id varchar [note: 'Loyalty member ID assigned to the referred guest upon successful enrollment conversion. Null if referral has not yet converted.']
  referral_referring_member_id varchar [note: 'Loyalty member who initiated the referral. Links to the member who shared the referral code or invitation.']
  referred_guest_profile_id varchar [note: 'Guest who received the referral invitation. May be pre-enrollment or post-enrollment guest identifier.']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Restaurant location where the referred guest completed enrollment. Null for online enrollments. Supports location-level referral performance tracking.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the referral invitation was shared. Supports attribution of viral growth mechanics across digital and physical touchpoints.']
  conversion_date date [note: 'Date when the referred guest successfully enrolled in the loyalty program and the referral converted. Null if referral has not yet converted.']
  conversion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the referred guest completed enrollment and the referral status changed to converted. Null if not yet converted.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this referral record was first created in the loyalty system. Audit trail for record creation.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the referral code expires and can no longer be used for enrollment. Supports time-bound referral campaigns and urgency mechanics.']
  first_transaction_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total amount of the referred member first purchase. Supports Average Check Value (ACV) lift analysis for referred members.']
  first_transaction_date date [note: 'Date of the referred member first purchase transaction after enrollment. Used to measure referral quality and time-to-first-purchase.']
  fraud_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this referral was flagged as fraudulent by automated or manual review. True if fraudulent activity detected.']
  fraud_reason varchar [note: 'Description of the fraud detection reason if fraud_flag is true. Examples include duplicate device, self-referral, velocity abuse, or manual review findings.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this referral record was last updated. Supports change tracking and audit compliance.']
  message varchar [note: 'Optional personalized message included by the referring member when sharing the referral. Supports engagement analysis and sentiment tracking.']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or system identifier that last modified this referral record. Supports audit trail and accountability.']
  referral_code varchar [note: 'Unique alphanumeric code used by the referring member to track the referral. Generated by the loyalty platform and shared via referral channels.']
  referral_date date [note: 'Date when the referring member initiated the referral event. Represents the business event timestamp for referral creation.']
  referral_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the referral. Pending indicates awaiting conversion; converted indicates successful enrollment; expired indicates referral window closed; fraudulent indicates detected abuse; cancelled indicates referrer or system cancellation.']
  referral_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Precise timestamp when the referral was created in the loyalty system. Supports detailed event sequencing and time-of-day analysis.']
  referred_bonus_awarded_date date [note: 'Date when the referred member bonus points were credited to the new member account. Null if bonus not yet awarded.']
  referred_bonus_points varchar [note: 'Loyalty points awarded to the referred member upon successful enrollment. Incentivizes new member acquisition and first-transaction engagement.']
  referrer_bonus_awarded_date date [note: 'Date when the referrer bonus points were credited to the referring member account. Null if bonus not yet awarded.']
  referrer_bonus_points varchar [note: 'Loyalty points awarded to the referring member upon successful referral conversion. Supports referral program ROI measurement and member incentive tracking.']
  source_platform varchar [note: 'Digital platform or system where the referral was initiated. Supports omnichannel referral attribution and platform performance analysis.']
  terms_version varchar [note: 'Version of the referral program terms and conditions accepted by the referring member at the time of referral creation. Supports compliance and audit trails.']

  Note: 'Tracks member-to-member referral events within the loyalty program. Captures referring member, referred guest (pre-enrollment), referral code used, referral channel (app share, email, social), referral date, referred member enrollment date (if converted), referral status (pending, converted, expired, fraudulent), and bonus points awarded to referrer upon successful conversion. Supports viral growth mechanics and referral program ROI measurement.'
}

Table loyalty.loyalty_segment {
  loyalty_segment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the segment data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'User identifier of the CRM or marketing team member responsible for managing this segment. Used for accountability, access control, and workflow routing.']
  owner_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'User identifier of the CRM or marketing team member responsible for managing this segment. Used for accountability, access control, and workflow routing.']
  trade_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to realestate.trade_area. Business justification: Segments are defined by trade‑area demographics; linking allows segment performance analysis by trade area.']
  activation_date date [note: 'Date when the segment was first activated and made available for campaign use. Used for segment lifecycle tracking and historical analysis.']
  acv_max_threshold decimal(10,2) [note: 'Maximum Average Check Value in USD for segment qualification. Used to define value-based cohorts and target budget-conscious guests with appropriate offers.']
  acv_min_threshold decimal(10,2) [note: 'Minimum Average Check Value in USD required for a member to qualify for this segment. Used to identify high-value guests for premium offers and personalized campaigns.']
  campaign_usage_count varchar [note: 'Number of active marketing campaigns currently using this segment for offer distribution or personalized messaging. Used to assess segment utilization and prevent accidental deletion of in-use segments.']
  channel_preference varchar [note: 'Primary ordering channel used by members in this segment. POS: in-store counter. OLO: online ordering (web/app). Drive-Thru: DT lane. 3PD: third-party delivery. Kiosk: self-service terminal. Used for channel-specific offer distribution.']
  control_group_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this segment serves as a control group (holdout) for campaign effectiveness measurement. True: control group, no offers sent. False: treatment group. Used for incrementality analysis and ROI measurement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the segment record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and segment lifecycle tracking.']
  current_tier_filter varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of loyalty tier codes that qualify for this segment. Used for tier-based segmentation to target premium or entry-level members with tier-appropriate offers. Null if segment is not tier-specific.']
  daypart_preference varchar [note: 'Primary daypart during which members in this segment typically visit. Used to target time-of-day specific offers and LTO promotions. Null if segment is not daypart-specific.']
  deactivation_date date [note: 'Date when the segment was deactivated or archived. Null if segment is currently active. Used for segment lifecycle tracking and campaign history analysis.']
  definition_criteria varchar [note: 'Structured or free-text representation of the business rules and thresholds used to assign members to this segment. Examples: visit_frequency >= 8 per month AND ACV >= $12; daypart = breakfast AND menu_category = coffee; tier = gold OR platinum; last_visit_days > 90.']
  exclusion_segment_ids varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of segment IDs whose members should be excluded from this segment. Used for negative targeting and campaign suppression logic. Null if no exclusions apply.']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic coverage level of the segment. National: all locations. Regional: multi-state region. Market: DMA or metro area. Restaurant: single location. Used to control offer distribution and campaign reach.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the segment record was last updated. Includes changes to definition criteria, status, or metadata. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  last_refresh_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent segment membership refresh execution. Segment membership is recalculated periodically based on definition criteria. Used to monitor data freshness and refresh cadence.']
  lifetime_points_min varchar [note: 'Minimum cumulative loyalty points earned over the member lifetime required for segment qualification. Used to identify long-term high-engagement members.']
  member_count varchar [note: 'Current number of active loyalty members assigned to this segment. Updated during segment refresh process. Used for campaign reach estimation and segment size monitoring.']
  menu_affinity_category varchar [note: 'Primary menu category or product line that members in this segment have high purchase affinity for. Examples: coffee, breakfast sandwiches, salads, value menu, premium burgers. Used for product-specific campaign targeting.']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User identifier of the person who last modified the segment record. Used for audit trail, accountability, and change management.']
  next_refresh_date date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next segment membership refresh execution. Calculated as last_refresh_timestamp + refresh_frequency_days. Used for operational planning and monitoring.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, business context, campaign history, or special handling instructions related to this segment. Used by CRM and marketing teams for knowledge sharing.']
  owner_team varchar [note: 'Business team or department responsible for this segment. Used for organizational reporting and cross-functional collaboration.']
  predicted_incremental_revenue decimal(12,2) [note: 'Machine learning model predicted incremental revenue in USD per member when this segment is targeted with offers, compared to control group baseline. Used for campaign ROI forecasting. Null if predictive model not applied.']
  predicted_response_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Machine learning model predicted response rate (0.0000 to 1.0000) for members in this segment when targeted with offers. Used for campaign ROI forecasting and segment prioritization. Null if predictive model not applied.']
  priority_rank varchar [note: 'Numeric priority ranking used when a member qualifies for multiple segments and campaign assignment logic requires a single segment selection. Lower values indicate higher priority. Used in offer conflict resolution.']
  recency_days_max varchar [note: 'Maximum number of days since last visit for segment qualification. Used to define active or highly engaged member cohorts. Null if recency is not a defining criterion.']
  recency_days_min varchar [note: 'Minimum number of days since last visit for segment qualification. Used in RFM segmentation to identify lapsed or at-risk members. Null if recency is not a defining criterion.']
  refresh_frequency_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between scheduled segment membership refresh executions. Common values: 1 (daily), 7 (weekly), 30 (monthly). Used to control segment data freshness and computational load.']
  segment_code varchar [note: 'Short alphanumeric code used to identify the segment in operational systems and reporting. Must be unique and human-readable for campaign execution workflows.']
  segment_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the segment purpose, target audience, and business rationale. Used by marketing and CRM teams to understand segment intent and usage.']
  segment_name varchar [note: 'Business-friendly name of the loyalty segment used in marketing campaigns and reporting dashboards. Examples: High-Value Breakfast Loyalists, Lapsed Drive-Thru Users, New Member Onboarding.']
  segment_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the segment. Active: available for campaign assignment. Inactive: temporarily disabled. Draft: under development. Archived: historical, no longer used. Scheduled: configured but not yet active.']
  segment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the segmentation methodology used to define member inclusion. Behavioral: visit frequency, menu affinity, daypart preference. Demographic: age, income, household. Tier-based: current loyalty tier. RFM: recency, frequency, monetary analysis. Lifecycle: new, active, at-risk, lapsed. Geographic: region, market, trade area. Channel preference: OLO, POS, 3PD. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: behavioral|demographic|tier_based|rfm|lifecycle|geographic|channel_preference — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  target_market_codes varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of market or DMA codes where this segment is active. Used for geo-targeted campaigns and regional offer distribution. Null if segment is national or not geographically constrained.']
  test_segment_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this segment is used for A/B testing, pilot campaigns, or experimental offer strategies. True: test segment. False: production segment. Used to isolate test populations and measure incremental lift.']
  visit_frequency_threshold varchar [note: 'Minimum number of visits within the qualification period required for a member to qualify for this segment. Used for behavioral segmentation based on transaction count.']

  Note: 'Master record for loyalty member segments used for targeted offer distribution, personalized campaigns, and cohort analysis. Captures segment name, segment type (behavioral, demographic, tier-based, RFM, lifecycle), definition criteria (visit frequency, ACV ranges, daypart preferences, menu affinity), segment size, last refresh timestamp, refresh frequency, and active status. Also owns the member-segment assignment detail: each member assignment captures assignment date, removal date, assignment method (rule-based, manual, ML-model), segment version at assignment, and assignment source system. Managed by CRM/marketing team and consumed by offer distribution and campaign execution workflows. Supports temporal segment membership queries and before/after campaign cohort comparisons.'
}

Table loyalty.loyalty_segment_membership {
  loyalty_segment_membership_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the loyalty_segment_membership data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']
  loyalty_segment_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.segment. Business justification: Segment membership must reference the loyalty segment it assigns.']
  member_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.member. Business justification: Segment membership must reference the member it applies to.']

  Note: 'Association table linking loyalty members to their assigned segments at a point in time. Captures member reference, segment reference, assignment date, removal date, assignment method (rule-based, manual, ML-model), and the segment version at time of assignment. Supports temporal segment membership queries and enables before/after campaign cohort comparisons.'
}

Table loyalty.loyalty_visit {
  loyalty_visit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the loyalty_visit data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']
  member_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.member. Business justification: A loyalty visit records which member performed the visit.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of each qualifying restaurant visit by a loyalty member that is recognized and attributed to their loyalty account — whether via POS scan, OLO order, or card-linked payment match. Captures member reference, restaurant unit, visit date, visit channel (dine-in, drive-thru, OLO, 3PD, kiosk), daypart (breakfast, lunch, dinner, late-night), order reference, check value (ACV), points earned on visit, and visit sequence number (lifetime visit count at time of visit). Core input for visit-frequency-based tier qualification and challenge progress.'
}

Table loyalty.loyalty_adjustment {
  loyalty_adjustment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the loyalty_adjustment data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']
  loyalty_member_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to loyalty.member. Business justification: Loyalty adjustments must be tied to the member whose points are adjusted; adding loyalty_member_id FK eliminates the silo and enables reporting of adjustments per member.']

  Note: 'Operational record of manual or system-initiated points adjustments made to a member\'s loyalty account outside of normal earn/redeem flows — covering goodwill credits, dispute resolutions, system error corrections, promotional credits, and fraud-related reversals. Captures member reference, adjustment type, points delta, monetary equivalent, reason code, authorizing agent or system, approval status, and adjustment timestamp. Provides full auditability for program integrity and member dispute resolution.'
}

Table loyalty.program_campaign_allocation {
  program_campaign_allocation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the ProgramCampaignAllocation association']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the marketing campaign']
  program_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the loyalty program']
  budget_allocation decimal [note: 'Budget amount allocated to the program for this campaign']
  target_audience varchar [note: 'Guest segment targeted by the campaign within the program']

  Note: 'This association product captures the allocation of marketing budget and target audience for each pairing of a loyalty program and a marketing campaign. Each record links one program to one campaign and stores attributes that exist only in the context of this relationship.. Existence Justification: Loyalty programs are actively linked to marketing campaigns; each link is created and maintained by marketing teams to specify how much budget is allocated and which guest segment is targeted. A single program can be promoted by many campaigns, and a single campaign can target multiple programs, with the association carrying its own data.'
}

Table marketing.campaign {
  campaign_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for each marketing campaign.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to franchise.franchise_agreement. Business justification: Needed to associate campaigns with the governing franchise agreement, ensuring marketing fees are charged per agreement and supporting audit of compliance with contract terms.']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.contract. Business justification: Required for Campaign Contract Management report linking each marketing campaign to its signed procurement contract for media spend compliance.']
  food_recall_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to foodsafety.food_recall. Business justification: Recall campaigns are created to communicate product recalls; linking campaign to recall record tracks effectiveness and regulatory compliance.']
  guest_segment_id varchar [note: 'Guest segment (e.g., families, millennials) the campaign targets.']
  ingredient_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.ingredient. Business justification: Required for Ingredient Launch Campaigns that promote a newly sourced ingredient; marketing needs to reference the specific ingredient being advertised.']
  owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Required for the Campaign Ownership Report that tracks which employee owns each marketing campaign, a standard operational metric in restaurant marketing.']
  supply_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supply_supplier. Business justification: Needed for Supplier Co‑Brand Campaigns where a marketing campaign is jointly run with a specific supplier; ties campaign to the supplier record.']
  territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to franchise.territory. Business justification: Required for campaign planning and ROI reporting by linking each marketing campaign to the specific franchise territory it targets, enabling territory-level spend allocation and compliance tracking.']
  trade_area_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to realestate.trade_area. Business justification: Campaign planning uses trade‑area segmentation to allocate spend; linking enables trade‑area ROI reporting and compliance with market‑targeting strategy.']
  actual_adt_lift_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Measured increase in Average Daily Transactions after campaign execution.']
  actual_comp_sales_lift_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Measured lift in comparable store sales after campaign execution.']
  actual_end_date date [note: 'Date the campaign actually ended.']
  actual_spend decimal(15,2) [note: 'Actual monetary spend recorded for the campaign.']
  actual_start_date date [note: 'Date the campaign actually started.']
  budget_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Planned monetary budget allocated to the campaign.']
  campaign_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or code used to reference the campaign in external systems.']
  campaign_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the purpose and scope of the campaign.']
  campaign_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the campaign.']
  campaign_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the campaign.']
  campaign_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the campaign by scope and delivery channel.']
  channel_mix varchar [note: 'Combination of media channels used (e.g., digital, social, TV, radio).']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the campaign complies with all applicable foodservice marketing regulations.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the campaign record was first created.']
  expected_adt_lift_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Planned increase in Average Daily Transactions expressed as a percentage.']
  expected_comp_sales_lift_pct decimal(5,2) [note: 'Planned lift in comparable store sales expressed as a percentage.']
  is_lto boolean [note: 'True if the campaign includes a Limited‑Time Offer (LTO).']
  is_test_campaign boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the campaign is a test or pilot (true) or a production campaign (false).']
  lto_end_date date [note: 'End date of the Limited‑Time Offer component, if applicable.']
  lto_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the Limited‑Time Offer component, if applicable.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments about the campaign.']
  objective varchar [note: 'Primary business goal of the campaign (e.g., ADT lift, comparable store sales, brand awareness).']
  objective_metric varchar [note: 'Metric used to measure the campaign\'s objective.']
  owning_brand varchar [note: 'Brand within Restaurants that owns and sponsors the campaign.']
  planned_end_date date [note: 'Date the campaign is scheduled to conclude.']
  planned_start_date date [note: 'Date the campaign is scheduled to begin.']
  target_daypart varchar [note: 'Daypart (e.g., breakfast, lunch, dinner) the campaign is aimed at.']
  target_geography varchar [note: 'Geographic region(s) targeted by the campaign (e.g., USA, CAN).']
  target_market varchar [note: 'Market segment or demographic the campaign is aimed at (e.g., urban millennials).']
  target_store_count varchar [note: 'Number of restaurant locations the campaign is intended to cover.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the campaign record.']

  Note: 'Master record for every marketing campaign executed by Restaurants, including promotional campaigns, LTO (Limited Time Offer) launches, brand awareness drives, and traffic-building initiatives. Captures campaign name, type (national/local/digital/traditional), objective (ADT lift, comp sales, brand awareness), status (planned/active/completed/cancelled), planned and actual start/end dates, target daypart, target guest segment, owning brand, channel mix, budget allocation, and campaign owner. SSOT for campaign identity across all marketing execution.'
}

Table marketing.campaign_execution {
  campaign_execution_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the campaign execution record.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to franchise.franchise_agreement. Business justification: Links each campaign execution to the specific franchise agreement authorizing it, required for royalty calculations and legal compliance verification.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Each execution belongs to a single campaign; linking enables traceability of spend and performance.']
  execution_owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Needed for the Campaign Execution Oversight Report, linking the employee responsible for executing a campaign to the execution record.']
  franchise_franchisee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the franchise associated with the execution (if applicable).']
  franchisee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the franchise associated with the execution (if applicable).']
  health_inspection_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to foodsafety.health_inspection. Business justification: Compliance check: marketing verifies latest health inspection before launching campaign execution to ensure unit meets safety standards.']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to restaurant.unit. Business justification: Required for budgeting, ROI calculation, and compliance reporting of campaign execution at a specific restaurant location.']
  actual_adt_lift_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Observed percentage increase in ADT after execution.']
  actual_comp_sales_lift_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Observed percentage lift in comparable store sales.']
  actual_end_date date [note: 'Actual date when the campaign ended.']
  actual_launch_date date [note: 'Actual date when the campaign went live.']
  campaign_execution_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the campaign execution.']
  channel_spend_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total monetary spend allocated to the execution channel.']
  clicks varchar [note: 'Number of clicks generated by the campaign.']
  conversions varchar [note: 'Number of conversion actions (e.g., orders) attributed to the campaign.']
  cost_per_click decimal(10,2) [note: 'Average cost incurred per click.']
  cost_per_impression decimal(10,4) [note: 'Average cost incurred per thousand impressions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the campaign execution record was created.']
  creative_version varchar [note: 'Identifier for the creative version used in the execution.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter currency code for monetary amounts.']
  deviation_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation of any deviation from the original campaign plan.']
  execution_channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the campaign was executed.']
  execution_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code assigned to the campaign execution.']
  execution_owner varchar [note: 'Person or team responsible for executing the campaign.']
  execution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the primary execution event (e.g., launch).']
  expected_adt_lift_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Projected percentage increase in ADT due to the campaign.']
  expected_comp_sales_lift_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Projected percentage lift in comparable store sales.']
  impressions varchar [note: 'Number of times the campaign was displayed.']
  launch_date date [note: 'Planned date for the campaign launch.']
  market_dma varchar [note: 'Geographic market or DMA where the campaign is executed.']
  media_vendor varchar [note: 'Name of the media vendor or agency delivering the campaign.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any additional information about the execution.']
  planned_end_date date [note: 'Planned date for the campaign to end.']
  restaurant_scope varchar [note: 'Scope of restaurants included in the execution.']
  roi_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Calculated ROI percentage for the campaign execution.']
  target_audience varchar [note: 'Description of the intended audience for the campaign.']
  target_segment varchar [note: 'Segment of customers the campaign targets.']
  tracking_url varchar [note: 'URL used to track digital campaign performance.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the actual execution of a campaign within a specific market, restaurant group, or channel. Tracks execution status, launch date, actual vs. planned go-live, execution channel (digital, social, OOH, radio, TV, in-store POS), market/DMA, restaurant scope (all units, franchise group, company-owned), execution owner, and any deviations from the campaign plan. Enables operational tracking of campaign rollout across thousands of restaurant units.'
}

Table marketing.marketing_lto {
  marketing_lto_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the marketing_lto data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: LTOs are created for specific campaigns; linking provides ownership.']

  Note: 'Master record for Limited Time Offers (LTOs) — the cornerstone of QSR marketing strategy. Captures LTO name, associated menu item(s), promotional price, offer window (start/end date), daypart applicability, channel availability (DT, OLO, 3PD, dine-in), target guest segment, PMIX impact forecast, sourcing readiness flag, and regulatory compliance status (FDA labeling). Links to campaign and menu domain for full LTO lifecycle management from ideation through execution.'
}

Table marketing.media_plan {
  media_plan_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the media plan.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the marketing campaign associated with this media plan.']
  agency_name varchar [note: 'Advertising agency partner responsible for executing the media plan.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the media plan.']
  brand_name varchar [note: 'Brand associated with the media plan.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the media plan record was created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the planned spend.']
  daypart_target varchar [note: 'Primary daypart(s) targeted by the media plan.']
  digital_spend decimal(15,2) [note: 'Planned spend allocated to digital advertising channels.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the media plan becomes effective.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the media plan ends or expires.']
  frequency_target varchar [note: 'Target average number of times each individual is exposed.']
  is_active boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the media plan is currently active.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the media plan.']
  ooh_spend decimal(15,2) [note: 'Planned spend allocated to out-of-home advertising.']
  plan_code varchar [note: 'External reference code for the media plan used in marketing systems.']
  plan_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the media plan objectives and scope.']
  plan_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the media plan.']
  plan_type varchar [note: 'Category of the media plan indicating its scope.']
  print_spend decimal(15,2) [note: 'Planned spend allocated to print advertising.']
  radio_spend decimal(15,2) [note: 'Planned spend allocated to radio advertising.']
  reach_target varchar [note: 'Target number of unique individuals to be reached.']
  social_spend decimal(15,2) [note: 'Planned spend allocated to social media advertising.']
  target_dma varchar [note: 'Designated Designated Market Area code for the media plan\'s geographic focus.']
  total_planned_spend decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total amount of advertising spend planned for the media plan.']
  tv_spend decimal(15,2) [note: 'Planned spend allocated to television advertising.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the media plan record.']

  Note: 'Master record for the media planning document that defines how advertising spend is allocated across channels (TV, radio, digital, social, OOH, print) for a given campaign or fiscal period. Captures plan name, planning period, total planned spend, channel mix breakdown, target DMA/market, agency partner, brand, daypart targeting, reach and frequency targets, and approval status. SSOT for pre-buy media strategy and spend authorization.'
}

Table marketing.media_buy {
  media_buy_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the media buy record.']
  ad_creative_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the creative asset used for this placement.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the marketing campaign associated with this media buy.']
  media_channel_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.media_channel. Business justification: Media buy records need to reference a specific media channel; replace string column with proper FK.']
  media_plan_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the overarching media plan that includes this buy.']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the media vendor or publisher providing the ad placement.']
  vendor_procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the media vendor or publisher providing the ad placement.']
  actual_cpm decimal(10,2) [note: 'Effective CPM based on actual impressions delivered.']
  actual_grps decimal(10,2) [note: 'GRPs measured after the flight completed.']
  actual_impressions varchar [note: 'Number of impressions actually served during the flight.']
  ad_format varchar [note: 'Creative format of the advertisement.']
  adjustment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of discounts, fees, or other adjustments applied to the contracted amount.']
  agency_name varchar [note: 'Name of the advertising agency managing the purchase, if applicable.']
  audience_segment varchar [note: 'Target audience segment or demographic for the placement.']
  budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum budget allocated for this media buy within the campaign.']
  buy_number varchar [note: 'Business-facing identifier or purchase order number for the media placement.']
  buy_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the media purchase was executed.']
  contract_end_date date [note: 'Effective end date of the media contract (null if open‑ended).']
  contract_start_date date [note: 'Effective start date of the media contract.']
  contracted_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total contracted gross spend for the media placement before discounts or fees.']
  contracted_grps decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total GRPs purchased for the flight.']
  contracted_impressions varchar [note: 'Number of impressions (or equivalent) guaranteed in the contract.']
  cpm_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'Agreed cost per thousand impressions, expressed in the transaction currency.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the media buy record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the transaction.']
  flight_end_date date [note: 'Last calendar date the ad is scheduled to run.']
  flight_start_date date [note: 'First calendar date the ad is scheduled to run.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'Invoice number issued by the vendor for this media purchase.']
  is_programmatic boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the buy was executed programmatically.']
  market_dma varchar [note: 'Geographic market or DMA where the media placement is targeted.']
  media_buy_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the media buy.']
  net_spend decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final net amount paid after adjustments.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments about the media buy.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current payment processing status for the media buy.']
  placement_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the specific ad placement or slot.']
  placement_size varchar [note: 'Dimensions or size specification of the ad slot (e.g., 300x250).']
  publisher_name varchar [note: 'Name of the network, platform, or publisher delivering the ad.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Status of financial reconciliation against the media plan.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system (e.g., Salesforce Marketing Cloud, MediaOcean).']
  targeting_criteria varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of targeting rules (e.g., geo, interest, device).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the media buy record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for each marketing expenditure line item — the single financial ledger for all campaign spending. Captures media placement purchases (TV spots, digital display, paid social, SEM, OOH panels, radio spots), production costs, agency fees, and in-store material costs. Records buy/spend date, spend category (media placement/production/agency fee/in-store materials), channel type, publisher/network/vendor, placement or service name, flight dates, contracted impressions/GRPs (for media), CPM/CPP rate, actual spend amount, invoice reference, market/DMA code, budget line item, variance to plan, reconciliation status against the media plan, and campaign budget pacing status. SSOT for all granular campaign expenditure tracking, post-buy analysis, spend pacing, and campaign-level financial reporting.'
}

Table marketing.media_channel {
  media_channel_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the media channel record.']
  active_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the channel.']
  average_cpm decimal(12,2) [note: 'Typical cost per thousand impressions for the channel.']
  channel_code varchar [note: 'Unique business code for the media channel.']
  channel_group varchar [note: 'Higher-level grouping such as Paid Media or Earned Media.']
  channel_owner varchar [note: 'Internal team or individual responsible for the channel.']
  compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Notes on regulatory compliance such as FDA advertising rules.']
  cost_model varchar [note: 'Pricing model for media buying on the channel.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the channel record was created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for cost values.']
  data_source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system of record for the channel data.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the channel is retired or no longer used.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the channel became active for use.']
  geographic_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic reach of the channel.']
  is_programmatic boolean [note: 'Indicates if the channel supports programmatic buying.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent compliance audit for the channel.']
  measurement_methodology varchar [note: 'Primary metric used to measure channel performance.']
  media_channel_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the channel.']
  media_channel_description varchar [note: 'Detailed description of the channel and its typical usage.']
  media_channel_name varchar [note: 'Human readable name of the media channel.']
  platform varchar [note: 'Specific platform or network hosting the channel, e.g., Google, Facebook, CBS.']
  primary_audience varchar [note: 'Main audience segment targeted by the channel.']
  sub_category varchar [note: 'More specific classification within the category, e.g., paid search, display, etc.']
  targeting_capabilities varchar [note: 'Supported targeting options such as demographic, geographic, or behavioral.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the channel record.']

  Note: 'Reference master for all marketing media channels and platforms used by Restaurants — TV, radio, digital display, paid search (SEM), paid social (Facebook, Instagram, TikTok, Snapchat), OOH (billboards, transit), email, SMS, in-app push, in-store POS signage, 3PD platform ads (DoorDash, Uber Eats, Grubhub). Captures channel name, channel category (traditional/digital/social/in-store/3PD), platform/network, targeting capabilities, measurement methodology (impressions/clicks/GRPs/reach), cost model (CPM/CPC/CPP/flat), and active status. Enables consistent channel classification across media plans, buys, and performance reporting.'
}

Table marketing.ad_creative {
  ad_creative_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the advertising creative asset.']
  primary_ad_campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the marketing campaign that uses this creative.']
  ad_creative_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the creative asset.']
  ad_format_specifications varchar [note: 'Technical specifications required for the ad format (e.g., bitrate, codec).']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the creative received legal approval.']
  archive_date date [note: 'Date when the creative was moved to archive.']
  asset_url varchar [note: 'Direct URL to the stored creative file in the DAM.']
  brand_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Result of brand compliance review for the creative.']
  budget_allocated decimal(12,2) [note: 'Marketing budget assigned to this creative.']
  call_to_action_text varchar [note: 'Text of the call‑to‑action displayed in the creative.']
  channel_suitability varchar [note: 'Media channels where the creative is approved for deployment.']
  compliance_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent brand compliance review.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the creative record was first created.']
  creative_category varchar [note: 'High‑level category grouping for the creative.']
  creative_code varchar [note: 'External code or SKU assigned to the creative for inventory and tracking.']
  creative_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the creative asset.']
  creative_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the creative asset used for reporting and search.']
  creative_owner varchar [note: 'Name of the internal team or individual responsible for the creative.']
  creative_subcategory varchar [note: 'More specific sub‑category within the main creative category.']
  creative_tags varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of tags for search and categorization.']
  creative_type varchar [note: 'Category of the creative asset indicating its media form.']
  dam_reference_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the asset within the Digital Asset Management system.']
  dimensions varchar [note: 'Pixel dimensions of the creative (e.g., 1920x1080).']
  duration_seconds varchar [note: 'Length of the creative in seconds; applicable to video and audio assets.']
  expiry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time after which the creative should no longer be used.']
  file_format varchar [note: 'File format of the creative asset (e.g., mp4, jpg, png).']
  file_size_bytes varchar [note: 'Size of the creative file in bytes.']
  is_archived boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the creative has been archived and is no longer active.']
  is_dynamic boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the creative contains dynamic or personalized content.']
  language varchar [note: 'Language of the creative content.']
  last_used_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent deployment of the creative.']
  legal_approval_status varchar [note: 'Legal department approval outcome for the creative.']
  legal_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent legal approval review.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments about the creative.']
  production_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total cost incurred to produce the creative, expressed in US dollars.']
  roi_estimate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Projected return on investment percentage for the creative.']
  target_audience varchar [note: 'Primary audience segment for which the creative is designed.']
  target_market varchar [note: 'Geographic or demographic market for which the creative is intended.']
  tracking_pixel_url varchar [note: 'URL of the tracking pixel embedded in the creative for performance measurement.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the creative record.']
  usage_rights varchar [note: 'Legal usage rights associated with the creative.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number of the creative asset.']

  Note: 'Master record for each advertising creative asset used in marketing campaigns — TV spots, radio scripts, digital banner ads, social media posts, email templates, in-store POS materials, menu board graphics, and app push notifications. Captures creative name, format (video/static/audio/copy), dimensions/specs, brand compliance status, legal approval status, version number, associated campaign, channel suitability, production cost, and asset URL/DAM reference. SSOT for creative asset management.'
}

Table marketing.promotion {
  promotion_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the promotion.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Promotions are launched under a campaign; linking enables aggregation of promotion performance.']
  food_safety_audit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to foodsafety.food_safety_audit. Business justification: Promotion eligibility is gated by food safety audit status; only units with passed audit can run promotions.']
  franchisee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to franchise.franchisee. Business justification: Allows tracking of promotions originated by individual franchisees, supporting franchisee-specific performance dashboards and reimbursement of promotion costs.']
  item_category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.item_category. Business justification: Promotion eligibility is defined by item categories; linking enables promotion performance reporting per category.']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to realestate.site. Business justification: Local promotions are often site‑specific (e.g., store‑level discounts); linking allows promotion scheduling, audit, and performance tracking per restaurant site.']
  supply_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to supply.supply_supplier. Business justification: Supplier‑Sponsored Promotions require tracking which supplier funded the promotion; creates a FK to the supplier entity.']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to restaurant.unit. Business justification: Store‑specific promotions must be tied to the unit for performance tracking, local compliance, and redemption reporting.']
  applicable_channels varchar [note: 'Sales channels where the promotion can be redeemed (POS, online ordering, third‑party delivery, mobile app).']
  discount_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Fixed monetary discount applied when the promotion is used.']
  discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount applied when the promotion is used.']
  eligibility_criteria varchar [note: 'Business rules defining eligibility (e.g., purchase of specific SKU, time of day).']
  eligible_guest_segments varchar [note: 'Guest segmentation criteria (e.g., loyalty tier, new guest, student) that qualify for the promotion.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date when the promotion expires; null if open‑ended.']
  is_exclusive boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the promotion excludes other concurrent promotions.']
  is_stackable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the promotion can be combined with other promotions.']
  minimum_purchase_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Minimum order total required for the promotion to be eligible.']
  promo_category varchar [note: 'High‑level business grouping of the promotion for reporting purposes.']
  promo_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the promotion (e.g., corporate, franchisee, external partner).']
  promotion_code varchar [note: 'Business code used to reference the promotion in POS and reporting systems.']
  promotion_description varchar [note: 'Detailed marketing description of the promotion displayed to guests.']
  promotion_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the promotional offer.']
  promotion_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the promotion.']
  promotion_type varchar [note: 'Category of the promotion defining the pricing mechanic (e.g., discount, buy‑one‑get‑one).']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the promotion record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the promotion record.']
  redemption_count varchar [note: 'Running total of how many times the promotion has been redeemed.']
  redemption_limit_per_customer varchar [note: 'Maximum number of times a single guest may redeem the promotion.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date when the promotion becomes effective and can be redeemed.']
  total_redemption_limit varchar [note: 'Overall cap on the number of redemptions across all guests.']

  Note: 'Master record for all guest-facing promotional offers and their issued instruments (coupons) — including discounts, BOGOs (buy-one-get-one), combo deals, value meals, loyalty reward redemptions, digital app deals, and physical/digital coupons. Captures promotion name, type (discount/BOGO/bundle/coupon/loyalty/app-exclusive), discount value or percentage, minimum purchase threshold, channel eligibility (POS/OLO/3PD/app), guest segment targeting, redemption limits, and associated campaign. For coupon-type promotions: coupon code, coupon format (single-use/multi-use/loyalty-exclusive/app-only/printed), eligible menu items or categories, issue date, expiry date, maximum redemption count, and fraud prevention flags (velocity checks, geo-fencing). SSOT for all promotional offer mechanics and issued coupon instruments.'
}

Table marketing.promotion_redemption {
  promotion_redemption_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the promotion redemption record.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the marketing campaign linked to the promotion.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who processed the redemption.']
  guest_profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the guest who redeemed the promotion.']
  loyalty_member_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the loyalty member associated with the redemption.']
  member_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the loyalty member associated with the redemption.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the guest who redeemed the promotion.']
  promotion_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the promotion that was redeemed.']
  restaurant_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant unit where redemption occurred.']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant unit where redemption occurred.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Channel through which the redemption was captured (POS, online ordering, third‑party delivery, mobile app).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the redemption record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for monetary values.']
  daypart varchar [note: 'Meal period during which the redemption occurred.']
  device_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the POS or kiosk device that captured the redemption.']
  discount_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the discount applied.']
  discount_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount applied when discount is percent‑based.']
  discount_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the discount is a fixed amount or a percentage.']
  is_test_redemption boolean [note: 'True if the redemption was a test or training transaction.']
  loyalty_member_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the guest is a loyalty program member at the time of redemption.']
  order_value_after_discount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total order amount after applying the promotion discount.']
  order_value_before_discount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total order amount before applying the promotion discount.']
  promotion_redemption_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the redemption.']
  redemption_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the redemption event, often displayed to staff.']
  redemption_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the promotion was redeemed.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'System of record that originated the redemption data (e.g., MICROS, OLO).']
  ticket_number varchar [note: 'POS ticket or order number associated with the redemption.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the redemption record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing each instance of a guest redeeming a promotional offer at a restaurant. Captures redemption timestamp, channel (POS/OLO/3PD/app), restaurant unit, promotion redeemed, discount amount applied, order value before and after discount, guest identifier (if known), and loyalty member flag. Enables measurement of promotion uptake, discount liability, and incremental traffic driven by promotional activity.'
}

Table marketing.marketing_guest_segment {
  marketing_guest_segment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the marketing_guest_segment data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Guest segments are defined for targeting specific campaigns.']

  Note: 'Master record for marketing-defined guest segments used to target campaigns and promotions. Captures segment name, segment type (behavioral/demographic/psychographic/occasion-based/value-tier), definition criteria (visit frequency, ACV, daypart preference, channel preference, cuisine affinity), segment size estimate, data source (CRM/loyalty/POS), creation date, and active status. Distinct from loyalty tiers — these are marketing targeting constructs used for campaign audience selection and personalization.'
}

Table marketing.local_store_marketing {
  local_store_marketing_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each local store marketing initiative.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: LSM initiatives are tied to a campaign; description duplicates campaign description.']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: Needed for Local Store Marketing Materials Procurement process to track which supplier provides promotional assets for each initiative.']
  sponsor_restaurant_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant or franchise group sponsoring the initiative.']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant or franchise group sponsoring the initiative.']
  actual_adt_lift_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Measured increase in Average Daily Transactions after execution.']
  actual_comp_sales_lift_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Measured lift in comparable store sales after the initiative.']
  actual_spend decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total amount actually spent on the initiative.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the initiative received approval.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the initiative.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name of the person or role that approved the initiative.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Primary media channel used for the initiative.']
  compliance_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the initiative complies with local marketing fund rules.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for spend amounts.']
  end_date date [note: 'Date the initiative is scheduled to conclude.']
  execution_end_date date [note: 'Actual date when execution of the initiative ended.']
  execution_start_date date [note: 'Actual date when execution of the initiative started.']
  expected_adt_lift_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Projected increase in Average Daily Transactions expressed as a percentage.']
  expected_comp_sales_lift_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Projected lift in comparable store sales expressed as a percentage.']
  initiative_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier or code used to reference the initiative across systems.']
  initiative_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the marketing initiative.']
  initiative_type varchar [note: 'Category of the local marketing initiative.']
  lmf_fund_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total amount allocated to the restaurant from the Local Marketing Fund for this initiative.']
  lmf_fund_used decimal(15,2) [note: 'Portion of the allocated fund that has been spent.']
  lmf_remaining_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Remaining balance of the allocated fund after spending.']
  local_store_marketing_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the initiative.']
  market_dma varchar [note: 'Geographic market or DMA where the initiative is targeted.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments about the initiative.']
  planned_spend decimal(15,2) [note: 'Budgeted amount allocated for the initiative before execution.']
  start_date date [note: 'Date the initiative is scheduled to begin.']
  target_audience varchar [note: 'Description of the guest segment the initiative aims to reach.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']

  Note: 'Master record for Local Store Marketing (LSM) initiatives executed at the individual restaurant or market level — distinct from national/regional campaigns. Captures LSM initiative name, sponsoring restaurant(s) or franchise group, initiative type (community event, local sponsorship, school partnership, sports team tie-in, neighborhood flyer, grand opening, catering push), planned spend, actual spend, execution dates, market/DMA code, target radius, estimated impressions, measured traffic lift, Local Marketing Fund (LMF) draw-down reference, and approval status from franchisor or co-op board. Enables franchisee-level marketing activity tracking, LMF compliance, and local initiative ROI measurement.'
}

Table marketing.fund {
  fund_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the marketing fund record.']
  balance_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Current remaining balance of the fund.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of the fund with regulatory requirements.']
  contribution_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of gross sales contributed to the fund.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the fund record was created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for monetary amounts.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the fund ends or is terminated, if applicable.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the fund becomes effective.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year to which the fund data pertains.']
  fund_code varchar [note: 'External code or number identifying the marketing fund.']
  fund_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the fund purpose and scope.']
  fund_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the marketing fund.']
  fund_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the fund.']
  fund_type varchar [note: 'Category of the fund based on its scope.']
  governing_body varchar [note: 'Entity responsible for overseeing the fund.']
  is_taxable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether fund contributions are subject to tax.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form field for any supplemental information about the fund.']
  reporting_requirements varchar [note: 'Text describing mandatory reporting obligations for the fund.']
  total_contributions_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total monetary contributions collected for the fund in the fiscal period.']
  total_spend_authorized_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total amount authorized for spending from the fund.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the fund record.']

  Note: 'Master record for marketing fund accounts — including National Advertising Fund (NAF), Regional Marketing Co-ops, and Local Marketing Funds (LMF) — with full contribution transaction history. Fund-level attributes: fund name, fund type (national/regional/local), contribution rate (% of gross sales), total contributions collected in period, total spend authorized, fund balance, governing body (franchisor/co-op board), and fiscal year. Contribution transaction detail: contributing restaurant unit, contribution period (week/month), gross sales basis, contribution rate applied, contribution amount, payment date, payment method, and reconciliation status. SSOT for marketing fund financial governance, franchisee contribution compliance monitoring, co-op spend authorization, fund balance tracking, and contribution audit trail.'
}

Table marketing.fund_contribution {
  fund_contribution_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each marketing fund contribution record.']
  franchisee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to franchise.franchisee. Business justification: Enables attribution of fund contributions to the contributing franchisee, essential for fund balance reporting and franchisee-level financial statements.']
  fund_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the marketing fund to which the contribution is applied.']
  restaurant_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant unit (company‑owned or franchised) that made the contribution.']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant unit (company‑owned or franchised) that made the contribution.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the contribution was approved.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who approved the contribution.']
  contribution_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary amount contributed to the marketing fund for the period.']
  contribution_number varchar [note: 'External reference number assigned to the contribution for tracking and audit purposes.']
  contribution_period_type varchar [note: 'Granularity of the contribution period (e.g., week, month).']
  contribution_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Percentage rate applied to the gross sales to determine the contribution amount (e.g., 0.0250 = 2.5%).']
  contribution_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the contribution was generated based on sales data.']
  contribution_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the contribution based on fund scope or program.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for the contribution amount (e.g., USD).']
  gross_sales_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total sales amount (pre‑deductions) used as the basis for calculating the contribution.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current processing state of the contribution record.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text for any additional comments or exceptions related to the contribution.']
  payment_date date [note: 'Date on which the contribution amount was paid to the fund.']
  period_end_date date [note: 'Last calendar date of the contribution period.']
  period_start_date date [note: 'First calendar date of the contribution period (week or month).']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the contribution\'s financial reconciliation.']
  record_created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the contribution record was first created in the system.']
  record_updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the contribution record.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system that supplied the contribution data.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing each contribution made to a marketing fund by a restaurant unit (company-owned or franchised). Captures contributing restaurant, fund, contribution period (week/month), gross sales basis, contribution rate applied, contribution amount, payment date, and reconciliation status. Enables fund balance tracking, franchisee contribution compliance monitoring, and co-op spend authorization.'
}

Table marketing.digital_campaign_performance {
  digital_campaign_performance_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate identifier for each digital campaign performance record.']
  ad_creative_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the specific creative asset (image, video, etc.) used in the ad.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the marketing campaign to which this performance record belongs.']
  actual_spend decimal(12,2) [note: 'Actual spend incurred to date for the campaign.']
  ad_format varchar [note: 'Creative format type used in the ad.']
  ad_group_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the ad group within the campaign.']
  ad_group_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the ad group.']
  attribution_model varchar [note: 'Method used to assign credit to ad interactions.']
  audience_segment varchar [note: 'Targeted guest segment (e.g., families, millennials, high‑spenders).']
  bidding_strategy varchar [note: 'Algorithmic bidding model applied to the campaign.']
  budget_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Allocated budget for the campaign period.']
  campaign_end_date date [note: 'Planned end date of the marketing campaign.']
  campaign_goal varchar [note: 'Primary business objective the campaign is intended to achieve.']
  campaign_start_date date [note: 'Planned start date of the marketing campaign.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Digital advertising channel or network delivering the impressions (e.g., Meta, Google, TikTok, programmatic DSPs).']
  click_through_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of impressions that resulted in clicks (CTR = clicks / impressions * 100).']
  clicks varchar [note: 'Number of user clicks on the ad.']
  conversion_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of clicks that resulted in a conversion.']
  conversions varchar [note: 'Number of desired actions (app downloads, OLO orders, coupon claims) attributed to the ad.']
  cost_per_acquisition decimal(10,4) [note: 'Average cost to acquire a conversion (spend divided by conversions).']
  cost_per_click decimal(10,4) [note: 'Average cost incurred for each click (spend divided by clicks).']
  cost_per_mille decimal(10,4) [note: 'Average cost per one thousand impressions.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the performance record was first created in the data lake.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for spend values.']
  daypart varchar [note: 'Time‑of‑day segment targeted by the campaign.']
  device_type varchar [note: 'Device category on which the ad was served.']
  digital_campaign_performance_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the performance record.']
  estimated_reach varchar [note: 'Projected number of unique users who could see the ad.']
  event_date date [note: 'Calendar date on which the performance metrics were recorded.']
  frequency_average decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average number of times each user was exposed to the ad.']
  geographic_region varchar [note: 'Three‑letter country code representing the primary market for the ad.']
  impressions varchar [note: 'Number of times the ad was displayed to users.']
  is_lto boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the campaign is tied to a limited‑time offer.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations about the performance record.']
  platform varchar [note: 'Higher‑level platform grouping (e.g., social, search, display).']
  revenue_attributed decimal(12,2) [note: 'Revenue directly linked to conversions from the campaign.']
  roi_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'ROI expressed as a percentage ((revenue - spend) / spend * 100).']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the external ad platform providing the data (e.g., Meta Ads, Google Ads).']
  spend decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total monetary amount spent on the campaign for the reporting period.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the performance record.']
  video_views varchar [note: 'Number of times a video ad was viewed.']
  view_through_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of impressions that resulted in a view of the video ad.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing daily or weekly digital and social channel performance metrics for active campaigns. Captures impressions served, clicks, click-through rate (CTR), video views, view-through rate (VTR), cost per click (CPC), cost per thousand impressions (CPM), conversions (app downloads, OLO orders, coupon claims), conversion rate, total spend by channel and creative, platform-specific metrics (Meta reach, Google Quality Score, TikTok completion rate), and A/B test variant performance. Sourced from digital ad platform APIs (Meta Ads Manager, Google Ads, TikTok Ads, programmatic DSPs). Enables real-time digital media optimization, creative performance comparison, and digital ROI measurement feeding into campaign_roi.'
}

Table marketing.campaign_spend {
  campaign_spend_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each campaign spend record.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the marketing campaign to which this spend belongs.']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the vendor or agency providing the service or media.']
  vendor_procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the vendor or agency providing the service or media.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the spend.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person who approved the spend.']
  budget_line_item_code varchar [note: 'Code of the budget line item to which this spend is allocated.']
  campaign_phase varchar [note: 'Lifecycle phase of the campaign to which the spend belongs.']
  campaign_spend_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the spend record.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Primary media channel through which the spend was executed.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Internal cost center to which the spend is charged.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the spend record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the spend amount.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Any discount applied to the spend before net calculation.']
  fiscal_quarter varchar [note: 'Fiscal quarter (Q1‑Q4) for financial reporting.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year in which the spend is recorded.']
  invoice_date date [note: 'Date on the vendor invoice.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'Reference number of the vendor invoice linked to the spend.']
  is_estimated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the spend amount is an estimate (true) or actual (false).']
  is_recurring boolean [note: 'True if the spend is part of a recurring contract or schedule.']
  media_type varchar [note: 'Specific media format or vehicle used for the spend.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final net amount after tax and discount adjustments.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form comments or remarks about the spend.']
  payment_date date [note: 'Date on which payment was made to the vendor.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Method used to settle the invoice.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Status of payment against the vendor invoice.']
