  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating source system (e.g., SAP, Coupa, Salesforce) that supplied the spend record.']
  spend_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total gross amount of the spend before taxes, discounts, or adjustments.']
  spend_category varchar [note: 'Classification of spend type for budgeting and reporting.']
  spend_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description providing context for the spend.']
  spend_reference varchar [note: 'External reference code or number assigned to the spend entry by the finance system.']
  spend_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the spend was incurred or recorded.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax component applied to the spend.']
  tax_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Tax rate percentage used to calculate tax_amount.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the spend record.']
  variance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Difference between actual spend and planned budget amount (positive if over, negative if under).']
  variance_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage variance between actual spend and budget.']
  vendor_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the vendor or agency associated with the spend.']

  Note: 'Transactional record tracking actual marketing spend against a campaign, broken down by channel, vendor/agency, and time period. Captures campaign reference, spend category (media/production/agency fees/in-store materials), vendor/agency name, invoice reference, spend amount, currency, spend date, budget line item, and variance to plan. Enables campaign budget management, spend pacing, and ROI calculation. Distinct from media_buy (which is placement-level) — this is the financial spend ledger for the campaign.'
}

Table marketing.coupon {
  coupon_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the coupon record.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the marketing campaign that issued the coupon.']
  menu_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to menu.menu_item. Business justification: Required for Coupon Management: link item‑specific coupons to the menu item they discount, enabling redemption tracking, inventory impact analysis, and compliance reporting.']
  channel_restriction varchar [note: 'Sales channel(s) where the coupon is valid.']
  coupon_code varchar [note: 'Human‑readable code assigned to the coupon for issuance and redemption tracking.']
  coupon_description varchar [note: 'Full marketing description and terms of the coupon.']
  coupon_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the coupon used in marketing materials.']
  coupon_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the coupon.']
  coupon_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the coupon indicating its usage rules.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the coupon record was first created in the system.']
  discount_amount decimal(10,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the discount when discount_type is fixed_amount.']
  discount_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage value of the discount when discount_type is percentage.']
  discount_type varchar [note: 'Mechanism by which the coupon provides a discount.']
  eligible_item_category varchar [note: 'Menu category or SKU that the coupon can be applied to.']
  expiry_date date [note: 'Date after which the coupon can no longer be redeemed.']
  fraud_prevention_flag boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the coupon is subject to fraud monitoring.']
  is_exclusive boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the coupon excludes the use of other promotions.']
  is_stackable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the coupon can be used together with other promotions.']
  issue_date date [note: 'Date the coupon was generated and made available.']
  max_redemptions varchar [note: 'Total number of times the coupon may be redeemed across all customers.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for internal remarks about the coupon.']
  per_customer_limit varchar [note: 'Maximum number of redemptions allowed per individual guest.']
  redemption_count varchar [note: 'Number of times the coupon has already been redeemed.']
  redemption_window_end date [note: 'End date of the period during which the coupon may be redeemed.']
  redemption_window_start date [note: 'Start date of the period during which the coupon may be redeemed.']
  store_scope varchar [note: 'Geographic or store‑level scope where the coupon is valid.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the coupon record.']
  usage_limit_type varchar [note: 'Specifies whether the coupon has a usage limit.']

  Note: 'Master record for physical and digital coupons issued as part of marketing campaigns or standalone promotional activity. Captures coupon code, coupon type (single-use/multi-use/loyalty-exclusive/app-only/printed), face value or discount type, eligible menu items or categories, channel restrictions (POS/OLO/3PD/app), issue date, expiry date, maximum redemption count, and fraud prevention flags. Distinct from promotion (which defines the offer mechanic) — coupon is the specific issued instrument.'
}

Table marketing.influencer {
  influencer_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the influencer record.']
  average_comments_per_post varchar [note: 'Mean number of comments per post across recent campaigns.']
  average_likes_per_post varchar [note: 'Mean number of likes per post across recent campaigns.']
  average_views_per_post varchar [note: 'Mean number of video or image views per post across recent campaigns.']
  brand_safety_rating varchar [note: 'Internal rating (1‑5) of the influencer\'s brand safety risk.']
  campaigns_participated_count varchar [note: 'Total number of marketing campaigns the influencer has been involved with.']
  content_category varchar [note: 'Primary content theme of the influencer.']
  contract_end_date date [note: 'Effective end date of the influencer contract; null if open‑ended.']
  contract_start_date date [note: 'Effective start date of the influencer contract.']
  contracted_fee decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total fee agreed for the influencer\'s participation in a campaign.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the influencer record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for the contracted fee.']
  email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for communication and invoicing.']
  engagement_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average engagement rate (likes+comments)/followers expressed as a percentage.']
  exclusivity_terms varchar [note: 'Text describing any exclusivity or non-compete conditions.']
  follower_count varchar [note: 'Total number of followers/subscribers on the primary platform at the time of record capture.']
  full_name varchar [note: 'Legal full name of the influencer as used in contracts and payments.']
  influencer_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the influencer record.']
  is_exclusive boolean [note: 'True if the influencer is under an exclusivity agreement for the brand.']
  last_campaign_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent campaign the influencer participated in.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form field for any supplemental information about the influencer.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Text describing payment schedule and method (e.g., net‑30, upfront).']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for the influencer.']
  platform_handle varchar [note: 'Username or handle of the influencer on the primary platform.']
  platform_url varchar [note: 'Direct URL to the influencer\'s profile on the primary platform.']
  primary_platform varchar [note: 'Main social media platform where the influencer creates content.']
  region varchar [note: 'Primary geographic region where the influencer\'s audience is located. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USA|CAN|MEX|GBR|FRA|DEU|JPN|CHN|IND — promote to reference product]']
  tier varchar [note: 'Tier classification based on audience size and impact.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the influencer record.']
  verified_status boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the influencer\'s account is verified on the platform.']

  Note: 'Master record for social media influencers and brand ambassadors engaged by Restaurants, including full activation/engagement history. Influencer profile: name, platform(s) (Instagram, TikTok, YouTube, X), follower count by platform, engagement rate, content category (food/lifestyle/family/gaming), tier (nano/micro/macro/mega), contracted fee, exclusivity terms, brand safety rating, and active status. Activation detail: activation type (sponsored post/story/reel/video/event appearance), campaign reference, contracted deliverables, content go-live dates, platform, actual impressions delivered, engagement metrics (likes/comments/shares), earned media value (EMV), payment amount, and FTC disclosure compliance status. SSOT for influencer program management, contract fulfillment tracking, and creator-led marketing ROI measurement.'
}

Table marketing.influencer_activation {
  influencer_activation_id varchar [pk, note: 'System‑generated unique identifier for each influencer activation record.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the marketing campaign to which this activation belongs.']
  influencer_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the influencer participating in the activation.']
  activation_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable identifier assigned to the activation for tracking and reconciliation.']
  activation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the activation was executed or went live.']
  activation_type varchar [note: 'Category of influencer deliverable (e.g., sponsored post, story, reel, video, event appearance).']
  actual_comments varchar [note: 'Number of comment interactions on the influencer content.']
  actual_impressions varchar [note: 'Number of times the influencer content was displayed to users.']
  actual_likes varchar [note: 'Count of \'like\' interactions on the influencer content.']
  actual_shares varchar [note: 'Number of times the influencer content was shared or retweeted.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance verification state of the activation.']
  content_go_live_date date [note: 'Planned or actual date when the influencer content was published.']
  contract_end_date date [note: 'Date when the influencer contract expires or is terminated.']
  contract_start_date date [note: 'Date when the influencer contract became effective.']
  contract_terms varchar [note: 'Key payment and deliverable terms defined in the influencer agreement.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the activation record was first created in the system.']
  deliverables varchar [note: 'Description of the content or service the influencer agreed to provide.']
  earned_media_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated monetary value of the organic reach generated by the influencer activation.']
  ftc_disclosure_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the influencer included the required FTC disclosure on the content.']
  influencer_activation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the influencer activation.']
  influencer_category varchar [note: 'Segment classification based on audience size and relationship type.']
  influencer_engagement_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average engagement rate (percentage) of the influencer\'s audience.']
  influencer_follower_count varchar [note: 'Number of followers the influencer had at the time of contract signing.']
  influencer_handle varchar [note: 'Username or handle of the influencer on the platform.']
  influencer_region varchar [note: 'ISO 3166‑1 alpha‑3 country code representing the influencer\'s primary market.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations about the activation.']
  payment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Compensation paid to the influencer for the activation.']
  payment_currency varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for influencer payment.']
  platform varchar [note: 'Social platform where the influencer content was posted.']
  total_engagement varchar [note: 'Aggregate of likes, comments, and shares for the activation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the activation record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for each influencer engagement or activation tied to a campaign. Captures influencer reference, campaign reference, activation type (sponsored post/story/reel/video/event appearance), contracted deliverables, content go-live date, platform, actual impressions delivered, engagement (likes/comments/shares), earned media value (EMV), payment amount, and compliance status (FTC disclosure). Enables influencer ROI measurement and contract fulfillment tracking.'
}

Table marketing.campaign_roi {
  campaign_roi_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each campaign ROI record.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the marketing campaign to which this ROI measurement belongs.']
  attribution_methodology varchar [note: 'Method used to attribute incremental revenue to the campaign.']
  campaign_roi_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the ROI measurement.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Primary media channel or delivery platform used for the campaign (e.g., digital, social, TV, radio).']
  cogs_impact_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Additional cost of goods sold directly attributable to the campaign.']
  confidence_level varchar [note: 'Statistical confidence in the ROI measurement (high, medium, low).']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary values.']
  incremental_revenue decimal(18,2) [note: 'Additional revenue attributed to the campaign during the measurement period.']
  incremental_transactions varchar [note: 'Count of additional average daily transactions generated by the campaign.']
  is_test_roi boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the ROI record was generated from a test or pilot campaign.']
  market_dma varchar [note: 'Geographic market area (DMA) where the campaign was executed.']
  measurement_period_end date [note: 'Last date of the period over which ROI is measured.']
  measurement_period_start date [note: 'First date of the period over which ROI is measured.']
  measurement_source varchar [note: 'Origin of the data used for ROI calculation.']
  measurement_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact point in time when the ROI measurement was recorded.']
  net_incremental_profit decimal(18,2) [note: 'Incremental profit after subtracting COGS and other direct costs from incremental revenue.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations about the ROI measurement.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the ROI record was first created in the system.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the ROI record.']
  roi_code varchar [note: 'Human‑readable code assigned to the ROI record for tracking and reporting.']
  roi_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'ROI expressed as a percentage: (Net Incremental Profit / Total Spend) * 100.']
  spend_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount of money spent on the campaign across all channels.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version of the ROI record for audit and change tracking.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the return on investment (ROI) and comparable store sales (comp sales / SSS) lift measurement for a completed campaign, calculated at the campaign, channel, or market level. Captures total campaign spend, incremental revenue attributed, incremental transactions (ADT lift), ACV change, comp sales lift percentage, COGS impact, net incremental profit, ROI percentage, measurement period, baseline sales (pre-campaign), actual sales during campaign, attribution methodology (matched market/MMM/last-touch/test-vs-control), and confidence level. SSOT for post-campaign financial performance evaluation, comp sales attribution, and future budget allocation decisions.'
}

Table marketing.content_template {
  content_template_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for content_template']
  base_content_template_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on content_template (base_content_template_id)']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Approval workflow status for the template.']
  audience_segment varchar [note: 'Target audience segment identifier for which the template is designed.']
  channel varchar [note: 'Primary channel(s) where the template is deployed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the template record was first created in the system.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date after which the template is no longer valid (nullable for open‑ended).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date from which the template becomes valid for use.']
  format varchar [note: 'File or rendering format of the template.']
  gdpr_compliant boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the template complies with GDPR requirements.']
  is_default boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this template is the default for its type/segment.']
  language varchar [note: 'ISO 639‑1 language code of the template content.']
  last_used_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent campaign that used this template.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or remarks about the template.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the template.']
  template_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier/code used to reference the template in marketing systems.']
  template_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the content template.']
  template_type varchar [note: 'Category of the template indicating its marketing purpose.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the template record.']
  usage_count varchar [note: 'Cumulative count of times the template has been used in campaigns.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Sequential version number of the template.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for content_template. Referenced by content_template_id.'
}

Table finance.gl_account {
  gl_account_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the general ledger account record. Primary key.']
  chart_of_accounts_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the chart of accounts framework this account belongs to. Supports multiple COA structures for different regions or business units.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the legal entity (company code) to which this GL account belongs. Supports multi-entity consolidation across franchise and company-owned operations.']
  parent_account_gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent account in the account hierarchy for roll-up reporting. Null for top-level accounts. Enables multi-level account group hierarchies.']
  account_currency varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for accounts that manage balances in a specific currency (e.g., \'USD\', \'EUR\', \'GBP\'). Null for accounts managed only in company code currency.']
  account_description varchar [note: 'Detailed business description of the account purpose, usage guidelines, and what types of transactions should be posted to this account.']
  account_group varchar [note: 'SAP account group code that defines the account\'s characteristics, field status, and number range. Controls account behavior and validation rules in SAP S/4HANA.']
  account_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable short name or title of the GL account (e.g., \'Food Cost - Beef\', \'Franchise Royalty Revenue\', \'Depreciation - Equipment\').']
  account_number varchar [note: 'The externally-known unique account code used across all legal entities in the chart of accounts. Typically 6-10 digit numeric code following SAP S/4HANA GL numbering convention.']
  account_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the GL account: \'active\' for normal use, \'blocked\' for temporarily suspended, \'closed\' for permanently retired, \'pending_activation\' for newly created accounts awaiting approval.']
  account_type varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the account into one of the five fundamental financial statement categories: asset, liability, equity, revenue, or expense.']
  alternate_account_number varchar [note: 'Alternative or legacy account code used for cross-reference, migration mapping, or external reporting (e.g., mapping to previous ERP system account numbers).']
  balance_sheet_classification varchar [note: 'Specific balance sheet line item classification for asset, liability, and equity accounts. Set to \'not_applicable\' for P&L (Profit and Loss) accounts (revenue/expense).']
  cash_flow_classification varchar [note: 'Classification of this account for cash flow statement presentation: \'operating\' for operational cash flows, \'investing\' for CapEx (Capital Expenditure) and asset transactions, \'financing\' for debt and equity, \'not_applicable\' for non-cash accounts.']
  consolidation_account_number varchar [note: 'Mapped account number used in corporate consolidation reporting to standardize accounts across multiple legal entities and chart of accounts structures.']
  cost_element_category varchar [note: 'Classification of cost element: \'primary\' for costs posted from external transactions (FI to CO), \'secondary\' for internal allocations and assessments, \'not_applicable\' if not a cost element.']
  cost_element_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this GL account is also defined as a cost element in SAP Controlling (CO) module for cost center and internal order postings.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who created this GL account record. Supports accountability and audit requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this GL account record was first created in the system. Audit trail for account master data governance.']
  field_status_group varchar [note: 'SAP field status group code that controls which fields are required, optional, or suppressed when posting transactions to this account.']
  financial_statement_category varchar [note: 'Detailed sub-classification within the account type for financial statement presentation (e.g., \'Current Assets\', \'Long-Term Liabilities\', \'Operating Revenue\', \'Cost of Goods Sold (COGS)\', \'Selling General and Administrative (SG&A)\').']
  functional_area varchar [note: 'Business function or operational area this account supports (e.g., \'Restaurant Operations\', \'Franchise\', \'Supply Chain\', \'Marketing\', \'Corporate\', \'Real Estate\'). Used for segment reporting and management accounting.']
  gaap_account_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this account is used in GAAP-compliant financial statements. Supports dual-ledger reporting for companies reporting under both GAAP and IFRS.']
  ifrs_account_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this account is used in IFRS-compliant financial statements. Supports dual-ledger reporting for companies reporting under both GAAP and IFRS.']
  intercompany_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this account is used for intercompany transactions between legal entities that require elimination during consolidation.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who last modified this GL account record. Supports change tracking and accountability.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when this GL account record was last updated. Tracks changes to account master data for audit and compliance.']
  line_item_display_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether individual line item details are stored and displayed for this account. True for most operational accounts; false for summary accounts.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, special instructions, or business context about this GL account (e.g., usage guidelines, approval requirements, historical context).']
  open_item_management_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this account uses open item management for tracking and clearing individual transactions (e.g., for clearing accounts, suspense accounts).']
  planning_level varchar [note: 'Granularity level at which budget planning and forecasting is performed for this account (e.g., \'corporate\', \'region\', \'restaurant\', \'cost_center\').']
  posting_allowed_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether direct transaction postings are allowed to this account. False for summary/header accounts that only receive totals from sub-accounts.']
  profit_loss_classification varchar [note: 'Specific P&L statement line item classification for revenue and expense accounts (e.g., \'Sales Revenue\', \'COGS (Cost of Goods Sold)\', \'Labor Cost\', \'Occupancy Cost\', \'EBITDA (Earnings Before Interest Taxes Depreciation and Amortization)\'). Null for balance sheet accounts.']
  reconciliation_account_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this is a reconciliation account (subledger control account) that summarizes detailed transactions from AP (Accounts Payable), AR (Accounts Receivable), or FA (Fixed Assets) subledgers.']
  segment_reporting_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether transactions posted to this account are included in segment reporting for business unit, geographic, or product line analysis.']
  sort_key varchar [note: 'SAP sort key that determines the default sorting and grouping of line items posted to this account (e.g., by posting date, document number, or business partner).']
  statistical_account_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this is a statistical account used for tracking non-monetary quantities (e.g., headcount, transaction counts, cover counts) without affecting financial balances.']
  tax_category varchar [note: 'Tax treatment classification for this account (e.g., \'taxable_revenue\', \'non_taxable_revenue\', \'input_tax\', \'output_tax\', \'exempt\'). Used for sales tax, VAT, and GST reporting.']
  valid_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this GL account becomes active and available for transaction posting. Supports time-dependent account master data.']
  valid_to_date date [note: 'Date until which this GL account remains active. Null for accounts with no planned end date. Used for account retirement and historical reporting.']

  Note: 'Chart of accounts master record for the general ledger in SAP S/4HANA (CO/GL modules). Defines every account code used across all legal entities, including P&L accounts, balance sheet accounts, cost element accounts, and statistical accounts. Supports multi-entity consolidation, GAAP/IFRS dual-ledger reporting, and account group hierarchies. Each record carries account type (asset, liability, equity, revenue, expense), functional area, account group, and currency settings.'
}

Table finance.cost_center {
  cost_center_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the cost center. Primary key.']
  franchisee_id varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of the franchisee who owns/operates this cost center. Nullable for company-owned units. Links to franchise agreement, royalty rate, and franchise fee structures in FranConnect.']
  hierarchy_node_id varchar [note: 'The identifier of the node in the cost center standard hierarchy. Used to roll up costs from individual units to regions to divisions to corporate totals for consolidated P&L reporting.']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the physical location or restaurant unit profile. Links cost center to real estate, address, and site selection data. Nullable for non-location-based cost centers (e.g., corporate departments).']
  territory_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to franchise.territory. Business justification: Cost allocation and budgeting are performed per territory; linking cost_center to territory enables territory‑level expense reporting.']
  brand_code varchar [note: 'The brand identifier for multi-brand restaurant portfolios. Examples: QSR1, CASUAL2, FINE3. Used for brand-level P&L reporting, brand standard compliance, and marketing campaign segmentation.']
  budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The approved annual budget amount for this cost center in local currency. Used for variance analysis, Labor% and COGS% target setting, and OpEx control. Updated during annual budgeting cycles and mid-year reforecasts.']
  budget_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year for which the budget_amount is applicable. Enables multi-year budget tracking and year-over-year variance analysis.']
  business_area_code varchar [note: 'The SAP business area classification. Used to segment financial reporting by line of business (e.g., QSR, casual dining, catering, franchise operations) for multi-brand or multi-format portfolios.']
  capex_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this cost center is eligible to receive capital expenditure allocations. True for restaurant units (equipment, R&M), distribution centers, and major corporate facilities. False for pure administrative departments. Used to distinguish CapEx from OpEx in financial reporting.']
  cogs_percent_target decimal(5,2) [note: 'The target COGS% (cost of goods sold as a percentage of revenue) for this cost center. Critical KPI for food cost management. Typical QSR targets range from 25-35%. Used for menu engineering, pricing strategy, and supplier negotiations.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'The SAP company code (legal entity) to which this cost center belongs. Used for GAAP/IFRS financial statement consolidation and statutory reporting. Company-owned units vs franchise entities are distinguished here.']
  controlling_area_code varchar [note: 'The SAP Controlling Area to which this cost center belongs. Controlling areas represent the highest level of cost accounting organization, typically aligned with legal entities or major geographic regions for multi-entity consolidation.']
  cost_center_category varchar [note: 'High-level categorization of the cost center\'s role. Revenue-generating centers (FOH, BOH) directly contribute to sales; support centers (maintenance, training) enable operations; administrative centers (HR, finance) provide governance; overhead centers (facilities, utilities) are indirect; capital project centers track CapEx initiatives.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'The externally-known alphanumeric code for the cost center as defined in SAP S/4HANA Controlling module. Used in financial reporting and P&L statements.']
  cost_center_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description providing additional context about this cost center\'s purpose, scope, or special characteristics. Used for documentation and reporting clarity.']
  cost_center_name varchar [note: 'The full business name of the cost center (e.g., \'Store #1234 - Downtown Chicago\', \'Regional Office - Midwest\', \'Corporate HR Department\').']
  cost_center_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the cost center. Active centers accept cost postings; inactive centers are temporarily disabled; pending centers are awaiting approval; closed centers are permanently retired; suspended centers are on hold; planned centers are scheduled for future activation (e.g., NRO pipeline).']
  cost_center_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the cost center by organizational function. Restaurant units are individual QSR locations; regional offices manage geographic territories; corporate departments are headquarters functions; shared service centers provide centralized support; distribution centers handle supply chain; franchise support manages franchisee relations.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code where this cost center operates. Used for multi-country consolidation, currency translation, and jurisdiction-specific compliance (FDA, USDA, local health departments).']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'The user ID of the person who created this cost center record. Used for audit trails and accountability.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this cost center record was first created in the system. Used for audit trails and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for this cost center\'s local currency. Used for budget planning, cost allocation, and multi-currency consolidation in GAAP/IFRS financial statements.']
  drive_thru_lanes varchar [note: 'The number of drive-thru lanes at this location. Nullable for non-DT formats. Critical for SOS (Speed of Service) analysis, throughput capacity planning, and DT-specific cost allocation.']
  format_code varchar [note: 'The restaurant format classification. Examples: DT (Drive-Thru), DINE_IN, KIOSK, FOOD_COURT, AIRPORT, GHOST_KITCHEN. Used for format-specific cost benchmarking, throughput analysis, and real estate site selection.']
  franchise_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this cost center represents a franchise-owned unit (true) or a company-owned unit (false). Critical for revenue recognition (royalty income vs direct sales), P&L consolidation, and franchise compliance tracking.']
  functional_area_code varchar [note: 'The functional area classification for cross-company-code reporting. Typical values include operations, marketing, R&D, administration, IT, finance. Used for functional P&L views and OpEx analysis.']
  labor_percent_target decimal(5,2) [note: 'The target Labor% (labor cost as a percentage of revenue) for this cost center. Critical KPI for restaurant operations. Typical QSR targets range from 20-30%. Used for workforce scheduling optimization and P&L performance evaluation.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'The user ID of the person who last modified this cost center record. Used for audit trails and change accountability.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this cost center record was last updated. Used for change tracking and data freshness validation.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for operational notes, special instructions, or historical context. Examples: \'Undergoing renovation Q2 2024\', \'Pilot location for new POS system\', \'Seasonal closure Nov-Mar\'.']
  opex_allocation_method varchar [note: 'The method used to allocate shared operating expenses to this cost center. Direct costs are posted directly; allocated methods distribute corporate overhead based on revenue, headcount, square footage, transaction volume, or fixed percentages. Used for full-absorption P&L reporting.']
  parent_cost_center_code varchar [note: 'The cost center code of the immediate parent in the organizational hierarchy. Nullable for top-level cost centers. Enables recursive hierarchy traversal for regional and corporate rollups.']
  profit_center_code varchar [note: 'The profit center assignment for this cost center. Profit centers are used for internal P&L reporting and EBITDA tracking. Restaurant units typically map 1:1 to profit centers; corporate departments may roll up to shared profit centers.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'The geographic region code for this cost center. Used for regional P&L rollups, SSS (Same-Store Sales) analysis, and regional manager accountability. Examples: NEAST, SWEST, MIDWEST, INTL.']
  seating_capacity varchar [note: 'The number of customer seats available at this cost center location. Nullable for non-dine-in formats (drive-thru only, ghost kitchens). Used for cover count analysis, table turn calculations, and throughput benchmarking.']
  square_footage varchar [note: 'The total interior square footage of this cost center facility. Used for OpEx allocation (utilities, CAM charges), productivity metrics (sales per square foot), and real estate valuation.']
  valid_from_date date [note: 'The date from which this cost center becomes effective and can accept cost postings. Aligns with NRO (New Restaurant Opening) dates for restaurant units or organizational change effective dates for corporate centers.']
  valid_to_date date [note: 'The date through which this cost center remains effective. Nullable for open-ended cost centers. Populated when a restaurant closes, a department is dissolved, or a regional office is consolidated.']

  Note: 'Organizational cost center master data and cost allocation activity from SAP S/4HANA CO module. Represents the smallest unit of cost accountability — individual restaurant units, regional offices, corporate departments, and shared service centers. Master data tracks cost center type (restaurant, regional, corporate), responsible manager, controlling area, profit center assignment, valid-from/to dates, and hierarchy node. Allocation activity captures periodic distributions of shared costs (corporate overhead, IT, HR, marketing fund) using defined allocation keys (headcount, revenue, square footage), including sender/receiver relationships, allocation cycles, allocation rules, basis amounts, and reversal indicators. Enables fully-loaded restaurant P&L, Labor%/COGS%/OpEx allocation, accurate EBITDA by unit, and transparent overhead burden rates for franchise vs. company-owned comparisons.'
}

Table finance.profit_center {
  profit_center_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the profit center. Primary key for the profit center master record in SAP S/4HANA EC-PCA module.']
  franchisee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to franchise.franchisee. Business justification: Consolidated profit‑center financial reporting per franchisee needs a direct FK; the business runs profit‑center performance dashboards by franchisee.']
  hierarchy_node_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the node in the profit center standard hierarchy. Used for roll-up reporting and multi-level P&L consolidation.']
  parent_profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent profit center in the organizational hierarchy. Enables recursive roll-up for regional and brand-level P&L aggregation.']
  responsible_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Required for profit center manager assignment used in monthly P&L reporting to attribute financial results to a responsible employee.']
  restaurant_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the physical restaurant unit if this profit center represents a single location. Null for aggregated profit centers (regional, brand-level).']
  aov_target_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Target annual revenue for this profit center expressed in the profit center\'s functional currency. Used for AUV benchmarking and performance variance analysis.']
  brand_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the restaurant brand or concept associated with this profit center (e.g., QSR Brand A, Casual Dining Brand B). Supports brand-level AUV and SSS reporting.']
  business_area_code varchar [note: 'SAP business area code for cross-company-code reporting. Enables consolidated P&L views across multiple legal entities within the same business segment.']
  closure_date date [note: 'Date the profit center ceased operations. Null for active profit centers. Used for historical P&L analysis and closed-unit portfolio reporting.']
  closure_reason varchar [note: 'Business reason for profit center closure (e.g., lease expiration, underperformance, market exit, franchise termination). Used for portfolio optimization analysis.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'SAP company code representing the legal entity to which this profit center belongs. Used for external financial reporting and GAAP/IFRS compliance.']
  consolidation_unit_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the consolidation unit for multi-entity financial consolidation. Used in GAAP/IFRS-compliant consolidated financial statement preparation.']
  controlling_area_code varchar [note: 'SAP controlling area to which this profit center is assigned. Controlling area represents the organizational unit for cost accounting and internal reporting.']
  cost_center_group_code varchar [note: 'Code of the cost center group associated with this profit center. Links profit center P&L to underlying cost center actuals for COGS and OpEx analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this profit center record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for P&L reporting (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). Determines the functional currency for this profit center\'s financial transactions.']
  ebitda_target_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Target EBITDA for this profit center expressed in the profit center\'s functional currency. Used for profitability benchmarking and executive performance evaluation.']
  geographic_region_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the geographic region (e.g., Northeast, Southwest, EMEA, APAC). Used for regional P&L consolidation and market performance analysis.']
  last_modified_by_user varchar [note: 'Username or identifier of the user who last modified this profit center record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this profit center record was last updated. Used for change tracking and data quality monitoring.']
  lock_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the profit center is locked for transaction posting. Locked centers cannot receive new P&L entries but remain visible for historical reporting.']
  marketing_fund_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Marketing fund contribution rate percentage for franchise profit centers. Represents the percentage of gross sales contributed to the brand marketing fund.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes and comments about the profit center. Used to document special circumstances, organizational changes, or reporting exceptions.']
  opening_date date [note: 'Date the profit center commenced operations. Used to calculate operating tenure for SSS eligibility and maturity-based performance benchmarking.']
  ownership_model varchar [note: 'Ownership structure of the profit center. Determines revenue recognition treatment, royalty income accounting, and CapEx/OpEx allocation rules.']
  profit_center_category varchar [note: 'Functional category of the profit center. Operating centers generate direct revenue; non-operating centers represent overhead or shared services allocated to operating units.']
  profit_center_code varchar [note: 'Business identifier code for the profit center as defined in SAP controlling area. Used in financial reporting and P&L statements.']
  profit_center_name varchar [note: 'Full descriptive name of the profit center (e.g., \'Downtown Chicago QSR Unit 1234\', \'Northeast Regional Franchise Group\', \'Company-Owned Casual Dining Segment\').']
  profit_center_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the profit center. Active centers participate in P&L reporting; inactive centers are excluded from current period consolidation.']
  royalty_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Royalty rate percentage applied to franchise profit centers. Represents the percentage of gross sales remitted to the franchisor as ongoing royalty income.']
  segment_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this profit center is a reportable segment under GAAP ASC 280 or IFRS 8. Reportable segments require separate disclosure in external financial statements.']
  segment_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the profit center by business segment type. Determines the level of P&L aggregation and reporting hierarchy.']
  short_name varchar [note: 'Abbreviated name or alias for the profit center used in reports and dashboards for space-constrained displays.']
  sss_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this profit center is included in Same-Store Sales (SSS) / Comparable Store Sales (Comp Sales) calculations. Typically requires 12+ months of operating history.']
  tax_jurisdiction_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the primary tax jurisdiction for this profit center. Determines applicable sales tax, VAT, and income tax treatment in P&L reporting.']
  valid_from_date date [note: 'Effective start date for this profit center record. Profit center becomes active for P&L posting and reporting from this date forward.']
  valid_to_date date [note: 'Effective end date for this profit center record. Profit center is excluded from P&L posting and reporting after this date. Null for open-ended validity.']

  Note: 'Profit center master record from SAP S/4HANA EC-PCA module. Represents a business segment for internal P&L reporting — individual restaurant units, brand segments, geographic regions, or franchise vs. company-owned groupings. Supports SSS (Same-Store Sales) reporting, AUV (Average Unit Volume) tracking, and EBITDA decomposition by segment. Carries hierarchy assignment, controlling area, and responsible executive.'
}

Table finance.legal_entity {
  legal_entity_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the legal entity record. Primary key.']
  chart_of_accounts_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the chart of accounts assigned to this legal entity in SAP S/4HANA FI. Defines the GL account structure for financial postings.']
  parent_entity_legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the parent legal entity in the corporate hierarchy. Null for the ultimate parent company.']
  primary_ultimate_parent_entity_legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the top-level parent entity in the corporate structure. Used for ultimate consolidation and group reporting.']
  accounting_standard varchar [note: 'Primary accounting framework used for financial statement preparation. GAAP for US entities, IFRS for international, or local statutory standards.']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'First line of the legal entity\'s registered office address as filed with corporate registry.']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the legal entity\'s registered office address (suite, floor, building name).']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality of the legal entity\'s registered office.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'Four-character alphanumeric code uniquely identifying the legal entity in SAP S/4HANA FI. Used as the organizational unit for financial accounting and external reporting.']
  consolidation_group_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the consolidation group or reporting segment this entity belongs to for multi-entity financial consolidation.']
  consolidation_method varchar [note: 'Method used to consolidate this entity\'s financial results into group reporting. Full for subsidiaries, equity for associates, none for non-consolidated entities.']
  controlling_area varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA organizational unit for cost accounting, internal reporting, and profitability analysis. May span multiple company codes.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the legal entity\'s registered office address.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this legal entity record was first created in the system.']
  credit_control_area varchar [note: 'SAP S/4HANA organizational unit for managing customer credit limits and credit exposure across one or more company codes.']
  dissolution_date date [note: 'Date the legal entity was officially dissolved or ceased operations. Null for active entities.']
  duns_number varchar [note: 'Nine-digit unique identifier assigned by Dun & Bradstreet for business credit and supplier management.']
  effective_from_date date [note: 'Date from which this legal entity record is effective for financial posting and reporting. Used for time-dependent organizational changes.']
  effective_to_date date [note: 'Date until which this legal entity record is effective. Null for currently active entities. Used for historical tracking of organizational changes.']
  entity_status varchar [note: 'Current operational and legal status of the entity. Active entities post transactions; inactive entities are retained for historical reporting.']
  entity_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the legal entity structure. Determines ownership model, consolidation treatment, and reporting obligations.']
  fiscal_year_end_month varchar [note: 'Month number (1-12) representing the end of the entity\'s fiscal year. 12 = December for calendar year entities.']
  fiscal_year_variant varchar [note: 'Code defining the fiscal year calendar structure (e.g., calendar year, 4-4-5 retail calendar, 52/53 week year) used by this entity.']
  incorporation_date date [note: 'Date the legal entity was officially registered or incorporated with the governing jurisdiction.']
  intercompany_clearing_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this entity participates in automated intercompany clearing and reconciliation processes for intra-group transactions.']
  jurisdiction_country varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code of the jurisdiction where the entity is incorporated and legally domiciled.']
  jurisdiction_state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or sub-national jurisdiction where the entity is registered. Relevant for federal systems (USA, Canada, etc.).']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this legal entity record was last updated.']
  legal_name varchar [note: 'Full registered legal name of the entity as filed with government authorities. Used on official financial statements, tax filings, and legal contracts.']
  lei_code varchar [note: '20-character alphanumeric Legal Entity Identifier issued by the Global LEI Foundation for financial transaction reporting and regulatory compliance.']
  local_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code representing the entity\'s functional currency for financial reporting and local statutory books.']
  ownership_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of equity ownership held by the parent entity. 100.00 for wholly-owned subsidiaries, less for joint ventures or minority interests.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code of the legal entity\'s registered office.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary business contact for the legal entity.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary business contact or registered agent for the legal entity.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the primary business contact for the legal entity.']
  profit_center_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether profit center assignment is mandatory for all financial postings in this legal entity. True enforces segment reporting compliance.']
  registration_number varchar [note: 'Official registration or incorporation number assigned by the jurisdiction\'s corporate registry.']
  reporting_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code used for consolidated group reporting. Typically USD for US-based parent companies.']
  segment_reporting_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this entity is subject to segment reporting requirements under GAAP ASC 280 or IFRS 8.']
  short_name varchar [note: 'Abbreviated or trade name used for internal reporting and operational reference.']
  state_province varchar [note: 'State, province, or region of the legal entity\'s registered office.']
  tax_identification_number varchar [note: 'Government-issued tax identifier (EIN in USA, VAT number in EU, etc.) used for tax filing and reporting.']
  vat_registration_number varchar [note: 'VAT or GST registration number for entities operating in jurisdictions with value-added tax systems. Used for tax reporting and invoice compliance.']

  Note: 'Master record for each legal entity (company code) within the Restaurants corporate structure and intercompany transaction activity from SAP S/4HANA FI. Covers company-owned operating entities, holding companies, franchise subsidiaries, and joint ventures across all geographies. Entity master stores fiscal year variant, chart of accounts assignment, local currency, tax jurisdiction, GAAP/IFRS reporting standard, and consolidation group membership. Intercompany activity captures management fee charges, shared service allocations, intercompany loans, cross-entity food supply transfers, netting status, elimination flags, and reconciliation periods. Foundation for multi-entity financial consolidation, intercompany elimination in consolidated financial statements, and segment reporting.'
}

Table finance.journal_entry {
  journal_entry_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the journal entry record. Primary key for the journal entry header in SAP S/4HANA FI-GL.']
  approver_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'SAP user ID of the person who approved this journal entry for posting. Populated only for entries requiring approval workflow. Used for segregation of duties compliance.']
  approver_user_id varchar [note: 'SAP user ID of the person who approved this journal entry for posting. Populated only for entries requiring approval workflow. Used for segregation of duties compliance.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'SAP user ID of the person or system account that posted this journal entry. Used for audit trail and segregation of duties monitoring.']
  last_modified_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'SAP user ID of the person who last modified this journal entry header (e.g., added notes, changed status). Blank if never modified after initial posting.']
  primary_journal_employee_id varchar [note: 'SAP user ID of the person or system account that posted this journal entry. Used for audit trail and segregation of duties monitoring.']
  adjustment_period_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this journal entry was posted in a special adjustment period (periods 13-16) for year-end close. True if adjustment period; False if regular period.']
  approval_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the journal entry was approved. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Null if no approval required or not yet approved.']
  audit_class varchar [note: 'Classification of the journal entry for audit sampling and risk assessment. Values: standard (routine posting), high_risk (unusual or large amount), manual_adjustment (manual journal entry requiring scrutiny), system_generated (automated posting).']
  baseline_payment_date date [note: 'The baseline date from which payment terms are calculated (typically document date or posting date). Used to determine net due date and discount periods. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  batch_input_session varchar [note: 'Name of the batch input session if this journal entry was posted via batch processing (e.g., mass upload, automated interface). Blank for manually posted entries.']
  clearing_date date [note: 'The date on which this journal entry was cleared. Populated only for cleared documents. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  clearing_document_number varchar [note: 'The document number of the clearing document if this journal entry has been cleared (e.g., open item cleared by payment or offsetting entry). Blank if not cleared.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'Four-character alphanumeric code identifying the legal entity within the SAP organizational structure. Represents the smallest organizational unit for which a complete self-contained set of accounts can be drawn up for purposes of external reporting.']
  consolidation_transaction_type varchar [note: 'Three-character code classifying the journal entry for consolidation purposes (e.g., 100=standard posting, 200=intercompany elimination, 300=currency translation adjustment, 400=consolidation adjustment). Used in multi-entity financial consolidation.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the document currency (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP). All line item amounts in this journal entry are denominated in this currency.']
  document_date date [note: 'The date printed on the source document (invoice date, receipt date, contract date). May differ from posting date. Used for aging analysis and payment terms calculation. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  document_header_text varchar [note: 'Free-text description or memo entered at the document header level. Provides business context for the journal entry (e.g., \'Monthly depreciation run\', \'Accrual for Q4 marketing expenses\').']
  document_number varchar [note: 'Ten-digit accounting document number assigned by SAP upon posting. The externally-known unique identifier for this journal entry within the company code and fiscal year. Used for audit trail and cross-reference.']
  document_type varchar [note: 'Two-character code classifying the nature of the accounting document (e.g., SA=GL account document, KR=vendor invoice, DR=customer invoice, AB=accounting document, AA=asset posting). Controls number ranges and posting keys.']
  entry_date date [note: 'The calendar date on which the document was entered into the SAP system by the user. Used for audit trail and processing time analysis. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  exchange_rate decimal(15,5) [note: 'The exchange rate applied to convert document currency to local currency at the time of posting. Expressed as local currency per one unit of document currency.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Numeric posting period within the fiscal year (1-12 for regular periods, 13-16 for special/adjustment periods). Used for monthly P&L reporting and period-end close.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Four-digit fiscal year in which the document is posted. Determines the accounting period structure and year-end closing scope.']
  intercompany_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this journal entry involves intercompany transactions between multiple legal entities within the corporate group. True if intercompany; False otherwise.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time of the last modification to this journal entry header. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Null if never modified.']
  ledger_group varchar [note: 'Two-character code identifying the ledger in which this entry is posted (e.g., 0L=leading ledger for GAAP, 2L=IFRS ledger, 3L=management ledger). Supports parallel accounting for multiple reporting standards.']
  local_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the company code local currency. Used for statutory reporting and consolidation.']
  net_due_date date [note: 'The calculated due date for payment based on payment terms and baseline date. Used for cash flow forecasting and AP/AR aging. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  parked_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this journal entry is in parked status (saved but not yet posted). True if parked; False if posted. Parked documents do not update GL balances until posted.']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'Four-character code defining payment terms for vendor invoices or customer invoices (e.g., net 30, 2/10 net 30). Used to calculate due dates and cash discount eligibility.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The date on which the journal entry is posted to the general ledger. Determines the fiscal period assignment and is the primary date for financial reporting. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  posting_key varchar [note: 'Two-digit numeric code that controls the type of posting (debit or credit) and the account type (e.g., 40=debit GL, 50=credit GL, 01=debit customer, 31=debit vendor). Determines field status and processing logic.']
  posting_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact date and time when the journal entry was posted to the general ledger. Format: yyyy-MM-ddTHH:mm:ss.SSSXXX. Used for audit trail and real-time reporting.']
  reference_document_number varchar [note: 'Free-text field for external reference numbers such as vendor invoice number, purchase order number, or intercompany document reference. Used for reconciliation and audit trail.']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'Boolean flag indicating whether this journal entry is a reversal of a prior posting. True if this document reverses another document; False otherwise.']
  reversal_reason_code varchar [note: 'Two-character code explaining why the document was reversed (e.g., 01=reversal in current period, 02=reversal in next period, 03=error correction). Populated only for reversal documents.']
  reversed_document_number varchar [note: 'The document number of the original journal entry that this entry reverses. Populated only when reversal_indicator is True. Maintains audit trail for accrual reversals and error corrections.']
  source_system_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the originating system for this journal entry (e.g., SAP_ECC, SAP_S4, LEGACY_GL, COUPA, WORKDAY). Used for data lineage and interface reconciliation.']
  tax_reporting_date date [note: 'The date used for tax reporting purposes (may differ from posting date based on jurisdiction rules). Used for VAT/GST returns and sales tax filings. Format: yyyy-MM-dd.']
  trading_partner_company_code varchar [note: 'Four-character company code of the intercompany trading partner. Populated only when intercompany_indicator is True. Used for intercompany reconciliation and elimination entries.']
  transaction_code varchar [note: 'SAP transaction code (T-code) used to create this journal entry (e.g., FB01, FB50, F-02). Indicates the posting method and user interface path.']
  workflow_status varchar [note: 'Current state of the journal entry in the approval workflow. Values: draft (initial entry), pending_approval (submitted for review), approved (authorized but not posted), posted (final state in GL), rejected (not approved), cancelled (voided before posting).']

  Note: 'General ledger journal entry from SAP S/4HANA FI-GL, encompassing both header and line-level detail. Captures every financial posting across all legal entities — accruals, reversals, intercompany eliminations, period-end adjustments, and manual corrections. Header stores document type, posting date, fiscal period, reference document, ledger (leading/non-leading), currency, and posting user. Each line carries GL account, cost center, profit center, functional area, business area, debit/credit amount in document and local currency, tax code, assignment field, and line item text. The authoritative audit trail for all GL movements supporting GAAP/IFRS compliance, P&L drill-down, balance sheet construction, intercompany reconciliation, and external audit evidence.'
}

Table finance.journal_entry_line {
  journal_entry_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the journal entry line item. Primary key for granular GL transaction tracking.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Link line to cost_center master to normalize cost center details.']
  customer_profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer master record if this line represents an accounts receivable transaction. Null for non-AR entries.']
  fixed_asset_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the fixed asset master record if this line represents a fixed asset transaction (acquisition, depreciation, disposal). Null for non-FA entries.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Replace gl_account_code with authoritative FK to gl_account for detailed account attributes.']
  journal_entry_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent journal entry header. Links this line to the overall accounting document.']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor master record if this line represents an accounts payable transaction. Null for non-AP entries.']
  profile_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the customer master record if this line represents an accounts receivable transaction. Null for non-AR entries.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.profit_center. Business justification: Link line to profit_center master for consistent profit center data.']
  vendor_procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the vendor master record if this line represents an accounts payable transaction. Null for non-AP entries.']
  amount_document_currency decimal(18,2) [note: 'Transaction amount in the original document currency. Used for multi-currency transactions before conversion to local currency.']
  amount_local_currency decimal(18,2) [note: 'Transaction amount converted to the local reporting currency of the legal entity. Used for consolidated financial statements.']
  assignment_field varchar [note: 'Free-text assignment field for additional reference information. Often used for invoice numbers, PO numbers, or internal tracking codes.']
  baseline_date date [note: 'Baseline date for payment terms calculation. Typically the invoice date or goods receipt date.']
  business_area_code varchar [note: 'Business area for segment reporting. May represent franchise vs. company-owned, or QSR vs. casual dining divisions.']
  clearing_date date [note: 'Date on which this open item was cleared (paid or settled). Null if still open.']
  clearing_document_number varchar [note: 'Document number of the clearing entry that settled this open item. Null if the line item is still open.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'Four-character company code representing the legal entity. Required for multi-entity consolidation and statutory reporting.']
  debit_credit_indicator varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this line is a debit (D) or credit (C) entry. Fundamental to double-entry bookkeeping.']
  document_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the document currency (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  document_date date [note: 'Date of the source document (invoice date, receipt date). May differ from posting date for backdated or future-dated entries.']
  due_date date [note: 'Payment due date for AP/AR line items. Calculated based on posting date and payment terms. Null for non-payable/receivable entries.']
  entry_date date [note: 'Date on which the journal entry was created in the system. Audit trail for data entry timing.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Currency exchange rate applied to convert document currency to local currency. Null if currencies are the same.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal period (month) within the fiscal year. Typically 1-12 for monthly periods, or 1-13 for year-end adjustments.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year to which this line item is posted. Derived from the posting date and fiscal calendar configuration.']
  functional_area_code varchar [note: 'Functional area classification for cost-of-sales accounting. Distinguishes COGS, labor, R&M, marketing, and administrative expenses.']
  line_item_text varchar [note: 'Descriptive text explaining the purpose or nature of this journal entry line. Provides business context for the transaction.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line number within the journal entry. Determines the order of debit and credit entries.']
  local_currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for the local reporting currency.']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'Payment terms code for AP/AR line items. Defines due date calculation and discount terms.']
  posting_date date [note: 'Date on which this line item was posted to the general ledger. Determines the fiscal period assignment.']
  posting_key varchar [note: 'Two-digit posting key that controls the account type (GL, vendor, customer, asset) and debit/credit indicator. SAP-specific control field.']
  reference_document_number varchar [note: 'External reference document number (e.g., vendor invoice number, customer receipt number). Links GL entry to source transaction.']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this line item is a reversal entry. True if this line reverses a prior posting.']
  reversed_document_number varchar [note: 'Document number of the original entry that this line reverses. Null if not a reversal.']
  special_gl_indicator varchar [note: 'Single-character code for special GL transactions (e.g., down payments, guarantees, bills of exchange). Blank for standard postings.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Tax amount calculated for this line item in local currency. Null if the line is non-taxable.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax code applied to this line item. Determines sales tax, VAT, or GST treatment for revenue and expense transactions.']
  trading_partner_code varchar [note: 'Trading partner code for intercompany transactions. Identifies the counterparty legal entity for consolidation and elimination entries.']
  transaction_code varchar [note: 'SAP transaction code (T-code) used to create this entry (e.g., FB01, FB50, MIRO). Audit trail for process identification.']
  user_name varchar [note: 'SAP user ID of the person who created this journal entry line. Audit trail for accountability.']
  value_date date [note: 'Value date for cash management and bank reconciliation. Represents the effective date funds are available or debited.']

  Note: 'Individual debit/credit line items within a GL journal entry from SAP S/4HANA FI-GL. Each line carries GL account, cost center, profit center, functional area, business area, amount in document and local currency, tax code, assignment field, and line item text. Enables granular P&L and balance sheet drill-down, cost center reporting, and intercompany reconciliation at the line level.'
}

Table finance.ap_invoice {
  ap_invoice_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the accounts payable invoice record. Primary key.']
  approver_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who approved the invoice for payment. Used for audit trail and segregation of duties compliance.']
  campaign_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to marketing.campaign. Business justification: Allows AP invoices for marketing media purchases to be tied to the originating campaign, supporting financial reconciliation and campaign ROI calculations.']
  created_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'The user ID of the person who created the invoice record in the system. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who approved the invoice for payment. Used for audit trail and segregation of duties compliance.']
  modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'The user ID of the person who last modified the invoice record. Used for audit trail and change accountability.']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the supplier or vendor who issued this invoice. Links to vendor master data.']
  restaurant_location_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the restaurant location or unit to which this invoice expense is allocated. Used for unit-level P&L and Average Unit Volume (AUV) analysis.']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier for the restaurant location or unit to which this invoice expense is allocated. Used for unit-level P&L and Average Unit Volume (AUV) analysis.']
  vendor_procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier for the supplier or vendor who issued this invoice. Links to vendor master data.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date the invoice was approved for payment. Used for tracking approval cycle time and compliance with payment policies.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Status of the invoice approval workflow. Indicates whether the invoice has been reviewed and approved for payment by authorized personnel.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'Four-character code representing the legal entity or company within the enterprise for which this invoice is recorded. Used for multi-entity consolidation and P&L reporting.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'The cost center to which the invoice expense is allocated. Used for departmental cost tracking and management reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the invoice record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and record lifecycle tracking.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the invoice amounts (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total discount amount applied to the invoice, including early payment discounts, volume discounts, or promotional allowances.']
  due_date date [note: 'The date by which payment is due to the vendor per the payment terms. Used for cash flow forecasting and aging analysis.']
  dunning_level varchar [note: 'The dunning level or escalation stage for overdue invoices. Zero indicates no dunning. Higher numbers indicate escalated collection efforts.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Exchange rate used to convert foreign currency invoice amounts to the company code local currency. Null for invoices in local currency.']
  expense_category varchar [note: 'High-level categorization of the invoice expense type. Used for Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) percentage and Operating Expense (OpEx) tracking.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period (month) within the fiscal year in which the invoice was posted. Used for monthly financial close and period-over-period analysis.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year in which the invoice was posted, derived from the posting date. Used for annual financial reporting and year-over-year analysis.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'The general ledger account code to which the invoice expense is posted. Used for P&L reporting and financial statement preparation.']
  goods_receipt_number varchar [note: 'The goods receipt document number confirming physical receipt of goods or services. Used for three-way matching.']
  gross_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total invoice amount before any deductions, including line item charges and taxes. Represents the full amount billed by the vendor.']
  invoice_date date [note: 'The date the vendor issued the invoice. This is the principal business event timestamp for the invoice transaction.']
  invoice_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description or memo field providing additional context about the invoice, such as the nature of goods or services purchased.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'The vendor-assigned invoice number as printed on the supplier invoice document. This is the externally-known business identifier for the invoice.']
  invoice_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the invoice in the accounts payable workflow.']
  invoice_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the invoice document type (standard invoice, credit memo for returns, debit memo for additional charges, prepayment, or recurring invoice).']
  local_currency_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net invoice amount converted to the company code local currency using the exchange rate. Used for consolidated financial reporting.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the invoice record was last modified. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net amount payable to the vendor after applying discounts and adjustments. This is the amount that will be paid.']
  payment_block_indicator varchar [note: 'Code indicating whether payment is blocked and the reason for the block (e.g., pricing dispute, quantity variance, quality issue). Empty if no block is active.']
  payment_date date [note: 'The date the invoice was actually paid to the vendor. Null if the invoice is unpaid.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'The payment instrument or method used to pay the invoice (check, ACH, wire transfer, credit card, cash).']
  payment_reference_number varchar [note: 'The payment document number or transaction reference assigned when the invoice was paid. Used for payment reconciliation and audit trail.']
  payment_terms_code varchar [note: 'Code representing the payment terms agreed with the vendor (e.g., Net 30, 2/10 Net 30). Determines due date calculation and early payment discount eligibility.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The date the invoice was posted to the general ledger. Used for period assignment and financial reporting.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'The purchase order number against which this invoice is being verified. Used for three-way matching and spend tracking.']
  reference_document_number varchar [note: 'Reference to a related document such as the original invoice for a credit memo, or a contract number. Provides audit trail and document linkage.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tax amount charged on the invoice, including sales tax, VAT, GST, or other applicable taxes.']
  three_way_match_status varchar [note: 'Status of the three-way match process comparing purchase order (PO), goods receipt (GR), and invoice. Indicates whether the invoice matches the PO and GR within tolerance or has variances.']
  withholding_tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount of withholding tax deducted from the invoice payment as required by tax regulations. Reduces the net payment to the vendor.']

  Note: 'Accounts payable vendor invoice and payment lifecycle from SAP S/4HANA FI-AP, encompassing invoice header, line-level detail, and outbound payment settlement. Records all supplier invoices for food and beverage purchases, equipment, R&M, utilities, and services. Header stores vendor ID, invoice date, due date, payment terms, gross/net amount, tax, currency, payment block, three-way match status, and dunning level. Each line captures purchased item/service, quantity, unit price, GL account, cost center, PO reference, goods receipt reference, and tax code. Payment data stores payment method (ACH, wire, check), payment date, bank account, cleared invoice references, discount taken, and payment run ID. Enables COGS% analysis by ingredient category, food vs. non-food spend split, three-way match reconciliation, DPO (Days Payable Outstanding) tracking, cash discount optimization, and cash flow forecasting.'
}

Table finance.ap_invoice_line {
  ap_invoice_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the accounts payable invoice line item. Primary key for the AP invoice line detail record.']
  ap_invoice_header_ap_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent AP invoice header record. Links this line item to its parent invoice document.']
  ap_invoice_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent AP invoice header record. Links this line item to its parent invoice document.']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier contract governing pricing and terms for this purchase. Enables contract compliance validation and price variance analysis.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: Normalize cost center reference by linking to cost_center master.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who approved this invoice line for payment. Provides audit trail for financial controls and segregation of duties compliance.']
  gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Replace gl_account_code with FK to gl_account for authoritative account details.']
  goods_receipt_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the goods receipt document confirming physical delivery of items. Critical for three-way match validation in procurement cycle.']
  ingredient_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the material master record for the purchased item. Links to inventory SKU for food ingredients, packaging, and supplies.']
  procurement_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the originating purchase order for this invoice line. Used for three-way match reconciliation between PO, goods receipt, and invoice.']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier or vendor providing the goods or services. Enables supplier spend analysis and vendor performance tracking.']
