  ap_reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Status of accounts payable sub-ledger reconciliation to general ledger for the period.']
  ar_reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Status of accounts receivable sub-ledger reconciliation to general ledger for the period.']
  audit_readiness_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the period close documentation and supporting evidence are ready for internal or external audit review.']
  bank_reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Status of bank account reconciliation activities for the period.']
  close_completed_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the period close process was fully completed and locked.']
  close_duration_hours decimal(10,2) [note: 'The total number of hours elapsed from close initiation to completion, used for process efficiency tracking.']
  close_initiated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the period close process was initiated.']
  close_phase varchar [note: 'The specific phase of the period close workflow (pre-close activities, soft close for preliminary reporting, hard close for final lock, post-close adjustments).']
  close_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the period close process indicating the phase of completion. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: not_started|in_progress|pre_close|soft_close|hard_close|completed|reopened — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  company_code varchar [note: 'Four-character SAP company code representing the legal entity for financial reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this period close record was first created in the system.']
  depreciation_run_date date [note: 'The date when the depreciation calculation run was executed for this period.']
  depreciation_run_status varchar [note: 'Status of the fixed asset depreciation calculation and posting run for the period.']
  exception_count varchar [note: 'The number of reconciliation exceptions or issues identified during the period close process.']
  financial_statement_status varchar [note: 'Status of Profit and Loss (P&L), balance sheet, and cash flow statement preparation and approval for the period.']
  gl_account_reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Status of general ledger account reconciliation activities for balance sheet and income statement accounts.']
  intercompany_reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Status of intercompany transaction reconciliation and elimination entries for multi-entity consolidation.']
  inventory_valuation_status varchar [note: 'Status of inventory valuation and Cost of Goods Sold (COGS) calculation for the period.']
  marketing_fund_accrual_status varchar [note: 'Status of marketing fund contribution accrual calculation and posting for the period.']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'The system user ID of the person who last modified this period close record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this period close record was last modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes capturing key issues, decisions, or commentary related to this period close.']
  period_type varchar [note: 'The type of financial period being closed (monthly, quarterly, annual, or special adjustment period).']
  reopen_authorized_by varchar [note: 'The name of the senior finance leader who authorized the reopening of the closed period.']
  reopen_reason varchar [note: 'The business justification for reopening a previously closed period (e.g., audit adjustment, error correction, late invoice).']
  reopen_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the period was reopened after initial close.']
  responsible_controller_email varchar [note: 'The email address of the financial controller responsible for this period close.']
  responsible_controller_name varchar [note: 'The name of the financial controller responsible for executing and signing off on this period close.']
  royalty_accrual_status varchar [note: 'Status of franchise royalty income accrual calculation and posting for the period.']
  scheduled_close_date date [note: 'The target date by which the period close should be completed according to the financial calendar.']
  sign_off_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when the responsible controller formally signed off on the completed period close.']
  tax_provision_status varchar [note: 'Status of income tax provision calculation and posting for the period.']
  variance_analysis_status varchar [note: 'Status of budget-to-actual variance analysis and commentary preparation for the period.']

  Note: 'Financial period-end close activity tracking, consolidation output, and compliance evidence record for each legal entity and fiscal period from SAP S/4HANA FI. Captures close phase (pre-close, soft close, hard close), task completion status, responsible controller, accrual posting status, depreciation run status, intercompany reconciliation status, elimination journal status, consolidated trial balance sign-off, and final sign-off timestamp. Includes SOX control evidence (segregation of duties verification, reconciliation sign-offs, management assertion documentation) and audit trail for external auditor access. Supports the monthly/quarterly/annual close calendar, consolidated financial statement preparation (balance sheet, income statement, cash flow statement), audit readiness, and GAAP/IFRS compliance timelines.'
}

Table finance.cost_allocation {
  cost_allocation_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the cost allocation record. Primary key.']
  allocation_rule_id varchar [note: 'The specific allocation rule or assessment within the cycle that governs how costs are distributed. Defines sender-receiver relationships and allocation basis.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'The SAP user ID of the person or system account that executed the allocation run.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of the franchise entity if the receiver is a franchised restaurant. Null for company-owned units. Used for franchise-level cost roll-up.']
  restaurant_unit_id varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of the restaurant unit receiving the allocated cost. Enables unit-level EBITDA and fully-loaded P&L reporting.']
  sender_gl_account_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.gl_account. Business justification: Replace cost allocation GL account code fields with FKs to gl_account for accurate allocation tracking.']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'The unique identifier of the restaurant unit receiving the allocated cost. Enables unit-level EBITDA and fully-loaded P&L reporting.']
  user_employee_id varchar [note: 'The SAP user ID of the person or system account that executed the allocation run.']
  allocated_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary amount allocated from the sender cost center to the receiver cost center in this record. This is the product of total sender cost and allocation percentage.']
  allocation_basis_type varchar [note: 'The method or driver used to allocate costs. Common bases include headcount (FTE), revenue (AUV), square footage, transaction count (ADT), labor hours, fixed percentage, or custom formula. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: headcount|revenue|square_footage|transaction_count|labor_hours|fixed_percentage|custom — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  allocation_basis_value decimal(18,4) [note: 'The quantity or weight of the allocation basis for the receiver. For example, if basis is headcount, this is the FTE count; if revenue, this is the AUV amount.']
  allocation_batch_number varchar [note: 'The batch or job identifier for the allocation run. All allocations executed in the same cycle run share the same batch ID for traceability.']
  allocation_cycle_code varchar [note: 'The identifier of the allocation cycle or segment that defines the allocation rules and sequence. Examples: CORP_OH (corporate overhead), IT_ALLOC (IT cost allocation), MKT_FUND (marketing fund distribution).']
  allocation_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the allocation. May include details about the allocation purpose, sender service, or special instructions.']
  allocation_document_number varchar [note: 'The SAP document number assigned to this cost allocation posting. Used for audit trail and reversal reference.']
  allocation_percentage decimal(7,4) [note: 'The percentage of the sender cost center total that is allocated to this receiver. Calculated as receiver basis value divided by total basis value across all receivers. Expressed as decimal (e.g., 0.0523 for 5.23%).']
  allocation_run_date date [note: 'The date on which the allocation cycle was executed. May differ from posting date if batch processing is used.']
  allocation_status varchar [note: 'The current status of the allocation record. Posted indicates successful posting; reversed indicates the allocation was reversed; parked indicates saved but not posted; error indicates posting failure.']
  allocation_type varchar [note: 'The CO allocation method used. Assessment allocates costs without detail traceability; distribution maintains sender cost element detail; periodic reposting moves costs between cost centers; settlement closes internal orders or projects.']
  brand_code varchar [note: 'The restaurant brand to which the receiver cost center belongs. Used for brand-level cost analysis and EBITDA reporting.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'The legal entity company code to which this allocation belongs. Links to the financial accounting entity.']
  controlling_area_code varchar [note: 'The controlling area in which this allocation is executed. Represents the organizational unit for cost accounting.']
  cost_element_code varchar [note: 'The controlling cost element that classifies the nature of the allocated cost (e.g., IT services, HR services, rent, utilities). Used for cost type analysis.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this cost allocation record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'The three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the allocated amount. Typically USD for US operations, but may vary for international entities.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period (month) within the fiscal year for this allocation. Typically 1-12, may include special periods 13-16 for year-end adjustments.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'The fiscal year in which the cost allocation is posted. Four-digit year format.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this cost allocation record was last modified. Updated on any change to the record.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-text notes or comments about this allocation record. May include manual adjustments, special circumstances, or audit annotations.']
  ownership_model varchar [note: 'The ownership model of the receiver restaurant unit. Company-owned units receive full allocations; franchise units may receive limited allocations per franchise agreement.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The date on which the cost allocation was posted to the general ledger and controlling documents.']
  receiver_cost_center_code varchar [note: 'The cost center to which costs are being allocated. Typically a restaurant unit cost center or operational department that consumes the shared service.']
  receiver_cost_center_name varchar [note: 'The descriptive name of the receiver cost center. Denormalized for reporting convenience.']
  receiver_profit_center_code varchar [note: 'The profit center to which the allocated cost is assigned. Used for segment reporting and restaurant-level P&L.']
  reversal_document_number varchar [note: 'The document number of the original allocation that this record reverses, or the document number of the reversal if this record was reversed. Null if no reversal relationship exists.']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether this allocation record is a reversal of a previous allocation. True if this is a reversal posting.']
  reversal_reason_code varchar [note: 'The reason code for reversing the allocation. Examples: incorrect period, incorrect basis, duplicate posting, correction required.']
  sender_cost_center_code varchar [note: 'The cost center from which costs are being allocated. Typically a shared service or corporate overhead cost center (e.g., IT, HR, Finance, Marketing Fund).']
  sender_cost_center_name varchar [note: 'The descriptive name of the sender cost center. Denormalized for reporting convenience.']

  Note: 'Cost allocation and distribution records from SAP S/4HANA CO module. Captures periodic allocations of shared costs (corporate overhead, IT, HR, marketing fund) to restaurant cost centers and profit centers using defined allocation keys (headcount, revenue, square footage). Stores sender cost center, receiver cost center, allocation cycle, allocation rule, allocation basis, allocated amount, fiscal period, and reversal indicator. Enables fully-loaded restaurant P&L, accurate EBITDA by unit, and transparent overhead burden rates for franchise vs. company-owned comparisons.'
}

Table finance.capex_project {
  capex_project_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the capital expenditure project record. Primary key.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to realestate.facility. Business justification: Needed for capital‑expenditure planning linking each capex project to the specific facility it upgrades, used in CAPEX approval and facility budgeting processes.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key to the franchise entity if this CapEx project is franchise-funded or co-funded. Null for company-owned projects.']
  restaurant_unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key to the restaurant location this CapEx project is associated with (for NRO, remodel, or site-specific equipment projects). Null for corporate-level technology or multi-site programs.']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key to the restaurant location this CapEx project is associated with (for NRO, remodel, or site-specific equipment projects). Null for corporate-level technology or multi-site programs.']
  actual_capitalization_date date [note: 'Actual date when the project was capitalized and assets placed in service. Null if not yet capitalized.']
  actual_completion_date date [note: 'Actual date when project work was completed and assets were ready for use. Null if project is still in progress.']
  actual_spend_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cumulative actual expenditures posted to the project to date. Includes invoiced costs, accruals, and internal labor capitalized.']
  approval_authority varchar [note: 'Name or title of the individual or committee that approved the project (e.g., CFO, CapEx Committee, Board of Directors).']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the CapEx project was formally approved by the governance committee or authorized signatory.']
  approved_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total capital budget approved for this project by the CapEx governance committee or executive leadership. Represents the authorized spending limit.']
  asset_class varchar [note: 'Fixed asset class that will receive the capitalized costs. Determines depreciation method and useful life.']
  auc_gl_account varchar [note: 'General ledger account number for the Asset Under Construction balance sheet line item. Costs accumulate here until capitalization.']
  business_justification varchar [note: 'Business case rationale for the capital investment, including strategic alignment, expected benefits, and financial analysis.']
  cancellation_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation for project cancellation if status is cancelled. Null for active or completed projects.']
  capex_category varchar [note: 'Strategic classification of the CapEx investment. Growth = revenue expansion, maintenance = asset replacement, compliance = regulatory requirement, efficiency = cost reduction, strategic = competitive positioning.']
  committed_cost_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total committed costs via purchase orders and contracts not yet invoiced. Used for budget consumption tracking and forecasting.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'SAP company code representing the legal entity responsible for this CapEx project. Links to finance.legal_entity for consolidation and reporting.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Cost center responsible for project oversight and ongoing asset maintenance post-capitalization.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who created this project record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this CapEx project record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 three-letter currency code for all monetary amounts in this project record (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  depreciation_method varchar [note: 'Depreciation method to be applied once the asset is capitalized (e.g., straight_line, declining_balance).']
  expected_capitalization_date date [note: 'Planned date when the project will be capitalized and transferred from AUC to fixed assets, triggering depreciation start.']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who last modified this project record.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this CapEx project record was last modified.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-form notes and comments for additional context, issues, or special considerations.']
  payback_period_months varchar [note: 'Expected number of months to recover the capital investment through incremental cash flows or cost savings.']
  planned_completion_date date [note: 'Target date for project completion as defined in the project plan.']
  profit_center_code varchar [note: 'Profit center to which this CapEx project is assigned. Used for segment reporting and P&L allocation of depreciation expense.']
  project_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative description of the project scope, objectives, and deliverables.']
  project_manager_email varchar [note: 'Corporate email address of the project manager for communication and escalation.']
  project_manager_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the project manager responsible for execution and delivery.']
  project_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name of the capital project (e.g., \'NRO - Dallas Market Street\', \'Remodel - Chicago Loop Kitchen Upgrade\').']
  project_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier for the CapEx project as assigned in SAP PS. Externally visible project code used in financial reporting and approvals.']
  project_phase varchar [note: 'Current phase of the project lifecycle. Initiation = business case development, design = architectural/engineering, procurement = vendor selection, construction = build-out, commissioning = testing and handover, closeout = final accounting and asset transfer.']
  project_start_date date [note: 'Official start date of the project, typically when budget is approved and work begins.']
  project_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the CapEx project. Planning = under development, approved = budget authorized, in_progress = construction/implementation underway, on_hold = temporarily suspended, completed = work finished, cancelled = terminated before completion, closed = financially closed and capitalized. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: planning|approved|in_progress|on_hold|completed|cancelled|closed — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  project_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the capital project. NRO = New Restaurant Opening, remodel = major renovation, equipment_replacement = asset refresh, technology_infrastructure = IT/POS systems, maintenance_capex = major repairs capitalized, facility_expansion = capacity additions.']
  remaining_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Calculated remaining budget: approved_budget_amount minus (actual_spend_amount + committed_cost_amount). Indicates available funds.']
  risk_rating varchar [note: 'Overall risk assessment for the project based on complexity, budget size, timeline, and dependencies. Used for governance oversight.']
  roi_target_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Target return on investment percentage established during project approval. Used for post-implementation performance evaluation.']
  sponsor_name varchar [note: 'Executive sponsor or business owner accountable for project outcomes and ROI realization.']
  useful_life_years varchar [note: 'Expected useful life in years for depreciation purposes once the asset is capitalized.']
  variance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Budget variance: actual_spend_amount minus approved_budget_amount. Positive indicates over-budget, negative indicates under-budget.']
  variance_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Budget variance as a percentage of approved budget: (variance_amount / approved_budget_amount) * 100.']

  Note: 'Capital expenditure project master record from SAP S/4HANA PS (Project System) and FI-AA. Tracks NRO (New Restaurant Opening) builds, remodel programs, equipment replacement initiatives, and technology infrastructure investments. Stores project type (NRO, remodel, maintenance CapEx), approved budget, actual spend-to-date, committed costs, project phase, asset under construction (AUC) account, expected capitalization date, and ROI target. Supports CapEx governance and CapEx vs. OpEx classification.'
}

Table finance.bank_account {
  bank_account_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the bank account record. Primary key. Role: MASTER_RESOURCE.']
  house_bank_id varchar [note: 'SAP house bank identifier. Groups multiple accounts under a single banking relationship for payment processing and cash management.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Bank accounts are owned by a legal entity; replace company_code string with FK to legal_entity for proper hierarchy.']
  account_holder_name varchar [note: 'Legal name of the account holder as registered with the bank. Typically matches the legal entity name but may differ for special-purpose accounts.']
  account_name varchar [note: 'The legal name or title of the bank account as registered with the financial institution.']
  account_number varchar [note: 'The bank account number. Stored in masked or encrypted format for PCI DSS compliance. Restricted access required.']
  account_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the bank account. Active accounts are available for transactions; inactive accounts are dormant but not closed; closed accounts are permanently terminated; frozen accounts are temporarily blocked; pending activation accounts are awaiting setup completion.']
  account_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the bank account by its primary business purpose: operating (daily transactions), payroll (employee payments), concentration (cash pooling), zero-balance (subsidiary sweep), escrow (restricted funds), reserve (regulatory or contractual hold).']
  bank_key varchar [note: 'Country-specific bank identifier. In the US this is the ABA routing number; in other countries it may be a sort code, bank code, or clearing number.']
  bank_name varchar [note: 'The legal name of the financial institution holding the account.']
  bank_statement_delivery_method varchar [note: 'How bank statements are delivered: electronic (email or portal), paper (postal mail), or both.']
  branch_address varchar [note: 'Full street address of the bank branch. Classified as confidential organizational contact data.']
  branch_name varchar [note: 'Name of the specific bank branch where the account is held, if applicable.']
  cash_pool_group varchar [note: 'Identifier for the cash pooling or notional pooling group this account belongs to. Used for multi-entity liquidity management and interest optimization.']
  closing_date date [note: 'The date the bank account was closed or terminated. Null for active accounts.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 3166 country code where the bank account is domiciled (e.g., USA, CAN, MEX, GBR).']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who created this bank account record in the system.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this bank account record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the account (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP, CAD, MXN). Defines the denomination of balances and transactions.']
  dual_signature_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether two authorized signatories are required for transactions above a certain threshold. True if dual control is mandated; false otherwise.']
  dual_signature_threshold_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary threshold above which dual signature is required. Null if dual signature is not applicable or always required.']
  electronic_banking_code varchar [note: 'Identifier or login ID for online banking or electronic funds transfer systems. Confidential to prevent unauthorized access.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'The GL account code in the chart of accounts that this bank account reconciles to. Used for daily bank reconciliation and cash position reporting.']
  iban varchar [note: 'International Bank Account Number for accounts in IBAN-participating countries. Up to 34 alphanumeric characters. Restricted due to account identification sensitivity.']
  interest_rate_percent decimal(7,4) [note: 'Annual interest rate applied to the account balance, expressed as a percentage. May be positive for interest-bearing accounts or negative for fee-based accounts.']
  last_reconciliation_date date [note: 'The date of the most recent successful bank reconciliation for this account.']
  minimum_balance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Minimum balance required to avoid fees or maintain account status. Null if no minimum is required.']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or name of the person who last modified this bank account record in the system.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this bank account record was last modified in the system.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional notes, special instructions, or comments about the bank account (e.g., usage restrictions, special terms, historical context).']
  opening_date date [note: 'The date the bank account was opened with the financial institution.']
  overdraft_facility_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the account has an overdraft or line of credit facility. True if overdraft is available; false otherwise.']
  overdraft_limit_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum overdraft or credit line available on this account. Null if no overdraft facility exists.']
  payment_method_supported varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of payment methods supported by this account (e.g., ACH, wire, check, EFT, SEPA). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: ACH|wire|check|EFT|SEPA|BACS|CHAPS|RTP — promote to reference product]']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary bank contact. Classified as confidential organizational contact data.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary relationship manager or contact person at the bank for this account.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the primary bank contact. Classified as confidential organizational contact data.']
  reconciliation_frequency varchar [note: 'How often this bank account is reconciled against the general ledger: daily (high-volume operating accounts), weekly (moderate activity), monthly (low activity or reserve accounts).']
  signatory_list varchar [note: 'Comma-separated list of employee IDs or names authorized to sign checks or approve wire transfers on this account. Confidential for fraud prevention.']
  swift_code varchar [note: 'Bank Identifier Code (BIC) or SWIFT code for international wire transfers. 8 or 11 character alphanumeric code identifying the bank and branch.']

  Note: 'Corporate bank account master record and daily transactional statement activity from SAP S/4HANA FI-TR (Treasury). Master data defines all operating, payroll, concentration, and zero-balance accounts across legal entities and geographies — storing bank name, account number (masked), IBAN/routing, account type, currency, cash pool group, signatory list, and reconciliation GL account. Statement lines capture daily electronic bank statement imports including value date, posting date, transaction type (credit/debit), amount, bank reference, counterparty name, clearing status, and matched GL posting. Supports daily automated bank reconciliation, real-time cash position reporting, fraud detection for restaurant cash deposits, POS settlement flow matching, and treasury cash pooling across entities.'
}

Table finance.bank_statement_line {
  bank_statement_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the bank statement line item. Primary key for this entity.']
  bank_account_id varchar [note: 'Internal identifier for the bank account in SAP S/4HANA FI-TR. Links to the house bank and account configuration.']
  bank_statement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the parent bank statement header containing this line item.']
  pos_settlement_batch_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the POS settlement batch that generated this deposit. Used to match bank deposits to daily POS sales.']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: REQUIRED: Bank statement reconciliation must reference the supplier to validate payments, a core accounting control.']
  restaurant_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant location associated with this transaction. Critical for restaurant-level cash reconciliation and POS settlement matching.']
  reversed_line_bank_statement_line_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the original bank statement line that this transaction reverses, if applicable.']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant location associated with this transaction. Critical for restaurant-level cash reconciliation and POS settlement matching.']
  amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'The monetary amount of the bank transaction in the statement currency. Always positive; sign is indicated by transaction_type.']
  bank_reference_number varchar [note: 'Unique reference number assigned by the bank to this transaction. Used for bank inquiries and dispute resolution.']
  clearing_date date [note: 'Date on which the bank statement line was successfully matched and cleared against GL postings in SAP.']
  clearing_document_number varchar [note: 'SAP document number of the GL posting that cleared this bank statement line. Used for audit trail and drill-down.']
  clearing_status varchar [note: 'Status of the bank statement line in the reconciliation process. Indicates whether the transaction has been matched to GL postings.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'SAP company code identifying the legal entity to which this bank transaction belongs. Used for multi-entity consolidation and financial reporting.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Cost center assignment for the transaction, if applicable. Used for internal cost allocation and management reporting.']
  counterparty_account_number varchar [note: 'Bank account number of the counterparty. May be masked or partial depending on bank reporting.']
  counterparty_bank_code varchar [note: 'Bank identifier for the counterparty financial institution (e.g., routing number, SWIFT code, sort code).']
  counterparty_name varchar [note: 'Name of the party on the other side of the transaction (payer for credits, payee for debits). Used for matching and reconciliation.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or system account that created this record in the lakehouse.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this record was first created in the lakehouse silver layer.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for the transaction amount (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Exchange rate used to convert transaction currency to local currency. Null if currencies are the same.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal period (month) within the fiscal year for financial reporting and period closing.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year in which the transaction was posted, based on the company code fiscal calendar.']
  fraud_detection_rule varchar [note: 'Name or code of the fraud detection rule that triggered the fraud flag, if applicable.']
  fraud_flag boolean [note: 'Indicator that this transaction has been flagged for potential fraud or suspicious activity requiring investigation.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'GL account to which this bank transaction was posted. Typically the main bank clearing account.']
  import_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this bank statement line was imported into SAP S/4HANA from the electronic bank statement file.']
  line_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential line number within the bank statement, used for ordering and reference.']
  local_currency_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Transaction amount converted to the company code local currency for general ledger posting and P&L reporting.']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or system account that last modified this record in the lakehouse.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this record was last modified in the lakehouse silver layer.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes added by treasury or accounting staff during reconciliation or investigation.']
  payment_reference varchar [note: 'Payment reference or remittance information provided by the payer. Critical for matching to invoices and customer accounts.']
  posting_date date [note: 'The date on which the transaction is posted to the general ledger in SAP S/4HANA. May differ from value date for accounting period control.']
  profit_center_code varchar [note: 'Profit center assignment for segment reporting and P&L analysis. May represent a restaurant unit or brand.']
  reconciliation_difference_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Difference amount identified during reconciliation between bank statement and GL postings. Zero when fully reconciled.']
  reversal_indicator boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this line item is a reversal of a previous transaction.']
  transaction_code varchar [note: 'Bank-specific transaction code identifying the type of banking operation (e.g., wire transfer, check deposit, ACH, card settlement). Maps to BAI or MT940 transaction codes.']
  transaction_date date [note: 'The actual date the transaction occurred at the bank. Represents the real-world business event timestamp.']
  transaction_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of the transaction provided by the bank, including purpose and additional details.']
  transaction_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the transaction is a credit (incoming funds) or debit (outgoing funds) to the bank account.']
  value_date date [note: 'The date on which funds become available or are debited from the account. Used for cash position reporting and interest calculation.']

  Note: 'Electronic bank statement line items imported into SAP S/4HANA FI-TR for daily cash reconciliation. Each line captures value date, posting date, transaction type (credit/debit), amount, bank reference, counterparty name, clearing status, and matched GL posting. Supports automated bank reconciliation, cash position reporting, and fraud detection for restaurant cash deposits and POS settlement flows.'
}

Table finance.lease_liability {
  lease_liability_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the lease_liability data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.legal_entity. Business justification: Lease liabilities are associated with a legal entity; add FK to capture ownership.']
  renewed_lease_liability_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on lease_liability (renewed_lease_liability_id)']

  Note: 'Lease obligation and right-of-use (ROU) asset master record for ASC 842/IFRS 16 compliance from SAP S/4HANA RE-FX. Tracks operating and finance leases for restaurant locations, equipment, and vehicles. Stores lease term, commencement date, monthly payment, discount rate, ROU asset value, lease liability balance, modification history, and renewal/termination options. Critical for restaurant operators where 80%+ of locations are leased — supports balance sheet presentation, lease expense recognition, and lease maturity analysis.'
}

Table finance.financial_period {
  financial_period_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the financial period record. Primary key.']
  comparative_prior_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the immediately preceding period (e.g., prior month). Used for sequential period-over-period analysis.']
  comparative_prior_year_period_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key reference to the equivalent period in the prior fiscal year. Used for year-over-year comparative analysis and SSS reporting.']
  ledger_id varchar [note: 'Two-character code identifying the ledger (leading ledger for primary GAAP, non-leading ledger for parallel IFRS, special purpose ledger for management reporting). Supports multi-GAAP parallel accounting.']
  prior_financial_period_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on financial_period (prior_financial_period_id)']
  accounting_principle varchar [note: 'Accounting standard applied in this ledger: US_GAAP (United States Generally Accepted Accounting Principles), IFRS (International Financial Reporting Standards), local_GAAP (country-specific standards), management (internal reporting).']
  adjustment_posting_allowed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether adjustment postings (corrections, reclassifications) are allowed in this period after initial close. Used for controlled reopening during audit or correction cycles.']
  adjustment_reason varchar [note: 'Reason code or description for why the period is an adjustment period.']
  audit_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this period is subject to external audit review. Typically true for year-end periods and quarterly periods for publicly traded entities.']
  audit_trail_flag boolean [note: 'True if changes to this period are tracked for audit purposes.']
  calendar_days_count varchar [note: 'Total number of calendar days in the period. Used for daily average calculations and period normalization in financial analysis.']
  calendar_month varchar [note: 'Month number (1‑12) in the calendar year.']
  calendar_quarter varchar [note: 'Quarter number (1‑4) in the calendar year.']
  calendar_year varchar [note: 'Gregorian calendar year of the period.']
  close_actual_date date [note: 'Actual date when the period-end close process was completed and the period status changed to closed. Null if period is still open.']
  close_scheduled_date date [note: 'Target date for completing the period-end close process. Used for close calendar planning and tracking adherence to financial reporting deadlines.']
  close_variance_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between scheduled and actual close date (positive if late, negative if early). Used to track period-end close efficiency and identify process improvement opportunities.']
  company_code varchar [note: 'Four-character alphanumeric code identifying the legal entity in SAP S/4HANA. Links this period to a specific company within the multi-entity structure.']
  consolidation_group_code varchar [note: 'Code identifying the consolidation group for multi‑entity reporting.']
  consolidation_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this period requires multi-entity consolidation processing. True for periods where intercompany eliminations and group reporting are performed.']
  created_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or username of the person who created this financial period record. Used for audit trail and accountability.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this financial period record was first created in the system. Used for audit trail and data lineage tracking.']
  day_count varchar [note: 'Total count of calendar days covered by the period.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the period ceases to be effective (nullable).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the period becomes effective for reporting purposes.']
  end_date date [note: 'Last calendar day of the financial period.']
  exchange_rate_to_reporting decimal(18,6) [note: 'Conversion rate from functional currency to reporting currency for the period.']
  exchange_rate_type varchar [note: 'Type of exchange rate applied for currency translation in this period: average (period average rate for P&L), closing (period-end rate for balance sheet), budget (planning rate), historical (acquisition date rate for fixed assets).']
  financial_period_description varchar [note: 'Optional free‑text description of the period.']
  financial_statement_type varchar [note: 'Type of financial statement the period primarily supports.']
  fiscal_month varchar [note: 'Month number (1‑12) within the fiscal year.']
  fiscal_period_code varchar [note: 'Standard code used by SAP for the fiscal period (e.g., "2023P01").']
  fiscal_quarter varchar [note: 'Quarter number (1‑4) within the fiscal year.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Four-digit fiscal year to which this period belongs. May differ from calendar year depending on fiscal year variant configuration.']
  fiscal_year_variant varchar [note: 'Two-character code defining the fiscal calendar structure (number of periods, special periods, year-end rules) used by the company code. Determines how the fiscal year is divided into posting periods.']
  holiday_indicator varchar [note: 'Degree to which holidays affect the period.']
  is_adjustment_period boolean [note: 'True if the period is used for post‑close adjustments.']
  is_current boolean [note: 'True if this period is the active reporting period.']
  is_estimated boolean [note: 'True if the period values are based on estimates rather than actuals.']
  is_holiday_period boolean [note: 'True if the period includes a recognized holiday schedule.']
  is_interim boolean [note: 'True if the period is an interim (non‑full) reporting period.']
  ledger_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the ledger: leading (primary GAAP ledger for statutory reporting), non_leading (parallel IFRS or local GAAP ledger), special_purpose (management or segment reporting ledger).']
  lock_flag boolean [note: 'True when the period is locked for posting to prevent changes.']
  modified_by_user varchar [note: 'User ID or username of the person who last modified this financial period record. Used for audit trail and change tracking.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this financial period record was last modified. Updated whenever period status, close dates, or other attributes change.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for recording period-specific notes, exceptions, or special circumstances (e.g., \'Includes acquisition of XYZ brand\', \'Impacted by COVID-19 closures\', \'Leap year adjustment\').']
  period_category varchar [note: 'Business classification of the period data.']
  period_end_date date [note: 'Last calendar date of the financial period. Defines the end of the posting window for transactions assigned to this period.']
  period_key varchar [note: 'Compact code representing the period (e.g., FY2023Q1, FY2023M04).']
  period_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or label for the period (e.g., \'January 2024\', \'Q1 FY2024\', \'Period 13 - Year-End Adjustments\'). Used for reporting and user interface display.']
  period_number varchar [note: 'Sequential period number within the fiscal year (typically 1-12 for monthly periods, or 1-13 for 4-4-5 retail calendars). Special periods (13, 14, 15, 16) used for year-end adjustments.']
  period_sequence_number varchar [note: 'Sequential order of the period within its fiscal year.']
  period_start_date date [note: 'First calendar date of the financial period. Defines the beginning of the posting window for transactions assigned to this period.']
  period_status varchar [note: 'Current posting status of the period: not_opened (future period, no posting allowed), open (active posting allowed), closed (period-end close completed, limited posting), locked (archived, no posting allowed).']
  period_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the period: regular (standard posting period), special (year-end adjustment period 13-16), or adjustment (mid-year correction period).']
  posting_allowed_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether new transactions can be posted to this period. True if period is open for posting, false if closed or locked.']
  quarter_number varchar [note: 'Fiscal quarter number (1-4) to which this period belongs. Supports quarterly financial reporting and Same-Store Sales (SSS) analysis.']
  reporting_currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for consolidated financial reporting in this period (e.g., USD for US Dollar, EUR for Euro). Used for multi-currency consolidation.']
  segment_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'True if the period is used for segment‑level reporting.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that defines the period (e.g., SAP S/4HANA).']
  sss_eligible_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this period is eligible for Same-Store Sales (SSS) or Comparable Store Sales (Comp Sales) analysis. Typically true for periods where stores have been open for at least 12-13 months.']
  start_date date [note: 'First calendar day of the financial period.']
  tax_reporting_flag boolean [note: 'True if the period is used for tax reporting calculations.']
  tax_reporting_period_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this period is a tax reporting period requiring submission of tax returns (e.g., quarterly estimated tax, annual tax filing). Used for tax compliance tracking.']
  working_days_count varchar [note: 'Number of business working days in the period, excluding weekends and holidays. Used for normalizing operational metrics and labor cost analysis.']
  year_period_key varchar [note: 'Concatenated key combining fiscal year and period number (format: YYYYPPP, e.g., 2024001 for FY2024 Period 1). Used for sorting and comparative period analysis.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for financial_period. '
}

Table finance.payment_run {
  payment_run_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for payment_run']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who triggered the payment run.']
  reversed_payment_run_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on payment_run (reversed_payment_run_id)']
  actual_completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payment run finished processing.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the payment run.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who approved the payment run.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payment run was approved.']
  batch_reference varchar [note: 'External reference or identifier for the batch used by downstream systems.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Internal cost center associated with the payment run for accounting allocation.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payment run record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the payment run amounts.']
  description varchar [note: 'Free‑form text describing the purpose or context of the payment run.']
  error_count varchar [note: 'Number of errors encountered during processing of the run.']
  external_reference_code varchar [note: 'Identifier used by external systems (e.g., banking partner) to reference the run.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'Fiscal period (e.g., Q1, Q2) for the payment run.']
  fiscal_year varchar [note: 'Fiscal year to which the payment run belongs.']
  is_automated boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the payment run was generated automatically by the system.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form notes captured for the payment run.']
  payment_channel varchar [note: 'Technical channel through which the payment run was processed.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Instrument used to execute the payments.']
  payment_run_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the run based on its business purpose.']
  processing_time_seconds varchar [note: 'Total elapsed time in seconds for the run to complete.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Geographic region identifier for the payment run (e.g., NA, EU, APAC).']
  retry_flag boolean [note: 'True if the run is a retry of a previously failed run.']
  run_number varchar [note: 'Business-visible sequential number assigned to the payment run.']
  run_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the payment run was initiated in the business process.']
  scheduled_date date [note: 'Planned calendar date for the payment run execution.']
  settlement_date date [note: 'Date on which the payments are settled with banks or vendors.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the payment run.']
  total_discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total discounts deducted from the gross amount.']
  total_gross_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of all payment amounts before taxes, discounts, and fees.']
  total_net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Net amount payable after taxes and discounts.']
  total_tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate tax amount applied to the payment run.']
  transaction_count varchar [note: 'Count of individual payment transactions included in the run.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the payment run record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for payment_run. Referenced by payment_run_id.'
}

  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Financial cost‑center code associated with the node for budgeting and reporting.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the hierarchy node record was first created.']
  description varchar [note: 'Optional free‑text description providing additional context about the node.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the node becomes effective in the hierarchy.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the node ceases to be effective; null if open‑ended.']

  hierarchy_level varchar [note: 'Numeric depth of the node within the hierarchy (root = 0).']
  hierarchy_path varchar [note: 'Delimited path representing the node\'s ancestry (e.g., /1/4/23).']
  is_leaf boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the node has no child nodes.']
  node_code varchar [note: 'Unique business code identifying the node within its hierarchy.']
  node_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the hierarchy node.']
  node_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the node indicating its role in the hierarchy (e.g., organization, region, brand).']
  node_value decimal(15,2) [note: 'Monetary or quantitative value associated with the node (e.g., budget allocation).']
  node_weight decimal(10,2) [note: 'Numeric weight assigned to the node for allocation or scoring purposes.']
  profit_center_code varchar [note: 'Financial profit‑center code linked to the node for performance measurement.']
  region_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code representing the geographic region of the node.']
  sort_order varchar [note: 'Integer used to order sibling nodes under the same parent.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the node.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the hierarchy node record.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for hierarchy_node. Referenced by hierarchy_node_id.'
}

  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form comments or remarks about the ledger.']
  opening_balance decimal(18,2) [note: 'Balance at the start of the fiscal period.']
  period varchar [note: 'Financial reporting period (quarter or full year) for the ledger.']
  reporting_currency_code varchar [note: 'Currency in which the ledger is reported for consolidated financial statements.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the ledger.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the ledger record.']
  variance_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Difference between budgeted and actual amounts.']
  variance_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage variance between budgeted and actual amounts.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for ledger. Referenced by ledger_id.'
}

  Note: 'Master reference table for chart_of_accounts. Referenced by chart_of_accounts_id.'

Table realestate.site {
  site_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the physical site record. Primary key for the site entity.']
  agreement_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the franchise agreement governing this site, if operated under franchise model. Links site to franchisee obligations and royalty calculations.']
  franchisee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to franchise.franchisee. Business justification: Site Assignment Report requires each property to be linked to the operating franchisee for lease compliance and royalty calculations.']
  landlord_id varchar [note: 'FK to realestate.landlord']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the legal entity or franchisee responsible for operating this site. Used for financial consolidation and P&L (Profit and Loss) reporting.']
  trade_area_id varchar [note: 'FK to realestate.trade_area']
  accessibility_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quantitative assessment of ease of ingress and egress, typically scored 0-100. Considers turn lanes, traffic signals, median cuts, and parking availability.']
  acquisition_date date [note: 'Date on which the site was acquired or lease was executed. Used for portfolio tenure analysis and depreciation start date for owned properties.']
  address_line_1 varchar [note: 'Primary street address line for the site location. Business-confidential organizational contact data.']
  address_line_2 varchar [note: 'Secondary address line for suite, unit, or building identifiers. Business-confidential organizational contact data.']
  building_square_footage varchar [note: 'Total interior square footage of the restaurant building. Used for lease valuation, R&M (Repairs and Maintenance) budgeting, and utility forecasting.']
  city varchar [note: 'City or municipality name where the site is located.']
  closure_date date [note: 'Date on which the restaurant ceased operations at this site. Used for portfolio reporting, lease termination tracking, and asset disposition.']
  country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the site location. Determines regulatory jurisdiction and reporting requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this site record was first created in the system. Used for data lineage and audit trail.']
  daily_traffic_count varchar [note: 'Average daily vehicle or pedestrian traffic count passing the site. Critical visibility and accessibility metric for site selection scoring.']
  drive_thru_capable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the site configuration and zoning permit a drive-thru lane. Critical for QSR (Quick-Service Restaurant) operational model and throughput forecasting.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this site record was last updated. Used for change tracking and data quality monitoring.']
  latitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic latitude coordinate of the site in decimal degrees. Used for GIS mapping, trade area analysis, and proximity calculations.']
  lease_commencement_date date [note: 'Date on which the lease agreement becomes effective and rent obligations begin. Critical for IFRS 16 / ASC 842 right-of-use asset recognition.']
  lease_expiration_date date [note: 'Date on which the current lease term expires. Used for lease renewal planning, portfolio optimization, and financial forecasting.']
  lifecycle_stage varchar [note: 'Current stage of the site within the NRO (New Restaurant Opening) pipeline or existing portfolio lifecycle. Drives workflow, reporting, and financial treatment. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: prospecting|under_negotiation|approved|under_construction|active|temporarily_closed|closed|disposed — 8 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  longitude decimal(10,7) [note: 'Geographic longitude coordinate of the site in decimal degrees. Used for GIS mapping, trade area analysis, and proximity calculations.']
  lot_square_footage varchar [note: 'Total land area of the site parcel in square feet. Used for property valuation, CAM (Common Area Maintenance) calculations, and site development planning.']
  market_classification varchar [note: 'Demographic and geographic market segment classification. Drives menu mix, pricing strategy, and operational model selection.']
  monthly_base_rent decimal(12,2) [note: 'Fixed monthly rent payment obligation under the lease agreement, excluding variable charges. Used for P&L (Profit and Loss) forecasting and lease liability calculation.']
  monthly_cam_charges decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monthly charges for common area maintenance, property taxes, and insurance allocated to the tenant. Variable component of total occupancy cost.']
  opening_date date [note: 'Date on which the restaurant began serving customers at this site. Used for SSS (Same-Store Sales) eligibility and maturity curve analysis.']
  ownership_status varchar [note: 'Legal ownership structure of the site. Determines accounting treatment under IFRS 16 / ASC 842 lease standards and CapEx (Capital Expenditure) responsibility.']
  parking_spaces varchar [note: 'Number of dedicated parking spaces available at the site. Must meet local zoning minimums and impacts customer accessibility scoring.']
  percentage_rent_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'Percentage of sales above the threshold that is paid as additional rent. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0600 for 6%).']
  percentage_rent_threshold decimal(12,2) [note: 'Annual sales breakpoint above which percentage rent becomes due to the landlord. Common in retail lease structures for high-traffic locations.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the site address. Business-confidential organizational contact data. Used for trade area analysis and demographic mapping.']
  projected_auv decimal(12,2) [note: 'Forecasted annual sales volume for the site based on site selection model. Used for NRO (New Restaurant Opening) approval and ROI (Return on Investment) analysis.']
  seating_capacity varchar [note: 'Total number of customer seats within the dining area. Used for capacity planning, labor scheduling, and health department permitting.']
  site_name varchar [note: 'Human-readable name or designation for the site, often incorporating location landmarks or address elements for easy identification.']
  site_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the site, used across real estate, operations, and finance systems. Typically assigned during site prospecting phase.']
  site_type varchar [note: 'Physical configuration and placement classification of the site within its development or property. Critical for site selection scoring and operational model determination. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: freestanding|inline|endcap|pad|food_court|airport|travel_center — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  state_province varchar [note: 'Two-letter state or province code for the site location. Used for tax jurisdiction, regulatory compliance, and market segmentation.']
  total_capex_investment decimal(14,2) [note: 'Total capital investment for site development, including land acquisition, construction, equipment, and pre-opening costs. Used for ROI calculation and capital planning.']
  visibility_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quantitative assessment of site visibility from primary traffic routes, typically scored 0-100. Incorporates signage sightlines, obstructions, and approach angles.']
  zoning_classification varchar [note: 'Local government zoning designation for the site parcel. Determines permitted uses, building restrictions, and signage allowances.']

  Note: 'Master record for every physical site under evaluation, active operation, or disposition in the restaurant real estate portfolio. Captures site identification, geographic coordinates, full address, market classification, trade area demographics, traffic counts (ADT), visibility scores, zoning classification, site type (pad, inline, endcap, freestanding, drive-thru capable), ownership status (owned/leased/ground-lease), parcel size, and current lifecycle stage (prospecting, under negotiation, LOI executed, active, temporarily closed, permanently closed, disposed). Serves as the SSOT for all site-level real estate data across the NRO pipeline and existing portfolio. Links to restaurant unit in the restaurant domain.'
}

Table realestate.lease {
  lease_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the lease agreement. Primary key for the lease entity.']
  franchisee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to franchise.franchisee. Business justification: Lease Management process tracks which franchisee is the tenant; needed for rent payment scheduling, compliance, and financial reporting.']
  landlord_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the landlord entity that owns the leased property.']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the restaurant site or location governed by this lease agreement.']
  accounting_classification varchar [note: 'Lease classification for financial reporting purposes under IFRS 16 / ASC 842: operating lease (most common for real estate) or finance lease (if lease transfers substantially all risks and rewards of ownership).']
  base_rent_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Fixed monthly or annual base rent payment amount specified in the lease agreement, excluding variable charges such as CAM, taxes, and percentage rent.']
  base_rent_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which base rent payments are due: monthly, quarterly, or annually.']
  cam_charges_annual decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated annual Common Area Maintenance charges for shared property expenses such as landscaping, parking lot maintenance, snow removal, and common utilities.']
  co_tenancy_clause_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the lease includes a co-tenancy provision allowing rent reduction or termination if anchor tenants or a minimum occupancy threshold is not maintained.']
  commencement_date date [note: 'Date when the lease agreement becomes effective and rent obligations begin. Critical for IFRS 16 / ASC 842 right-of-use asset recognition.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this lease record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this lease (e.g., USD, CAD, EUR). Critical for multi-currency lease portfolios.']
  discount_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Interest rate used to discount future lease payments to present value for IFRS 16 / ASC 842 calculations. Represents the rate the lessee would pay to borrow funds to purchase a similar asset.']
  document_url varchar [note: 'Reference URL or file path to the digitally stored lease agreement document for audit and compliance purposes.']
  exclusive_use_rights varchar [note: 'Description of exclusive use provisions that restrict the landlord from leasing to competing restaurant concepts within the same property or trade area.']
  execution_date date [note: 'Date when the lease agreement was signed by all parties, establishing the legal contract.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date when the initial lease term ends, excluding any renewal options. Used to calculate remaining lease term for accounting and planning purposes.']
  guarantor_entity varchar [note: 'Name of the corporate entity providing a lease guarantee, typically the parent company for franchise or subsidiary-operated locations.']
  insurance_annual decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated annual property insurance premium obligation passed through to the tenant under a triple-net (NNN) lease structure.']
  lease_number varchar [note: 'Externally-known unique business identifier for the lease agreement, used in legal documents and correspondence.']
  lease_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the lease agreement: draft (under negotiation), active (in force), expired (term ended), terminated (ended early), renewed (extended), or pending renewal (approaching expiration with renewal option).']
  lease_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the lease arrangement: ground lease (land only), building lease (structure and land), sublease (tenant subleases from another tenant), or license agreement.']
  liability_value decimal(15,2) [note: 'Initial measurement of the lease liability recognized on the balance sheet under IFRS 16 / ASC 842, representing the present value of future lease payments discounted at the incremental borrowing rate.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this lease record was last modified in the system.']
  percentage_rent_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of gross sales above the threshold that is paid as additional rent to the landlord. Typically ranges from 3% to 8% in QSR leases.']
  percentage_rent_threshold decimal(15,2) [note: 'Annual sales breakpoint above which percentage rent becomes due. Common in retail leases where landlord receives a percentage of sales exceeding this threshold.']
  permitted_use varchar [note: 'Legal description of the permitted business activities and operations allowed under the lease, typically specifying restaurant or foodservice use.']
  property_tax_annual decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated annual property tax obligation passed through to the tenant under a triple-net (NNN) lease structure.']
  remaining_term_months varchar [note: 'Number of months remaining from the current date until lease expiration, used for lease liability calculations under IFRS 16 / ASC 842.']
  renewal_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of days prior to lease expiration by which the tenant must notify the landlord of intent to exercise a renewal option. Typically 180-365 days.']
  renewal_option_count varchar [note: 'Number of renewal option periods available to the tenant after the initial lease term expires. Common in restaurant leases to provide long-term site control.']
  renewal_option_term_months varchar [note: 'Duration in months of each renewal option period. Typically 5 years (60 months) per option in QSR leases.']
  rent_escalation_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Annual percentage rate of rent increase if escalation type is fixed percentage. Null if escalation is CPI-indexed or fair market value.']
  rent_escalation_type varchar [note: 'Method by which base rent increases over the lease term: fixed percentage (e.g., 3% annually), CPI-indexed (tied to Consumer Price Index), fair market value (periodic reappraisal), or none (flat rent).']
  rou_asset_value decimal(15,2) [note: 'Initial measurement of the right-of-use asset recognized on the balance sheet under IFRS 16 / ASC 842, representing the present value of future lease payments plus initial direct costs.']
  security_deposit_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Refundable deposit held by the landlord to cover potential damages or unpaid rent. Typically equivalent to 1-3 months of base rent.']
  term_months varchar [note: 'Total duration of the initial lease term expressed in months, excluding renewal options.']
  termination_clause_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the lease includes an early termination clause allowing the tenant to exit the lease before expiration under specified conditions.']
  termination_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of days advance notice required to exercise the early termination clause, if applicable.']
  termination_penalty_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Financial penalty or buyout amount required to exercise the early termination clause, if applicable.']

  Note: 'Authoritative master record for every lease agreement governing a restaurant location, including ground leases, building leases, and sublease arrangements. Captures lease type, commencement and expiration dates, base rent, rent escalation schedule, renewal option terms, termination clauses, co-tenancy provisions, exclusive use rights, permitted use language, landlord entity, guarantor, and IFRS 16 / ASC 842 classification (operating vs. finance lease). Tracks right-of-use (ROU) asset value, lease liability, discount rate, and remaining lease term for accounting compliance. Includes structured clause abstractions: clause type (rent escalation, renewal option, termination right, co-tenancy, exclusivity, assignment, subletting, HVAC responsibility, CAM cap, audit right), clause text, effective date range, financial impact flag, and compliance obligation indicator. Links to site and landlord records. Serves as the SSOT for all lease agreement data including clause-level detail.'
}

Table realestate.landlord {
  landlord_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the landlord entity. Primary key.']
  billing_address_line1 varchar [note: 'First line of the landlord\'s billing address for rent payments and invoicing.']
  billing_address_line2 varchar [note: 'Second line of the billing address (suite, unit, building number).']
  billing_city varchar [note: 'City for the landlord\'s billing address.']
  billing_country_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO country code for the landlord\'s billing address.']
  billing_postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal or ZIP code for the landlord\'s billing address.']
  billing_state_province varchar [note: 'State or province for the landlord\'s billing address.']
  cam_billing_method varchar [note: 'Method by which CAM charges are calculated and billed by this landlord.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this landlord record was first created in the system.']
  dba_name varchar [note: 'Trade name or DBA name under which the landlord operates, if different from legal name.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date when the landlord relationship became effective or when the landlord record was first established.']
  entity_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the landlord\'s organizational structure. REIT = Real Estate Investment Trust.']
  escalation_history_count varchar [note: 'Total number of formal escalations or disputes raised with this landlord over the relationship history.']
  insurance_certificate_frequency varchar [note: 'Frequency at which insurance certificates must be submitted to this landlord.']
  insurance_certificate_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this landlord requires periodic submission of insurance certificates.']
  landlord_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the landlord relationship.']
  last_escalation_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent formal escalation or dispute with this landlord.']
  last_modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when this landlord record was last updated.']
  legal_name varchar [note: 'The full legal name of the landlord entity as registered with governing authorities.']
  negotiation_posture varchar [note: 'Assessment of the landlord\'s typical approach to lease negotiations and dispute resolution.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for capturing additional context, special requirements, or relationship notes about the landlord.']
  payment_terms_days varchar [note: 'Standard number of days for rent payment terms agreed with this landlord (e.g., Net 30).']
  portfolio_property_count varchar [note: 'Number of properties currently leased from this landlord across the restaurant portfolio.']
  preferred_communication_channel varchar [note: 'The landlord\'s preferred method for receiving communications and notices.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary contact for landlord communications.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Full name of the primary contact person for landlord communications and relationship management.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for the landlord contact.']
  primary_contact_title varchar [note: 'Job title or role of the primary contact person (e.g., Property Manager, Leasing Director, Asset Manager).']
  relationship_health_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quantitative score (0-100) representing the overall health of the landlord relationship based on payment history, dispute frequency, and collaboration quality.']
  relationship_tier varchar [note: 'Classification of the landlord relationship based on portfolio size, negotiation history, and strategic importance.']
  status_reason varchar [note: 'Explanation or reason code for the current status, particularly for inactive or suspended landlords.']
  tax_number varchar [note: 'Federal Employer Identification Number (EIN) or Tax ID for the landlord entity.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date when the landlord relationship was terminated or when the last lease with this landlord expired.']
  w9_last_updated_date date [note: 'Date when the W-9 form was last received or updated for this landlord.']
  w9_on_file_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a current IRS Form W-9 is on file for this landlord for tax reporting purposes.']
  website_url varchar [note: 'Official website URL for the landlord entity.']

  Note: 'Master record for every landlord, property owner, or lessor entity with whom the company maintains a real estate relationship. Captures landlord legal name, entity type (REIT, private owner, developer, municipality), primary contact details, billing address, tax identification, preferred communication channel, relationship tier, and portfolio overlap count. Tracks relationship health score, escalation history, and negotiation posture. Serves as the SSOT for landlord identity within the real estate domain.'
}

Table realestate.rent_schedule {
  rent_schedule_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the rent schedule record. Primary key. Represents one billing period for one lease agreement.']
  landlord_id varchar [note: 'FK to realestate.landlord']
  lease_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the executed lease agreement that governs this rent schedule period. Links to the master lease agreement entity.']
  restaurant_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the restaurant location occupying the leased premises for this rent schedule period.']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the restaurant location occupying the leased premises for this rent schedule period.']
  base_rent_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The fixed minimum rent amount due for this billing period as stipulated in the lease agreement, excluding percentage rent, CAM, taxes, and insurance.']
  billing_period_end_date date [note: 'The last day of the rent billing period covered by this schedule record. Defines the period for which rent and occupancy costs are calculated.']
  billing_period_start_date date [note: 'The first day of the rent billing period covered by this schedule record. Typically the first day of a calendar month or lease anniversary month.']
  cam_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The tenant\'s share of common area maintenance charges for this billing period, including property management, landscaping, parking lot maintenance, and shared facility upkeep. May be estimated or reconciled.']
  cam_reconciliation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this billing period includes a CAM reconciliation adjustment, where estimated CAM charges are trued up to actual landlord expenses.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'The cost center or profit center code to which this rent expense is allocated, typically the restaurant location or regional operating unit.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this rent schedule record was first created in the system. Audit trail for data lineage.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for all monetary amounts in this rent schedule record (e.g., USD, CAD, EUR, GBP, MXN).']
  escalation_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'The annual escalation rate applied to base rent for this period, expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0300 for 3% annual increase). Null if escalation type is none or stepped.']
  escalation_type varchar [note: 'The method by which base rent increases over the lease term. Fixed = predetermined amount, CPI = Consumer Price Index linked, Percentage = annual percentage increase, Stepped = scheduled step increases, None = flat rent.']
  gl_account_code varchar [note: 'The general ledger account code to which this rent expense is posted in the financial system. Typically maps to rent expense or occupancy cost accounts.']
  insurance_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The tenant\'s share of property insurance premiums for this billing period, covering landlord\'s building and liability insurance. May be estimated and reconciled.']
  interest_expense decimal(15,2) [note: 'The interest expense component of the lease payment for this period under IFRS 16 / ASC 842 finance lease accounting. Calculated using the incremental borrowing rate.']
  lease_accounting_classification varchar [note: 'The accounting treatment classification for this lease under IFRS 16 / ASC 842. Operating leases recognize rent expense; finance leases recognize depreciation and interest expense.']
  lease_liability_reduction decimal(15,2) [note: 'The portion of the total occupancy cost that reduces the lease liability on the balance sheet for this period under IFRS 16 / ASC 842. Represents principal payment component.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The date and time when this rent schedule record was last updated. Audit trail for change tracking.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional context, explanations, or special circumstances related to this rent schedule period (e.g., rent abatement, dispute details, payment plan arrangements).']
  occupancy_cost_percentage decimal(5,4) [note: 'The total occupancy cost as a percentage of restaurant sales for this period, calculated as (total_occupancy_cost / reported_sales_amount). Key performance metric for site profitability. Null if sales not reported.']
  other_charges_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Additional charges not classified as base rent, percentage rent, CAM, taxes, or insurance. May include utilities, signage fees, parking fees, or special assessments.']
  payment_date date [note: 'The actual date the rent payment was remitted to the landlord. Null if payment has not yet been made.']
  payment_due_date date [note: 'The date by which the total rent and occupancy cost payment must be remitted to the landlord to avoid late fees or default.']
  payment_reference_number varchar [note: 'The check number, wire transfer reference, or electronic payment confirmation number used to remit this rent payment. Null if payment has not yet been made.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current payment status for this rent schedule period. Tracks whether the scheduled payment has been made, is outstanding, or is under dispute.']
  percentage_rent_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The calculated percentage rent due for this billing period based on actual sales performance exceeding the threshold. Zero if sales did not exceed threshold or lease has no percentage rent clause.']
  percentage_rent_rate decimal(5,4) [note: 'The percentage of sales revenue above the threshold that is owed as additional rent. Expressed as a decimal (e.g., 0.0600 for 6%). Null if lease does not include percentage rent clause.']
  percentage_rent_threshold decimal(15,2) [note: 'The sales revenue breakpoint above which percentage rent becomes due. Also known as natural breakpoint or sales threshold. Null if lease does not include percentage rent clause.']
