  property_address varchar [note: 'The full street address of the leased restaurant property for this rent schedule. Organizational contact data classified as confidential.']
  real_estate_tax_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The tenant\'s proportionate share of property taxes for this billing period. May be estimated monthly and reconciled annually based on actual tax assessments.']
  rent_per_square_foot decimal(10,4) [note: 'The base rent amount divided by square footage for this billing period, expressed as cost per square foot. Key benchmarking metric for real estate portfolio management.']
  reported_sales_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The gross sales revenue reported to the landlord for this billing period, used to calculate percentage rent. Confidential business performance data. Null if sales reporting is not required.']
  right_of_use_asset_depreciation decimal(15,2) [note: 'The depreciation expense for the right-of-use asset for this period under IFRS 16 / ASC 842. Typically straight-line over the lease term.']
  sales_reporting_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the tenant is contractually required to report sales revenue to the landlord for this billing period, typically for percentage rent calculation or lease compliance.']
  square_footage decimal(10,2) [note: 'The total leasable square footage of the restaurant premises as defined in the lease agreement. Used to calculate per-square-foot rent metrics.']
  tax_reconciliation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this billing period includes a real estate tax reconciliation adjustment, where estimated tax charges are trued up to actual property tax assessments.']
  total_occupancy_cost decimal(15,2) [note: 'The sum of all rent and occupancy charges for this billing period: base rent + percentage rent + CAM + real estate tax + insurance + other charges. Represents total cash outflow for occupancy.']

  Note: 'Periodic rent obligation schedule derived from executed lease agreements, capturing each scheduled rent payment period, base rent amount, percentage rent threshold and rate, CAM (Common Area Maintenance) estimate, real estate tax contribution, insurance contribution, total occupancy cost, and payment due date. Supports IFRS 16 / ASC 842 lease liability amortization schedules, cash flow forecasting, and occupancy cost benchmarking against AUV (Average Unit Volume). One record per lease per billing period.'
}

Table realestate.rent_payment {
  rent_payment_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the rent payment transaction. Primary key.']
  approved_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user or employee who approved this payment for disbursement.']
  bank_account_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the company bank account from which this payment was disbursed.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the cost center to which this rent payment is allocated for internal cost tracking and P&L reporting.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the user or employee who approved this payment for disbursement.']
  landlord_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the landlord or property owner receiving the payment.']
  lease_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the lease agreement under which this payment is made.']
  profit_center_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the profit center to which this rent payment is allocated for segment reporting and profitability analysis.']
  restaurant_unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the restaurant location for which this rent payment applies.']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the restaurant location for which this rent payment applies.']
  approval_date date [note: 'The date on which this payment was approved for disbursement by the authorized approver.']
  base_rent_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The base rent amount paid for the lease period, excluding additional charges such as CAM, taxes, and insurance.']
  cam_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The Common Area Maintenance charge paid as part of this rent payment, covering shared facility costs such as landscaping, parking lot maintenance, and common utilities.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this rent payment record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code indicating the currency in which this payment was made (e.g., USD, EUR, GBP).']
  days_late varchar [note: 'The number of days between the payment due date and the actual payment date, indicating payment timeliness.']
  dispute_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether this payment is under dispute with the landlord due to billing discrepancies or lease interpretation issues.']
  dispute_reason varchar [note: 'Description of the reason for dispute if the payment is flagged as disputed (e.g., incorrect CAM charges, billing error, lease term interpretation).']
  exchange_rate decimal(15,6) [note: 'The exchange rate applied if this payment was made in a foreign currency, used to convert to the company\'s reporting currency.']
  fiscal_period varchar [note: 'The fiscal period (e.g., 2024-01, 2024-Q1) to which this payment is allocated for financial reporting.']
  gl_posting_date date [note: 'The date on which this payment was posted to the general ledger for financial reporting purposes.']
  insurance_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The insurance premium amount paid as part of this rent payment, covering property and liability insurance as required by the lease.']
  late_fee_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The amount of late fee charged for this payment if payment was made after the due date.']
  late_fee_applied_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether a late fee was applied to this payment due to payment being made after the due date.']
  lease_period_end_date date [note: 'The end date of the lease period covered by this rent payment.']
  lease_period_start_date date [note: 'The start date of the lease period covered by this rent payment.']
  modified_timestamp timestamp [note: 'The timestamp when this rent payment record was last modified or updated.']
  other_charges_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Any additional charges or fees included in this rent payment that are not categorized as base rent, CAM, taxes, or insurance (e.g., utilities, parking fees, signage fees).']
  payment_date date [note: 'The date on which the rent payment was made or disbursed to the landlord.']
  payment_due_date date [note: 'The date by which this rent payment was contractually due per the lease agreement.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'The payment instrument or method used to disburse the rent payment (e.g., ACH, wire transfer, check).']
  payment_notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes or comments related to this payment, capturing special circumstances, adjustments, or additional context.']
  payment_reference_number varchar [note: 'External reference number or check number assigned to this payment for tracking and reconciliation purposes.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the payment transaction indicating whether it has been processed, cleared, or is pending.']
  payment_variance_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The difference between the scheduled payment amount and the actual total payment amount, indicating overpayment or underpayment.']
  property_tax_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The property tax amount paid as part of this rent payment, representing the tenant\'s share of real estate taxes.']
  reconciliation_date date [note: 'The date on which this payment was reconciled and approved in the accounts payable system.']
  reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether this payment has been reconciled against the lease schedule and landlord invoice, and whether any disputes exist.']
  scheduled_payment_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The originally scheduled or expected payment amount per the lease agreement for this period.']
  total_payment_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'The total amount paid in this rent payment transaction, including base rent, CAM, taxes, insurance, and any other charges.']

  Note: 'Transactional record of each rent and occupancy cost payment made to a landlord, capturing payment date, payment method, amount paid (base rent, CAM, taxes, insurance), payment reference number, lease period covered, variance from scheduled amount, late fee applied flag, and reconciliation status. Supports accounts payable integration with SAP S/4HANA, landlord dispute resolution, and occupancy cost audit trails. Distinct from the rent schedule (obligation) — this captures actual cash disbursements.'
}

Table realestate.cam_reconciliation {
  cam_reconciliation_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for each CAM reconciliation record.']
  landlord_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the landlord party associated with the lease.']
  tenant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant (tenant) for which CAM is being reconciled.']
  audit_user varchar [note: 'User ID of the person who performed the last audit action on the record.']
  cam_adjustments_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Adjustments applied after audit (e.g., credits, penalties).']
  cam_billed_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total CAM charges billed by the landlord for the period.']
  cam_cap_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Maximum CAM charge allowed under the lease cap provision.']
  cam_estimated_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated CAM payment made by the tenant during the period.']
  cam_exclusions_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Amount of CAM charges excluded per lease terms (e.g., non‑recoverable items).']
  cam_final_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Final net CAM amount after all adjustments, exclusions, and caps.']
  cam_itemization_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether detailed CAM line‑item breakdown is attached.']
  cam_reconciliation_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the reconciliation record.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the reconciliation record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for monetary values.']
  dispute_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the reconciliation is under dispute.']
  dispute_resolution_date date [note: 'Date when the CAM dispute was resolved.']
  dispute_status varchar [note: 'Current status of any CAM dispute.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or observations about the reconciliation.']
  overpayment_credit_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Credit amount returned to the tenant for over‑payment of CAM.']
  period_end_date date [note: 'End date of the CAM billing period covered by this reconciliation.']
  period_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the CAM billing period covered by this reconciliation.']
  reconciliation_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the reconciliation (e.g., CAM-2024-001).']
  reconciliation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the reconciliation was performed.']
  reconciliation_type varchar [note: 'Frequency or nature of the reconciliation.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that supplied the CAM data.']
  underpayment_due_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Amount the tenant owes to the landlord after reconciliation.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the reconciliation record.']

  Note: 'Annual or periodic CAM (Common Area Maintenance) reconciliation record capturing the landlord\'s actual CAM charges versus estimated CAM payments made during the reconciliation period. Tracks gross CAM billed, allowable CAM exclusions, CAM cap application, audit adjustments, final reconciled amount, overpayment credit or underpayment due, dispute flag, and resolution outcome. Supports CAM audit rights enforcement, CapEx vs. OpEx classification of CAM items, and occupancy cost management.'
}

Table realestate.site_selection {
  site_selection_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each site selection evaluation record.']
  approval_authority_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee or executive who approved the site selection.']
  created_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'System user who created the record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee responsible for leading the evaluation.']
  franchise_franchisee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the franchisee or corporate unit for which the site is being evaluated.']
  franchisee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the franchisee or corporate unit for which the site is being evaluated.']
  last_modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'System user who last updated the record.']
  primary_site_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee responsible for leading the evaluation.']
  quaternary_site_last_modified_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'System user who last updated the record.']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the master record of the candidate location.']
  site_master_site_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the master record of the candidate location.']
  tertiary_site_created_by_user_employee_id varchar [note: 'System user who created the record.']
  auv_projection decimal(12,2) [note: 'Projected average weekly sales per unit (in local currency).']
  cannibalization_risk_score varchar [note: 'Score (1‑10) estimating risk of cannibalizing existing stores.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes entered by evaluators.']
  competition_score varchar [note: 'Score (1‑10) indicating competitive pressure in the trade area.']
  compliance_haccp_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the site meets HACCP food‑safety requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary fields.']
  decision_date date [note: 'Date the evaluation committee rendered its final decision.']
  demographic_score varchar [note: 'Numeric score (1‑10) reflecting demographic suitability.']
  environmental_impact_score varchar [note: 'Score (1‑10) assessing environmental sustainability considerations.']
  evaluation_code varchar [note: 'Human‑readable code assigned to the evaluation for tracking and reference.']
  evaluation_stage varchar [note: 'Most recent stage of the evaluation workflow.']
  evaluation_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the evaluation process was initiated.']
  expected_employee_count varchar [note: 'Projected number of staff required to operate the site.']
  expected_fte varchar [note: 'Projected full‑time equivalent labor units.']
  lease_term_years varchar [note: 'Length of the lease agreement for the site, if applicable.']
  lease_type varchar [note: 'Classification of lease cost structure.']
  market_share_estimate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Projected share of the local market captured by the new site.']
  overall_site_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Weighted composite score summarizing all evaluation criteria.']
  payback_period_months varchar [note: 'Estimated number of months to recoup the investment.']
  projected_annual_sales decimal(12,2) [note: 'Estimated total sales for the first fiscal year.']
  projected_capex_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Projected CAPEX specific to construction, equipment, and fit‑out.']
  projected_cogs_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated COGS as a percentage of sales.']
  projected_investment_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Estimated total capital required to open the site.']
  projected_labor_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated labor cost as a percentage of sales.']
  projected_roi_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated ROI expressed as a percentage over the analysis horizon.']
  rejection_reason_code varchar [note: 'Standardized code indicating why a site was rejected.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Overall risk classification for the site selection.']
  site_area_sqft decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total usable square footage of the candidate location.']
  site_selection_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the site selection evaluation.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system that originated the record (e.g., FranConnect).']
  traffic_score varchar [note: 'Numeric score (1‑10) based on vehicle and foot traffic analysis.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record capturing the formal site selection evaluation process for a candidate restaurant location. Tracks evaluation stage (initial screening, trade area analysis, financial modeling, committee review, approved/rejected), site scoring criteria (demographics, traffic, competition proximity, cannibalization risk, AUV projection, payback period), evaluation team members, committee decision date, approval authority, and rejection reason code. Links to the site master and NRO pipeline. Supports FranConnect site selection workflows.'
}

Table realestate.nro_project {
  nro_project_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for the NRO development project.']
  franchise_franchisee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the franchise entity that owns the new restaurant.']
  franchisee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the franchise entity that owns the new restaurant.']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: New‑restaurant opening projects use a general contractor (supplier); linking supports cost tracking and risk management.']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to realestate.site. Business justification: A NRO project is always associated with a physical site; linking to site eliminates silo and removes duplicated address columns.']
  actual_opening_date date [note: 'Actual date the restaurant opened for service.']
  architect_name varchar [note: 'Name of the architect or design firm responsible for the building design.']
  capex_actual_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total actual spend recorded against the project to date.']
  capex_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Approved budget amount for the project before any spend is incurred.']
  capex_committed_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount of budget that has been formally committed to vendors or contracts.']
  certificate_of_occupancy_date date [note: 'Date the Certificate of Occupancy was issued, allowing the restaurant to operate.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status with local health, safety, and regulatory requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the project record was first created in the lakehouse.']
  ifr16_lease_asset_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the lease is accounted for as a right‑of‑use asset under IFRS 16.']
  ifr16_lease_end_date date [note: 'Effective end date of the IFRS 16 lease accounting period.']
  ifr16_lease_start_date date [note: 'Effective start date of the IFRS 16 lease accounting period.']
  lease_obligation_end_date date [note: 'End date of the lease obligation for the site.']
  lease_obligation_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the lease obligation for the site (IFRS 16 / ASC 842).']
  lease_term_years varchar [note: 'Total lease term expressed in years.']
  lease_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the lease (e.g., gross, net, percentage rent).']
  nro_project_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the project\'s scope and objectives.']
  nro_project_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the NRO project.']
  permit_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the permit, if applicable.']
  permit_issue_date date [note: 'Date the permit was issued.']
  permit_number varchar [note: 'Official permit identifier issued by the permitting authority.']
  permitting_approval_date date [note: 'Date when all required permits were approved.']
  permitting_status varchar [note: 'Current status of required construction and health permits.']
  project_code varchar [note: 'External business code or identifier used for the project in franchise and CapEx systems.']
  project_manager_name varchar [note: 'Name of the internal manager responsible for the NRO project.']
  project_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the development project.']
  project_phase varchar [note: 'Current high‑level phase of the NRO project lifecycle.']
  project_phase_end_date date [note: 'Projected or actual end date of the current project phase.']
  project_phase_start_date date [note: 'Date the current project phase began.']
  project_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the NRO project indicating whether it is a new build, remodel, relocation, or conversion.']
  risk_assessment_date date [note: 'Date the most recent risk assessment was performed.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Overall risk rating assigned to the project.']
  target_opening_date date [note: 'Planned date for the restaurant to open to the public.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the project record.']

  Note: 'Master record for each New Restaurant Opening (NRO) development project, tracking the full lifecycle from site approval through construction completion and restaurant opening. Captures project name, restaurant unit number, project type (new build, relocation, conversion), target and actual opening dates, project manager, general contractor, architect of record, total CapEx budget reference, and blocking issues. Includes detailed construction milestone tracking: each milestone (site plan approval, permit submission, permit issuance, groundbreaking, foundation complete, steel erection, MEP rough-in, drywall, equipment installation, punch list, final inspection, certificate of occupancy, opening day) with planned date, actual date, variance in days, responsible party, completion status, and blocking issues. Supports critical path management, CapEx draw schedule alignment, and integration with SAP S/4HANA CapEx modules and FranConnect NRO tracking.'
}

Table realestate.capex_budget {
  capex_budget_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each CapEx budget record.']
  capex_project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the CapEx project to which this budget belongs.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the legal entity owning the budget.']
  amendment_date date [note: 'Date the latest amendment was recorded.']
  amendment_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the most recent budget amendment.']
  amendment_reason varchar [note: 'Reason or justification for the most recent budget amendment.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date the budget was formally approved.']
  approving_authority varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person/role that approved the budget.']
  budget_code varchar [note: 'External code or number used to reference the budget in finance systems.']
  budget_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the budget (e.g., "Downtown Store Build 2025").']
  budget_phase varchar [note: 'Current phase of the budget lifecycle.']
  budget_revision_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Amount change applied during the latest budget revision.']
  budget_revision_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent budget reforecast or revision.']
  budget_type varchar [note: 'Category of the CapEx project the budget supports.']
  building_shell_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Budget for structural construction of the building shell.']
  capex_budget_description varchar [note: 'Free‑form description of the budget purpose and scope.']
  capex_budget_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the budget record.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Internal cost‑center code responsible for the budget.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the budget record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the budget amounts.']
  end_date date [note: 'Planned end date of the budget period.']
  ffe_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Budget for furniture, fixtures, and equipment.']
  funding_source varchar [note: 'Source of capital funding (e.g., corporate cash, debt, lease financing).']
  land_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Approved budget amount allocated for land acquisition.']
  leasehold_improvements_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Budget for interior build‑out and leasehold improvements.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional remarks, comments, or special conditions.']
  other_costs decimal(18,2) [note: 'Any additional budgeted cost items not captured by other categories.']
  signage_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Budget for exterior and interior signage.']
  soft_costs decimal(18,2) [note: 'Budget for professional services, permits, design, and other indirect costs.']
  start_date date [note: 'Planned start date of the budget period.']
  technology_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Budget for POS, kitchen display, networking, and other technology.']
  total_budget_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate approved budget amount across all cost categories.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the budget record.']

  Note: 'CapEx (Capital Expenditure) planning, budgeting, and spend tracking record for each real estate project (NRO, remodel, relocation, or major facility upgrade). Budget envelope: total approved budget, budget by cost category (land, building shell, leasehold improvements, FF&E, signage, technology, soft costs), budget version, approval date, approving authority, funding source. Actual spend detail: each invoice-level expenditure event with vendor, cost category, amount, payment date, PO reference, SAP cost element, budget line item, cumulative spend to date, and variance to budget. Enables real-time CapEx tracking, budget burn rate monitoring, cost-to-complete forecasting, and draw schedule management. Integrates with SAP S/4HANA CO (Controlling) and Coupa Procurement for PO-to-invoice matching. Serves as the single source of truth for both authorized budgets and actual capital expenditures.'
}

Table realestate.facility {
  facility_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the facility record.']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to realestate.site. Business justification: Facility belongs to a Site; linking provides parent relationship and removes duplicated address fields.']
  ada_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates compliance with the Americans with Disabilities Act.']
  building_material varchar [note: 'Primary material used in the construction of the facility (e.g., brick, steel).']
  cam_charges decimal(10,2) [note: 'Common Area Maintenance charges associated with the facility.']
  capex_spent decimal(12,2) [note: 'Capital expenditures incurred to date for the facility.']
  condition_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Overall condition rating of the facility (e.g., 0-100 scale).']
  construction_status varchar [note: 'Status of any ongoing construction projects.']
  energy_rating varchar [note: 'Energy efficiency rating of the facility.']
  facility_code varchar [note: 'External business identifier or code for the facility used in operational systems.']
  facility_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the facility (e.g., restaurant location name).']
  facility_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the facility.']
  facility_type varchar [note: 'Category of the facility based on its operational model.']
  fire_safety_compliance_status varchar [note: 'Indicates compliance with fire safety regulations.']
  health_inspection_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score from the latest health inspection.']
  hvac_type varchar [note: 'Heating, ventilation, and air conditioning system type.']
  insurance_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the facility\'s insurance policy.']
  insurance_policy_number varchar [note: 'Policy number for the facility\'s insurance coverage.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent health or safety inspection.']
  lease_end_date date [note: 'End date of the lease agreement.']
  lease_rate decimal(10,2) [note: 'Periodic lease payment amount (e.g., monthly rent).']
  lease_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the lease agreement for the facility.']
  maintenance_last_date date [note: 'Date when the most recent maintenance activity was performed.']
  maintenance_next_due date [note: 'Scheduled date for the next maintenance activity.']
  ownership_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the facility is owned by the company or leased.']
  property_tax_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Applicable property tax rate (percentage).']
  r_and_m_status varchar [note: 'Current status of repairs and maintenance activities.']
  remodel_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent remodel or renovation.']
  remodel_type varchar [note: 'Scope of the most recent remodel.']
  roof_type varchar [note: 'Type of roof installed on the facility (e.g., flat, pitched).']
  seating_capacity varchar [note: 'Number of guest seats (covers) available in the front of house.']
  square_footage decimal(10,2) [note: 'Total building area in square feet.']
  tax_assessment_value decimal(12,2) [note: 'Assessed property tax value of the facility.']
  waste_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of food waste relative to total food usage.']
  year_built varchar [note: 'Calendar year the facility was originally constructed.']
  yield_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of usable product yield from raw inputs.']
  zoning_type varchar [note: 'Zoning classification of the property (e.g., commercial, mixed-use).']

  Note: 'Master record for the physical facility (building and improvements) associated with each restaurant location, capturing building square footage, seating capacity (FOH covers), drive-thru lane configuration (DT lanes, stacking capacity), kitchen layout type (BOH configuration), building ownership (owned/leased shell), year built, last remodel date, remodel type, ADA compliance status, energy rating, utility account references, and facility condition score. Distinct from the site (land/location) — this represents the built structure. Links to the restaurant unit in the restaurant domain.'
}

Table realestate.maintenance_work_order {
  maintenance_work_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the maintenance work order record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal or contracted technician who performed the work.']
  franchisee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to franchise.franchisee. Business justification: Maintenance Approval workflow requires the franchisee to approve work orders for their locations, linking orders to the responsible franchisee.']
  maintenance_contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to realestate.maintenance_contract. Business justification: Work orders are issued under a Maintenance Contract; linking captures this relationship.']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the external vendor or service provider assigned to the work order.']
  restaurant_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant facility where the work order originated.']
  technician_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the internal or contracted technician who performed the work.']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant facility where the work order originated.']
  completion_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the work order was completed.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the work order record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for cost amounts.']
  issue_category varchar [note: 'Category of the maintenance issue (e.g., HVAC, plumbing, electrical, equipment, structural, exterior, signage, other). [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: hvac|plumbing|electrical|equipment|structural|exterior|signage|other — promote to reference product]']
  issue_description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the maintenance issue reported.']
  labor_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary cost of labor associated with the work order.']
  labor_hours decimal(6,2) [note: 'Total labor hours expended to resolve the work order.']
  maintenance_work_order_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the work order.']
  parts_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary cost of parts and materials used for the work order.']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business-assigned priority indicating urgency of the work order.']
  reported_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the maintenance issue was reported.']
  resolution_notes varchar [note: 'Notes describing how the issue was resolved, including any follow‑up actions.']
  scheduled_date date [note: 'Planned date for the work to be performed.']
  total_cost decimal(12,2) [note: 'Aggregate cost (labor + parts) for the work order.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the work order record.']
  warranty_claim_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the work order is covered under a warranty claim.']
  work_order_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier assigned to the work order, used in communications and reporting.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for each R&M (Repairs and Maintenance) work order issued for a restaurant facility, capturing work order number, facility, issue category (HVAC, plumbing, electrical, equipment, structural, exterior, signage), issue description, priority level, reported date, assigned vendor/technician, scheduled date, completion date, labor hours, parts cost, total cost, warranty claim flag, and resolution notes. Supports OpEx R&M spend tracking, vendor performance management, and preventive maintenance compliance. Integrates with facility management workflows.'
}

Table realestate.maintenance_contract {
  maintenance_contract_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the maintenance contract record.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to realestate.facility. Business justification: Maintenance contracts are scoped to a specific Facility; linking enables hierarchy and reporting.']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: Maintenance contracts are agreements with specific suppliers; linking enables contract management and compliance tracking.']
  annual_contract_value decimal(15,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the contract on an annual basis.']
  applicable_restaurant_ids varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of restaurant identifiers covered by the contract.']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract automatically renews at the end of its term.']
  compliance_requirements varchar [note: 'Specific regulatory or internal compliance obligations tied to the contract (e.g., HACCP, OSHA).']
  contract_document_url varchar [note: 'Link to the stored contract document (PDF, scanned copy, etc.).']
  contract_manager_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the contract manager.']
  contract_manager_name varchar [note: 'Name of the internal employee responsible for overseeing the contract.']
  contract_manager_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the contract manager.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'External contract reference number used in vendor and internal communications.']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Category of maintenance service covered by the contract.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the contract record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary amounts (e.g., USD).']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the contract expires or is scheduled to terminate; null for open‑ended agreements.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the contract becomes binding.']
  equipment_covered varchar [note: 'List or description of equipment, systems, or assets that are covered by the contract.']
  escalation_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address for escalation communications.']
  escalation_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the person to contact for escalations.']
  escalation_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number for escalation communications.']
  insurance_certificate_url varchar [note: 'Link to the uploaded insurance certificate document.']
  insurance_requirements varchar [note: 'Insurance coverage requirements the vendor must maintain.']
  invoice_due_days varchar [note: 'Number of days after invoice receipt that payment is due.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date when the contract was last reviewed for compliance or performance.']
  maintenance_contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the contract.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next contract performance or compliance review.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments about the contract.']
  payment_frequency varchar [note: 'How often payments are made under the contract.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms agreed with the vendor.']
  penalty_clause varchar [note: 'Description of penalties for non‑performance or breach of contract.']
  regulatory_certifications varchar [note: 'Certifications or approvals required for the service provider under applicable regulations.']
  renewal_term_months varchar [note: 'Length of the renewal period in months if auto‑renewal is enabled.']
  service_area varchar [note: 'Geographic region or set of restaurants the contract applies to.']
  service_frequency varchar [note: 'How often the maintenance service is scheduled to be performed.']
  service_provider_contact_email varchar [note: 'Primary email address for the service provider\'s point of contact.']
  service_provider_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Primary telephone number for the service provider\'s point of contact.']
  sla_description varchar [note: 'Narrative description of service level commitments.']
  sla_response_time_hours varchar [note: 'Maximum response time promised by the vendor for service requests, expressed in hours.']
  termination_notice_days varchar [note: 'Number of days notice required to terminate the contract early.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the contract record.']
  warranty_coverage_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract includes warranty coverage for the equipment.']
  warranty_expiration_date date [note: 'Date when any warranty coverage provided by the contract expires.']

  Note: 'Master record for each preventive maintenance and service contract covering restaurant facility equipment and systems, capturing contract type (HVAC PM, hood cleaning, grease trap, pest control, elevator, fire suppression, refrigeration), service provider, covered equipment list, service frequency, contract start and end dates, annual contract value, SLA response time, and auto-renewal terms. Enables R&M budget forecasting, vendor contract compliance tracking, and equipment warranty management. Distinct from procurement contracts — this is facility-specific service agreements.'
}

Table realestate.lease_amendment {
  lease_amendment_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the lease amendment record.']
  landlord_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the landlord party involved in the amendment.']
  lease_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the original lease agreement to which this amendment applies.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the tenant (restaurant entity) affected by the amendment.']
  tenant_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the tenant (restaurant entity) affected by the amendment.']
  amendment_document_reference varchar [note: 'Link or identifier to the stored amendment contract document.']
  amendment_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the amendment (e.g., AM-2024-001).']
  amendment_reason varchar [note: 'Business rationale or driver for executing the amendment.']
  amendment_summary varchar [note: 'Brief textual summary of the amendment purpose and key changes.']
  amendment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the amendment event was recorded in the system.']
  amendment_type varchar [note: 'Category of the lease amendment describing the nature of the change. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: rent_modification|term_extension|space_change|permitted_use_change|assignment_consent|landlord_work_letter|co_tenancy_waiver — 7 candidates stripped; promote to reference product]']
  co_tenancy_waiver_flag boolean [note: 'True if the amendment includes a waiver of co-tenancy requirements.']
  effective_date date [note: 'Date on which the amendment terms become legally effective.']
  execution_date date [note: 'Date the amendment was signed/executed by the parties.']
  free_rent_months varchar [note: 'Number of months of rent abatement granted by the amendment.']
  ifrs16_impact_flag boolean [note: 'True if the amendment triggers a remeasurement under IFRS 16 / ASC 842.']
  lease_amendment_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the amendment.']
  legal_review_status varchar [note: 'Current status of the legal department\'s review of the amendment.']
  net_impact_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Net financial impact of the amendment after considering rent change, free rent, TI allowance, etc.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional free-form comments or observations about the amendment.']
  permitted_use_change_flag boolean [note: 'True if the amendment modifies the permitted use of the leased space.']
  record_audit_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the amendment record was first created in the data lake.']
  record_audit_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the amendment record.']
  rent_change_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary amount by which the periodic rent is increased (positive) or decreased (negative).']
  rent_change_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for the rent change amount.']
  space_change_sqft decimal(10,2) [note: 'Change in leased square footage (positive for expansion, negative for reduction).']
  space_change_type varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the amendment expands, reduces, or leaves space unchanged.']
  ti_allowance_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary amount of tenant improvement allowance provided under the amendment.']
  ti_allowance_currency varchar [note: 'ISO 4217 currency code for the TI allowance amount.']

  Note: 'Transactional record for each lease lifecycle change event including executed amendments, renewal exercises, option exercises, and lease modifications. Captures event type (rent modification, term extension, renewal exercise, space expansion/reduction, permitted use change, assignment consent, landlord work letter, co-tenancy waiver, early termination), effective date, financial impact (rent change amount, TI allowance received, free rent granted, new rent amount, rent delta versus expiring rent), negotiation details (start date, option exercise deadline, negotiating broker, approval authority, negotiation outcome), option number exercised (for renewals), renewal term length, new expiration date, and execution date. Maintains complete modification and renewal history for IFRS 16 / ASC 842 lease modification accounting, proactive lease expiration management, and portfolio renewal pipeline tracking.'
}

Table realestate.property_acquisition {
  property_acquisition_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the property acquisition record.']
  capex_project_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the capital expenditure project linked to this acquisition.']
  legal_entity_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the legal entity that owns the acquired property.']
  seller_entity_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the selling party (owner) of the property.']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to realestate.site. Business justification: A property acquisition represents the purchase of a site; linking to site centralizes location data and removes duplicated address fields.']
  acquisition_date date [note: 'Date the property acquisition became effective (closing date).']
  acquisition_number varchar [note: 'Business-assigned identifier for the acquisition transaction, used in external reporting and contracts.']
  acquisition_price decimal(18,2) [note: 'Purchase price paid to acquire the property before closing costs.']
  capitalization_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Rate used to estimate the property\'s value based on net operating income.']
  closing_costs decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total ancillary costs incurred at closing (legal, escrow, fees).']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Cost center responsible for the acquisition expense.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the acquisition record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for monetary amounts.']
  deed_recording_reference varchar [note: 'Reference identifier for the recorded deed in the county recorder.']
  due_diligence_completion_date date [note: 'Date when all due‑diligence activities were finalized.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date from which the acquisition record is considered effective for reporting.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date until which the acquisition record remains active (null if open‑ended).']
  environmental_assessment_status varchar [note: 'Result of the environmental impact assessment.']
  financing_structure varchar [note: 'Method used to finance the acquisition.']
  lease_end_date date [note: 'End date of the lease term.']
  lease_obligation_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the acquisition includes a lease obligation subject to IFRS 16 / ASC 842.']
  lease_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the lease term.']
  lease_term_years varchar [note: 'Duration of the lease component, if applicable.']
  lender_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the financial institution providing the loan.']
  lender_name varchar [note: 'Name of the lender or bank.']
  loan_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Principal amount of any mortgage or loan used for the acquisition.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or remarks about the acquisition.']
  property_acquisition_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the acquisition transaction.']
  property_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the property asset.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating operational system (e.g., SAP S/4HANA, Coupa).']
  title_company_code varchar [note: 'Identifier of the title company that performed the title search and insurance.']
  title_company_name varchar [note: 'Name of the title company handling the transaction.']
  title_insurance_policy_number varchar [note: 'Policy number of the title insurance covering the acquisition.']
  total_acquisition_cost decimal(18,2) [note: 'Sum of acquisition price and all closing costs.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the acquisition record.']

  Note: 'Master record for each owned property acquisition transaction, capturing property address, acquisition date, purchase price, closing costs, total acquisition cost, seller entity, title company, financing structure (cash, mortgage, sale-leaseback), loan amount, lender, capitalization rate, due diligence completion date, environmental assessment status, title insurance policy number, and deed recording reference. Supports fixed asset creation in SAP S/4HANA FA module and owned property portfolio management.'
}

Table realestate.site_permit {
  site_permit_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the site permit record.']
  franchisee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to franchise.franchisee. Business justification: Permit Management reports tie each permit to the franchisee responsible for compliance and renewal tracking.']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant site associated with the permit.']
  application_date date [note: 'Date the permit application was initially submitted.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date the permit was approved by the authority.']
  audit_trail varchar [note: 'Audit trail details for changes to the permit record.']
  compliance_deadline date [note: 'Deadline for achieving compliance as per permit conditions.']
  compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Notes related to compliance actions or observations.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of the permit.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the permit record was created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO currency code for the permit fee.']
  document_storage_path varchar [note: 'File system path or storage identifier for the permit document.']
  document_url varchar [note: 'Link to the digital copy of the permit document.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date the permit becomes effective (usually approval date).']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date the permit ceases to be effective (expiration or revocation).']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date the permit expires and must be renewed.']
  fee_paid_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the permit fee has been paid.']
  fee_payment_date date [note: 'Date the permit fee was paid.']
  inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent inspection.']
  inspection_due_date date [note: 'Date by which the required inspection must be completed.']
  inspection_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if an inspection is required for this permit.']
  inspection_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the most recent inspection.']
  inspection_schedule varchar [note: 'Scheduled date(s) or frequency for required inspections.']
  issuing_authority varchar [note: 'Governmental or municipal authority that issues the permit.']
  last_inspection_comments varchar [note: 'Comments recorded during the last inspection.']
  last_inspection_observer varchar [note: 'Name or identifier of the person/agency who performed the last inspection.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text notes regarding the permit.']
  permit_category varchar [note: 'High-level category of the permit purpose.']
  permit_expiry_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the permit expires.']
  permit_fee decimal(12,2) [note: 'Fee charged for the permit.']
  permit_issue_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Exact timestamp when the permit was officially issued.']
  permit_number varchar [note: 'Official permit number assigned by the issuing authority.']
  permit_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the permit application.']
  permit_subtype varchar [note: 'More specific subtype of the permit, if applicable.']
  permit_type varchar [note: 'Category of the permit.']
  renewal_date date [note: 'Date by which the permit must be renewed.']
  renewal_required_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the permit requires renewal.']
  revocation_date date [note: 'Date the permit was revoked, if applicable.']
  revocation_reason varchar [note: 'Reason for permit revocation.']
  site_permit_status varchar [note: 'Overall lifecycle status of the permit record.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Source system where the permit data originated (e.g., ERP, compliance system).']
  submission_date date [note: 'Date the permit application was formally submitted to the authority.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the permit record.']

  Note: 'Transactional record tracking each regulatory permit required for restaurant construction, remodel, or operation at a site, capturing permit type (building, electrical, plumbing, mechanical, fire, health department, signage, drive-thru, outdoor seating, liquor), issuing authority (municipality, county, state), application date, submission date, approval date, expiration date, permit number, permit fee, inspection schedule, and compliance status. Supports NRO critical path management and ongoing operational compliance.'
}

Table realestate.trade_area {
  trade_area_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique system-generated identifier for each trade area record.']
  area_sq_miles decimal(8,2) [note: 'Surface area of the primary trade area measured in square miles.']
  average_household_size decimal(4,2) [note: 'Mean number of individuals per household in the trade area.']
  average_income_per_capita decimal(12,2) [note: 'Mean individual income within the trade area, expressed in US dollars.']
  cannibalization_risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Quantitative score (0‑100) estimating sales erosion risk due to nearby existing units.']
  city varchar [note: 'Municipality of the restaurant location.']
  competition_count varchar [note: 'Count of direct competitor locations inside the trade area.']
  competition_names varchar [note: 'Names of competing restaurant brands present in the trade area.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the trade area record was initially inserted into the system.']
  data_vintage_date date [note: 'Reference date indicating the snapshot date of the demographic and traffic data.']
  daytime_population varchar [note: 'Estimated total population present in the trade area during typical business hours.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Start date of the trade area’s validity period.']
  effective_until date [note: 'End date of the trade area’s validity period; null if open‑ended.']
  employment_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the working‑age population that is employed.']
  geographic_region varchar [note: 'Higher‑level region classification for reporting purposes.']
  latitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic latitude in decimal degrees.']
  longitude decimal(9,6) [note: 'Geographic longitude in decimal degrees.']
  market_share_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Analyst‑derived score (0‑100) indicating the site’s potential market share.']
  median_age varchar [note: 'Median age of residents within the trade area.']
  median_household_income decimal(12,2) [note: 'Median annual household income in US dollars for households within the trade area.']
  methodology varchar [note: 'Technique applied to generate the trade area boundaries (drive‑time, radius, or Voronoi).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional remarks or observations entered by analysts.']
  population_density_per_sq_mile decimal(8,2) [note: 'Number of people per square mile in the primary trade area.']
  primary_boundary_drive_time_minutes decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum travel time in minutes for the primary trade area; null if not applicable.']
  primary_boundary_geojson varchar [note: 'GeoJSON representation of the primary trade‑area polygon.']
  primary_boundary_radius_miles decimal(6,2) [note: 'Radius in miles for a circular primary trade area; null if not applicable.']
  projected_auv decimal(12,2) [note: 'Forecasted annual sales for the restaurant location, expressed in US dollars.']
  projected_cogs_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated percentage of sales that will be spent on food and beverage costs.']
  projected_labor_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Estimated percentage of sales allocated to labor expenses.']
  residential_population varchar [note: 'Count of people who reside in the trade area.']
  secondary_boundary_drive_time_minutes decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum travel time in minutes for the secondary trade area; null if not applicable.']
  secondary_boundary_geojson varchar [note: 'GeoJSON representation of the secondary trade‑area polygon.']
  secondary_boundary_radius_miles decimal(6,2) [note: 'Radius in miles for a circular secondary trade area; null if not applicable.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the operational system of record (e.g., FranConnect) that generated the trade‑area data.']
  state varchar [note: 'Standard US state abbreviation (e.g., CA, NY).']
  trade_area_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive label used by analysts and planners to identify the trade area.']
  trade_area_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the trade area is currently used for planning, retired, or archived.']
  traffic_adt varchar [note: 'Average number of vehicles passing per day on primary arteries within the trade area.']
  traffic_peak_hour varchar [note: 'Maximum vehicle count observed during the busiest hour of the day.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the latest modification to the trade area record.']
  version varchar [note: 'Incremental version used to track changes and resolve concurrent updates.']
  zip_code varchar [note: 'Five‑digit (or ZIP+4) postal code of the restaurant location.']

  Note: 'Master record for trade area analysis and market development planning, serving both site-level evaluation and market-level strategic planning. Site level: captures trade area methodology (drive-time, radius, Voronoi), primary and secondary trade area boundaries, daytime and residential population, median household income, traffic count (ADT), competition count, cannibalization risk score, projected AUV, and data vintage date. Market level: captures market name, tier classification, total addressable restaurant count, current open unit count, pipeline unit count, whitespace unit count, development priority score, target opening cadence by year, franchise vs. company-owned split target, market development agreement reference, and plan approval date. Supports site selection scoring, market planning, portfolio optimization, NRO pipeline prioritization, franchise territory allocation, and strategic real estate portfolio planning.'
}

Table realestate.realestate_remodel_project {
  realestate_remodel_project_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the realestate_remodel_project data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.contract. Business justification: Each remodel project is executed under a procurement contract with a supplier; required for budgeting and regulatory audit.']
  facility_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to realestate.facility. Business justification: Remodel projects are executed on a Facility; linking provides context and removes duplicate geolocation fields.']

  Note: 'Master record for each restaurant remodel or refresh project, capturing remodel type (full remodel, image refresh, drive-thru upgrade, digital menu board installation, accessibility upgrade), scope of work, CapEx budget reference, funding source (company-funded, franchisee-funded, co-investment), required completion date per brand standards, actual completion date, remodel generation/prototype version, before and after facility condition scores, franchisee consent status, and project milestone tracking. Distinct from NRO projects — remodels apply to existing operating restaurants. Supports brand image compliance, franchise agreement remodel obligations, and facility lifecycle management.'
}

Table realestate.menu_item_site_offering {
  menu_item_site_offering_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the MenuItemSiteOffering association']
  menu_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the menu_item']
  site_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the site']
  discontinue_date date [note: 'Date the menu item is removed from the site menu']
  is_lto boolean [note: 'Indicates if the offering is a limited‑time promotion at the site']
  launch_date date [note: 'Date the menu item becomes available at the site']
  lto_end_date date [note: 'End date of the limited‑time offer for this site']
  lto_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the limited‑time offer for this site']

  Note: 'Represents the assignment of a menu_item to a site with site‑specific availability dates and limited‑time‑offer status. Each record links one menu_item to one site and captures attributes that exist only in the context of this relationship.. Existence Justification: A menu item can be sold at many restaurant sites, and each site serves many menu items. The business actively manages which items are offered at each site, tracking site‑specific launch and discontinue dates as well as limited‑time‑offer flags.'
}

  is_primary_tenant boolean [note: 'True if this tenant is the primary occupant of the property; false otherwise.']

  state_province varchar [note: 'State or province component of the tenant\'s address.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the tenant relationship.']
  tenant_type varchar [note: 'Category describing the nature of the tenant entity.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for tenant. Referenced by tenant_id.'

Table procurement.procurement_supplier {
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the supplier record.']

  address_line varchar [note: 'First line of the supplier\'s mailing address.']
  average_lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Average number of days between purchase order issuance and receipt.']
  bank_account_number varchar [note: 'Supplier\'s bank account number for electronic payments.']
  bank_routing_number varchar [note: 'Routing number (or equivalent) for the supplier\'s bank.']
  city varchar [note: 'City component of the supplier\'s mailing address.']
  classification varchar [note: 'Diversity status of the supplier (e.g., Minority‑Owned Business).']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance standing of the supplier with regulatory and internal standards.']
  contract_end_date date [note: 'Scheduled termination date of the supplier contract (null if open‑ended).']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the active contract with the supplier.']
  contract_start_date date [note: 'Effective start date of the supplier contract.']
  country varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO country code where the supplier is located.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the supplier record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code used for transactions with the supplier.']
  default_tax_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Standard tax percentage applied to purchases from this supplier.']
  discount_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Default discount percentage applied to purchases from this supplier.']
  email_address varchar [note: 'General email address for the supplier organization.']
  global_supplier_number varchar [note: 'External identifier used across multiple enterprise systems to reference the supplier.']
  insurance_expiry_date date [note: 'Expiration date of the supplier\'s liability or general insurance coverage.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent compliance audit performed on the supplier.']
  legal_name varchar [note: 'Full legal entity name of the supplier as registered with government authorities.']
  liability_limit decimal(12,2) [note: 'Maximum liability coverage amount provided by the supplier\'s insurance.']
  max_order_quantity varchar [note: 'Maximum quantity that can be ordered from the supplier per transaction.']
  min_order_quantity varchar [note: 'Minimum quantity that can be ordered from the supplier per transaction.']
  onboarding_status varchar [note: 'Progress of the supplier through the onboarding workflow.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment condition agreed with the supplier.']
  phone_number varchar [note: 'General telephone number for the supplier organization.']
  postal_code varchar [note: 'Postal/ZIP code of the supplier\'s mailing address.']
  preferred_supplier_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the supplier is marked as a preferred vendor.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary supplier contact.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the main point‑of‑contact for the supplier.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the primary supplier contact.']
  procurement_supplier_name varchar [note: 'Primary display name of the supplier used in reports and UI.']
  procurement_supplier_status varchar [note: 'Current operational status of the supplier record.']
  remittance_address varchar [note: 'Address to which payment remittances should be sent.']
  risk_tier varchar [note: 'Risk assessment tier based on financial, compliance, and operational factors.']
  spend_ytd decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total spend with the supplier for the current fiscal year.']
  state varchar [note: 'State or province component of the supplier\'s mailing address.']
  supplier_type varchar [note: 'Broad category describing the primary goods or services supplied.']
  tax_identifier varchar [note: 'Government‑issued tax identifier (e.g., EIN) for the supplier.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time of the most recent update to the supplier record.']

  Note: 'Master record for every approved and prospective supplier/vendor managed in Coupa Procurement and synchronized to SAP S/4HANA vendor master. Captures supplier identity, legal entity details, tax IDs (EIN/TIN), diversity classifications (MBE/WBE/SBE/LGBTBE), payment terms, remittance addresses, risk tier, onboarding status, preferred supplier flags, and contact details (account managers, sales reps, quality liaisons). SSOT for commercial supplier identity in procurement — distinct from supply domain which tracks physical shipment performance. Covers food, packaging, equipment, and services categories.'
}

  approved_vendor_list_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the approved vendor list record.']

  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: Approved vendor list must reference the supplier it approves; this creates the missing inbound/outbound link for the table.']
  approval_date date [note: 'Date when the vendor was approved.']
  approved_status varchar [note: 'Current approval status of the vendor.']
  audit_requirement varchar [note: 'Specific audit or compliance requirements for the vendor (e.g., HACCP, FDA).']
  bank_account_number varchar [note: 'Bank account number used for vendor payments.']
  category_scope varchar [note: 'Product or service categories the vendor is approved to supply.']
  compliance_documents varchar [note: 'Identifiers or references to compliance documents on file for the vendor.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Current compliance status of the vendor.']
  contract_end_date date [note: 'End date of the vendor\'s contract.']
  contract_start_date date [note: 'Start date of the vendor\'s contract.']
  contract_terms_summary varchar [note: 'Brief summary of key contract terms.']

  disqualification_date date [note: 'Date when the vendor was disqualified.']

  expiry_date date [note: 'Date when the vendor\'s approval expires.']
