  insurance_certificate_expiry date [note: 'Expiration date of the vendor\'s liability insurance certificate.']
  is_currently_approved boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the vendor is presently approved for procurement.']
  last_audit_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent audit performed on the vendor.']
  last_audit_result varchar [note: 'Outcome of the most recent audit.']
  last_modified_by varchar [note: 'User or system that performed the most recent update.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑text notes regarding the vendor.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment terms agreed with the vendor (e.g., Net 30).']
  preferred_vendor_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the vendor is marked as a preferred supplier.']
  primary_contact_email varchar [note: 'Email address of the primary business contact.']
  primary_contact_name varchar [note: 'Name of the primary business contact for the vendor.']
  primary_contact_phone varchar [note: 'Phone number of the primary business contact.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Risk assessment score (0‑100) for the vendor.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Source system where the vendor record originated.']
  tax_id_number varchar [note: 'Vendor\'s tax identification number for reporting.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the record.']
  vendor_category_code varchar [note: 'Internal code used to categorize the vendor.']
  vendor_identifier varchar [note: 'Unique vendor code used in Coupa Procurement.']
  vendor_rating decimal(3,2) [note: 'Average performance rating (0‑5) for the vendor.']
  vendor_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the vendor based on its role in the supply chain.']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User or system that created the record.']

  Note: 'Authoritative registry of suppliers approved to supply specific categories, items, or restaurant segments. Captures approval status, approval date, expiry date, approving authority, category scope, geographic scope (national/regional), audit requirements, and disqualification history. Governs which suppliers may receive purchase orders — a critical food safety and compliance control.'
}

Table procurement.category {
  category_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the procurement_category data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']

  Note: 'Procurement spend category hierarchy used for category management — food ingredients, packaging materials, smallwares, equipment, facilities services, marketing materials, and technology. Captures category code, parent category, category manager assignment, strategic classification (direct/indirect), spend threshold, and sourcing strategy. Enables PMIX-aligned category management and COGS% analysis.'
}

Table procurement.sourcing_event {
  sourcing_event_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the sourcing event.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Internal employee responsible for managing the sourcing event.']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the supplier that won the event.']
  stakeholder_employee_id varchar [note: 'Internal employee responsible for managing the sourcing event.']
  award_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the contract awarded to the supplier.']
  award_date timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the award was formally issued to the winning supplier.']
  award_decision varchar [note: 'Result of the sourcing event indicating whether a supplier was awarded.']
  category_scope varchar [note: 'Business category or commodity group the sourcing event targets.']
  compliance_requirements varchar [note: 'Regulatory or internal compliance standards applicable to the event (e.g., FDA, OSHA, ISO 22000).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the sourcing event record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 currency code for award_amount.']
  evaluation_criteria varchar [note: 'Textual definition of the criteria used to evaluate supplier responses.']
  event_code varchar [note: 'External business code or number assigned to the sourcing event for reference in contracts and reports.']
  event_end_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the sourcing event closes for further submissions.']
  event_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the sourcing event, e.g., "2024 Q1 Food Commodity RFQ".']
  event_notes varchar [note: 'Additional free‑form notes captured by the procurement team.']
  event_start_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the sourcing event officially opens for supplier participation.']
  event_type varchar [note: 'Type of sourcing event indicating the procurement method used.']
  is_confidential boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the sourcing event details are confidential.']
  lifecycle_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the sourcing event.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system that originated the sourcing event record (e.g., Coupa).']
  sourcing_event_description varchar [note: 'Free‑text description providing context, objectives, and any special notes for the event.']
  submission_deadline timestamp [note: 'Date‑time by which suppliers must submit their responses.']
  total_budget decimal(18,2) [note: 'Maximum budget allocated for the sourcing event.']
  updated_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the employee who last modified the sourcing event record.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the sourcing event record.']
  weighting_scheme varchar [note: 'Details of how each evaluation criterion is weighted (e.g., price 40%, quality 30%).']
  created_by varchar [note: 'User identifier of the employee who created the sourcing event record.']

  Note: 'Formal competitive sourcing event (RFI, RFP, RFQ, reverse auction) managed through Coupa. Captures event type, event name, category scope, participating suppliers, submission deadlines, evaluation criteria, weighting, award decision, and event status. Supports strategic sourcing for food commodities, packaging, equipment, and services. Tracks LTO-driven sourcing events and annual contract renewals.'
}

Table procurement.sourcing_response {
  sourcing_response_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the supplier\'s response to a sourcing event.']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the supplier submitting the response.']
  sourcing_event_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the sourcing event (RFI/RFP/RFQ) to which this response belongs.']
  award_status varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the response resulted in a contract award.']
  bid_type varchar [note: 'Type of sourcing solicitation the response addresses.']
  compliance_attestations varchar [note: 'Supplier’s attestations to regulatory or contractual compliance (e.g., FDA, OSHA).']
  compliance_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score reflecting the supplier’s compliance with regulatory and contractual requirements.']
  contract_term_months varchar [note: 'Proposed duration of the contract in months.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the response record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for pricing.']
  delivery_terms varchar [note: 'Conditions governing delivery (e.g., FOB, DDP).']
  discount_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Discount applied to the base price before tax.']
  disqualification_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided if the response was disqualified.']
  is_eligible boolean [note: 'True if the supplier meets all mandatory eligibility criteria.']
  is_preferred_supplier boolean [note: 'Indicates if the supplier is designated as a preferred vendor.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Estimated number of days from order receipt to delivery.']
  minimum_order_quantity varchar [note: 'Smallest quantity the supplier will accept for this offer.']
  net_price decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final price after tax and discount adjustments.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Supplier\'s payment conditions (e.g., Net 30).']
  price_per_unit decimal(12,4) [note: 'Unit price quoted for the primary SKU or service.']
  quality_certifications varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of quality or safety certifications supplied (e.g., ISO 22000, HACCP).']
  response_comments varchar [note: 'Free‑form notes or comments supplied by the supplier.']
  response_number varchar [note: 'Business identifier assigned to the response for tracking and reference.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Assessed risk category of the supplier’s proposal.']
  scoring_rank varchar [note: 'Rank position of the response among all submitted bids.']
  scoring_total decimal(5,2) [note: 'Aggregated score assigned by the evaluation team.']
  sourcing_response_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the response.']
  submission_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the supplier submitted the response.']
  supplier_rating decimal(4,2) [note: 'Overall rating of the supplier based on historical performance.']
  tax_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Tax component included in the total price.']
  total_price decimal(18,2) [note: 'Aggregate monetary amount quoted in the response, before any adjustments.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit associated with the unit price (e.g., each, kg, liter).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the response record.']
  valid_until date [note: 'Date until which the quoted terms and pricing remain valid.']
  warranty_period_months varchar [note: 'Length of warranty offered, expressed in months.']

  Note: 'Supplier bid or response submitted to a sourcing event (RFI/RFP/RFQ). Captures responding supplier, submitted pricing, proposed lead times, quality certifications offered, compliance attestations, scoring results, disqualification reasons, and award status. Enables side-by-side supplier evaluation and supports audit trails for sourcing decisions.'
}

Table procurement.contract {
  contract_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the contract record.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Internal employee responsible for overall contract stewardship.']
  franchisee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to franchise.franchisee. Business justification: Required for Contract Management Report: each franchisee signs supplier contracts; linking enables tracking contract compliance per franchisee.']
  owner_employee_id varchar [note: 'Internal employee responsible for overall contract stewardship.']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the supplier party associated with the contract.']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to restaurant.unit. Business justification: Contracts are tied to specific restaurant units for equipment leases and services; unit-level contract reporting depends on this FK.']
  amendment_count varchar [note: 'Number of amendments or addenda applied to the contract.']
  auto_renewal_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the contract automatically renews at expiry.']
  compliance_requirements varchar [note: 'Regulatory or internal compliance obligations tied to the contract.']
  confidentiality_level varchar [note: 'Data classification level for the contract record.']
  contract_name varchar [note: 'Descriptive name of the contract for easy identification.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier or reference number assigned to the contract.']
  contract_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the contract.']
  contract_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the agreement (e.g., MSA, BPA, framework).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the contract record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for the contract value.']
  discount_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount applied to the base price, if any.']
  document_url varchar [note: 'Link to the stored electronic contract document.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the contract expires or ends; null for open‑ended agreements.']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the contract becomes legally binding.']
  exclusivity_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the supplier has exclusive rights for the supplied goods/services.']
  governing_body varchar [note: 'Regulatory or standards organization governing the contract terms.']
  last_review_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent contract performance or compliance review.']
  manager_contact varchar [note: 'Email address of the contract manager or primary point of contact.']
  next_renewal_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next contract renewal cycle.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment condition agreed with the supplier.']
  penalty_clause varchar [note: 'Financial or operational penalties for SLA breaches or contract violations.']
  pricing_model varchar [note: 'Method used to calculate pricing under the contract.']
  rebate_terms varchar [note: 'Conditions under which rebates are earned and applied.']
  regulatory_approval_status varchar [note: 'Status of required regulatory approvals for the contract.']
  renewal_term_months varchar [note: 'Length of each renewal period in months when auto‑renewal is enabled.']
  scope_of_supply varchar [note: 'Description of goods, services, or deliverables covered by the contract.']
  sla_commitment varchar [note: 'Key performance metrics and service levels promised by the supplier.']
  termination_date date [note: 'Date on which the contract was terminated, if applicable.']
  termination_reason varchar [note: 'Reason provided for early termination of the contract.']
  total_value decimal(18,2) [note: 'Monetary value of the contract covering all line items and services.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the contract record.']
  version_number varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the contract document (e.g., v1.0, v2.1).']

  Note: 'Master procurement contract governing the commercial relationship with a supplier — supply agreements, master service agreements (MSAs), pricing agreements, and blanket purchase orders — managed in Coupa Contract Management. Captures contract type, effective/expiry dates, auto-renewal flags, royalty/rebate terms, exclusivity clauses, SLA commitments, termination conditions, total contract value, and amendment history. SSOT for contractual terms in the procurement domain. Supports annual contract renewal cycles aligned with commodity market timing and LTO launch schedules.'
}

Table procurement.contract_line {
  contract_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for each contract line item.']
  approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Contract line changes require manager approval; linking to employee enables contract amendment audit.']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent procurement contract to which this line belongs.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: REQUIRED: Contract line items are budgeted to specific cost centers, essential for capex tracking and compliance reporting.']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the supplier providing the item.']
  stock_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_item. Business justification: Enables mapping contract line SKUs to inventory items for cost tracking, COGS calculation, and contract performance reporting.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the contract line was approved.']
  compliance_requirements varchar [note: 'Regulatory or certification requirements applicable to the line (e.g., FDA, ISO 22000).']
  contract_line_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the contract line.']
  contract_line_type varchar [note: 'Category of the line item (e.g., goods, service).']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time when the contract line record was created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the price.']
  delivery_location_code varchar [note: 'Internal code representing the delivery destination for the item.']
  effective_end_date date [note: 'Date when the contract line expires or is superseded (null for open‑ended).']
  effective_start_date date [note: 'Date when the contract line becomes binding.']
  is_price_locked boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the negotiated price is locked for the contract duration.']
  is_renewable boolean [note: 'True if the contract line can be automatically renewed.']
  item_description varchar [note: 'Human‑readable description of the product or service.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of days from order to delivery for this line.']
  line_sequence varchar [note: 'Ordinal position of the line within the contract for ordering and display.']
  maximum_order_quantity varchar [note: 'Largest quantity allowed per order for this line.']
  minimum_order_quantity varchar [note: 'Smallest quantity that can be ordered under this contract line.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Additional remarks or special conditions.']
  price_escalation_clause varchar [note: 'Text describing any price escalation mechanisms (e.g., CPI‑linked).']
  price_escalation_frequency varchar [note: 'How often the escalation clause is applied.']
  price_escalation_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage increase applied at each escalation interval.']
  price_tier_end_quantity varchar [note: 'Upper bound quantity for a volume‑based price tier (null if open‑ended).']
  price_tier_start_quantity varchar [note: 'Lower bound quantity for a volume‑based price tier.']
  regulatory_approval_status varchar [note: 'Current status of required regulatory approvals.']
  renewal_option varchar [note: 'Method by which renewal is handled.']
  sku varchar [note: 'Standardized product code identifying the item covered by the contract line.']
  tax_rate_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Applicable tax rate for the line item.']
  tier_price decimal(12,4) [note: 'Unit price that applies when order quantity falls within the tier range.']
  unit_price decimal(12,4) [note: 'Agreed price per unit for the line item.']
  uom varchar [note: 'Measurement unit used for quantity and pricing.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date‑time of the most recent modification to the record.']
  waste_percentage_allowed decimal(5,2) [note: 'Maximum permitted waste percentage for the supplied item.']
  yield_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Expected usable yield of the product after processing.']

  Note: 'Individual line items within a procurement contract or standalone pricing agreement specifying the exact SKU, ingredient, packaging item, or service covered. Captures negotiated unit price, UOM, minimum order quantity (MOQ), volume tiers, price escalation clauses (CPI-linked, commodity index-linked), volume commitments, effective date ranges, and approval status. Covers both full contract lines and standalone price agreements. Enables granular contract compliance checking against actual PO prices and supports commodity cost management.'
}

Table procurement.requisition {
  requisition_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the purchase requisition.']
  approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who approved the requisition.']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Link to a master agreement governing the purchase, if applicable.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: REQUIRED: Requisition approval must allocate costs to a cost center for budgeting and expense tracking, a standard finance‑procurement process.']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee who initially entered the requisition into the system.']
  department_id varchar [note: 'Organizational department responsible for the requisition.']
  employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who originated the requisition.']
  franchisee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to franchise.franchisee. Business justification: Supports Requisition Approval Workflow: franchisee initiates requisitions for supplies; FK ties request to owning franchisee for budgeting.']
  requisition_approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who approved the requisition.']
  requisition_created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Employee who initially entered the requisition into the system.']
  requisition_employee_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the employee who originated the requisition.']
  restaurant_unit_id varchar [note: 'Restaurant unit or site for which the requisition is raised.']
  stock_item_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_item. Business justification: Requisition process requests specific inventory SKUs; linking enables inventory reservation and demand forecasting.']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Restaurant unit or site for which the requisition is raised.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval state of the requisition.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the requisition received final approval.']
  budget_code varchar [note: 'Budget line or code governing the allowable spend.']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'True if the requisition must satisfy specific regulatory or food‑safety compliance.']
  compliance_notes varchar [note: 'Additional notes regarding compliance requirements or certifications.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the requisition record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the estimated amounts.']
  delivery_method varchar [note: 'Preferred method for receiving the goods.']
  discount_estimate decimal(15,2) [note: 'Projected discount or rebate amount applied to the requisition.']
  expected_delivery_date date [note: 'Target date for receipt of goods or services.']
  justification_text varchar [note: 'Free‑form description explaining the need for the purchase.']
  line_item_count varchar [note: 'Count of distinct line‑item entries attached to the requisition.']
  net_estimated_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Estimated total after tax and discount adjustments.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Negotiated payment conditions (e.g., Net 30, Net 60).']
  priority_level varchar [note: 'Business‑defined priority used for routing and approval.']
  procurement_method varchar [note: 'Method used to acquire the goods or services.']
  required_by_date date [note: 'Date by which the requested items must be available for operation.']
  requisition_number varchar [note: 'Human‑readable business identifier assigned to the requisition.']
  requisition_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the requisition.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating source system for the requisition record (e.g., Coupa, SAP).']
  spend_category_code varchar [note: 'Classification of spend (e.g., food, packaging, equipment).']
  supplier_preference varchar [note: 'Indicates if a specific supplier is preferred for this requisition.']
  tax_estimate decimal(15,2) [note: 'Projected tax amount based on applicable tax rules.']
  tax_exempt_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the requisition is tax‑exempt.']
  tax_exempt_reason varchar [note: 'Reason or code for tax exemption, if applicable.']
  total_estimated_amount decimal(15,2) [note: 'Sum of estimated line‑item costs before taxes, discounts, or adjustments.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the requisition record.']
  urgency_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the requisition is marked as urgent.']

  Note: 'Internal purchase requisition (PR) initiating the procure-to-pay workflow in Coupa. Captures requesting department, restaurant unit or support center, requested items, estimated cost, business justification, budget code, urgency flag, preferred supplier, and multi-level approval status with routing history. Represents the demand signal before a purchase order is raised — the first formal step in authorized procurement spend.'
}

Table procurement.procurement_purchase_order {
  procurement_purchase_order_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the purchase order record.']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Reference to a master contract governing the purchase order, if applicable.']
  cost_center_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to finance.cost_center. Business justification: REQUIRED: Purchase orders are charged to cost centers; linking enables accurate financial reporting and spend analysis.']
  created_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Required for PO creation audit report that tracks which employee created each purchase order for accountability.']
  delivery_location_unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant, distribution center, or commissary where the goods are to be delivered.']
  delivery_stock_location_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to inventory.stock_location. Business justification: POs must specify the receiving stock location to schedule receiving, temperature control, and HACCP monitoring.']
  franchisee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to franchise.franchisee. Business justification: Needed for PO Accountability Dashboard: purchase orders are issued by individual franchisees; FK supports audit of spend by franchisee.']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the supplier (vendor) to whom the purchase order is issued.']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the restaurant, distribution center, or commissary where the goods are to be delivered.']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'Date the goods were actually received at the delivery location.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Result of the internal approval workflow for the purchase order.']
  approved_by varchar [note: 'Identifier of the system user who approved the purchase order.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the purchase order received final approval.']
  category_code varchar [note: 'Internal classification of the purchase order line items (e.g., FOOD, PACKAGING, EQUIPMENT).']
  compliance_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the purchase order complies with food safety and regulatory requirements.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the purchase order record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used for the purchase order.']
  delivery_address varchar [note: 'Physical address of the delivery location for the purchase order.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total discount granted on the purchase order.']
  external_reference_number varchar [note: 'Vendor‑provided reference number for the purchase order.']
  freight_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Cost of transportation and shipping associated with the purchase order.']
  internal_comments varchar [note: 'Internal remarks visible only to authorized personnel.']
  is_consolidated boolean [note: 'True if the purchase order aggregates multiple requisitions or suppliers.']
  is_urgent boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the purchase order requires expedited processing.']
  last_received_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent goods receipt against this purchase order.']
  line_item_count varchar [note: 'Number of distinct line items included on the purchase order.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final payable amount after taxes, discounts, and freight are applied.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form text field for additional information or special instructions.']
  order_date date [note: 'Date the purchase order was created and issued to the supplier.']
  payment_due_date date [note: 'Date by which payment for the purchase order must be received.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Contractual payment condition (e.g., Net30, Net45).']
  po_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the purchase order.']
  po_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the purchase order based on its contractual nature.']
  priority varchar [note: 'Business priority assigned to the purchase order for processing and fulfillment.']
  promised_delivery_date date [note: 'Date the supplier has committed to deliver the goods.']
  purchase_order_number varchar [note: 'External business identifier assigned to the purchase order, used in vendor communications and reporting.']
  receipt_status varchar [note: 'Current status of goods receipt for the purchase order.']
  regulatory_approval_status varchar [note: 'Status of any required regulatory approvals (e.g., FDA, USDA) for the ordered items.']
  requested_delivery_date date [note: 'Date the purchasing organization requests the goods to be delivered.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that created the purchase order record.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tax applied to the purchase order.']
  tax_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Applicable tax rate percentage for the purchase order.']
  total_amount_gross decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total monetary value of the purchase order before taxes, discounts, and freight.']
  total_weight_kg decimal(12,3) [note: 'Aggregate weight of all items on the purchase order, expressed in kilograms.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the purchase order record.']

  Note: 'Formal purchase order (PO) issued to a supplier following requisition approval, generated in Coupa Procurement and transmitted via EDI/cXML to suppliers. Captures PO number, supplier, delivery location (restaurant, DC, or commissary), ordered items, quantities, agreed unit prices, requested delivery date, payment terms, freight terms, and PO status (open/partially received/closed/cancelled). SSOT for commercial purchase commitments — distinct from the supply domain\'s goods receipt and physical movement tracking. Supports auto-PO generation from PAR-level triggers and blanket PO release scheduling.'
}

Table procurement.po_line {
  po_line_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the purchase order line.']
  budget_line_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the budget line to which this purchase is charged.']
  contract_line_id varchar [note: 'Reference to the contract line that governs pricing or terms for this PO line.']
  po_header_procurement_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent purchase order header to which this line belongs.']
  procurement_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the parent purchase order header to which this line belongs.']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the supplier providing the item.']
  product_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the product or SKU referenced by this line.']
  actual_delivery_date date [note: 'Date the line items were actually received.']
  compliance_flag varchar [note: 'Indicates whether the item meets required food safety or regulatory standards.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Internal cost center associated with the expense.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the purchase order line record was created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three-letter ISO 4217 currency code for monetary values on the line.']
  delivery_status varchar [note: 'Status of the line\'s delivery to the restaurant.']
  discount_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Monetary discount applied to the line.']
  expected_delivery_date date [note: 'Planned date for receipt of the line items.']
  extended_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Total amount for the line before discounts and taxes (ordered_quantity * unit_price).']
  invoice_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the line was invoiced.']
  invoiced_quantity decimal(12,4) [note: 'Quantity of the item that has been invoiced.']
  is_late boolean [note: 'True if actual delivery date is later than expected delivery date.']
  is_three_way_match boolean [note: 'Indicates whether PO, receipt, and invoice have been fully matched.']
  item_description varchar [note: 'Free-text description of the purchased item.']
  item_sku varchar [note: 'Stock Keeping Unit code for the purchased item.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between PO creation and actual delivery.']
  line_number varchar [note: 'Sequential number of the line within the purchase order.']
  line_status varchar [note: 'Current processing status of the purchase order line.']
  line_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the line content (e.g., goods, service, fee).']
  net_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Final amount after discounts and taxes (extended_amount - discount_amount + tax_amount).']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free-text field for additional comments or special instructions.']
  ordered_quantity decimal(12,4) [note: 'Quantity of the item requested on the purchase order line.']
  receipt_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when receipt of the line was recorded.']
  received_quantity decimal(12,4) [note: 'Quantity of the item that has been received against this line.']
  tax_amount decimal(12,2) [note: 'Tax calculated for the line based on applicable tax rules.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Internal tax code used to determine tax treatment.']
  tax_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Applicable tax rate percentage for the line.']
  unit_price decimal(12,4) [note: 'Negotiated price per unit of the item, before taxes and discounts.']
  uom varchar [note: 'Unit of measure for quantity fields (e.g., each, kg, lb).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the line record.']
  waste_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of the ordered quantity that was wasted or scrapped.']

  Note: 'Individual line items on a purchase order specifying the exact item (food SKU, packaging SKU, equipment, or service), ordered quantity, UOM, negotiated unit price, extended amount, contract line reference, and line-level delivery status. Enables COGS% tracking at the ingredient/SKU level and supports three-way match (PO → receipt → invoice).'
}

Table procurement.supplier_invoice {
  supplier_invoice_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the supplier invoice record.']
  approved_by_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Invoice approval must be recorded against an employee to satisfy internal control and audit requirements.']
  contract_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supplier contract governing the invoice.']
  procurement_purchase_order_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.purchase_order. Business justification: Supplier invoices are generated against a purchase order; adding purchase_order_id FK creates the proper parent link and removes the redundant free‑text PO number.']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the supplier who issued the invoice.']
  supplier_tax_id varchar [note: 'Government‑issued tax identifier for the supplier.']
  unit_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to restaurant.unit. Business justification: Financial reporting allocates invoice costs to the restaurant unit that incurred the expense; unit_id on invoice enables unit‑level P&L.']
  approval_status varchar [note: 'Current approval workflow state for the invoice.']
  approved_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the invoice was approved.']
  attached_document_url varchar [note: 'Link to the scanned PDF or electronic copy of the invoice.']
  category_code varchar [note: 'Internal classification code for reporting (e.g., food, packaging, equipment).']
  cogs_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'COGS% derived from the invoice for profitability analysis.']
  cost_center_code varchar [note: 'Financial cost center to which the invoice expense is charged.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the invoice record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO 4217 code of the currency used on the invoice.']
  discount_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total discount applied to the invoice (e.g., early‑payment discount).']
  dispute_reason varchar [note: 'Free‑text description of why the invoice is disputed.']
  due_date date [note: 'Date by which payment must be received to avoid penalties.']
  early_payment_discount_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage discount offered for early payment.']
  early_payment_due_date date [note: 'Last date to capture the early‑payment discount.']
  exchange_rate decimal(12,6) [note: 'Currency conversion rate to the corporate reporting currency at invoice posting.']
  external_comments varchar [note: 'Supplier‑provided remarks visible to external stakeholders.']
  gross_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total amount before taxes, discounts, or other adjustments.']
  internal_comments varchar [note: 'Private notes for internal accounting or procurement teams.']
  invoice_date date [note: 'Date the supplier issued the invoice.']
  invoice_number varchar [note: 'External invoice identifier assigned by the supplier.']
  invoice_type varchar [note: 'Classification of the invoice content.']
  is_disputed boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the invoice is currently under dispute.']
  line_item_count varchar [note: 'Number of distinct line items on the invoice.']
  net_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Final amount payable after taxes and discounts.']
  payment_date date [note: 'Date on which payment was posted.']
  payment_method varchar [note: 'Method used to settle the invoice.']
  payment_status varchar [note: 'Current status of payment against the invoice.']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Standard payment condition agreed with the supplier.']
  receipt_date date [note: 'Date the goods/services were received.']
  receipt_number varchar [note: 'Identifier of the goods receipt document.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Originating system that supplied the invoice data.']
  supplier_invoice_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the invoice.']
  tax_amount decimal(18,2) [note: 'Total tax charged on the invoice.']
  tax_exempt_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the invoice is exempt from tax.']
  tax_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Applicable tax rate (percentage) for the invoice.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the invoice record.']
  vat_number varchar [note: 'VAT registration number of the supplier, if applicable.']

  Note: 'Supplier-submitted invoice for goods or services delivered, including header and line-level detail. Captures invoice number, invoice date, supplier reference, PO match status, line-level quantities/amounts/GL coding, tax amounts, currency, payment due date, early payment discount terms, dispute flags, three-way match status (PO → receipt → invoice), and AP processing status. Feeds SAP S/4HANA AP module. Supports COGS% reconciliation and contract compliance verification at the SKU level.'
}

Table procurement.supplier_scorecard {
  supplier_scorecard_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the supplier scorecard record.']
  evaluator_employee_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to workforce.employee. Business justification: Scorecard evaluations are performed by employees; linking captures evaluator identity for performance and conflict‑of‑interest checks.']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the supplier being evaluated.']
  average_lead_time_days decimal(5,2) [note: 'Average number of days between order placement and delivery for the supplier.']
  comments varchar [note: 'Free-text comments from the evaluator regarding supplier performance.']
  compliance_status varchar [note: 'Overall compliance status of the supplier with contractual and regulatory requirements.']
  contract_number varchar [note: 'Reference to the supplier contract associated with this scorecard.']
  corrective_action_count varchar [note: 'Number of corrective actions issued to the supplier during the evaluation period.']
  cost_savings_percent decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of cost savings achieved through the supplier during the period.']
  evaluation_period_end date [note: 'End date of the period covered by this scorecard.']
  evaluation_period_start date [note: 'Start date of the period covered by this scorecard.']
  evaluation_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the supplier performance evaluation was completed.']
  evaluator_department varchar [note: 'Department or business unit of the evaluator.']
  fill_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of order lines fully supplied.']
  invoice_accuracy_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of invoices that matched purchase order terms without discrepancies.']
  next_review_due_date date [note: 'Planned date for the next supplier performance review.']
  on_time_delivery_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of deliveries that arrived on or before the agreed delivery date.']
  overall_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Aggregated overall performance score for the supplier.']
  quality_rejection_rate decimal(5,2) [note: 'Percentage of received items that failed quality inspection.']
  record_created timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the scorecard record was initially created in the system.']
  record_updated timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the scorecard record.']
  region varchar [note: 'Geographic region of the supplier. [ENUM-REF-CANDIDATE: USA|CAN|MEX|GBR|FRA|DEU|JPN|CHN|IND|BRA — promote to reference product]']
  responsiveness_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score reflecting supplier responsiveness to inquiries and issue resolution.']
  risk_level varchar [note: 'Risk classification based on supplier performance.']
  scorecard_number varchar [note: 'External reference number for the scorecard as used in procurement processes.']
  scorecard_version varchar [note: 'Version identifier for the scorecard methodology.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Source system where the scorecard data originated.']
  supplier_category varchar [note: 'Business category of the supplier.']
  supplier_scorecard_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the scorecard.']
  sustainability_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score reflecting the supplier\'s environmental and sustainability performance.']

  Note: 'Periodic and event-driven performance and risk evaluation of suppliers across quality, delivery, pricing compliance, service, and risk dimensions. Captures evaluation period, overall score, on-time delivery rate, fill rate, quality rejection rate, invoice accuracy rate, responsiveness score, corrective action count, individual compliance events (price deviations, off-contract spend, SLA breaches, unauthorized usage), and risk assessments (financial stability, geographic concentration, single-source dependency, food safety compliance, geopolitical risk with risk scores and mitigation plans). Supports AVL maintenance decisions, contract renewal negotiations, supplier accountability tracking, and supply chain resilience planning.'
}

Table procurement.supplier_risk {
  supplier_risk_id varchar [pk, note: 'System-generated unique identifier for the supplier risk assessment record.']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Unique identifier of the supplier to which this risk assessment applies.']
  assessment_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Date and time when the risk assessment was performed.']
  compliance_fda_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the supplier meets FDA food safety compliance requirements.']
  compliance_osha_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the supplier complies with OSHA workplace safety standards.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the risk record was first created in the system.']
  dependency_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Proportion of total spend (as a percentage) that is allocated to this supplier.']
  financial_stability_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Score (0‑100) reflecting the supplier\'s financial health and stability.']
  geographic_region varchar [note: 'Primary geographic region or country where the supplier operates.']
  mitigation_plan varchar [note: 'Narrative description of actions planned or taken to mitigate the identified risk.']
  next_review_date date [note: 'Calendar date when the next risk review is due.']
  review_frequency_days varchar [note: 'Number of days between scheduled risk review cycles.']
  risk_category varchar [note: 'High-level classification of the risk type affecting the supplier.']
  risk_description varchar [note: 'Detailed narrative describing the nature and impact of the risk.']
  risk_factor_details varchar [note: 'Structured or free‑text details of individual risk factors contributing to the overall score.']
  risk_score decimal(5,2) [note: 'Numeric score (0‑100) representing the overall risk severity for the supplier.']
  risk_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the risk assessment record.']
  risk_tier varchar [note: 'Risk tier derived from the risk score, indicating priority for mitigation.']
  single_source_dependency boolean [note: 'True if the organization relies on this supplier for a critical product or service with no viable alternatives.']
  source_system varchar [note: 'Name of the source system where the risk data originated (e.g., Coupa, Zenput).']
  supplier_financial_rating varchar [note: 'Credit rating of the supplier (e.g., AAA, AA, A, BBB, BB, B, CCC).']
  supplier_primary_contact varchar [note: 'Primary contact name or identifier for the supplier (e.g., procurement liaison).']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent modification to the risk record.']

  Note: 'Risk assessment record for a supplier capturing financial stability risk, geographic concentration risk, single-source dependency risk, food safety compliance risk, and geopolitical risk. Captures risk category, risk score, risk tier (critical/high/medium/low), assessment date, mitigation plan, and review frequency. Supports supply chain resilience and business continuity planning.'
}

Table procurement.procurement_supplier_certification {
  procurement_supplier_certification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the procurement_supplier_certification data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']
  secondary_supplier_procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.procurement_supplier. Business justification: Certification records belong to a supplier; adding supplier_id FK eliminates the silo and enables joins to supplier details.']

  Note: 'Compliance certifications held by a supplier — FDA food facility registration, USDA organic certification, SQF/BRC/FSSC 22000 food safety certifications, GMP compliance, allergen management certifications, and sustainability certifications (Rainforest Alliance, Fair Trade). Captures certification type, issuing body, certificate number, issue date, expiry date, and renewal status. Critical for AVL maintenance and HACCP compliance.'
}

Table procurement.item_specification {
  item_specification_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique surrogate key for the item specification record.']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.procurement_category. Business justification: Item specifications belong to a procurement category; adding the FK removes the redundant free‑text category column.']
  allergen_declaration varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of allergens present in the item (e.g., peanuts, dairy).']
  approved_substitutes varchar [note: 'Comma‑separated list of item codes that may replace this item.']
  certification_status varchar [note: 'Current certification state of the item.']
  compliance_fda_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether FDA compliance is mandatory for this item.']
  compliance_usda_required boolean [note: 'Indicates whether USDA compliance is mandatory for this item.']
  cost_per_unit decimal(12,4) [note: 'Standard cost charged by the supplier for one unit.']
  country_of_origin varchar [note: 'ISO‑3166‑1 alpha‑3 code of the country where the item originates.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the specification record was first created.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for cost fields.']
  dietary_restriction varchar [note: 'Dietary attributes applicable to the item.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the specification becomes active.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the specification is retired or superseded.']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the item must not be used.']
  hazardous_material_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the item is classified as hazardous under OSHA regulations.']
  is_perishable boolean [note: 'Indicates whether the item has a limited usable life after receipt.']
  item_specification_code varchar [note: 'Unique business code used to identify the specification across systems.']
  item_specification_name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the item specification.']
  item_specification_status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle status of the specification.']
  last_inspection_date date [note: 'Date of the most recent regulatory or internal inspection.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Expected number of days from order placement to delivery.']
  material varchar [note: 'Primary material composition of the item or its packaging.']
  maximum_order_quantity varchar [note: 'Largest quantity that can be ordered in a single transaction.']
  minimum_order_quantity varchar [note: 'Smallest quantity that can be ordered for this item.']
  notes varchar [note: 'Free‑form comments or special handling instructions.']
  packaging_type varchar [note: 'Primary packaging form for the item.']
  quality_grade varchar [note: 'Quality classification assigned by supplier or internal QA.']
  quantity_per_unit decimal(10,2) [note: 'Number of base units contained in one purchase unit.']
  shelf_life_days varchar [note: 'Maximum number of days the item can be stored before expiration.']
  storage_temperature_c decimal(5,2) [note: 'Recommended storage temperature in degrees Celsius.']
  supplier_requirements varchar [note: 'Specific criteria that suppliers must meet to provide this item.']
  temperature_control_required boolean [note: 'True if the item must be stored under controlled temperature conditions.']
  temperature_range_c varchar [note: 'Acceptable temperature range expressed as min‑max (e.g., "2-8").']
  traceability_required boolean [note: 'Indicates if the item must be tracked through the supply chain for compliance.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Standard unit used for quantity fields.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the specification.']
  volume_liters decimal(10,3) [note: 'Physical volume of the item in liters.']
  waste_percentage decimal(5,2) [note: 'Expected percentage of the item that may be wasted during processing.']
  weight_kg decimal(10,3) [note: 'Net weight of the item in kilograms.']

  Note: 'Procurement item specification defining the exact quality, safety, and technical requirements for a purchased food ingredient, packaging material, or equipment item. Captures item code, item description, category, unit of measure, quality grade, allergen declarations, country of origin requirements, shelf life requirements, temperature requirements, and approved substitute items. Links procurement to menu BOM and food safety requirements.'
}

Table procurement.vendor_rebate {
  vendor_rebate_id varchar [pk, note: 'Unique identifier for the vendor_rebate data product (auto-inserted pre-linking).']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to procurement.supplier. Business justification: Vendor rebates are always tied to a supplier; the FK enables proper reporting and eliminates orphan rebate records.']
  superseded_vendor_rebate_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on vendor_rebate (superseded_vendor_rebate_id)']

  Note: 'Tracks volume rebates, marketing development funds (MDF), promotional allowances, and deviated pricing agreements with suppliers. Captures rebate type (volume tier, growth incentive, marketing allowance, new item introduction), calculation basis (percentage of spend, fixed amount per case, tiered brackets), accrual period, qualifying spend threshold, earned amount, claimed amount, settlement status, and payment method (credit memo, check, offset against AP). Critical for foodservice P&L management where vendor rebates can represent 2-5% of total food cost and directly impact COGS% and operator profitability.'
}

Table procurement.supplier_category_contract {
  supplier_category_contract_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the SupplierCategoryContract association']
  category_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the procurement category master']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the supplier master']
  contract_end_date date [note: 'Date the supplier-category contract expires']
  contract_start_date date [note: 'Date the supplier-category contract becomes effective']
  payment_terms varchar [note: 'Payment terms specific to this supplier-category agreement']
  preferred_vendor_flag boolean [note: 'Indicates if the supplier is marked as preferred for this category']
  vendor_rating varchar [note: 'Rating assigned to the supplier for this category']

  Note: 'Represents the contractual approval linking a supplier to a procurement category, capturing the period of validity, vendor rating, payment terms, and preferred‑supplier flag. Each record links one supplier to one procurement category and stores data that belongs to the relationship itself.. Existence Justification: Suppliers are approved to provide goods or services for multiple procurement categories, and each procurement category can have many approved suppliers. The approval is managed as a contract that includes start/end dates, vendor rating, payment terms, and a preferred‑supplier flag. Because the business actively creates, updates, and deletes these contracts, the relationship is a true many‑to‑many with its own attributes.'
}

Table procurement.supply_agreement {
  supply_agreement_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for the SupplyAgreement association']
  ingredient_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the ingredient']
  procurement_supplier_id varchar [note: 'Foreign key linking to the supplier']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Currency of the agreed price']
  price_amount decimal [note: 'Unit price agreed for the ingredient from this supplier']
  price_tier_max_qty varchar [note: 'Maximum quantity for this price tier']
  price_tier_min_qty varchar [note: 'Minimum quantity for this price tier']

  Note: 'Represents the contractual relationship between a supplier and an ingredient. Each record captures the price, quantity tiers, and currency that apply to that specific supplier‑ingredient pairing.. Existence Justification: Suppliers provide many ingredients and each ingredient can be sourced from multiple suppliers. The business tracks pricing tiers, minimum/maximum order quantities, and currency per supplier‑ingredient pair, which are managed as a contract-like agreement. This relationship is actively created, updated, and deleted by procurement teams.'
}

Table procurement.product {
  product_id varchar [pk, note: 'Primary key for product']
  parent_product_id varchar [note: 'Self-referencing FK on product (parent_product_id)']
  supplier_id varchar [note: 'Identifier of the primary supplier for this product.']
  allergen_info varchar [note: 'Allergens present in the product, listed as a comma‑separated string.']
  brand varchar [note: 'Manufacturer or brand associated with the product.']
  category varchar [note: 'High‑level classification of the product (e.g., food, packaging, equipment, service).']
  cost_price decimal(12,2) [note: 'Internal cost incurred to acquire or produce the product.']
  created_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp when the product record was first created in the system.']
  currency_code varchar [note: 'Three‑letter ISO currency code for the price.']
  description varchar [note: 'Detailed textual description of the product.']
  discontinued_date date [note: 'Date on which the product was officially discontinued.']
  effective_from date [note: 'Date when the product becomes available for procurement.']
  effective_until date [note: 'Date when the product is retired or no longer purchasable (null if open‑ended).']
  expiration_date date [note: 'Date after which the product should not be used or sold.']
  hazardous_material boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the product is classified as hazardous.']
  height_cm decimal(10,3) [note: 'Height dimension of the product packaging.']
  is_perishable boolean [note: 'Flag indicating whether the product has a limited shelf life.']
  lead_time_days varchar [note: 'Average number of days from order to receipt.']
  length_cm decimal(10,3) [note: 'Length dimension of the product packaging.']
  lifecycle_stage varchar [note: 'Strategic stage of the product within its overall lifecycle.']
  name varchar [note: 'Human‑readable name of the product.']
  nutritional_info varchar [note: 'Key nutrition facts (calories, fat, protein, etc.) stored as a structured string.']
  packaging_type varchar [note: 'Description of the packaging (e.g., box, pallet, bulk).']
  price decimal(12,2) [note: 'Current list price charged to internal buyers (before discounts).']
  product_line varchar [note: 'Higher‑level grouping of related products.']
  reorder_point_quantity varchar [note: 'Inventory level that triggers a new purchase requisition.']
  safety_stock_quantity varchar [note: 'Minimum inventory level maintained to avoid stockouts.']
  sku varchar [note: 'Internal code used to track the product in inventory and ordering systems.']
  status varchar [note: 'Current lifecycle state of the product.']
  subcategory varchar [note: 'More detailed classification within the main category.']
  tax_code varchar [note: 'Tax classification code applied to the product for sales tax calculations.']
  unit_of_measure varchar [note: 'Measurement unit used for quantity ordering.']
  upc varchar [note: '12‑digit barcode identifier for the product.']
  updated_timestamp timestamp [note: 'Timestamp of the most recent update to the product record.']
  volume_liters decimal(10,3) [note: 'Physical volume of a single unit of the product.']
  weight_kg decimal(10,3) [note: 'Physical weight of a single unit of the product.']
  width_cm decimal(10,3) [note: 'Width dimension of the product packaging.']

  Note: 'Master reference table for product. Referenced by product_id.'
}

  procurement.item_specification
  procurement.vendor_rebate
  procurement.supplier_category_contract
  procurement.supply_agreement
  procurement.product
}
